New serial number 36B is added for services of insurance provided by the Motor Vehicle Accident Fund, with a tax rate of Nil.
An additional item (f) is added under serial number 69 for training partners approved by the National Skill Development Corporation.
Item (w) is omitted from paragraph 2 of the parent notification effective from 2025-04-01.
A new item (zja) is added to define 'insurer' as per the Insurance Act, 1938.
amended_refs: 08/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) published in the Gazette of India Extraordinary

70.summary: On January 16, 2025, the Government of India issued Notification No. 07/2025, which amends the earlier notification 13/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This amendment specifically alters the definitions in the Table of the previous notification. For serial number 4, it now specifies that 'Any person' excludes 'a body corporate', clarifying that corporate entities are not included in this category. Additionally, for serial number 5AB, the amendment states that 'Any registered person' does not include those who have opted for the composition levy, which is a simplified tax scheme for small businesses. These changes are significant for businesses operating in Union Territories, as they refine the categories of taxpayers subject to different tax rates. Companies and registered individuals should review these changes to understand their tax obligations better, especially in relation to corporate structures and composition schemes.
key_points: This notification is issued on 16th January 2025.
It amends notification number 13/2017 - Union Territory (Rate) published on 28th June 2017.
In the Table of the earlier notification, against serial number 4, the phrase 'other than a body corporate' is added after 'Any person'.
For serial number 5AB, the phrase 'other than a person who has opted to pay tax under composition levy' is added after 'Any registered person'.
amended_refs: 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was published in the Gazette of India

71.summary: On January 16, 2025, the Government of India issued Notification No. 08/2025, which amends the earlier Notification No. 17/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This amendment specifically changes the definition of 'specified premises' in the Explanation section of the original notification. The new definition now aligns with clause (xxxvi) of paragraph 4 from Notification No. 11/2017, which was also related to Union Territory Tax. This change is significant as it clarifies the meaning of 'specified premises' for tax purposes. The updated definition will be effective from April 1, 2025, giving businesses time to adjust to this change. It's important to note that the last amendment to Notification No. 17/2017 was made on October 19, 2023, indicating that the government is actively updating tax regulations. This amendment will affect all entities operating in Union Territories that are subject to these tax regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 08/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 2025-01-16.
This notification amends notification number 17/2017-Union Territory (Rate) published on 28th June 2017.
The amendment substitutes item (c) in the Explanation with a new definition of 'specified premises'.
The new definition refers to clause (xxxvi) of paragraph 4 of notification number 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28.06.2017.
The changes will come into effect from 1st April 2025.
The last amendment to notification 17/2017 was made by notification number 16/2023 on 19th October 2023.

72.summary: On September 18, 2025, a corrigendum was issued to amend the Union Territory Tax (Rate) notification dated September 17, 2025. This corrigendum specifically addresses errors in the numbering of items listed on page 106 of the original notification. The items were incorrectly numbered, and the corrigendum corrects these to ensure proper alignment: 'b.' is changed to 'a.', 'c.' to 'b.', 'd.' to 'c.', and so forth, up to 'i.' being changed to 'h.'. These adjustments are crucial for maintaining clarity regarding the goods and their respective tax rates, which are part of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework. All stakeholders involved in the supply of the goods specified in the notification will be affected by these corrections, as they ensure that the tax rates are accurately associated with the correct items. This amendment helps prevent confusion and ensures compliance with the intended tax structure.
key_points: The corrigendum was issued on 2025-09-18.
It amends notification 09/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 2025-09-17.
The changes involve corrections to the numbering of items listed on page 106.
Specifically, the items were renumbered from 'b.' to 'a.', 'c.' to 'b.', 'd.' to 'c.', 'e.' to 'd.', 'f.' to 'e.', 'g.' to 'f.', 'h.' to 'g.', and 'i.' to 'h.'.
These changes affect the clarity of the listed goods and their respective tax rates.
The corrections ensure that the tax rates correspond accurately to the correct items in the notification.
The notification is part of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework.
The changes are applicable to all stakeholders involved in the supply of goods listed in the notification.

73.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 09/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on September 17, 2025, which introduces new tax rates for various goods under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act. This notification supersedes the earlier Notification No. 01/2017, which was effective from June 28, 2017. The new tax rates are structured across several schedules: 2.5% for goods in Schedule I, 9% for Schedule II, 20% for Schedule III, 1.5% for Schedule IV, 0.125% for Schedule V, 0.75% for Schedule VI, and 14% for Schedule VII. These rates will apply to intra-State supplies of the specified goods, which are detailed in the respective Schedules. The changes are effective immediately from the date of the notification, impacting businesses involved in the supply of these goods within the Union Territories. This update is crucial for businesses to align their pricing and compliance strategies accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 09/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 2025-09-17.
Supersedes Notification No. 01/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Establishes new union territory tax rates for various goods as specified in Schedules I to VII.
Tax rates include 2.5% for goods in Schedule I, 9% for Schedule II, 20% for Schedule III, 1.5% for Schedule IV, 0.125% for Schedule V, 0.75% for Schedule VI, and 14% for Schedule VII.
The rates apply to intra-State supplies of goods listed in the corresponding entries of the Schedules.
The notification is effective from the date of issuance, 17th September 2025.

74.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to the notification number 10/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 17th September 2025. This corrigendum, released on 18th September 2025, corrects the numbering of certain entries listed on page 72 of the original notification. The changes involve adjusting the entries from (xxi) through (xxx) to a new numbering format starting from (i) to (x). This means that the previous Roman numeral designations have been replaced with standard numeric designations. These corrections are important for clarity and accuracy in the application of the tax rates specified in the original notification. Affected parties should refer to the updated numbering to ensure they are using the correct references in their compliance and reporting processes.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to notification number 10/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 17th September 2025.
The corrigendum was published on 18th September 2025.
Changes were made to the numbering of entries on page 72 of the original notification.

Specifically, the entries were corrected from (xxi) to (i), (xxii) to (ii), (xxiii) to (iii), (xxiv) to (iv), (xxv) to (v), (xxvi) to (vi), (xxvii) to (vii), (xxviii) to (viii), (xxix) to (ix), and (xxx) to (x).

75.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 10/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 17th September 2025, which supersedes the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This new notification exempts intra-State supplies of a wide range of specified goods from the entire Union Territory tax as per section 7 of the UTGST Act. The exemption covers various categories of goods, including live animals such as poultry and bovine, fresh or chilled meat, fish, dairy products like milk and curd, and a variety of fruits and vegetables. In total, there are 139 entries in the Schedule detailing the specific goods that qualify for this exemption. This change is aimed at supporting local businesses and is based on recommendations from the GST Council, highlighting the government's commitment to promoting economic activity in the Union Territories. Businesses dealing with these goods will benefit from this tax exemption, which is intended to ease their financial burden and encourage trade.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 17th September 2025.
Supersedes Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Exempts intra-State supplies of specified goods from the whole of Union Territory tax under section 7 of the UTGST Act.
The exemption applies to various goods listed in the Schedule, including live animals, fresh or chilled meat, fish, dairy products, fruits, vegetables, and certain agricultural products.
The Schedule includes 139 entries detailing specific goods eligible for the exemption.
The exemption is applicable to goods such as live poultry, fresh milk, fresh fruits, and various seeds.
The notification emphasizes that the exemption is in the public interest and follows recommendations from the GST Council.

76.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 11/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 17th September 2025, which amends the earlier notification No. 3/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, this amendment alters the tax rate listed under column (4) for S. No. 1 in the table, changing it to '9%'. This change is significant as it directly affects the applicable tax rate for goods and services under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework. The new rate will take effect from 22nd September 2025, meaning businesses operating in the Union Territories should prepare for this adjustment in their tax calculations. This amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to update tax rates in response to economic conditions and public interest. Businesses should ensure they are aware of this change to remain compliant with the new tax rate.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 17th September 2025.
Amends notification No. 3/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the entry under column (4) for S. No. 1 in the table from the previous rate to '9%'.
This amendment will come into force on 22nd September 2025.

77.summary: On 17th September 2025, the Government of India issued Notification No. 12/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 25th January 2018. The key change involves substituting the reference from 'Schedule IV of Notification No. 1/2017-Union Tax (Rate)' to 'Schedule II or Schedule III of Notification No. 9/2025 – Union Territory Tax (Rate)'. This means that the applicable schedules for certain tax rates have been updated to align with the new notification issued in 2025. Businesses and individuals affected by this change should note that these amendments will take effect from 22nd September 2025. This update is part of the government's ongoing efforts to streamline tax regulations and ensure they reflect current economic conditions and policy objectives. Stakeholders should review the new schedules to understand how their tax obligations may change as a result of this amendment.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 17th September 2025.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 25th January 2018.
It substitutes the reference from 'Schedule IV of Notification No. 1/2017-Union Tax (Rate)' to 'Schedule II or Schedule III of Notification No. 9/2025 – Union Territory Tax (Rate)'.
The changes will come into effect on 22nd September 2025.

78.summary: Notification No. 13/2025 amends the earlier Notification No. 21/2018 regarding the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax rates. Effective from September 22, 2025, this notification introduces a uniform tax rate of 2.5% for a wide range of goods, including handmade candles, wooden products, and various handicrafts. It specifically lists items such as handbags, jewelry boxes, and decorative products, which will now be taxed at this new rate. This change affects manufacturers and sellers of these goods in the Union Territories, as they will need to adjust their pricing and tax calculations accordingly. The previous amendment to the tax rates was made through Notification No. 20/2021, which was issued on December 28, 2021. This latest notification aims to streamline the tax structure for these products, making it easier for businesses to comply with GST regulations.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 21/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated July 26, 2018.
The new rates for various goods have been established at 2.5%.
The effective date for this notification is September 22, 2025.
Specific goods affected include handmade candles, wooden products, and various handicrafts.
The notification includes detailed entries for tax rates on items like handbags, jewelry boxes, and decorative items.
The previous amendment was made by Notification No. 20/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated December 28, 2021.

79.summary: Notification No. 14/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 17th September 2025, establishes a new 6% Union Territory tax on certain goods. This tax will apply to intra-State supplies of goods listed in the accompanying Schedule, which includes fly ash bricks, bricks of fossil meals, building bricks, and earthen or roofing tiles. The notification will take effect from 22nd September 2025. This change is based on recommendations from the GST Council and aims to clarify the tax obligations for these specific goods. The tariff classifications for these items are aligned with the Customs Tariff Act, 1975, ensuring consistency in how these goods are categorized. Businesses dealing with these products should prepare for the new tax rate as it will directly impact their pricing and compliance from the effective date.
key_points: The notification is issued on 17th September 2025.
It introduces a 6% Union Territory tax on specific goods.
The tax applies to intra-State supplies of goods listed in the Schedule.
The goods affected include fly ash bricks, bricks of fossil meals, building bricks, and earthen or roofing tiles.
The notification will come into force on 22nd September 2025.
The relevant tariff items and descriptions are specified in the Schedule.
The notification is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
It references the Customs Tariff Act, 1975 for tariff classifications.

80.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 15/2025, which amends the earlier notification No. 11/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax (Rate). This new notification, effective from 22nd September 2025, updates the tax rates for various services, particularly in the transport and job work sectors, with many entries now set at either 9% or 2.5%. Additionally, it introduces specific conditions for claiming input tax credit on transport services, ensuring that businesses understand when they can benefit from these credits. The definitions of 'goods transport agency' and 'multimodal transporter' have also been clarified to enhance compliance. Furthermore, the notification includes new explanations related to hotel accommodation services and the process for amending registration, which will take effect from 1st April 2025. Certain items have been removed from the tax rate table, and existing entries have been modified for better clarity. Lastly, it provides definitions for terms like 'recognised sporting event' and 'handicraft goods' to align with previous regulations, ensuring that businesses are well-informed about the applicable tax rates and conditions.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 17th September 2025.
Amends notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) effective from 22nd September 2025.
Changes tax rates for certain services, including transport and job work, with several entries updated to a rate of 9 or 2.5.
Introduces new conditions for input tax credit related to transport services, specifying when credit can be claimed.
Updates definitions for terms like 'goods transport agency' and 'multimodal transporter' in the context of the Union Territory GST.
Inserts new explanations regarding hotel accommodation services and registration amendments effective from 1st April 2025.
Removes certain items from the tax rate table and modifies existing entries for clarity and compliance.
Clarifies the meaning of 'recognised sporting event' and 'handicraft goods' as per previous notifications.

81.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 16/2025 amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This new notification, effective from September 22, 2025, introduces several important changes. Firstly, it clarifies that local delivery services provided by Electronic Commerce Operators are not eligible for certain exemptions. Additionally, it adds new entries for life insurance and health insurance services, specifying that these exemptions apply only to individual contracts, including family members. Furthermore, a new entry for reinsurance services related to these insurance types has been included. The definition of 'goods transport agency' has also been revised to exclude electronic commerce operators involved in local deliveries. Lastly, a new definition of 'group' has been introduced to clarify the relationships among individuals in insurance contracts. These changes will primarily affect insurance providers and electronic commerce operators operating within the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 2025-09-17.
This notification amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) published on 2017-06-28.
An explanation has been added to serial number 18, clarifying that local delivery services provided by an Electronic Commerce Operator are excluded from the exemption.
New entries have been added after serial number 36B for services related to life insurance (36C) and health insurance (36D) provided to individuals, with specific exemptions.
Entry 36E has been added for reinsurance services related to life and health insurance.
The definition of 'goods transport agency' has been updated to exclude electronic commerce operators providing local delivery services.
A new clause defining 'group' for insurance purposes has been added, clarifying the relationships involved.
The notification will come into effect on 2025-09-22.
amended_refs: 06/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) published in the Gazette of India Extraordinary

82.summary: On 17th September 2025, the Government of India issued Notification No. 17/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 17/2017-Union Territory (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The key change is the addition of a new clause (v) that addresses services related to local delivery. This new clause states that such services are exempt from Union Territory GST unless the service provider is required to register under specific provisions of the UTGST Act. This means that if a person supplying local delivery services is not liable for registration, they will not have to charge GST on those services. The amendment will take effect from 22nd September 2025. This change is particularly relevant for businesses involved in local delivery services, especially those using electronic commerce platforms, as it clarifies their tax obligations under the UTGST framework. The last amendment to the principal notification was made earlier in January 2025, indicating ongoing adjustments to the tax regulations.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 17/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 17th September 2025.
It amends Notification No. 17/2017-Union Territory (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new clause (v) is added after clause (iv) in the original notification.
The new clause (v) covers services by way of local delivery, with specific conditions regarding electronic commerce operators.
The amendment specifies that the local delivery services are exempt unless the supplier is liable for registration under clause (vi) of section 21 of the UTGST Act.
This notification will come into effect on 22nd September 2025.
The last amendment to the principal notification was made by Notification No. 08/2025-Union Territory (Rate) on 16th January 2025.

84.summary: Notification No. 19/2025, issued on December 31, 2025, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 9/2025 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it adds new entries to Schedule II and Schedule III, which detail the tax rates for various tobacco products. For instance, Biris will now be classified under HS Codes 2403 19 21 and 2403 19 29 at a 9% tax rate. Additionally, Schedule III will include Pan masala, Unmanufactured tobacco, and Cigars among others, all subject to a 20% tax rate. Notably, the notification also omits Schedule VII, which previously listed items at a 14% tax rate. These changes will take effect from February 1, 2026, impacting businesses dealing with these tobacco products by updating their tax obligations accordingly. This amendment reflects the government's ongoing adjustments to the GST framework in response to market needs and regulatory requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 19/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st December 2025.
This notification amends Notification No. 9/2025-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 17th September 2025.
Inserts new entries in Schedule II – 9% after S. No. 4.
Inserts new entries in Schedule III – 20% after S. No. 13.
New entries include Biris under HS Code 2403 19 21 and 2403 19 29.
New entries also include Pan masala, Unmanufactured tobacco, Cigars, and other tobacco products under various HS Codes.
Omission of Schedule VII – 14% and its related entries.
This notification will come into force on 1st February 2026.

85.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2024-Compensation Cess (Rate) on July 12, 2024, which introduces an important exemption for certain goods. Specifically, it exempts the supply of goods classified under heading 2202 by Unit Run Canteens (URCs) to authorized customers from the entire Goods and Services Tax Compensation Cess. This exemption is applicable under section 8 of the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017, and will come into effect on July 15, 2024. The notification also clarifies that the definitions of tariff items and headings will follow the First Schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This change is significant for URCs as it reduces their tax burden when supplying these specific goods, thereby potentially lowering costs for authorized customers. Overall, this move is aimed at benefiting both the URCs and their customers by alleviating the financial impact of the compensation cess on these supplies.
key_points: The notification is titled '01/2024-Compensation Cess (Rate)' and was issued on 2024-07-12.
It exempts the supply of goods under heading 2202 by a Unit Run Canteen (URC) to authorized customers from the entire Goods and Services Tax Compensation Cess.
This exemption is granted under section 8 of the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017.
The exemption is effective from 2024-07-15.
The notification references the First Schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975 for definitions of tariff items and headings.

86.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2024-Central Tax, which extends the deadline for filing the GSTR-3B return for the month of November 2023. The new due date is now set for January 10, 2024. This extension specifically applies to registered taxpayers whose principal place of business is located in the districts of Tirunelveli, Tenkasi, Kanyakumari, Thoothukudi, and Virudhunagar in Tamil Nadu. The decision was made under the authority of sub-section (6) of section 39 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This notification will take effect from December 20, 2023. Taxpayers in the specified districts should take note of this change to ensure they file their returns by the new deadline.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for November 2023 to January 10, 2024.
Applicable to registered persons in Tirunelveli, Tenkasi, Kanyakumari, Thoothukudi, and Virudhunagar districts of Tamil Nadu.
This extension is granted under sub-section (6) of section 39 of the CGST Act, 2017.
The notification is effective from December 20, 2023.

87.summary: The recent Notification No. 01/2024-Integrated Tax, issued on 10th July 2024, brings a significant change to the tax rate specified in Notification No. 02/2018-Integrated Tax from 20th September 2018. Specifically, the tax rate has been reduced from 'one per cent.' to 'half per cent.' This change is enacted under the second proviso to section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and also references section 52 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This amendment will be effective immediately upon its publication in the official gazette. Businesses and individuals who are subject to this tax will need to adjust their calculations and compliance accordingly. The reduction in the tax rate may provide some relief to taxpayers, potentially lowering their tax liabilities. It is essential for affected parties to be aware of this change to ensure accurate reporting and compliance moving forward.
key_points: Notification No. 01/2024-Integrated Tax issued on 10th July 2024.
Amends Notification No. 02/2018-Integrated Tax dated 20th September 2018.
Changes the tax rate from 'one per cent.' to 'half per cent.'
The amendment is made under the second proviso to section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Also refers to sub-section (1) of section 52 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The amendment takes effect from the date of its publication in the official gazette.

88.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 02/2024-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, effective from December 31, 2023. This amendment introduces new deadlines for registered persons in specific districts of Tamil Nadu for the financial year 2022-2023. Specifically, businesses located in Chennai, Tiruvallur, Chengalpattu, Kancheepuram, Tirunelveli, Tenkasi, Kanyakumari, Thoothukudi, and Virudhunagar must submit their annual return and a self-certified reconciliation statement by January 10, 2024. The amendment adds sub-rules (1B) and (3B) to rule 80, clearly outlining these requirements. This change is significant for businesses in the mentioned districts, as it provides a specific deadline for compliance that differs from previous regulations. The amendment follows earlier updates made in Notification No. 52/2023-Central Tax, ensuring that businesses are aware of their obligations for the specified financial year.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Rules, 2024'.
It comes into force on December 31, 2023.
For the financial year 2022-2023, registered persons in specific Tamil Nadu districts must file their annual return by January 10, 2024.
The affected districts in Tamil Nadu include Chennai, Tiruvallur, Chengalpattu, Kancheepuram, Tirunelveli, Tenkasi, Kanyakumari, Thoothukudi, and Virudhunagar.
A new sub-rule (1B) is added to rule 80, specifying the January 10, 2024 deadline for the annual return.
A new sub-rule (3B) is also added to rule 80, requiring the self-certified reconciliation statement to be submitted by the same deadline.
This amendment follows the previous notification No. 52/2023-Central Tax, dated October 26, 2023.

89.summary: Notification No. 03/2024-Central Tax, issued on January 5, 2024, rescinds the earlier Notification No. 30/2023-CT, which was published on July 31, 2023. This means that any provisions or rules established in the previous notification are no longer in effect from January 1, 2024, except for any actions that were already taken under that notification prior to its rescission. The decision to rescind was made under the authority of section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change affects all stakeholders who were adhering to the rules set forth in Notification No. 30/2023-CT, as they will need to adjust their practices in light of this rescission. It is important for businesses to review their compliance and operational procedures to align with the current regulations following this change.
key_points: Notification No. 03/2024-Central Tax was issued on January 5, 2024.
This notification rescinds Notification No. 30/2023-CT dated July 31, 2023.
The rescission applies except for actions taken before the rescission.
The effective date of this notification is January 1, 2024.
The rescission is based on the powers conferred by section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

90.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 04/2024-Central Tax, effective from 1st April 2024, establishes a special procedure for registered manufacturers of specific goods, particularly pan masala and various tobacco products. Manufacturers are required to submit details of their packing machines using FORM GST SRM-I within 30 days of the notification's effect. For those newly registered after this notification, the deadline is shortened to 15 days. Any changes in the capacity of these machines must be reported within 24 hours in Table 6A of the same form. Additionally, manufacturers must submit a special monthly statement in FORM GST SRM-II by the 10th of the following month. A certificate from a Chartered Engineer must also be uploaded in FORM GST SRM-III for the machines declared. This notification specifically outlines the goods covered under Chapters 21 and 24, ensuring that manufacturers are aware of their obligations regarding the reporting and registration of their packing machines.
key_points: Notification No. 04/2024-Central Tax is issued on 5th January 2024.
The notification introduces a special procedure for registered persons manufacturing specific goods listed in the Schedule.
Manufacturers must submit details of packing machines in FORM GST SRM-I within 30 days of the notification's effect on 1st April 2024.
New manufacturers must submit packing machine details in FORM GST SRM-I within 15 days of registration.
Changes in machine capacity must be reported within 24 hours in Table 6A of FORM GST SRM-I.
A special monthly statement in FORM GST SRM-II must be submitted by the 10th of the following month.
A certificate from a Chartered Engineer must be uploaded in FORM GST SRM-III for the declared machines.
The notification specifies goods under Chapter 21 and 24, including pan masala and various types of tobacco.

91.summary: The recent Notification No. 05/2024-Central Tax, issued on 30th January 2024, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax, which was originally published on 19th June 2017. Specifically, this amendment affects Table II, serial number 83, in column (3), clause (ii). The change involves the insertion of the figure and letter '411069' immediately following '411060'. This adjustment is significant for businesses that deal with the specified goods or services associated with these codes, as it updates the classification under the GST framework. The amendment ensures that the latest codes are recognized for tax purposes, which may impact how businesses report their transactions. Companies should review their GST filings and ensure they are aligned with this updated classification to maintain compliance with the GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2024-Central Tax was issued on 30th January 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.
In Table II of Notification No. 02/2017, serial number 83, column (3), clause (ii) is amended.
The amendment inserts the figure and letter '411069' after '411060' in the specified clause.

92.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 06/2024-Central Tax on 22nd February 2024 introduces the 'Public Tech Platform for Frictionless Credit', which is designed to enhance information sharing among financial service providers. This platform operates under the consent provisions of subsection (2) of Section 158A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Developed by the Reserve Bank Innovation Hub, this initiative aims to streamline access to various data sources digitally, thereby improving the efficiency of credit operations. The platform employs an enterprise-grade open architecture and utilizes a standard Application Programming Interface (API) framework, allowing multiple data service providers to converge on a single platform. This move aligns with the Reserve Bank of India's broader objectives outlined in its 'Statement on Developmental and Regulatory Policies' from 10th August 2023. Businesses and financial institutions will benefit from this enhanced digital infrastructure, which is expected to simplify processes and improve access to credit information.
key_points: Notification No. 06/2024-Central Tax issued on 22nd February 2024.
Introduces the 'Public Tech Platform for Frictionless Credit' for information sharing.
This platform is based on consent under subsection (2) of Section 158A of the CGST Act, 2017.
The platform is developed by the Reserve Bank Innovation Hub.
It aims to facilitate access to information from various data sources digitally.
The platform uses an open architecture and standard API framework.
This initiative is part of the RBI's 'Statement on Developmental and Regulatory Policies' dated 10th August 2023.

93.summary: Notification No. 07/2024-Central Tax, issued on April 8, 2024, establishes a 'Nil' interest rate for certain registered persons who were unable to file their GSTR-3B returns on time due to technical issues with the GST portal. This applies specifically to those who had sufficient balances in their electronic cash or credit ledgers or had made the necessary payments through challans. The months affected by this notification include June 2018, October 2018, and from July 2017 to February 2018. The interest relief is effective from the original due date for filing these returns until the actual date they were filed. This decision is based on the recommendations of the GST Council and is grounded in sections 50(1) and 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Registered persons with specific GST Identification Numbers are the ones benefiting from this notification, which aims to alleviate the burden caused by the technical glitches experienced during the filing period.
key_points: The interest rate per annum is set to 'Nil' for specific registered persons.
This applies to those who failed to file GSTR-3B due to technical glitches on the portal.
The affected months include June 2018, October 2018, July 2017, August 2017, and from July 2017 to February 2018.
The interest relief is applicable from the due date of filing GSTR-3B to the actual date of filing.
The notification is issued under section 50(1) and section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The registered persons affected are identified by specific GST Identification Numbers.
This notification is dated April 8, 2024.

94.summary: Notification No. 08/2024-Central Tax, issued on 10th April 2024, amends the earlier Notification No. 04/2024-Central Tax from 5th January 2024. The key change is the extension of a deadline, moving it from the 1st of April 2024 to the 15th of May 2024. This amendment is made under section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, which allows the government to make such changes based on recommendations from the GST Council. The new deadline will be applicable from 1st April 2024. This change affects businesses and taxpayers who were expected to meet the original deadline, providing them with additional time to comply with the requirements set forth in the earlier notification. It is important for affected parties to note this new date and adjust their plans accordingly to ensure they meet the revised timeline.
key_points: Notification No. 08/2024-Central Tax was issued on 10th April 2024.
This notification amends Notification No. 04/2024-Central Tax dated 5th January 2024.
The amendment changes the deadline from '1st day of April, 2024' to '15th day of May, 2024'.
The amendment is made under the powers conferred by section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The changes will come into effect from 1st April 2024.

95.summary: On 12th April 2024, the Government of India issued Notification No. 09/2024-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax from 10th November 2020. This amendment introduces a new proviso that extends the deadline for filing the GSTR-1 form for the tax period of March 2024. Specifically, registered persons who are required to furnish returns under section 39(1) of the CGST Act now have until 12th April 2024 to submit their GSTR-1. This extension is significant for those taxpayers who may have faced challenges in meeting the original deadline. The amendment is effective from 11th April 2024, meaning that the new deadline applies to any filings due after this date. This change aims to provide relief to businesses during the tax filing process, ensuring they have adequate time to comply with the requirements.
key_points: The notification is issued on 12th April 2024.
It amends Notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax dated 10th November 2020.
A new proviso is added after the fourth proviso in Notification No. 83/2020.
The new proviso extends the deadline for filing GSTR-1 for March 2024 to 12th April 2024.
This extension applies to registered persons required to furnish returns under section 39(1) of the CGST Act.
The amendment is effective from 11th April 2024.

96.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 10/2024-Central Tax, dated 29th May 2024, amends the earlier Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax from 19th June 2017. This amendment specifically updates the entries in Table II concerning various districts in Rajasthan. Effective from 5th August 2023, the new entries replace the previous ones for serial numbers 7, 49, 53, and 102. For serial number 7, the Alwar District and its associated tehsils have been redefined. Similarly, serial number 49 now includes updated details for the Jaipur District and its tehsils. The Jodhpur District's classification has also been revised under serial number 53, and the Udaipur District's details have been updated under serial number 102. These changes are significant for businesses operating in these regions as they affect how GST is applied and reported in these specific districts and tehsils of Rajasthan.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2024-Central Tax issued on 29th May 2024.
Amends Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.
Substitutes entries for serial number 7 in Table II, effective from 5th August 2023, for Alwar District and specific tehsils in Rajasthan.
Substitutes entries for serial number 49 in Table II for Jaipur District and specific tehsils in Rajasthan.
Substitutes entries for serial number 53 in Table II for Jodhpur District and surrounding areas in Rajasthan.
Substitutes entries for serial number 102 in Table II for Udaipur District and surrounding areas in Rajasthan.
These changes affect the classification of districts and tehsils for GST purposes in Rajasthan.

97.summary: The recent Notification No. 11/2024-Central Tax, issued on 30th May 2024, brings specific amendments to the earlier Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax from 19th June 2017. Notably, at serial number 7 in Table II, the description of tehsils has been revised; it now includes only 'Neem ka Thana, Jhunjunu and', removing references to Behror, Bansur, Neemrana, Mandan, and Narayanpur tehsils. Additionally, at serial number 49, the mention of 'Kotputli, Viratnagar and Shahpura tehsils of district Kotputli-Behror' has been completely omitted. These changes may affect businesses operating in these regions by clarifying the specific areas covered under the GST provisions. It is essential for businesses to review these amendments to understand their implications on compliance and reporting requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2024-Central Tax was issued on 30th May 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.

At serial number 7 in Table II, the description for certain tehsils is changed from 'Neem ka Thana and Jhunjhunu and Behror, Bansur, Neemrana, Mandan and Narayanpur tehsils of district' to 'Neem ka Thana, Jhunjunu and'.
At serial number 49 in Table II, the words 'and Kotputli, Viratnagar and Shahpura tehsils of district Kotputli-Behror' are omitted.

98.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 12/2024-Central Tax, dated 10th July 2024, brings several amendments to the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. A significant change is the new requirement in rule 8(4A) that applicants, who have not opted for Aadhaar authentication, must take a photograph and verify their documents at designated Facilitation Centers. Additionally, rule 21 now allows for amendments in FORM GSTR-1 through FORM GSTR-1A. The notification also revises rule 39, detailing how Input Service Distributors should distribute input tax credit, ensuring it is done in the same month and based on turnover. Furthermore, rule 59 introduces the option for registered persons to amend details in FORM GSTR-1A before filing FORM GSTR-3B. A notable change effective from 1st August 2024 is the reduction of the threshold for certain provisions from two and a half lakh rupees to one lakh rupees. Lastly, new requirements for additional details in FORM GSTR-1A regarding inter-State and intra-State supplies have been established, impacting how businesses report their transactions.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2024-Central Tax issued on 10th July 2024.
Amends Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
Inserts a new proviso in rule 8(4A) requiring applicants to take a photograph and verify documents at designated Facilitation Centers.
In rule 21, adds that FORM GSTR-1 may be amended in FORM GSTR-1A.
In rule 39, substitutes sub-rule (1) to detail how Input Service Distributors must distribute input tax credit.
In rule 59, allows amendments in FORM GSTR-1A before filing FORM GSTR-3B.
Changes the threshold for certain provisions in rule 59(4) from two and a half lakh rupees to one lakh rupees effective 1st August 2024.
Introduces new requirements for additional details in FORM GSTR-1A regarding inter-State and intra-State supplies.

99.summary: Notification No. 13/2024-Central Tax, issued on 10th July 2024, rescinds the earlier Notification No. 27/2022-Central Tax, which was published on 26th December 2022. This means that all provisions and rules established under the 2022 notification are no longer in effect, except for any actions that were taken before this new notification was published. The rescission is authorized under sub-rule (4B) of rule 8 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This change affects all stakeholders who were relying on the provisions of the 2022 notification, as they will need to adjust their practices and compliance measures accordingly. The new notification comes into force immediately upon its publication in the Official Gazette, signaling a significant shift in the regulatory landscape for GST compliance. Businesses and tax professionals should take note of this change to ensure they are aligned with the current requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2024-Central Tax was issued on 10th July 2024.
This notification rescinds Notification No. 27/2022-Central Tax dated 26th December 2022.
The rescission applies to all provisions of Notification No. 27/2022 except for actions taken before this rescission.
The rescission is based on the powers conferred by sub-rule (4B) of rule 8 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.

100.summary: Notification No. 14/2024-Central Tax, issued on July 10, 2024, introduces an important exemption for registered persons under the GST framework. Specifically, those whose aggregate turnover does not exceed two crore rupees in the financial year 2023-24 are now exempt from filing their annual return for that year. This change is made under the first proviso to section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's effort to ease compliance burdens for smaller businesses. The exemption is based on recommendations from the GST Council, indicating a focus on supporting businesses with lower turnover. This means that if your business falls within this turnover limit, you will not need to submit the annual return for the specified financial year, simplifying your reporting obligations significantly. This change is particularly beneficial for small enterprises, allowing them to allocate resources more efficiently without the need for extensive paperwork related to annual returns.
key_points: Exempts registered persons with aggregate turnover up to two crore rupees from filing the annual return for FY 2023-24.
The notification is issued under the first proviso to section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The exemption applies specifically to the financial year 2023-24.
This decision is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.

101.summary: On 10th July 2024, the Central Government issued Notification No. 15/2024-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 52/2018-Central Tax from 20th September 2018. The key change is the reduction of the tax rate from 'half per cent.' to '0.25 per cent.' This adjustment is made under section 52 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, which pertains to the tax collection at source (TCS) for certain transactions. The new rate of 0.25 per cent. will take effect immediately upon publication in the official gazette. This change will impact businesses that are required to collect TCS, as they will now need to apply the lower rate in their transactions. It is essential for affected businesses to update their systems and processes to reflect this new tax rate to with the revised regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2024-Central Tax was issued on 10th July 2024.
This notification amends Notification No. 52/2018-Central Tax dated 20th September 2018.
The amendment changes the tax rate from 'half per cent.' to '0.25 per cent.'
The change applies to the provisions outlined in section 52 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The new rate of 0.25 per cent. will be effective from the date of publication in the official gazette.

102.summary: Notification No. 16/2024-Central Tax, issued on 6th August 2024, outlines the effective dates for specific sections of the Finance Act, 2024. Specifically, it states that Section 13 will come into effect on 1st October 2024, while Sections 11 and 12 will take effect on 1st April 2025. This means that businesses and individuals should prepare for the changes introduced by these sections, as they will impact various aspects of tax compliance and administration. The notification is part of the government's ongoing efforts to implement the provisions of the Finance Act, which aims to streamline tax processes. The publication in the Gazette of India ensures that the information is officially recorded and accessible. Stakeholders should take note of these dates to align their operations with the new regulations as they come into force.
key_points: The notification is issued on 6th August 2024.
Sections 13 of the Finance Act, 2024 will come into force on 1st October 2024.
Sections 11 and 12 of the Finance Act, 2024 will come into force on 1st April 2025.
This notification is issued under the powers of clause (b) of sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Finance Act, 2024.
The notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (ii).

103.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 17/2024-Central Tax on 27th September 2024, which outlines the commencement dates for specific sections of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2024. Sections 118, 142, 148, and 150 will take effect immediately upon publication of this notification. Meanwhile, a broader set of sections, specifically 114 to 117, 119 to 141, 143 to 147, 149, and 151 to 157, will come into force on 1st November 2024. This means that businesses and stakeholders should prepare for the implications of these sections as they will introduce new provisions or amend existing ones related to GST. The notification is issued under the authority granted by the Finance Act, ensuring that the changes are legally binding. It is crucial for affected parties to be aware of these dates to align their operations and compliance strategies accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 17/2024-Central Tax issued on 27th September 2024.
Sections 118, 142, 148, and 150 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2024 come into force on the date of publication.
Sections 114 to 117, 119 to 141, 143 to 147, 149, and 151 to 157 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2024 will come into force on 1st November 2024.
This notification is issued under the powers of clause (b) of sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2024.

104.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 18/2024-Central Tax, effective from 1st October 2024, introduces a significant change in how input tax credits and tax rate reductions are monitored. It empowers the Principal Bench of the Appellate Tribunal to assess whether the input tax credits claimed by registered persons or any reductions in tax rates have led to a corresponding decrease in the prices of goods or services provided by them. This means that businesses claiming these credits or benefiting from lower tax rates will now be subject to scrutiny to ensure that the benefits are passed on to consumers. The notification is grounded in specific provisions of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, particularly sections 171 and 109. This change aims to enhance transparency and ensure that tax benefits are reflected in pricing, ultimately benefiting consumers. All registered persons should be aware of this new oversight mechanism as it may impact their pricing strategies and compliance practices.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2024-Central Tax issued on 30th September 2024.
Effective from 1st October 2024.
Empowers the Principal Bench of the Appellate Tribunal to examine input tax credits.
Focuses on whether tax rate reductions lead to a corresponding decrease in prices.
Based on sub-section (2) of section 171 and sub-section (1) and second proviso to sub-section (5) of section 109 of the CGST Act, 2017.
Applies to all registered persons availing input tax credits or benefiting from tax rate reductions.

105.summary: Notification No. 19/2024-Central Tax, issued on 30th September 2024, introduces a significant change regarding the examination of input tax credits and tax rate reductions under the GST framework. Effective from 1st April 2025, the Authority will no longer accept requests to determine whether the input tax credits availed by registered persons or any reductions in tax rates have resulted in a corresponding decrease in the prices of goods or services supplied. This decision is based on the recommendations made by the Goods and Services Tax Council. The change aims to streamline processes and reduce administrative burdens for businesses. It is essential for registered persons to be aware that after this date, they will not be able to seek validation from the Authority regarding the impact of tax credits or rate changes on pricing. This notification will take effect as soon as it is published in the Official Gazette, marking a new phase in the GST compliance landscape.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 19/2024-Central Tax.
It is dated 30th September 2024.
The effective date for the changes is 1st April 2025.
The notification pertains to section 171 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
From 1st April 2025, the Authority will not accept requests to examine if input tax credits or tax rate reductions have led to a corresponding price reduction.
This change is based on recommendations from the Goods and Services Tax Council.
The notification will come into force upon its publication in the Official Gazette.

106.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 20/2024-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This notification, effective from October 8, 2024, introduces several changes, including the addition of 'under section 74' in Rule 36(3) related to the suppression of facts. Notably, Rule 46 will be modified, omitting the second proviso and changing the third proviso, effective November 1, 2024. A new Rule 47A mandates that tax invoices must be issued within 30 days when the recipient is required to issue them. Additionally, Rule 66(1) now requires FORM GSTR-7 to be filed by the 10th of the following month starting November 1, 2024. Other amendments across various rules include references to section 74A, which expands the scope of certain provisions. Lastly, a new Rule 164 outlines the procedure for applying for waivers of ies under section 128A, providing a structured approach for eligible taxpayers. These changes will affect registered persons and businesses involved in GST compliance and invoicing.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Second Amendment) Rules, 2024'.
It comes into effect on the date of publication, which is October 8, 2024.
Rule 36(3) is amended to include 'under section 74' after 'suppression of facts'.
Rule 46 will see the second proviso omitted and the third proviso modified effective November 1, 2024.
A new Rule 47A is added, requiring tax invoices to be issued within 30 days when the recipient must issue the invoice.
Rule 66(1) is amended to require FORM GSTR-7 to be filed by the 10th of the month following the calendar month, effective November 1, 2024.
Several amendments are made to rules 86, 88B, 88D, 89, 96, 96B, 121, and 142, mostly inserting references to section 74A and omitting certain clauses.

107.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 21/2024-Central Tax on 8th October 2024, which introduces important deadlines for registered persons regarding tax payments. Specifically, those who have received a notice or order under section 128A can make their tax payments until 31st March 2025 to qualify for a waiver of any ies.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2024-Central Tax issued on 8th October 2024.
Registered persons with a notice under section 74 will have payment deadlines based on the completion of six months from the order issuance date.
The notification will come into effect from 1st November 2024.

108.summary: Notification No. 22/2024-Central Tax, issued on 8th October 2024, establishes a new procedure for registered persons to rectify orders related to the wrong availment of input tax credit under sections 73, 74, 107, or 108 of the CGST Act. This applies specifically to those who have received a demand due to violations of section 16(4) but can now claim the credit under sections 16(5) or 16(6). Affected individuals must submit their rectification applications electronically within six months from the notification date, using the proforma in Annexure A. The original authority that issued the order will be responsible for processing these applications and must provide a decision within three months. Additionally, any rectified orders must be documented in either FORM GST DRC-08 or FORM GST APL-04, depending on the nature of the original order. Importantly, if the rectification negatively impacts the applicant, the authority must follow principles of natural justice during the process.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2024-Central Tax issued on 8th October 2024.
Introduces a special procedure for rectification of orders under sections 73, 74, 107, or 108 of the CGST Act.
Applies to registered persons who have received a demand for wrong availment of input tax credit due to contravention of section 16(4), but can now claim credit under sections 16(5) or 16(6).
Registered persons must file an application for rectification electronically within six months from the notification date.
The application must include information in the proforma provided in Annexure A.
The authority that issued the original order will handle the rectification and must issue a decision within three months of the application.
Rectified orders must be uploaded in FORM GST DRC-08 or FORM GST APL-04, depending on the original order type.
The rectification process must adhere to principles of natural justice if it adversely affects the applicant.

109.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 23/2024-Central Tax, effective from 1st November 2024, introduces significant changes regarding late fees for registered persons required to deduct tax at source. This notification supersedes the earlier Notification No. 22/2021-Central Tax from June 2021. It specifically waives the late fees under section 47 for failures to submit GSTR-7 returns starting from June 2021. The waiver applies to amounts exceeding twenty-five rupees for each day of delay, with a total cap of one thousand rupees on the late fees. Additionally, if a registered person has no central tax deducted at source for a month, the late fee will be entirely waived. This change is aimed at easing the financial burden on taxpayers who may have struggled to meet their filing deadlines during this period.
key_points: Notification No. 23/2024-Central Tax issued on 8th October 2024.
Supersedes Notification No. 22/2021-Central Tax dated 1st June 2021.
Waives late fee under section 47 for registered persons required to deduct tax at source.
Applies to failures in furnishing GSTR-7 returns for the month of June 2021 onwards.
Late fee waiver is limited to amounts exceeding twenty-five rupees per day.
Total late fee waiver capped at one thousand rupees.
Additional waiver for registered persons with nil central tax deducted at source.
This notification comes into effect on 1st November 2024.

110.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 24/2024-Central Tax on 9th October 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Central Tax from 19th June 2017. A significant change is the addition of a new proviso that exempts individuals engaged in the supply of metal scrap from the provisions of the original notification. Specifically, this exemption applies to those dealing with metal scrap classified under Chapters 72 to 81 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This means that businesses involved in the supply of these specific metal scrap items will not be subject to the regulations outlined in Notification No. 5/2017. The changes will come into effect starting 10th October 2024, impacting suppliers of metal scrap who will now have different compliance requirements. This amendment aims to clarify the GST obligations for those in the metal scrap industry.
key_points: Notification No. 24/2024-Central Tax issued on 9th October 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.
A new proviso is added stating that the notification does not apply to persons supplying metal scrap.
Metal scrap is defined as items falling under Chapters 72 to 81 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
The amendments take effect from 10th October 2024.

111.summary: Notification No. 25/2024-Central Tax, issued on 9th October 2024, introduces amendments to Notification No. 50/2018-Central Tax, which was originally published on 13th September 2018. A significant change is the addition of a new clause (d), which includes registered persons who receive supplies of metal scrap categorized under Chapters 72 to 81 of the Customs Tariff Act from other registered persons. This means that these transactions are now specifically recognized under the GST framework. Furthermore, the third proviso of the original notification has been replaced to clarify that the supply of goods or services between certain specified persons does not apply to those mentioned in the new clause (d). These amendments will come into effect on 10th October 2024, impacting registered persons involved in the supply and receipt of metal scrap, ensuring they are aware of their obligations under the updated GST provisions.
key_points: Notification No. 25/2024-Central Tax was issued on 9th October 2024.
This notification amends Notification No. 50/2018-Central Tax, originally published on 13th September 2018.
A new clause (d) is added, which states that registered persons receiving supplies of metal scrap (Chapters 72 to 81 of the Customs Tariff Act) from other registered persons are included.

The third proviso in Notification No. 50/2018 is replaced with a new proviso that excludes the supply of goods or services between specified persons, except for those referred to in the new clause (d).
The changes will take effect from 10th October 2024.

112.summary: The recent Notification No. 26/2024-Central Tax, issued on November 18, 2024, extends the deadline for filing the GSTR-3B return for the month of October 2024. The new due date is now set for November 21, 2024. This extension specifically applies to registered persons whose principal place of business is located in the states of Maharashtra and Jharkhand. The decision to extend the deadline was made based on recommendations from the GST Council, reflecting the government's responsiveness to the needs of businesses in these regions. The extension is authorized under sub-section (6) of section 39 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and relates to the filing requirements outlined in the CGST Rules. This change provides additional time for businesses in these states to prepare and submit their returns, ensuring they can comply with the tax regulations without undue pressure.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for October 2024.
New due date is November 21, 2024.
Applicable to registered persons in Maharashtra and Jharkhand.
This extension is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The extension is granted under sub-section (6) of section 39 of the CGST Act, 2017.
The relevant rules are sub-section (1) of section 39 and clause (i) of sub-rule (1) of rule 61 of the CGST Rules, 2017.

113.summary: Notification No. 27/2024-Central Tax, issued on 25th November 2024, brings significant changes to the powers of tax officials under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. Specifically, it amends the earlier Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax by substituting Table V, which outlines the powers of Additional Commissioners and Joint Commissioners of Central Tax. The new table details the authority these officials have in passing orders related to notices from the Directorate General of Goods and Services Tax Intelligence. It includes a list of Principal Commissioners and Commissioners from various regions, such as Ahmedabad South, Bengaluru East, and Chennai South, who are now empowered to make decisions on specific sections of the GST Act. This amendment will take effect from 1st December 2024, and it is important for those in the affected regions to be aware of these changes. The last update to the principal notification was in March 2022, making this a notable revision in the framework governing GST enforcement.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 27/2024-Central Tax on 25th November 2024.
It amends Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.
The amendment substitutes Table V in the earlier notification.
The new Table V specifies the powers of Additional Commissioner or Joint Commissioner of Central Tax.
The powers listed include passing orders regarding notices issued by the Directorate General of Goods and Services Tax Intelligence.
The new table includes Principal Commissioners and Commissioners from various regions, including Ahmedabad South, Bengaluru East, and Chennai South.
This notification will come into effect on 1st December 2024.
The last amendment to the principal notification was made on 11th March 2022.

114.summary: Notification No. 28/2024-Central Tax, issued on 27th November 2024, designates adjudicating officers for a series of show cause notices related to GST compliance. These notices, numbered DGGI/INV/GST/3110/2022/Gr-C/11181 to 11887, were issued by the Directorate General of Goods and Services Tax Intelligence between 2nd September 2023 and 9th October 2023. The appointed adjudicating authorities are from the Thane Commissionerate, specifically Joint Commissioner or Additional Commissioner. The notification affects a total of 32 companies, including Memo Technology Private Limited and Belz Tech Private Limited, with addresses located in states such as Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Delhi. Each company has been issued specific show cause notices, which will now be adjudicated by the appointed officers. This move aims to streamline the adjudication process for these cases, ensuring that the relevant authorities are in place to handle the proceedings effectively.
key_points: Notification No. 28/2024-Central Tax issued on 27th November 2024.
This notification appoints adjudicating officers for specific show cause notices issued by the Directorate General of Goods and Services Tax Intelligence.
The show cause notices are numbered DGGI/INV/GST/3110/2022/Gr-C/11181 to 11887, issued between 2nd September 2023 and 9th October 2023.
The appointed adjudicating authorities include Joint Commissioner or Additional Commissioner from the Thane Commissionerate.
The noticees include various companies such as Memo Technology Private Limited, Belz Tech Private Limited, and Bytechat Business Solutions Pvt. Ltd.
The addresses of the noticees span multiple states including Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu, and Delhi.
The notification covers a total of 32 noticees with specific addresses and show cause notice details.

115.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 29/2024-Central Tax, which extends the due date for filing the GSTR-3B return for the month of October 2024. The new deadline is now set for November 30, 2024, specifically for registered persons whose principal place of business is in the State of Manipur. This extension is granted under the provisions of section 39(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, along with rule 61(1)(i) of the CGST Rules. The notification was officially issued on November 27, 2024, but it is effective from November 20, 2024. This change is particularly relevant for businesses operating in Manipur, allowing them additional time to prepare and submit their returns for the specified month. It is important for affected businesses to take note of this revised deadline to with the updated requirements.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for October 2024.
New due date is November 30, 2024.
Applies to registered persons in the State of Manipur.
Filing is required under section 39(1) and rule 61(1)(i) of the CGST Rules.
Notification issued on November 27, 2024.
Notification is effective from November 20, 2024.

116.summary: Notification No. 30/2024-Central Tax, issued on December 10, 2024, extends the deadline for filing the GSTR-3B return for October 2024. The new due date is now set for December 11, 2024. This extension specifically applies to registered persons whose principal place of business is in the Murshidabad district of West Bengal. The decision to extend the deadline is made under the authority of sub-section (6) of section 39 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Importantly, this notification will be considered effective from November 20, 2024. This means that businesses in the specified district will have additional time to prepare and submit their returns without facing the usual deadlines. This change is particularly beneficial for those who may need extra time to ensure their filings are accurate and complete.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for October 2024.
New due date is December 11, 2024.
Applies to registered persons in Murshidabad district, West Bengal.
This extension is based on sub-section (6) of section 39 of the CGST Act, 2017.
The notification is effective from November 20, 2024.

117.summary: Notification No. 31/2024-Central Tax, issued on December 13, 2024, appoints adjudicating authorities for specific show cause notices related to GST compliance issued by the Directorate General of Goods and Services Tax Intelligence. These notices, which were issued on August 4, 2024, pertain to various businesses, including M/s Sushil Kumar Munish Kumar & Co. and M/s Aggarwal Enterprises, among others, all of which have been assigned unique GSTINs. The appointed adjudicating authorities are the Additional Commissioner or Joint Commissioner of CGST and Central Excise, Chandigarh Commissionerate. This notification specifically addresses cases under sections 73, 74, 122, 125, and 127 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The businesses affected are primarily located in Punjab and Haryana, indicating a focused approach to adjudication in these regions. 118.summary: On January 5, 2024, the Government of India issued a corrigendum to the Integrated Tax (Rate) notification originally published on January 3, 2024. This corrigendum corrects a key detail in the tax rate, changing it from '2.5' to '5' in line 19. Additionally, it clarifies the principal notification, which is No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017, and notes that the last amendment was made by notification No. 20/2023-Integrated Tax (Rate) on October 19, 2023. The previous notification mistakenly referred to the principal notification as 'Central Tax (Rate)', which has now been corrected. This change is significant for businesses and taxpayers as it ensures they are aware of the accurate tax rate applicable under the Integrated Tax framework. The correction helps eliminate confusion regarding the classification of the tax rate and ensures compliance with the correct provisions.

119.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2024-Central Tax (Rate) on January 3, 2024, which amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. This notification follows the last amendment made by notification No. 17/2023-Central Tax (Rate) on October 19, 2023, ensuring that the tax structure remains updated and relevant for stakeholders in the industry.

121.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 02/2024-Central Tax (Rate) on 12th July 2024, which introduces several amendments to the existing GST rates. Effective from 15th July 2024, new entries have been added to Schedule II, including serial number 121A for cartons made of corrugated and non-corrugated paper, serial number 180A for milk cans made of iron, steel, or aluminum, and serial number 183A for solar cookers. Additionally, Schedule III has been amended, with a new substitution for serial number 153A, which now excludes specific types of cartons, and modifications to entries at serial numbers 224, 235, 273, 275A, and 378A concerning milk cans and solar cookers. A new proviso has also been added, clarifying that agricultural farm produce packaged in quantities over 25 kilograms or liters will not be classified as 'pre-packaged and labelled' under the Legal Metrology Act. These changes aim to clarify GST rates and definitions for specific goods, impacting manufacturers and suppliers in these categories.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2024-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 12th July 2024.
Inserts new entries in Schedule II at serial numbers 121A, 180A, and 183A for specific goods.
Amends Schedule III by substituting entry at serial number 153A and modifying entries at serial numbers 224, 235, 273, 275A, and 378A.
New proviso added regarding agricultural farm produce packaging in the Explanation after Schedule VII.
The changes will come into effect on 15th July 2024.
The principal notification being amended is No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The last amendment before this was made by notification No. 01/2024 on 3rd January 2024.

122.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 02/2024-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 12th July 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This new notification introduces several changes to the GST rates and classifications. Specifically, it adds new entries under Schedule II for 12% tax, including cartons, boxes, and cases made of paper or paperboard, as well as milk cans made of iron, steel, or aluminum. Additionally, solar cookers are now classified under Schedule II with designated HS codes. Changes to Schedule III include the exclusion of certain goods from the 18% tax rate, such as milk cans made of iron or steel. A new proviso clarifies that agricultural farm produce packaged in quantities exceeding 25 kg or 25 liters will not be classified as 'pre-packaged and labelled'. These amendments will come into effect on 15th July 2024, impacting businesses dealing with these specific goods and classifications.
key_points: This notification is issued on 12th July 2024.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries are added to Schedule II under 12% tax rate for cartons, boxes, and cases made of paper or paperboard.
New entries are added for milk cans made of iron, steel, or aluminum under Schedule II.
Solar cookers are now included under Schedule II with specific HS codes.
Amendments in Schedule III include changes to the classification of goods, specifically excluding certain items from the 18% tax rate.
A new proviso is added stating that agricultural farm produce in packages over 25 kg or 25 liters is not considered 'pre-packaged and labelled'.
The changes will take effect from 15th July 2024.

123.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 03/2024-Central Tax (Rate) on 12th July 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new proviso in the Explanation of clause (ii) after the Schedule. Specifically, it clarifies that agricultural farm produce packaged in quantities exceeding 25 kilograms or 25 liters will not be classified as 'pre-packaged and labelled' under the Legal Metrology Act, 2009. This change is significant for businesses involved in the supply of agricultural products, as it alters how such products are categorized for regulatory purposes. The new rule will come into effect on 15th July 2024. This amendment follows the previous update made by Notification No. 18/2023-Central Tax (Rate) on 19th October 2023, which also addressed aspects of the original notification. Businesses should take note of this change to with the updated definitions and classifications.
key_points: Notification No. 03/2024-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 12th July 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

The new proviso states that agricultural farm produce packaged in quantities over 25 kilograms or 25 liters is not considered 'pre-packaged and labelled'.
This change is effective from 15th July 2024.
The previous amendment to Notification No. 2/2017 was made by Notification No. 18/2023-Central Tax (Rate) on 19th October 2023.

124.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 03/2024-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 12th July 2024, which amends the earlier notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. A significant change introduced is the addition of a new proviso that clarifies the treatment of agricultural farm produce. Specifically, it states that any supply of agricultural products packaged in quantities exceeding 25 kilograms or 25 liters will not fall under the definition of 'pre-packaged and labelled' as per the Legal Metrology Act, 2009. This means that such larger packages will not be subject to the same regulations as smaller pre-packaged goods. This amendment will come into effect on 15th July 2024, impacting suppliers of agricultural products who package their goods in larger quantities. The last amendment to the original notification was made on 19th October 2023, indicating ongoing adjustments in the GST framework to better accommodate agricultural practices.
