127.summary: The recent Notification No. 05/2024-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 8th October 2024, introduces several amendments to the existing GST rates. Effective from 10th October 2024, new items have been added to various schedules. In Schedule I, three new pharmaceutical products—Trastuzumab Deruxtecan, Osimertinib, and Durvalumab—will now be taxed at 2.5%. Schedule II sees the addition of extruded or expanded savoury products under item number 32C, which will be taxed at 6%. In Schedule III, the description for item number 16 has been updated to include these extruded products, while item number 435A has been revised to clarify that it pertains to seats not used in aircraft or motor vehicles. Additionally, Schedule IV introduces a new item for seats specifically used in motor vehicles, taxed at 14%. These changes affect manufacturers and suppliers of the specified goods, ensuring they are aware of the updated tax implications.
key_points: New items added to Schedule I - 2.5%: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan, Osimertinib, Durvalumab.
New item added to Schedule II - 6%: Extruded or expanded products, savoury or salted (item number 32C).
Amendment in Schedule III - 9%: Substitution of description for item number 16 to include extruded or expanded products.
Substitution in Schedule III - 9%: Item number 435A now specifies seats other than those used in aircraft or motor vehicles.
New item added to Schedule IV - 14%: Seats of a kind used for motor vehicles (item number 210A).
The notification comes into force on 10th October 2024.
This notification amends the principal notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) and the last amendment was made on 12th July 2024.

128.summary: The recent Notification No. 05/2024-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 8th October 2024, introduces several amendments to the existing GST rates. Effective from 10th October 2024, new items have been added to various schedules. In Schedule I, three new pharmaceutical products—Trastuzumab Deruxtecan, Osimertinib, and Durvalumab—are now subject to a 5% tax rate. Schedule II sees the addition of extruded or expanded savoury products, classified under item number 32C at a 12% rate. Additionally, Schedule III has updated the description for S. No. 16 to include these new products, while S. No. 435A has been revised to clarify the types of seats for motor vehicles that are taxed at 18%. Lastly, Schedule IV introduces a new entry for seats specifically used in motor vehicles, now listed as item number 210A at a 28% rate. These changes affect manufacturers and suppliers of these products, ensuring they are aware of the updated tax implications.
key_points: New items added to Schedule I - 5%: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan, Osimertinib, Durvalumab.
New item added to Schedule II - 12%: Extruded or expanded products, savoury or salted (item number 32C).
In Schedule III - 18%, the description for S. No. 16 is updated to include 'extruded or expanded products, savoury or salted'.
S. No. 435A in Schedule III - 18% is substituted to specify seats for motor vehicles, excluding certain types.
New item added to Schedule IV - 28%: Seats of a kind used for motor vehicles (item number 210A).
This notification comes into force on 10th October 2024.
This notification amends the principal notification No. 1/2017 - Integrated Tax (Rate) and the last amendment was made on 12th July 2024.

129.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 06/2024-Central Tax (Rate) on 8th October 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new entry in the tax rate table, specifically after S. No. 7, which relates to metal scrap. The new entry, numbered 8, includes metal scrap classified under codes 72 to 81. It clarifies that the supply of metal scrap will be applicable to both unregistered and registered persons. This change is significant as it expands the scope of GST applicability on metal scrap transactions. The new provisions will take effect from 10th October 2024, meaning that businesses dealing in metal scrap should prepare for these changes in their tax obligations. This amendment aims to streamline the tax framework for metal scrap, ensuring clarity for all stakeholders involved.

130.summary: The recent Notification No. 06/2024-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on October 8, 2024, introduces an important amendment to the existing notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry to the tax table, designated as S. No. 8, which pertains to 'Metal scrap' categorized under HSN codes 72, 73, 74, 75, 76, 77, 78, 79, 80, or 81. This update is significant for businesses involved in the trading of metal scrap, as it clarifies the tax implications for both types of taxpayers. 131.summary: The recent Notification No. 07/2024-Central Tax (Rate), issued on October 8, 2024, introduces a new provision to the existing GST framework. Specifically, it amends the earlier notification 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. A new item (ivb) has been added, which pertains to the transportation of passengers by helicopter on a seat share basis, and this service will be taxed at a rate of 2.5%. Importantly, the notification stipulates that if a supplier has already claimed input tax credit on goods used for providing this service, they cannot claim it again. Additionally, the existing item (vii) has been updated to reference this new item (ivb). This change will take effect from October 10, 2024, impacting businesses involved in helicopter passenger transport services, as they will need to adjust their tax calculations and credit claims accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2024-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 2024-10-08.
Amends notification 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts a new item (ivb) for transportation of passengers by helicopter on a seat share basis at a rate of 2.5%.
The new item (ivb) specifies that input tax credit on goods used in supplying the service cannot be claimed if already taken.
Updates item (vii) to include the new item (ivb) in its description.
This notification comes into effect on 2024-10-10.

132.summary: The recent Notification No. 07/2024-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on October 8, 2024, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. A new item (ivb) has been added to the table, which pertains to the transportation of passengers by air in a helicopter on a seat share basis. This means that businesses offering this service can now classify it under a specific tax category. However, it's important to note that input tax credit cannot be claimed on goods used in providing this service, as specified in the new entry. Additionally, item (vii) has been updated to reflect this new item (ivb). These changes will come into effect on October 10, 2024, impacting businesses involved in helicopter passenger transport. This amendment aims to clarify the tax treatment for this specific mode of transport, ensuring compliance with the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework.

133.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 08/2024-Central Tax (Rate) on 8th October 2024, which amends the earlier notification 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces new classifications for certain services, all of which are exempt from tax. Specifically, services related to metering equipment, such as renting and testing, are now categorized under serial number 25A. Additionally, research and development services funded by government entities or specified institutions are classified under serial number 44A. Educational affiliation services provided by government-controlled bodies are now listed under serial number 66A. Furthermore, the entry for serial number 69 has been revised to include various recognized bodies involved in vocational training, also exempt from tax. Lastly, the term 'National Council for Vocational Training' has been updated to 'National Council for Vocational Education and Training' in several places. These changes will take effect from 10th October 2024, impacting service providers in these sectors.

It amends notification number 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries are added for services related to metering equipment, research and development services, and educational affiliation services.
Services related to metering equipment on rent and electricity connection are now classified under serial number 25A with a tax rate of Nil.
Research and development services funded by government entities or specified institutions are now classified under serial number 44A with a tax rate of Nil.
Educational affiliation services provided by government-controlled bodies are classified under serial number 66A with a tax rate of Nil.
The entry for serial number 69 is revised to include various recognized bodies providing vocational training services, with a tax rate of Nil.
The term 'National Council for Vocational Training' is replaced with 'National Council for Vocational Education and Training' in multiple instances.

134.summary: The recent Notification No. 08/2024-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the existing GST framework, specifically targeting various services and their tax implications. A new entry, 10L, has been added for the import of services by foreign airline companies in India, which will be exempt from GST if the Ministry of Civil Aviation certifies compliance with specific conditions. Additionally, entry 26A has been created for services related to metering and electricity supply, also exempting them from GST. Research and development services funded by government entities or specified institutions are now covered under entry 46A, providing them with GST exemption. Furthermore, entry 69A addresses services of affiliation provided by educational boards to government-controlled schools, exempting them from GST as well. The amendment also updates entry 72 to include various skill development services provided by recognized bodies, ensuring they are exempt from GST. Lastly, the term 'National Council for Vocational Training' has been corrected to 'National Council for Vocational Education and Training' in relevant entries. These changes will take effect from October 10, 2024, impacting businesses and entities involved in these sectors.
key_points: Inserts new entry 10L for 'Import of services by an establishment of a foreign airline company in India' under Chapter 99.
New entry 10L specifies that such imports are exempt from GST if certain conditions are met, including certification from the Ministry of Civil Aviation.
Inserts new entry 26A for services related to metering equipment and electricity supply under headings 9969 and 9986, exempting them from GST.
Inserts new entry 46A for research and development services funded by government entities or specified institutions under heading 9981, exempting them from GST.
Inserts new entry 69A for services of affiliation provided by educational boards to government-controlled schools under heading 9992, exempting them from GST.
Substitutes entry 72 to include various services provided by recognized bodies related to skill development under headings 9983, 9991, and 9992, exempting them from GST.
Corrects the term 'National Council for Vocational Training' to 'National Council for Vocational Education and Training' in entries 72 and 74.
This notification comes into effect on October 10, 2024.

135.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 09/2024-Central Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 8th October 2024. This corrigendum, dated 22nd October 2024, specifically addresses an error in the notification regarding the definition of property. In the original notification, the term 'any property' has been corrected to 'any immovable property' in the table under serial number 5AB, line 12. This change clarifies that the tax implications pertain specifically to immovable properties, rather than all types of property. This amendment is important for businesses and individuals dealing with property transactions, as it specifies the scope of the tax rate applicable. The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, and this correction ensures that the legal language accurately reflects the government's intent. The reference number for this corrigendum is F.No. 190354/149/2024-TO (TRU-II)-Part-I CBEC.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 09/2024-Central Tax (Rate) dated 8th October 2024.

It amends serial number 5AB in the table of the original notification.
In column (2), line 12, the phrase 'any property' is corrected to 'any immovable property'.
The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The original notification was published under G.S.R. 623(E) on 8th October 2024.
The corrigendum is referenced under F.No. 190354/149/2024-TO (TRU-II)-Part-I CBEC.

137.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 09/2024-Central Tax (Rate) on October 8, 2024, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry in the tax rate table, designated as serial number 5AB, which addresses services related to the renting of any property that is not a residential dwelling. This means that both unregistered and registered persons providing such rental services will now have a specific tax classification under GST. The amendment will take effect from October 10, 2024. This change is significant for businesses involved in property rental, as it clarifies the GST treatment for non-residential property rentals. The last amendment to the parent notification was made on October 19, 2023, under Notification No. 14/2023-Central Tax (Rate), indicating ongoing updates to the GST framework.

This update reflects the government's ongoing efforts to refine GST regulations and ensure clarity in tax obligations for various services. 139.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2024-Union Territory Tax on 10th July 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2018-Union Territory Tax dated 28th September 2018. The key change in this amendment is the reduction of the tax rate from 'half per cent.' to '0.25 per cent.' This adjustment is made under the authority of sections 21 and 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, as well as section 52 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The new tax rate will apply to the relevant transactions and will come into effect immediately upon publication in the official gazette. This change is significant for businesses operating in Union Territories, as it directly impacts the tax they will need to pay. Companies and individuals engaged in taxable activities in these regions should take note of this reduced rate, as it may affect their pricing and compliance strategies moving forward.

Amends Notification No. 12/2018-Union Territory Tax dated 28th September 2018.

The amendment is made under the powers of sections 21 and 22 of the Union Territory GST Act, 2017.
Also invokes section 52 of the Central GST Act, 2017.
The amendment takes effect from the date of publication in the official gazette.

140.summary: On January 5, 2024, the Government of India issued a corrigendum to correct an error in notification No. 01/2024 -Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on January 3, 2024. The correction clarifies that the principal notification being referenced is No. 1/2017 -Union Territory Tax (Rate), dated June 28, 2017, rather than Central Tax (Rate) as previously stated. This notification was last amended by notification No. 17/2023 – Union Territory Tax (Rate) on October 19, 2023. The corrected reference now accurately lists the G.S.R. number as G.S.R. 710(E) for the principal notification. This change is important for businesses and stakeholders involved in Union Territory tax matters, ensuring they have the correct legal references. The corrigendum was published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that the updated information is officially documented and accessible.
key_points: The notification is dated January 5, 2024.
It corrects the previous notification No. 01/2024 -Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on January 3, 2024.
The principal notification being referenced is No. 1/2017 -Union Territory Tax (Rate), dated June 28, 2017.
The previous notification incorrectly referred to the principal notification as Central Tax (Rate) instead of Union Territory Tax (Rate).
The last amendment to the principal notification was by notification No. 17/2023 – Union Territory Tax (Rate) on October 19, 2023.
The corrected reference includes the correct G.S.R. number as G.S.R. 710(E) for the principal notification.
The correction was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The corrigendum was issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.

141.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on January 3, 2024, which amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. This amendment specifically alters the entries under Schedule I for the 2.5% tax rate. For S. No. 165 and S. No. 165A, the previous entries have been replaced with '2711 12 00, 2711 13 00, 2711 19 10'. This means that the classification of certain goods under these specific codes has been updated, which could affect how businesses report and pay their Union Territory tax. The changes will take effect from January 4, 2024, so businesses should be aware of these new codes and ensure their systems are updated accordingly to reflect this change in tax classification.
key_points: Notification No. 01/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 2024-01-03.
This notification amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments specifically affect Schedule I, 2.5% tax rate entries.
For S. No. 165, the entry is changed to '2711 12 00, 2711 13 00, 2711 19 10'.
For S. No. 165A, the entry is also changed to '2711 12 00, 2711 13 00, 2711 19 10'.
These changes will come into effect from 4th January 2024.

142.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 02/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 12th July 2024, which introduces several amendments to the existing tax rates under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act. Effective from 15th July 2024, new entries have been added to Schedule II, specifically at serial numbers 121A for cartons and boxes made of paper, 180A for milk cans made of iron, steel, or aluminum, and 183A for solar cookers. Additionally, Schedule III has been amended, with a substitution at serial number 153A and new entries added at serial numbers 224, 235, 273, 275A, and 378A, affecting various goods and their classifications. A new proviso has also been introduced, clarifying that agricultural farm produce packaged in quantities exceeding 25 kg or 25 liters will not be classified as 'pre-packaged and labelled' under the Legal Metrology Act. This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017, which was last updated on 3rd January 2024, ensuring that businesses are aware of the latest tax implications for these goods.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 12th July 2024.
New entries added to Schedule II at serial numbers 121A, 180A, and 183A for specific goods.
Amendments made to Schedule III, including substitution at serial number 153A and additional entries at serial numbers 224, 235, 273, 275A, and 378A.
A new proviso added regarding the supply of agricultural farm produce in packages over 25 kg or 25 liters.
The changes will come into effect from 15th July 2024.
This notification amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The last amendment to the principal notification was made by notification No. 01/2024 on 3rd January 2024.

143.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 03/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 12th July 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new proviso that clarifies the treatment of agricultural farm produce. Specifically, it states that any agricultural produce packaged in quantities exceeding 25 kilograms or 25 liters will not be classified as 'pre-packaged and labelled' under the Legal Metrology Act, 2009. This change is significant for businesses involved in the supply of agricultural products, as it affects how they package and label their goods. The new rules will take effect from 15th July 2024, meaning businesses should prepare for this change in classification and ensure their packaging complies with the new guidelines. This amendment aims to simplify regulations for agricultural suppliers and is based on recommendations from the GST Council.
key_points: Notification No. 03/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 12th July 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added after the Explanation in clause (ii) of the earlier notification.
The new proviso states that agricultural farm produce in packages over 25 kg or 25 liters is not considered 'pre-packaged and labelled'.
The changes will come into effect on 15th July 2024.

144.summary: The Notification No. 04/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on January 1, 2024. However, the text provided does not contain any specific details regarding changes to tax rates, affected goods or services, or any other relevant information. As a result, there are no key points to summarize or explain. Without additional context or content from the notification, it is not possible to provide a detailed summary or identify any changes that may impact business owners or clients. It is essential to refer to the complete notification for comprehensive information regarding the Union Territory Tax (Rate) for the year 2024.

145.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 05/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 8th October 2024, which introduces several amendments to the existing tax rates under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act. Effective from 10th October 2024, new items have been added to various schedules. Specifically, Schedule I now includes Trastuzumab Deruxtecan, Osimertinib, and Durvalumab at a tax rate of 2.5%. Schedule II has a new entry for extruded or expanded savoury products at a 6% rate. Additionally, Schedule III has undergone changes, including a revised description for S. No. 16 to encompass both snack pellets and extruded products, and a new specification for S. No. 435A regarding seats that are not used in aircraft or motor vehicles. Lastly, Schedule IV now includes a new entry for seats specifically designed for motor vehicles at a 14% rate. These updates are part of the ongoing adjustments to the tax framework and aim to clarify and expand the taxable items under the Union Territory GST.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 8th October 2024.
New items added to Schedule I at 2.5%: Trastuzumab Deruxtecan, Osimertinib, and Durvalumab.
New entry added to Schedule II at 6%: Extruded or expanded products, savoury or salted (S. No. 32C).
Amendment in Schedule III at 9%: Substitution of description for S. No. 16 to include extruded or expanded products.
Substitution of S. No. 435A in Schedule III to specify seats other than those used in aircraft or motor vehicles.
New entry added to Schedule IV at 14%: Seats of a kind used for motor vehicles (S. No. 210A).
The changes will come into effect on 10th October 2024.
This notification amends the principal notification No. 1/2017 and the last amendment was made by notification No. 2/2024.

146.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 06/2024 on 8th October 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 4/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. A new entry has been added to the existing Table of the notification, specifically after S. No. 7, which is now S. No. 8. This new entry pertains to 'Metal scrap' and includes HSN codes ranging from 72 to 81. Importantly, this amendment applies to both unregistered and registered persons dealing with metal scrap. The changes will take effect from 10th October 2024. This means that businesses involved in the trading of metal scrap should be aware of this new classification and its implications for their tax obligations under the Union Territory GST framework. The amendment aims to clarify the tax treatment of metal scrap, ensuring that all relevant parties are informed and compliant with the updated regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 06/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 8th October 2024.
This notification amends the earlier notification No. 4/2017 - Union Territory Tax (Rate).
A new entry is added to the Table after S. No. 7, specifically S. No. 8.
S. No. 8 includes 'Metal scrap' under HSN codes 72 to 81.
The new entry applies to both unregistered and registered persons.
This amendment will come into force on 10th October 2024.

147.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 07/2024 on 8th October 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. A significant change is the introduction of a new entry (ivb) for the transportation of passengers by air in helicopters on a seat share basis, which will be taxed at a rate of 2.5%. This new entry will be added to the existing Table against serial number 8. Additionally, it is important to note that businesses providing this service cannot claim input tax credit on goods used for supplying this service. Furthermore, the existing item (vii) in the Table will now reference this new entry by including '(ivb)' after '(iva)'. These changes will take effect from 10th October 2024, impacting businesses involved in helicopter passenger transport services in Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 8th October 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry (ivb) is added for 'Transportation of passengers, with or without accompanied baggage, by air, in a helicopter on seat share basis' in the Table against serial number 8.
The applicable tax rate for this new entry is 2.5%.
A condition is included stating that input tax credit on goods used in supplying this service cannot be claimed.
In item (vii) of the same Table, '(ivb)' is inserted after '(iva)'.
This amendment will come into effect from 10th October 2024.

148.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 08/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on October 8, 2024, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017. This amendment introduces several new entries in the tax rate table. Specifically, a new entry (25A) has been added for services related to metering equipment and electricity connections, which will be taxed at Nil. Another new entry (44A) addresses research and development services funded by government entities or specified institutions, also at Nil tax. Additionally, entry 66A now covers affiliation services provided by educational boards to government-controlled schools, maintaining a Nil tax rate. The amendment also revises entry 69 to encompass services from the National Skill Development Corporation and related organizations, again with a Nil tax rate. Furthermore, the terminology has been updated to replace 'National Council for Vocational Training' with 'National Council for Vocational Education and Training' in several places. These changes will take effect from October 10, 2024, impacting service providers in these sectors by clarifying their tax obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 08/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 2024-10-08.
Inserts new entries for services related to metering equipment, research and development services, and educational affiliation services in the existing table of Notification No. 12/2017.
New entry 25A covers services related to metering equipment and electricity connection, with a tax rate of Nil.
New entry 44A pertains to research and development services funded by government entities or specified institutions, also with a tax rate of Nil.
New entry 66A includes affiliation services provided by educational boards to government-controlled schools, with a tax rate of Nil.
Replaces entry 69 to include various services provided by the National Skill Development Corporation and related bodies, with a tax rate of Nil.
Updates the terminology from 'National Council for Vocational Training' to 'National Council for Vocational Education and Training' in multiple instances.
This notification comes into effect on 2024-10-10.

149.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 09/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 8th October 2024. This corrigendum, dated 22nd October 2024, specifically amends the entry at serial number 5AB in the notification's table. The key change is the replacement of the term 'any property' with 'any immovable property' in column (2), line 13. This adjustment clarifies that the tax rate applies specifically to immovable properties, rather than any type of property. This change is important for businesses and individuals dealing with property transactions, as it delineates the scope of the tax more clearly. The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, and this corrigendum is referenced under the official document number F.No. 190354/149/2024-TO (TRU-II)-Part-I CBEC. Stakeholders should take note of this clarification to ensure proper compliance with the updated tax provisions.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 09/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 8th October 2024.
The corrigendum was issued on 22nd October 2024.
It amends the entry at serial number 5AB in the table of the original notification.
In column (2), line 13, the phrase 'any property' is replaced with 'any immovable property'.
This change clarifies the scope of the tax rate applicable under the specified entry.
The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The corrigendum is referenced under F.No. 190354/149/2024-TO (TRU-II)-Part-I CBEC.

150.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 09/2024 on 8th October 2024, which amends the previous Notification No. 13/2017 concerning Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, a new entry has been added to the existing table in the earlier notification, designated as serial number 5AB. This entry addresses the taxation of services related to renting any property that is not a residential dwelling. Importantly, this new provision applies to both unregistered and registered persons, meaning that all individuals and businesses involved in such rental services will be affected. The amendment is set to take effect from 10th October 2024, which means that any rental services provided after this date will fall under this new tax regulation. This change aims to clarify the tax obligations for renting non-residential properties in Union Territories, ensuring that both types of service providers are aware of their tax responsibilities.
key_points: Notification No. 09/2024-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 8th October 2024.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry, serial number 5AB, is added to the Table in the earlier notification.
The new entry 5AB pertains to 'Service by way of renting of any property other than residential dwelling.'
The new entry applies to both unregistered and registered persons.
This amendment will come into effect on 10th October 2024.

151.summary: Notification No. 01/2023-Compensation Cess (Rate), issued on 28th February 2023, introduces an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. The amendment specifically alters the entry against Sl. No. 41A in the Schedule. The revised entry clarifies that it pertains to 'Coal rejects supplied to a coal washery or by a coal washery, arising out of coal on which compensation cess has been paid and input tax credit thereof has not been availed by any person.' This change is significant for businesses involved in coal washery operations, as it defines the conditions under which coal rejects are treated concerning compensation cess. The new provisions will take effect from 1st March 2023, meaning that businesses must be aware of this change and adjust their operations accordingly to with the updated regulations.
key_points: The notification is dated 28th February 2023.
It amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The specific change is in the Schedule against Sl. No. 41A.

The new entry states: 'Coal rejects supplied to a coal washery or by a coal washery, arising out of coal on which compensation cess has been paid and input tax credit thereof has not been availed by any person.'
This amendment comes into effect on 1st March 2023.

152.summary: Notification No. 01/2023-Compensation Cess, issued on March 31, 2023, announces that the provisions of section 163 of the Finance Act, 2023, will take effect from April 1, 2023. This means that any regulations or changes outlined in section 163 will now be applicable starting on this date. The notification is part of the government's efforts to implement the provisions of the Finance Act, which may include adjustments related to compensation cess. Businesses and individuals affected by these changes should be aware that the new rules will be in force from the specified date. The notification is officially published by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue, ensuring that stakeholders are informed about the upcoming changes in the law. It is important for those impacted to review the provisions of section 163 to understand how it may affect their operations or tax obligations.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 01/2023-Compensation Cess.
It was issued on March 31, 2023.
The provisions of section 163 of the Finance Act, 2023 (08 of 2023) will come into force on April 1, 2023.
This notification is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.

153.summary: Notification No. 02/2023-Compensation Cess (Rate), issued on 31st March 2023, introduces several amendments to the previous notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017, effective from 1st April 2023. This update modifies the compensation cess rates for various items listed in the Schedule. For instance, the cess for Pan Masala (S. No. 1) is now set at 0.32R per unit, while other items like S. No. 5 and S. No. 6 have been adjusted to 0.36R per unit. Additionally, new entries for S. No. 36A and S. No. 36B have been introduced, applying a rate of 0.43R per unit for specific tobacco products. The notification also clarifies that 'R' refers to the retail sale price, enhancing understanding for businesses. An illustrative example has been provided to demonstrate how to calculate the compensation cess based on the retail price of Pan Masala, making it easier for businesses to comply with the new rates. This notification follows the previous amendment made on 28th February 2023, ensuring that businesses are aware of the latest rates applicable to their products.
key_points: The notification is dated 31st March 2023 and comes into force on 1st April 2023.
It amends the previous notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

New entries for compensation cess rates have been substituted for several items in the Schedule, including S. No. 1 (0.32R), S. No. 5 (0.36R), S. No. 6 (0.36R), S. No. 7 (0.32R), S. No. 19 (0.36R), S. No. 20 (0.12R), S. No. 21 (0.08R), S. No. 22 (0.69R), S. No. 23 (0.28R), and S. No. 24 (0.08R).
New entries for S. No. 36A and S. No. 36B have been added for goods containing tobacco, with a rate of 0.43R per unit.
The explanation now includes a definition of 'R' as 'retail sale price' for clarity.
An illustration for calculating the compensation cess on Pan Masala has been added, showing how to compute the cess based on the retail sale price.
The notification was last amended by notification No. 1/2023-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th February 2023.

154.summary: The recent Notification No. 03/2023-Compensation Cess (Rate), issued on 26th July 2023, introduces significant amendments to the existing Compensation Cess rates, effective from 27th July 2023. This notification modifies the earlier Notification No. 1/2017 by adding new entries for various tobacco products and Pan Masala, including S. No. 1A for Pan Masala at a rate of 60% and S. No. 5A for unmanufactured tobacco at 71%. Additionally, the phrase 'with declared retail sale price' has been incorporated into multiple entries to ensure clarity regarding pricing. New entries have also been established for products like 'hookah' tobacco and chewing tobacco, with specific rates assigned. Furthermore, the explanation section now defines 'declared retail sale price' in accordance with the Legal Metrology Act, 2009, ensuring compliance with pricing regulations. Lastly, the notification updates the description for utility vehicles, specifying criteria such as engine capacity and ground clearance. These changes will impact manufacturers and sellers of these products, requiring them to adjust their pricing and compliance strategies accordingly.
key_points: The notification is dated 26th July 2023 and comes into force on 27th July 2023.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries for Pan Masala and various types of tobacco products have been added, including S. No. 1A for Pan Masala at 60% and S. No. 5A for unmanufactured tobacco at 71%.
The term 'declared retail sale price' has been inserted in multiple entries for clarity on pricing requirements.
Several existing entries have been substituted to include the phrase 'with declared retail sale price' for better compliance.
New entries have been added for products like 'hookah' tobacco, chewing tobacco, and other smoking mixtures with specified rates.
The explanation section now clarifies that 'declared retail sale price' refers to prices required under the Legal Metrology Act, 2009.
The notification also updates the description for utility vehicles exceeding certain specifications.

155.summary: On 4th January 2023, the Central Government issued Notification No. 01/2023-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 14/2017-Central Tax from 1st July 2017. The key change is the addition of a new entry, Sl. No. 8A, in the Table of the earlier notification. This new entry designates the Additional Assistant Director, Goods and Services Tax Intelligence, Additional Assistant Director, Goods and Services Tax, and Additional Assistant Director, Audit Superintendent as officers who will exercise specific powers under the GST framework. This amendment clarifies the roles and responsibilities of these officers, ensuring that there is a clear understanding of who is authorized to act in these capacities. This change is significant for those involved in GST compliance and enforcement, as it outlines the hierarchy and authority within the GST administration. Overall, this amendment aims to streamline the functioning of GST authorities and enhance the effectiveness of tax administration.
key_points: Notification No. 01/2023-Central Tax was issued on 4th January 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 14/2017-Central Tax dated 1st July 2017.
A new entry, Sl. No. 8A, has been added to the Table in the earlier notification.

Sl. No. 8A specifies the Additional Assistant Director, Goods and Services Tax Intelligence, Additional Assistant Director, Goods and Services Tax, or Additional Assistant Director, Audit Superintendent as officers whose powers are to be exercised.

156.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2023-Integrated Tax on 31st July 2023, which outlines specific goods and services that can be exported with the payment of integrated tax. This notification identifies 25 items, primarily focusing on various forms of tobacco, such as pan-masala, unmanufactured tobacco, and different types of smoking mixtures, as well as essential oils. Notably, these goods can be exported with integrated tax, allowing suppliers to claim refunds on the tax paid. The notification also clarifies the definition of 'brand name,' which includes any name or mark that indicates a connection between the goods and the supplier. Importantly, this notification will take effect from 1st October 2023, meaning that businesses involved in the export of these specified goods should prepare for the new provisions by this date.
key_points: Notification No. 01/2023-Integrated Tax issued on 31st July 2023.
This notification specifies goods and services that may be exported on payment of integrated tax.
The notification lists 25 specific goods, including various types of tobacco and essential oils, that are exempt from this tax.
The notification defines 'brand name' as any name or mark indicating a connection between goods and a person.
The notification comes into effect from 1st October 2023.

157.summary: Notification No. 02/2023-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2023, introduces a significant amendment to Notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax. A new proviso has been added, which waives late fees exceeding ₹250 for registered persons who fail to file their GSTR-4 returns on time. This applies specifically to returns for the quarters from July 2017 to March 2019, as well as for the financial years 2019-20 to 2021-22. The waiver is available for those who file their returns between 1st April 2023 and 30th June 2023. Furthermore, if the total central tax payable in the return is nil, the late fee will be fully waived. This change is aimed at providing relief to taxpayers who may have missed the deadlines for these specific periods, allowing them to comply without incurring excessive penalties.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2023-Central Tax was issued on 31st March 2023.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax.
A new proviso is added after the sixth proviso in Notification No. 73/2017.
The new proviso waives late fees exceeding ₹250 for registered persons who file GSTR-4 late.
This waiver applies to GSTR-4 returns for the quarters from July 2017 to March 2019 and for FY 2019-20 to 2021-22.
The late fee waiver is valid for returns filed between 1st April 2023 and 30th June 2023.
The waiver is fully applicable if the total central tax payable in the return is nil.

158.summary: Notification No. 02/2023-Integrated Tax, issued on 29th September 2023, announces that the provisions of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Act, 2023, will take effect from 1st October 2023. This means that any changes or updates introduced by this Amendment Act will be applicable starting on that date. The notification is a formal declaration from the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs, which oversees GST regulations in India. Business owners and stakeholders in the GST framework should be aware that the new provisions will impact their compliance and reporting obligations from the specified date. It is essential for those affected to review the changes brought about by the Amendment Act to ensure they are prepared for the new requirements. This notification marks an important step in the ongoing evolution of GST regulations in India.
key_points: The notification is dated 29th September 2023.
It is issued under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Act, 2023.
The provisions of the Amendment Act will come into force on 1st October 2023.
The notification is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs.

159.summary: The recent Notification No. 03/2023-Central Tax, issued on March 31, 2023, introduces a special procedure for registered persons whose GST registration was cancelled under specific clauses before December 31, 2022. These individuals now have until June 30, 2023, to apply for revocation of their registration cancellation. However, they must first ensure that all returns due up to the cancellation date are filed and any outstanding tax, ies, or late fees are paid. Importantly, this notification states that no further extensions will be provided for applying for revocation after the June deadline. Additionally, it includes those who failed to apply for revocation in the stipulated time, even if their appeals against cancellation were rejected due to late filing. This change is significant for affected registered persons, as it provides a limited opportunity to restore their GST registration status.
key_points: Registered persons whose GST registration was cancelled under clauses (b) or (c) of section 29(2) before December 31, 2022, are affected.
These registered persons can apply for revocation of their registration cancellation until June 30, 2023.
No further extensions for applying for revocation of cancellation will be granted beyond June 30, 2023.
The notification clarifies that this applies to those who did not apply for revocation in time, including those whose appeals against cancellation were rejected for late filing.

160.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 03/2023-Integrated Tax, effective from 1st October 2023, which classifies online money gaming as goods for the purposes of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST). This means that when online money gaming is imported, it will be subject to integrated tax. Importantly, the existing proviso to sub-section (1) of section 5 of the IGST Act will not apply to this category of goods. This change is significant for businesses involved in online gaming, as they will now need to account for integrated tax on imports of these goods. The notification reflects the government's intent to regulate and tax the online gaming sector more effectively. Businesses engaged in this industry should prepare for the implications of this new tax treatment starting from the specified date. Overall, this move aims to bring clarity and compliance to the online gaming market in India.
key_points: Notification No. 03/2023-Integrated Tax issued on 29th September 2023.
Effective date of the notification is 1st October 2023.
Online money gaming is now classified as goods for GST purposes.
Integrated tax will be levied on the import of online money gaming.
The proviso to sub-section (1) of section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will not apply to this supply.

161.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 04/2023-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, effective from December 26, 2022. A significant change is in Rule 8(4A), which now mandates that applicants opting for Aadhaar authentication must complete this process for their application to be considered submitted. The submission date will be the earlier of the Aadhaar authentication date or fifteen days after submitting the application in FORM GST REG-01. Additionally, a new proviso requires biometric Aadhaar authentication and verification of documents at designated Facilitation Centres for certain applicants. This amendment aims to streamline the application process and enhance security measures. Furthermore, Rule 8(4B) has been updated to replace 'provisions of' with 'proviso to', clarifying the language in the rules. These changes are based on recommendations from the GST Council and follow previous amendments made in December 2022.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Rules, 2023'.
It comes into effect from December 26, 2022.
Rule 8(4A) is amended to require Aadhaar authentication for applicants opting for it.
The date of application submission will be the earlier of the Aadhaar authentication date or fifteen days from the application submission in FORM GST REG-01.
A new proviso mandates biometric-based Aadhaar authentication and document verification at designated Facilitation Centres for certain applicants.
In Rule 8(4B), 'provisions of' is replaced with 'proviso to'.
This amendment follows the recommendations of the GST Council.
The principal rules were last amended by notification No. 26/2022-Central Tax.

162.summary: Notification No. 04/2023-Integrated Tax, issued on September 29, 2023, introduces a significant change regarding the registration process for online money gaming. It designates the Principal Commissioner of Central Tax in Bengaluru West, along with his subordinate officers, as the authorities responsible for granting registration for online money gaming services. This applies specifically to services provided from non-taxable territories to individuals in India. The term 'online money gaming' is defined as per clause (80B) of section 2 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This new regulation will take effect from October 1, 2023, meaning that any entity involved in online money gaming must seek registration through the specified authorities in Bengaluru West. This change aims to streamline the registration process for businesses operating in this sector, ensuring they comply with the GST framework in India.
key_points: The notification is issued on September 29, 2023.
It empowers the Principal Commissioner of Central Tax, Bengaluru West, to grant registration for online money gaming.
This applies to supplies of online money gaming from non-taxable territories to persons in India.
The definition of 'online money gaming' refers to clause (80B) of section 2 of the CGST Act, 2017.
The notification comes into effect on October 1, 2023.

163.summary: Notification No. 05/2023-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2023, brings an important amendment to Notification No. 27/2022-Central Tax, which was originally published on 26th December 2022. The key change involves replacing the phrase 'provisions of' with 'proviso to' in the earlier notification. This alteration clarifies the language used in the GST rules, ensuring that the reference is more precise. The amendment is retroactively effective from the same date as the original notification, 26th December 2022. This change is relevant for businesses and tax professionals who refer to the provisions outlined in Notification No. 27/2022, as it impacts the interpretation of the rules. It is essential for stakeholders to be aware of this change to ensure accurate compliance with the GST framework. Overall, this amendment aims to enhance clarity in the GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2023-Central Tax was issued on 31st March 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 27/2022-Central Tax dated 26th December 2022.
The amendment substitutes the phrase 'provisions of' with 'proviso to' in the earlier notification.
The changes are deemed to have come into force from 26th December 2022.

164.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 05/2023-Integrated Tax on 26th October 2023, which amends the earlier Notification No. 01/2023-Integrated Tax from 31st July 2023. This amendment changes the eligibility criteria for goods and services that can be exported with an integrated tax refund. Specifically, it introduces exceptions for certain goods listed in column (3) of a new table, effective from 1st October 2023. Additionally, it clarifies that suppliers to Developers or units in Special Economic Zones (SEZ) can also claim refunds on integrated tax paid for their supplies, provided they are not among the exceptions. The definitions for key terms such as 'authorised operations', 'Developer', 'Special Economic Zone', and 'unit' are now consistent with the Special Economic Zone Act, 2005. This notification will take effect as soon as it is published in the Official Gazette, impacting businesses involved in exports and those supplying to SEZs.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2023-Integrated Tax issued on 26th October 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 01/2023-Integrated Tax dated 31st July 2023.
The amendment substitutes the clause regarding goods or services eligible for export with integrated tax refund.
Effective from 1st October 2023, the new clause specifies exceptions for certain goods listed in column (3) of the accompanying table.
Suppliers to Developers or units in Special Economic Zones (SEZ) are included as eligible suppliers for tax refunds.
Definitions for 'authorised operations', 'Developer', 'Special Economic Zone', and 'unit' are aligned with the Special Economic Zone Act, 2005.
The notification will come into force upon its publication in the Official Gazette.

165.summary: Notification No. 06/2023-Central Tax, issued on March 31, 2023, introduces a significant relief for registered persons who failed to file valid returns following assessment orders issued by February 28, 2023. If these individuals submit their returns by June 30, 2023, their assessment orders will be considered withdrawn, provided they adhere to certain conditions. Specifically, they must include payment of any ies of the assessment order remaining in effect.
key_points: Registered persons who did not submit a valid return within 30 days of an assessment order issued on or before February 28, 2023, are affected.
These registered persons can have their assessment order deemed withdrawn if they comply with specific procedures.
They must submit the required return by June 30, 2023.
The return must include payment of interest due under section 50(1) and any late fees under section 47, regardless of any appeals filed against the assessment order.

166.summary: Notification No. 07/2023-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2023, introduces a waiver of late fees for GST returns under section 44 for the financial year 2022-23 and onwards. For registered persons with an aggregate turnover of up to five crore rupees, the late fee is set at twenty-five rupees per day, capped at 0.02% of their turnover. Those with a turnover exceeding five crore but up to twenty crore rupees will face a late fee of fifty rupees per day, also capped at 0.02% of their turnover. Additionally, for returns related to the financial years 2017-18 to 2021-22, if these are filed between 1st April 2023 and 30th June 2023, any late fee exceeding ten thousand rupees will be waived. This change is significant for registered persons who have missed their filing deadlines, as it reduces their financial burden related to late fees for both current and past returns.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2023-Central Tax was issued on 31st March 2023.
The notification waives late fees for returns under section 44 of the CGST Act for the financial year 2022-23 onwards.
Registered persons with an aggregate turnover of up to five crore rupees will incur a late fee of twenty-five rupees per day, capped at 0.02% of their turnover.
Registered persons with an aggregate turnover of more than five crore rupees and up to twenty crore rupees will incur a late fee of fifty rupees per day, also capped at 0.02% of their turnover.
For returns due for financial years 2017-18 to 2021-22, if filed between 1st April 2023 and 30th June 2023, the late fee exceeding ten thousand rupees will be waived.
This waiver applies to registered persons who fail to furnish their returns by the due date.

167.summary: Notification No. 08/2023-Central Tax, issued on March 31, 2023, introduces a waiver for late fees related to the final return in FORM GSTR-10. Specifically, registered persons who fail to file their GSTR-10 by the due date will not have to pay any late fee that exceeds 500 rupees, provided they submit their return between April 1, 2023, and June 30, 2023. This change is significant for those who may have missed the original deadline, as it offers financial relief by capping the late fee. The waiver is applicable under section 128 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's intent to ease compliance burdens during this period. Taxpayers should take advantage of this opportunity to file their returns without incurring excessive penalties. This notification aims to support registered persons in maintaining their compliance with GST regulations while managing their financial obligations more effectively.
key_points: Waives late fee exceeding 500 rupees for late filing of GSTR-10.
Applicable for registered persons who miss the due date for GSTR-10.
Late fee waiver is valid for returns filed between April 1, 2023, and June 30, 2023.
This notification is issued under section 128 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

168.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 09/2023-Central Tax on 31st March 2023, which extends the deadlines for issuing orders related to the recovery of unpaid or short-paid taxes and wrongly availed input tax credits. Specifically, for the financial year 2017-18, the deadline has been extended to 31st December 2023. For the financial year 2018-19, the new deadline is now 31st March 2024, and for the financial year 2019-20, it has been extended to 30th June 2024. This notification modifies previous notifications, namely No. 35/2020, No. 14/2021, and No. 13/2022, which had set earlier deadlines. The changes primarily affect businesses that may have tax recovery issues from these financial years, allowing them more time to address any discrepancies related to tax payments or input tax credits. This extension is aimed at easing compliance burdens for taxpayers during these periods.
key_points: Notification No. 09/2023-Central Tax issued on 31st March 2023.
Extends the time limit for issuing orders under section 73(9) for recovery of tax not paid or short paid.
For the financial year 2017-18, the new deadline is 31st December 2023.
For the financial year 2018-19, the new deadline is 31st March 2024.
For the financial year 2019-20, the new deadline is 30th June 2024.
This notification modifies earlier notifications: No. 35/2020, No. 14/2021, and No. 13/2022.
The extension applies to recovery of tax and input tax credit issues.

169.summary: Notification No. 10/2023-Central Tax, issued on 10th May 2023, introduces a significant change to the existing GST framework by amending Notification No. 13/2020-Central Tax from 21st March 2020. Specifically, the amendment reduces the threshold for certain compliance requirements from 'ten crore rupees' to 'five crore rupees'. This change will take effect from 1st August 2023. The amendment is made under sub-rule (4) of rule 48 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, which governs the conditions for filing certain returns. This adjustment may impact businesses whose turnover is between five crore and ten crore rupees, as they will now be subject to different compliance obligations. The last amendment to Notification No. 13/2020 was made on 1st August 2022, indicating ongoing adjustments to the GST regulations to better align with current economic conditions.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2023-Central Tax was issued on 10th May 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 13/2020-Central Tax dated 21st March 2020.
The amendment changes the threshold from 'ten crore rupees' to 'five crore rupees'.
The new threshold will be effective from 1st August 2023.
The amendment is made under sub-rule (4) of rule 48 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
The last amendment to Notification No. 13/2020 was made by Notification No. 17/2022-Central Tax on 1st August 2022.

170.summary: Notification No. 11/2023-Central Tax, issued on 24th May 2023, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax, which was originally published on 10th November 2020. This amendment adds a new proviso that specifically extends the deadline for registered persons in Manipur to file their GSTR-1 for the tax period of April 2023. The new deadline is set for 31st May 2023, allowing additional time for these taxpayers to submit their details of outward supplies. This extension is particularly relevant for businesses operating in Manipur, as they are now required to comply with this new timeline. The amendment is retroactively effective from 11th May 2023, meaning that the extension applies to any filings due from that date onward. This change aims to provide relief to businesses in Manipur, ensuring they have adequate time to meet their GST filing obligations for the specified period.
key_points: The notification is dated 24th May 2023.
It amends Notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax, originally issued on 10th November 2020.
A new proviso is added after the third proviso in Notification No. 83/2020.
The new proviso extends the deadline for filing GSTR-1 for April 2023.
The extended deadline for GSTR-1 applies to registered persons in Manipur.
The new deadline for filing GSTR-1 is 31st May 2023.
The amendment is effective from 11th May 2023.

171.summary: Notification No. 12/2023-Central Tax, issued on May 24, 2023, extends the deadline for filing the GSTR-3B return for the month of April 2023. The new due date is now May 31, 2023, specifically for registered persons whose principal place of business is in the State of Manipur. This extension applies to those required to file under section 39(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act and rule 61(1)(i) of the CGST Rules. Although the notification was published on May 24, it is effective from May 20, 2023. This change is significant for businesses in Manipur, providing them with additional time to prepare and submit their returns for the specified tax period.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for April 2023 to May 31, 2023.
Applicable to registered persons in the State of Manipur.
Filing is required under section 39(1) of the CGST Act and rule 61(1)(i) of the CGST Rules.
Notification issued on May 24, 2023, but effective from May 20, 2023.

172.summary: Notification No. 13/2023-Central Tax, issued on 24th May 2023, introduces an amendment to Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax, originally published on 28th June 2019. This amendment adds a new proviso that specifically affects registered persons in the State of Manipur who are required to deduct tax at source under section 51 of the GST Act. These individuals must now file their GSTR-7 return for the month of April 2023 electronically through the common portal by the deadline of 31st May 2023. This change is significant as it clarifies the filing requirements for taxpayers in Manipur, ensuring they are aware of their obligations. The amendment is retroactively effective from 10th May 2023, which means that the provisions apply from that date. This update is crucial for compliance and helps streamline the tax filing process for those affected in Manipur.
key_points: This notification is issued on 24th May 2023.
It amends Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax dated 28th June 2019.
A new proviso is added after the fourth proviso in the first paragraph of the original notification.
The new proviso states that registered persons in Manipur required to deduct tax at source must file FORM GSTR-7 for April 2023.
The deadline for filing FORM GSTR-7 is set for 31st May 2023.
The amendment is effective from 10th May 2023.
The amendment relates to section 51 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

173.summary: Notification No. 14/2023-Central Tax, issued on June 19, 2023, amends the earlier notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax. Specifically, it modifies the fourth proviso to include both 'April 2023 and May 2023' as the relevant tax periods for compliance. Additionally, the due date for filing related returns has been extended from May 31, 2023, to June 30, 2023. This change means that businesses that were required to file returns for these two months now have additional time to do so, with the new deadline set for the end of June. The amendment is effective retroactively from May 31, 2023, ensuring that the extended deadline applies to all relevant filings. This update is particularly important for businesses that may have faced challenges in meeting the original deadline, providing them with a necessary reprieve to without penalties.
key_points: Amends notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax dated 10th November 2020.
Changes the fourth proviso regarding tax periods from 'April 2023' to 'April 2023 and May 2023'.
Extends the due date from '31st May 2023' to '30th June 2023'.
This amendment is effective from 31st May 2023.

174.summary: Notification No. 15/2023-Central Tax, issued on 19th June 2023, amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2023-Central Tax from 24th May 2023. The key changes include an extension of the tax period to cover both April and May 2023, rather than just April. Additionally, the due date for filing has been pushed back from the 31st of May 2023 to the 30th of June 2023. This means that businesses now have more time to prepare and submit their returns for these two months. The amendment is effective retroactively from the 31st of May 2023, allowing taxpayers to benefit from the extended deadline. This change is particularly relevant for those who may have faced challenges in meeting the original deadline, providing them with additional time to for both months.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 12/2023-Central Tax dated 24th May 2023.
Changes the tax period from 'month of April, 2023' to 'months of April, 2023 and May, 2023'.
Extends the due date from 'thirty-first day of May, 2023' to 'thirtieth day of June, 2023'.
Notification No. 15/2023-Central Tax was issued on 19th June 2023.
This amendment is effective from 31st May 2023.

175.summary: On 19th June 2023, the government issued Notification No. 16/2023-Central Tax, which amends an earlier notification, specifically Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax from 28th June 2019. The key changes involve the fifth proviso in the first paragraph of the earlier notification. It now includes the months of April and May 2023 for certain compliance requirements, replacing the previous mention of only April 2023. Additionally, the deadline for a specific compliance action has been extended from the thirty-first day of May 2023 to the thirtieth day of June 2023. These amendments are deemed effective from 31st May 2023, which means that businesses and taxpayers who were required to comply with the earlier deadlines now have an extended timeframe to meet their obligations for both April and May 2023. This change is particularly relevant for those who may have faced challenges in meeting the original deadlines.
key_points: This notification is issued on 19th June 2023.
It amends Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax dated 28th June 2019.
The amendment changes the fifth proviso in the first paragraph.
It substitutes 'month of April, 2023' with 'months of April 2023 and May 2023'.
It also changes 'thirty-first day of May, 2023' to 'thirtieth day of June, 2023'.
The changes are effective from 31st May 2023.

176.summary: Notification No. 17/2023-Central Tax, issued on June 27, 2023, extends the deadline for filing GSTR-3B for the month of May 2023 to June 30, 2023. This extension specifically affects registered persons whose principal place of business is located in the districts of Kutch, Jamnagar, Morbi, Patan, and Banaskantha in the state of Gujarat. The decision is made under the authority of sub-section (6) of section 39 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Importantly, this notification is deemed to have come into force from June 20, 2023, allowing affected taxpayers a little more time to comply with their filing obligations. This change aims to provide relief to businesses in these districts, ensuring they have adequate time to prepare and submit their returns without the pressure of the original deadline.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for May 2023 to June 30, 2023.
Applies to registered persons in Kutch, Jamnagar, Morbi, Patan, and Banaskantha districts of Gujarat.
The extension is based on the powers conferred by sub-section (6) of section 39 of the CGST Act, 2017.
The notification is effective from June 20, 2023.

177.summary: The recent Notification No. 18/2023-Central Tax, issued on July 17, 2023, brings important changes to the earlier Notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax. Specifically, it expands the tax periods covered for filing from just April and May 2023 to now include June 2023 as well. This means that businesses will now have to consider their tax obligations for three months instead of two. Additionally, the deadline for filing has been extended from June 30, 2023, to July 31, 2023. This extension allows businesses more time to prepare their filings for these three months. The changes are effective retroactively from June 30, 2023, which means that the new provisions apply as if they were in place from that date. This notification is particularly relevant for businesses that are required to file their GST returns for the specified periods, as they will benefit from the extended deadline and the inclusion of June in their reporting requirements.
key_points: Amends notification No. 83/2020-Central Tax dated 10th November 2020.
Substitutes 'tax periods April 2023 and May 2023' with 'tax periods April 2023, May 2023 and June 2023'.
Changes the due date from 'thirtieth day of June, 2023' to 'thirty-first day of July, 2023'.
Notification is effective from 30th June 2023.

178.summary: Notification No. 19/2023-Central Tax, issued on 17th July 2023, brings important changes to the earlier Notification No. 12/2023-Central Tax dated 24th May 2023. Specifically, it extends the tax periods to include April, May, and June 2023, allowing businesses to account for these months in their filings. Additionally, the due date for submitting the relevant returns has been pushed back from 30th June 2023 to 31st July 2023. This change is significant for businesses that may have been struggling to meet the original deadline, providing them with additional time to prepare their returns. The amendment is effective retroactively from 30th June 2023, meaning that businesses can now align their compliance efforts with the new timeline. This notification aims to ease the filing process for taxpayers during this period, reflecting the government's responsiveness to the needs of businesses.

This notification amends Notification No. 12/2023-Central Tax dated 24th May 2023.
The tax periods specified are now extended to include April, May, and June 2023.
The due date for filing has been changed from 30th June 2023 to 31st July 2023.
The amendment is effective from 30th June 2023.

179.summary: Notification No. 20/2023-Central Tax, issued on July 17, 2023, extends the deadline for filing the GSTR-3B return for the quarter ending June 2023. The new due date is now set for July 31, 2023. This extension specifically applies to registered persons whose principal place of business is in the State of Manipur. These businesses are required to file their returns under the provisions outlined in the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, specifically the proviso to sub-section (1) of section 39 and clause (ii) of sub-rule (1) of rule 61 of the GST Rules. This change aims to provide additional time for compliance for those affected in Manipur, ensuring they can meet their filing obligations without undue pressure. It is important for businesses in this region to take note of this revised deadline to ensure their returns are submitted on time.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-3B for the quarter ending June 2023.
New due date is July 31, 2023.
Applicable to registered persons in the State of Manipur.
Filing is required under the proviso to sub-section (1) of section 39.
Also relates to clause (ii) of sub-rule (1) of rule 61 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
Notification issued on July 17, 2023.

180.summary: The recent Notification No. 21/2023-Central Tax, issued on July 17, 2023, amends the earlier Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax from June 28, 2019. Specifically, it modifies the fifth proviso in the first paragraph. The amendment expands the tax periods covered by changing 'months of April 2023 and May 2023' to include 'June 2023' as well. Additionally, the due date for compliance has been extended from June 30, 2023, to July 31, 2023. This change is significant for businesses that were required to file returns for these months, as they now have additional time to complete their filings. The amendment is effective retroactively from June 30, 2023, meaning it applies to obligations that were due on that date. This notification was issued under the authority of section 39(6) and section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring that businesses are aware of their updated filing timelines.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax dated 28th June 2019.
Changes the fifth proviso in the first paragraph.
Substitutes 'months of April 2023 and May 2023' with 'months of April 2023, May 2023 and June 2023'.
Extends the due date from 'thirtieth day of June, 2023' to 'thirty-first day of July, 2023'.
Notification is effective from 30th June 2023.
This amendment was made under the powers of section 39(6) and section 168 of the CGST Act, 2017.

181.summary: The recent Notification No. 22/2023-Central Tax, issued on July 17, 2023, modifies the seventh proviso of Notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax. Specifically, it changes the deadline for compliance from June 30, 2023, to August 31, 2023. This amendment is deemed effective from June 30, 2023, and is particularly relevant for registered persons who need to file returns under the specified provisions. The earlier notification, No. 02/2023-Central Tax, issued on March 31, 2023, set the context for this change. By extending the deadline, the government aims to provide additional time for taxpayers to meet their filing obligations. This adjustment is crucial for those who may have faced challenges in meeting the original deadline, ensuring they have a fair opportunity to comply without penalties. Overall, this amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to facilitate smoother compliance processes for businesses under the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2023-Central Tax was issued on 2023-07-17.
This notification amends the seventh proviso of Notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax.
The date '30th day of June, 2023' in the seventh proviso is replaced with '31st day of August, 2023'.
The changes are effective from 30th June 2023.
The parent notification being amended is Notification No. 02/2023-Central Tax issued on 2023-03-31.
The amendment allows for an extended deadline for certain compliance requirements.
The amendment affects registered persons required to file returns under the specified provisions.

182.summary: Notification No. 23/2023-Central Tax, issued on 17th July 2023, brings an important change to the earlier Notification No. 03/2023-Central Tax, which was published on 31st March 2023. The key change is the extension of a deadline, moving it from the 30th of June 2023 to the 31st of August 2023. This means that businesses and taxpayers who were required to meet certain obligations by the end of June now have additional time until the end of August to comply. The amendment is retroactively effective from June 30, 2023, which means it applies as if it were in place from that date. This extension may provide relief to many businesses that were struggling to meet the original deadline. It is crucial for affected parties to take note of this new deadline to ensure they can fulfill their requirements without the pressure of the previous timeline.
key_points: Notification No. 23/2023-Central Tax was issued on 17th July 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 03/2023-Central Tax dated 31st March 2023.
The amendment changes the deadline from '30th day of June, 2023' to '31st day of August, 2023'.
The amendment is effective from 30th June 2023.

183.summary: Notification No. 24/2023-Central Tax, issued on 17th July 2023, brings an important change regarding the due date for certain compliance under GST. Specifically, it amends the earlier Notification No. 06/2023-Central Tax, which was published on 31st March 2023. The key change is the extension of the due date from 30th June 2023 to 31st August 2023. This means that businesses and taxpayers who were required to meet certain obligations by the end of June now have additional time until the end of August to fulfill those requirements. The amendment is retroactively effective from 30th June 2023, which means it applies as if it were in place from that date. This extension provides relief to those who may have faced challenges in meeting the original deadline, allowing them more time to with GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 24/2023-Central Tax was issued on 17th July 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 06/2023-Central Tax dated 31st March 2023.

The amendment is effective from 30th June 2023.

184.summary: Notification No. 25/2023-Central Tax, issued on 17th July 2023, brings an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 07/2023-Central Tax dated 31st March 2023. Specifically, it extends the deadline for a certain compliance requirement from 30th June 2023 to 31st August 2023. This change is significant for businesses and taxpayers who were facing challenges in meeting the original deadline. The amendment is retroactively effective from 30th June 2023, meaning that any compliance obligations that were due by that date now have an extended timeline. This extension provides additional time for businesses to prepare and ensure they meet their GST obligations without the pressure of the previous deadline. It is crucial for affected businesses to take note of this new date and adjust their plans accordingly to benefit from the additional time provided.
key_points: Notification No. 25/2023-Central Tax was issued on 17th July 2023.
This notification amends Notification No. 07/2023-Central Tax dated 31st March 2023.
The amendment changes the deadline from '30th day of June, 2023' to '31st day of August, 2023'.
