383.summary: Notification No. 21/2021-Central Tax, issued on June 1, 2021, introduces changes to the existing provisions in Notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax. Specifically, it adds a new proviso that waives the late fee for registered persons who fail to file FORM GSTR-4 for the financial year 2021-22 and onwards. This waiver is structured based on the amount of central tax payable. If the total central tax is nil, the late fee is capped at 250 rupees. For registered persons with a central tax liability, the late fee waiver is limited to 1,000 rupees. This amendment aims to provide relief to taxpayers by reducing the financial burden associated with late filing of returns, particularly for those with lower tax liabilities. It is important for businesses to be aware of these changes as they may impact their compliance costs moving forward.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2021-Central Tax was issued on 2021-06-01.
This notification amends Notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax dated 29th December 2017.
A new proviso is added after the fourth proviso in Notification No. 73/2017.
The new proviso waives the late fee for FORM GSTR-4 for financial year 2021-22 onwards.
For nil central tax payable, the late fee waiver is limited to 250 rupees.
For all other registered persons, the late fee waiver is limited to 1,000 rupees.

384.summary: Notification No. 22/2021-Central Tax, issued on June 1, 2021, introduces a waiver of late fees for registered persons who are required to deduct tax at source under section 51 of the GST Act. This waiver specifically applies to the GSTR-7 returns for the month of June 2021 and onwards. Under this notification, if these registered persons fail to file their GSTR-7 returns by the due date, the late fee that exceeds ₹25 per day will be waived. However, there is a cap on the total late fee waiver, which cannot exceed ₹1,000. This means that while there is still a nominal fee for late filing, the government is providing significant relief by eliminating higher penalties for those who may struggle to file on time. This change is particularly beneficial for businesses that may face challenges in meeting the filing deadlines during this period.
key_points: Waives late fee for GSTR-7 returns for June 2021 onwards.
Applicable to registered persons required to deduct tax at source under section 51.
Late fee waiver applies to amounts exceeding ₹25 per day.
Total late fee waiver capped at ₹1,000 for GSTR-7 returns.

385.summary: Notification No. 23/2021-Central Tax, issued on 1st June 2021, brings an important amendment to Notification No. 13/2020-Central Tax, which was originally published on 21st March 2020. The key change is the insertion of the phrase 'a government department, a local authority' in the first paragraph of Notification No. 13/2020, specifically after the words 'notifies registered person, other than'. This means that the provisions of the original notification now explicitly exclude government departments and local authorities from being classified as registered persons under certain conditions. This amendment clarifies the scope of the notification and ensures that these entities are treated differently in the context of GST compliance. The previous amendment to Notification No. 13/2020 was made through Notification No. 05/2021-Central Tax on 8th March 2021, which is relevant for understanding the evolution of these regulations. Overall, this change affects how government departments and local authorities interact with GST provisions, providing clearer guidelines for their status.
key_points: Notification No. 23/2021-Central Tax was issued on 1st June 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 13/2020-Central Tax, dated 21st March 2020.
The amendment inserts the phrase 'a government department, a local authority' after 'notifies registered person, other than' in the first paragraph of Notification No. 13/2020.
The principal notification No. 13/2020 was last amended by Notification No. 05/2021-Central Tax, dated 8th March 2021.

386.summary: Notification No. 24/2021-Central Tax, issued on 1st June 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 14/2021-Central Tax from 1st May 2021. The key changes involve extending the due dates for certain GST filings. Specifically, the due date for submissions originally set for 30th May 2021 has been moved to 29th June 2021, while another due date that was 31st May 2021 is now 30th June 2021. Additionally, the proviso has been updated to change the due date from 31st May 2021 to 30th June 2021, and the date of 15th June 2021 has been extended to 15th July 2021. These changes affect the same tax periods as mentioned in the original notification. Importantly, this notification is effective from 30th May 2021, meaning that the new deadlines apply retroactively from that date. Business owners and taxpayers should take note of these revised dates to with the updated timelines.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 14/2021-Central Tax dated 1st May 2021.
Changes the due date for certain filings from 30th May 2021 to 29th June 2021.
Changes the due date for another filing from 31st May 2021 to 30th June 2021.
Changes the due date in the proviso from 31st May 2021 to 30th June 2021.
Changes the due date in the proviso from 15th June 2021 to 15th July 2021.
Applies to the same tax periods as specified in the original notification.
Notification comes into effect from 30th May 2021.

387.summary: Notification No. 25/2021-Central Tax, issued on June 1, 2021, brings an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax, which was originally published on April 23, 2019. The key change in this amendment is the extension of a deadline; specifically, the date has been moved from May 31, 2021, to July 31, 2021. This means that any compliance or filing requirements that were due by the end of May are now extended to the end of July. The amendment is effective retroactively from May 31, 2021, which means it applies as if it were in place from that date. This change will primarily affect businesses and taxpayers who were required to meet obligations by the original deadline, providing them with additional time to comply. It is crucial for those impacted to take note of this new deadline to ensure they meet their obligations accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 25/2021-Central Tax was issued on 1st June 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax dated 23rd April 2019.
The amendment changes the deadline from '31st day of May, 2021' to '31st day of July, 2021'.
The change is effective from 31st May 2021.

388.summary: Notification No. 26/2021-Central Tax, issued on 1st June 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2021-Central Tax from 1st May 2021. The key change is the extension of a due date, which has been moved from 31st May 2021 to 30th June 2021. This amendment is effective retroactively from 31st May 2021, meaning it applies as if it were in place at that earlier date. The amendment is made under the authority of section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act and rule 45(3) of the CGST Rules. This change primarily affects businesses that were required to comply with the original deadline, giving them additional time to meet their obligations. It is important for affected businesses to note this new deadline and adjust their compliance schedules accordingly.
key_points: The notification is issued on 1st June 2021.
It amends Notification No. 11/2021-Central Tax dated 1st May 2021.
The amendment changes the due date from '31st May 2021' to '30th June 2021'.
This change is effective from 31st May 2021.
The amendment is made under section 168 of the CGST Act and rule 45(3) of the CGST Rules.

389.summary: The Central Goods and Services Tax (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2021, issued on June 1, 2021, introduces several important changes affecting GST compliance. Firstly, the deadline for a specific requirement has been extended from May 31, 2021, to August 31, 2021, providing additional time for businesses. Additionally, the rules now require that input tax credit adjustments for the months of April, May, and June 2021 be made cumulatively in the GSTR-3B for June 2021. This means businesses will need to account for these months together when filing their returns. Furthermore, a new provision allows registered persons to submit details for May 2021 using the Invoice Furnishing Facility (IFF) from June 1 to June 28, 2021. These amendments aim to ease compliance burdens during a challenging period and provide businesses with more flexibility in managing their GST obligations.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2021'.
It was issued on June 1, 2021, and comes into force on the date of publication.
The fourth proviso in sub-rule (1) of rule 26 is amended to extend the date from May 31, 2021, to August 31, 2021.
The second proviso in sub-rule (4) of rule 36 is replaced to require cumulative input tax credit adjustments for April, May, and June 2021 in the GSTR-3B for June 2021.
A new proviso is added to sub-rule (2) of rule 59, allowing registered persons to furnish details for May 2021 using IFF from June 1, 2021, to June 28, 2021.

390.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 28/2021-Central Tax on 30th June 2021, which supersedes the earlier Notification No. 89/2020-Central Tax dated 29th November 2020. This new notification waives the penalties that registered persons would have incurred under section 125 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act for failing to comply with the provisions outlined in Notification No. 14/2020-Central Tax, which was issued on 21st March 2020. The waiver applies specifically to non-compliance that occurred between 1st December 2020 and 30th September 2021. This means that businesses that faced penalties during this period for not adhering to the earlier notification will not have to pay those penalties. This change is significant for registered persons who may have struggled to comply with the requirements during the specified timeframe, providing them with some relief from financial penalties.
key_points: Notification No. 28/2021-Central Tax issued on 30th June 2021.
Supersedes Notification No. 89/2020-Central Tax dated 29th November 2020.
Waives penalties under section 125 of the CGST Act for non-compliance.
Applicable for non-compliance of Notification No. 14/2020-Central Tax dated 21st March 2020.
Penalty waiver period is from 1st December 2020 to 30th September 2021.

391.summary: Notification No. 29/2021-Central Tax, issued on 30th July 2021, announces that sections 110 and 111 of the Finance Act, 2021 will come into effect on 1st August 2021. This means that the provisions outlined in these sections will be applicable starting from that date. Business owners and stakeholders in the GST framework should be aware that these sections may introduce new compliance requirements or changes in existing procedures. The specific details of sections 110 and 111 are not provided in this notification, but their implementation signifies a shift in the regulatory landscape. It is important for affected parties to review these sections to understand how they may impact their operations and compliance obligations moving forward. This change is part of the government's ongoing efforts to update and refine the GST framework as per the latest legislative developments.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 29/2021-Central Tax.
The notification is dated 30th July 2021.
Sections 110 and 111 of the Finance Act, 2021 (13 of 2021) are being brought into force.
The effective date for these provisions is 1st August 2021.

392.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has issued Notification No. 30/2021-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, effective from August 1, 2021. The key change is the substitution of Rule 80, which mandates that all registered persons, except certain specified categories, must file their annual return in FORM GSTR-9 by December 31 of the following financial year. Additionally, those with an aggregate turnover exceeding five crore rupees are required to submit a self-certified reconciliation statement in FORM GSTR-9C along with their annual return. The amendments also expand FORM GSTR-9 to include financial year 2020-21, updating various entries and instructions to reflect this. Notably, new entries for FY 2020-21 regarding transactions and ITC reversals have been added, while the certification part of FORM GSTR-9C has been removed. These changes primarily impact registered persons who need to comply with the updated filing requirements, especially those with higher turnover.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Sixth Amendment) Rules, 2021' and comes into effect from August 1, 2021.
Rule 80 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017 is substituted to specify that registered persons must file their annual return in FORM GSTR-9 by December 31 following the financial year.
Registered persons with an aggregate turnover exceeding five crore rupees must also submit a self-certified reconciliation statement in FORM GSTR-9C along with their annual return.
FORM GSTR-9 is amended to include financial year 2020-21 alongside 2019-20 in various entries and instructions.
Changes in FORM GSTR-9 include new entries for FY 2020-21 regarding transactions and ITC reversals, as well as adjustments to existing entries.
FORM GSTR-9C is updated to include new entries and changes in the verification section, and the certification part is omitted.
The amendments affect all registered persons required to file annual returns and reconciliation statements, particularly those with significant turnover.

393.summary: Notification No. 31/2021-Central Tax, issued on July 30, 2021, introduces an important exemption for certain registered persons under the GST framework. Specifically, those whose aggregate turnover for the financial year 2020-21 is up to two crore rupees are now exempt from filing their annual return for that financial year. This change is made under the first proviso to section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The exemption will take effect from August 1, 2021. This means that smaller businesses with lower turnover will have one less compliance requirement, easing their administrative burden. It is a significant relief for many small taxpayers, allowing them to focus on their business operations without the added pressure of annual return filing. This notification aims to support smaller businesses during challenging times by reducing their compliance costs.
key_points: Exempts registered persons with an aggregate turnover of up to two crore rupees from filing the annual return for the financial year 2020-21.
This exemption applies to the annual return due for the financial year 2020-21.
The notification is issued under the first proviso to section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The exemption is effective from the 1st day of August, 2021.

394.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has issued Notification No. 32/2021-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This amendment, effective from August 29, 2021, includes several key changes. Notably, the deadline for certain compliance under rule 26 has been extended from August 31, 2021, to October 31, 2021. Furthermore, all provisos in this rule will be removed starting November 1, 2021. A new provision in rule 138E allows businesses to bypass certain restrictions if they have not filed their GSTR-3B, GSTR-1, or GST CMP-08 returns for the period of March to May 2021, during the window from May 1, 2021, to August 18, 2021. Additionally, FORM GST ASMT-14 will now include a reference to an order, remove a phrase about conducting business without registration, and add 'Address' after 'Designation'. These changes aim to provide relief and clarity to businesses regarding their compliance obligations.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Seventh Amendment) Rules, 2021'.
The rules come into force on the date of publication in the Official Gazette, which is August 29, 2021.
In rule 26, the fourth proviso is amended to change the date from '31st day of August, 2021' to '31st day of October, 2021'.
All provisos in rule 26 will be omitted effective from November 1, 2021.
A new proviso is added to rule 138E stating that restrictions do not apply from May 1, 2021, to August 18, 2021, if GSTR-3B, GSTR-1, or GST CMP-08 returns for March to May 2021 are not filed.
In FORM GST ASMT-14, a reference to an order will be added, a phrase regarding conducting business without registration will be removed, and 'Address' will be added after 'Designation'.
This notification amends the principal rules last updated by notification No. 30/2021-Central Tax on July 30, 2021.

395.summary: Notification No. 33/2021-Central Tax, issued on 29th August 2021, brings an important amendment to Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax, originally published on 31st December 2018. Specifically, this new notification changes the ninth and tenth provisos by extending the deadline from '31st day of August, 2021' to '30th day of November, 2021'. This extension impacts the timelines for compliance requirements under the GST framework, providing additional time for businesses to meet their obligations. The amendment is made under section 128 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's ongoing efforts to facilitate compliance for taxpayers. Businesses that were facing challenges in meeting the earlier deadline will benefit from this extension, allowing them to better manage their GST filings and related processes. Overall, this change aims to ease the compliance burden on registered persons under the GST regime.
key_points: Notification No. 33/2021-Central Tax was issued on 29th August 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax dated 31st December 2018.
The ninth and tenth provisos in Notification No. 76/2018 are amended.
The date '31st day of August, 2021' is replaced with '30th day of November, 2021' in the ninth and tenth provisos.
This change affects the deadlines for certain compliance requirements under GST.
The amendment is made under the powers conferred by section 128 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

396.summary: Notification No. 34/2021-Central Tax, issued on August 29, 2021, extends the deadline for taxpayers to apply for the revocation of GST registration cancellations. This applies specifically to registrations that were cancelled under clauses (b) or (c) of sub-section (2) of section 29 of the GST Act. Previously, the time frame for submitting these applications was from March 1, 2020, to August 31, 2021. With this new notification, taxpayers now have until September 30, 2021, to submit their applications for revocation. This change is significant for those affected by registration cancellations during the specified period, as it provides additional time to rectify their registration status. The notification also modifies earlier notifications, specifically No. 35/2020-Central Tax and No. 14/2021-Central Tax, ensuring that the extended deadline is clearly communicated to all stakeholders involved.
key_points: Extends the time limit for revocation of GST registration cancellation applications.
Applies to registrations cancelled under clause (b) or (c) of sub-section (2) of section 29.
Original time limit for revocation applications was from 1st March 2020 to 31st August 2021.
New extended deadline for these applications is now 30th September 2021.
This notification modifies earlier notifications No. 35/2020-Central Tax and No. 14/2021-Central Tax.

397.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 35/2021-Central Tax on 24th September 2021, which amends several rules under the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. Key changes include an amendment to Rule 10A, which now requires that bank accounts must be in the name of the registered person and linked to their Permanent Account Number (PAN). Additionally, a new Rule 10B mandates Aadhaar authentication for various entities, such as proprietorships and partnerships, for specific applications like revocation of registration and refund claims. Rule 23 has been updated to reference Rule 10B in the context of revocation of registration. Starting from 1st October 2021, Rule 45 will change the terminology from 'during a quarter' to 'during a specified period'. Furthermore, Rule 59 will adjust the reference period from 'preceding two months' to 'preceding month' effective from 1st January 2022. Rule 89 introduces a provision for refunds related to intra-State supplies later classified as inter-State, allowing applications within two years. Lastly, Rule 96 now requires Aadhaar authentication for refund applications, ensuring compliance with the new regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 35/2021-Central Tax was issued on 24th September 2021.
Rule 10A is amended to require bank accounts to be in the name of the registered person and linked to their Permanent Account Number (PAN).
A new Rule 10B mandates Aadhaar authentication for various entities, including proprietorships and partnerships, for specific applications.
Rule 23 is amended to include a reference to Rule 10B regarding revocation of registration.
Rule 45 changes the term from 'during a quarter' to 'during a specified period' effective from 1st October 2021.
Rule 59 will change the reference from 'preceding two months' to 'preceding month' effective from 1st January 2022.
Rule 89 introduces a new sub-rule allowing refunds for intra-State supplies later deemed inter-State, with a two-year application window.
Rule 96 is amended to require Aadhaar authentication for refund applications.

398.summary: On 24th September 2021, the Government of India issued Notification No. 36/2021-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 03/2021-Central Tax from 23rd February 2021. The key change involves the insertion of the phrase 'sub-section (6A) or' into the first paragraph of the previous notification. This amendment is made under the authority of sub-section (6D) of section 25 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The inclusion of this phrase clarifies the provisions applicable under the GST framework. This change is significant for businesses and taxpayers who need to be aware of the specific provisions being referenced in the context of GST compliance. It ensures that the legal framework remains clear and up-to-date, reflecting the latest recommendations from the GST Council. Stakeholders should review this amendment to understand its implications on their GST obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 36/2021-Central Tax was issued on 24th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 03/2021-Central Tax dated 23rd February 2021.
The amendment adds the phrase 'sub-section (6A) or' in the first paragraph of the earlier notification.
The amendment is made under the powers conferred by sub-section (6D) of section 25 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

399.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 37/2021-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This notification, effective from December 1, 2021, introduces several important changes. Firstly, Rule 137 is amended to extend the period for certain actions from four years to five years, effective November 30, 2021. Additionally, FORM GST DRC-03 has been updated to include 'or intimation of tax ascertained through FORM GST DRC-01A' in its heading, which broadens the scope of this form. The item 3 of the form now includes new categories like 'scrutiny' and specific mismatches between GSTR forms, enhancing clarity in reporting. Furthermore, item 5 has been revised to include scrutiny and other actions that must be addressed within 30 days. Lastly, the entire table under serial number 7 in FORM GST DRC-03 has been replaced with a new format for better organization of tax-related information. These changes will affect businesses and taxpayers who need to comply with the updated reporting requirements.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Ninth Amendment) Rules, 2021'.
It comes into effect on the date of its publication, which is December 1, 2021.
Rule 137 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017 is amended to change the period from 'four years' to 'five years' effective November 30, 2021.
FORM GST DRC-03 is amended to include 'or intimation of tax ascertained through FORM GST DRC-01A' in its heading.
Item 3 of FORM GST DRC-03 is updated to include additional categories such as 'scrutiny' and 'Mismatch (Form GSTR-1 and Form GSTR-3B)'.
Item 5 of FORM GST DRC-03 now includes 'scrutiny, intimation of tax ascertained through Form GST DRC-01A, audit, inspection or investigation' after the phrase 'within 30 days of its issue'.
The table under serial number 7 in FORM GST DRC-03 is completely substituted with a new format for reporting tax details.

400.summary: Notification No. 38/2021-Central Tax, issued on December 21, 2021, announces that several provisions from the Central Goods and Services Tax (Eighth Amendment) Rules, 2021, will come into effect on January 1, 2022. Specifically, this includes sub-rule (2), sub-rule (3), clause (i) of sub-rule (6), and sub-rule (7) of rule 2. These changes are part of the earlier Notification No. 35/2021-Central Tax, which was published on September 24, 2021. Businesses and taxpayers should be aware that these new rules will impact their compliance and reporting obligations starting from the specified date. It is essential for those affected to review the amendments to understand how they may need to adjust their processes or systems in light of these changes. The notification aims to clarify and implement the updated GST rules effectively.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 38/2021-Central Tax.
The effective date for the new provisions is January 1, 2022.
The provisions coming into force include sub-rule (2), sub-rule (3), clause (i) of sub-rule (6), and sub-rule (7) of rule 2.
This notification is in pursuance of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Eighth Amendment) Rules, 2021.
The parent notification for this change is No. 35/2021-Central Tax, published on September 24, 2021.

401.summary: Notification No. 39/2021-Central Tax, issued on December 21, 2021, establishes January 1, 2022, as the effective date for certain provisions of the Finance Act, 2021. Specifically, sections 108, 109, and 113 to 122 will be implemented starting on this date. This means that businesses and individuals will need to be aware of the changes introduced by these sections as they will now be enforceable. The notification is part of the government's ongoing efforts to update and streamline tax regulations. It is important for stakeholders to review these sections to understand how they may impact their operations or compliance requirements. The provisions aim to enhance the framework of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) and related processes, thereby affecting various aspects of tax administration. This change is significant for those involved in GST compliance and administration, as it marks the introduction of new rules and guidelines that must be followed.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 39/2021-Central Tax.
The effective date for the provisions is set for January 1, 2022.
Sections 108, 109, and 113 to 122 of the Finance Act, 2021 will come into force on this date.
This notification is issued by the Central Government's Ministry of Finance.

402.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 40/2021-Central Tax, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, effective January 1, 2022. A significant change is in Rule 36(4), where input tax credit can now only be claimed if the supplier has reported the invoice in FORM GSTR-1 and the recipient has received the details in FORM GSTR-2B. Additionally, for the financial year 2020-2021, the deadline for filing the annual return and the self-certified reconciliation statement has been extended to February 28, 2022. A new rule, 144A, has been introduced to outline the process for recovering penalties through the sale of goods or conveyances. Furthermore, Rule 142(3) now requires a notice period of seven days for detention, replacing the previous fourteen days. The order of appropriation of sale proceeds has also been clarified in the revised Rule 154. Lastly, several forms, including GST DRC-10, DRC-11, DRC-12, and DRC-22, have been updated to align with these changes. These amendments will impact registered persons and businesses involved in GST compliance and reporting.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Tenth Amendment) Rules, 2021' and comes into effect on January 1, 2022.
Rule 36(4) is amended to state that input tax credit can only be claimed if the supplier has reported the invoice in FORM GSTR-1 and the recipient has received details in FORM GSTR-2B.
For the financial year 2020-2021, the deadline for filing the annual return and self-certified reconciliation statement is extended to February 28, 2022, as per the new sub-rules added to Rule 80.
A new rule (144A) is introduced regarding the recovery of penalties through the sale of goods or conveyances, effective January 1, 2022.
Rule 142(3) is amended to change the notice period for detention from fourteen days to seven days after issuing a notice under Section 129.
Rule 154 is replaced to specify the order of appropriation of sale proceeds from the sale of goods or conveyances.
Changes are made to various forms (GST DRC-10, DRC-11, DRC-12, DRC-22) to reflect the new rules and requirements, effective January 1, 2022.

403.summary: Notification No. 01/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 2nd June 2021, introduces amendments to the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it modifies Schedule I by changing the entry against S. No. 259A to '9503'. Additionally, a new item, 'Diethylcarbamazine', has been added as S. No. 231 in List 1, following the existing entries. These changes are effective immediately from the date of the notification. Businesses dealing with these specific goods should take note of the updated classification and tax rates to with the new provisions. The amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to refine the GST framework based on recommendations from the GST Council. This notification is crucial for manufacturers and suppliers of the affected goods, as it directly impacts their tax obligations and compliance requirements.
key_points: This notification is No. 01/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 2nd June 2021.
It amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, the entry against S. No. 259A is changed to substitute '9503' in column (2).
A new entry for 'Diethylcarbamazine' is added as S. No. 231 after serial number 230 in List 1.
The changes take effect immediately on 2nd June 2021.

404.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on June 2, 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Specifically, in Schedule I, the entry for S. No. 259A has been updated to '9503'. Additionally, a new entry for 'Diethylcarbamazine' has been introduced as S. No. 231 in List 1, following serial number 230. These changes are effective immediately from June 2, 2021. This notification will impact businesses dealing with these specific goods, as they will need to refer to the updated entries for compliance and tax calculation purposes. The amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to refine the GST framework and ensure that the tax structure remains relevant and effective for various products.
key_points: Notification No. 01/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 2021-06-02.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, for S. No. 259A, the entry in column (2) is changed to '9503'.
A new entry for 'Diethylcarbamazine' is added as S. No. 231 after serial number 230 in the List 1.
The changes are effective from 2021-06-02.

405.summary: On 2nd June 2021, the Government of India issued Notification No. 02/2021-Central Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. One significant change is the introduction of a new clause (iii) in the fourth proviso of serial number 3. This clause allows landowner-promoters to use the tax credit charged by developer-promoters for the tax on apartments they supply in their projects. Additionally, in serial number 25, a new item (ib) has been added, which pertains to maintenance, repair, or overhaul services for ships and other vessels, with a tax rate set at 2.5%. The existing item (ii) in this serial number has also been updated to reflect the inclusion of this new item. These amendments are effective immediately from the date of the notification, impacting landowner-promoters and service providers in the maritime sector.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 2nd June 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new clause (iii) is added to the fourth proviso in serial number 3, allowing landowner-promoters to utilize tax credit charged by developer-promoters for tax on apartments supplied.
In serial number 25, a new item (ib) is added for maintenance, repair, or overhaul services for ships and vessels, with a tax rate of 2.5%.
The existing item (ii) in serial number 25 is amended to include the new item (ib) in its description.
The changes are effective from 2nd June 2021.

406.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 02/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on June 2, 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. A significant change is the addition of a new clause (iii) in the fourth proviso of serial number 3. This clause allows landowner-promoters to utilize the tax credit charged by developer-promoters for the tax on apartments they supply. Additionally, in serial number 25, a new item (ib) has been introduced, which pertains to maintenance, repair, or overhaul services for ships and other vessels. The existing item (ii) has also been updated to include this new item (ib) in its description. These amendments are effective immediately from June 2, 2021, impacting landowner-promoters and service providers in the maritime sector.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 2021-06-02.
This notification amends Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new clause (iii) is added to the fourth proviso in serial number 3, allowing landowner-promoters to utilize tax credit charged by developer-promoters for tax on apartments supplied.
In serial number 25, a new item (ib) is added for maintenance, repair, or overhaul services related to ships and vessels.
The existing item (ii) in serial number 25 is amended to include the new item (ib) in its description.
These changes are effective from 2nd June 2021.

407.summary: Notification No. 03/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on June 2, 2021, brings amendments to the earlier Notification No. 06/2019-Central Tax (Rate) from March 29, 2019. The key change involves the language regarding tax liability. The phrase 'in whose case the liability to' has been replaced with 'who shall', clarifying the responsibility for tax. Additionally, the timing for when the tax liability arises has been modified. It now states that the liability must occur in a tax period that is not later than the period in which the completion certificate is issued or the date of first occupation, whichever comes first. These amendments are effective immediately from June 2, 2021. This notification primarily affects businesses involved in construction and real estate, as it clarifies when they need to account for GST in relation to project completion and occupation.
key_points: This notification is issued under Notification No. 03/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on June 2, 2021.
It amends Notification No. 06/2019-Central Tax (Rate) dated March 29, 2019.
The phrase 'in whose case the liability to' is replaced with 'who shall'.
The wording regarding the timing of tax liability is changed to specify it must occur in a tax period not later than when the completion certificate is issued or the first occupation date.
The changes are effective from June 2, 2021.

408.summary: Notification No. 03/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 2nd June 2021, brings important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 06/2019 - Integrated Tax (Rate) from 29th March 2019. The first change replaces the phrase 'in whose case the liability to' with 'who shall', clarifying the language used in the notification. Additionally, the timing for tax liability has been modified; it now states that the tax liability will occur in a tax period not later than the one in which the completion certificate is issued or the project is first occupied, whichever comes first. This change affects how businesses recognize tax liability related to project completion and occupation. The amendments are effective immediately, meaning businesses must adapt to these changes without delay. Overall, this notification aims to streamline the tax process for projects, ensuring clarity on when tax obligations arise.
key_points: This notification is issued on 2nd June 2021.
It amends Notification No. 06/2019 - Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019.
The amendment substitutes 'in whose case the liability to' with 'who shall' in the first paragraph.

It also changes the phrase regarding the timing of tax liability from 'shall arise on the date of issuance of completion certificate' to 'in a tax period not later than the tax period in which the date of issuance of the completion certificate... falls'.
The changes apply to the tax periods related to the issuance of completion certificates or first occupation of projects.
The notification comes into effect immediately on 2nd June 2021.

409.summary: On 14th June 2021, the Government of India issued Notification No. 04/2021-Central Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically alters the provisions in the table under serial number 3, column (3), item (iv). A new proviso has been added, which states that from 14th June 2021 until 30th September 2021, the central tax on certain specified services will be levied at a reduced rate of 2.5%. This change applies regardless of the rates that were previously specified in column (4) of the table. The amendment is made under several sections of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, indicating its legal basis. This notification follows the last amendment made by Notification No. 02/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 2nd June 2021, ensuring that businesses and service providers are aware of the temporary tax rate adjustment during this period.
key_points: Notification No. 04/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 14th June 2021.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment specifically affects the Table in Notification No. 11/2017, serial number 3, column (3), item (iv).
A new proviso is added stating that from 14th June 2021 to 30th September 2021, the central tax on specified services will be 2.5%.
This change applies irrespective of the previously specified rates in column (4) of the table.
The amendment is made under various sections of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The last amendment to Notification No. 11/2017 was made by Notification No. 02/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 2nd June 2021.

410.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 04/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 14 June 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28 June 2017. This amendment introduces a new proviso in the Table against serial number 3, column (3), item (iv). It specifies that from 14 June 2021 until 30 September 2021, the integrated tax on certain services, as described in clause (f), will be charged at a reduced rate of 5%. This is a significant change as it overrides the previously specified rates in column (4) of the same notification. The last amendment to Notification No. 08/2017 was made on 2 June 2021 through Notification No. 02/2021. Businesses providing the specified services should take note of this temporary rate adjustment, which aims to provide relief during the specified period.
key_points: Notification No. 04/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 14 June 2021.
Amends Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28 June 2017.
Inserts a new proviso in the Table against serial number 3, column (3), item (iv).
The new proviso states that from 14 June 2021 to 30 September 2021, integrated tax on specified services will be levied at 5%.
This change applies irrespective of the previously specified rates in column (4).
The last amendment to Notification No. 08/2017 was made by Notification No. 02/2021 on 2 June 2021.

411.summary: On 15th June 2021, a corrigendum was issued for Notification No. 05/2021-Central Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 14th June 2021. This corrigendum specifically addresses an error in the Table of the original notification. In the entry for Sl. No. 8, the code '3804 94' has been corrected to '3808 94'. This change is important for businesses that deal with goods classified under these codes, as it ensures that the correct classification is used for tax purposes. The correction helps to avoid any confusion regarding the applicable tax rates for these goods. It is essential for affected businesses to update their records accordingly to reflect this change. This amendment clarifies the correct tax treatment and ensures compliance with the updated classification under GST.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 05/2021-Central Tax (Rate) dated 14th June 2021.
The corrigendum was issued on 15th June 2021.
In the Table of the original notification, against Sl. No. 8, the entry in column (2) has been corrected.
The incorrect entry '3804 94' has been changed to '3808 94'.

412.summary: Notification No. 05/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on June 14, 2021, provides a tax exemption for specific goods related to healthcare and COVID-19 management. This includes items like Medical Grade Oxygen, Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B, and Remdesvir, with most goods attracting a reduced central tax rate of 2.5%. Notably, Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B are completely exempt from tax. The notification lists a total of 18 items, which also includes essential medical equipment such as ventilators, hand sanitizers, and temperature check equipment. Ambulances are included but are taxed at a higher rate of 6%. This exemption is effective until September 30, 2021, aiming to support public health efforts during the pandemic. Businesses dealing with these goods should take note of the reduced tax rates to benefit from the exemptions provided in this notification.
key_points: Exempts certain goods from central tax under section 9 of the CGST Act, 2017.
Applicable goods include Medical Grade Oxygen, Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B, and Remdesvir.
The exemption rate for most goods listed is 2.5%, with Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B being exempt at 0%.
The notification specifies a total of 18 items, including hand sanitizers, ventilators, and ambulances.
The exemption is valid until September 30, 2021.
Ambulances are subject to a higher tax rate of 6% under this notification.

413.summary: The Notification No. 05/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on June 14, 2021, provides tax exemptions for specific goods under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This includes a range of medical supplies and equipment critical for healthcare, particularly in the context of Covid-19. For instance, Medical Grade Oxygen and Remdesvir are taxed at 5%, while Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B are completely exempt from tax. The notification lists a total of 18 items, including essential medical devices like Ventilators and BiPAP Machines, which also fall under the 5% tax rate. Additionally, ambulances are taxed at a higher rate of 12%. This exemption is intended to support public health efforts and will be in effect until September 30, 2021. Businesses dealing with these goods should take note of these changes to benefit from the tax exemptions during this period.
key_points: Exempts certain goods from integrated tax under section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Applicable goods include Medical Grade Oxygen, Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B, and Remdesvir, among others.
The exemption rates vary, with some items like Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B being exempt from tax, while others like Medical Grade Oxygen and Remdesvir are taxed at 5%.
The notification specifies a total of 18 items, including medical equipment like Ventilators and BiPAP Machines, with tax rates ranging from 5% to 12%.
This notification is effective from June 14, 2021, and will remain in force until September 30, 2021.

414.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 06/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Effective from 1st October 2021, several changes have been made to the tax rates and classifications. Notably, the phrase 'or 12AB' has been added to serial number 3, enhancing the definition of certain tax exemptions. In serial number 17, item (i) has been removed, while item (ii) now specifies the tax treatment for Intellectual Property rights, set at 9%. Additionally, a new entry for job work related to alcoholic liquor has been introduced under serial number 26, also taxed at 9%. Serial number 27 sees the omission of item (i) and a new definition for other manufacturing services, maintaining a 9% tax rate. Changes to admission services under serial number 34 now differentiate between theme parks and casinos, with respective tax rates of 9% and 14%. Lastly, a new classification for multimodal transport services has been added, indicating a structured approach to service categorization.
key_points: Notification No. 06/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In the Table, against serial number 3, 'or 12AB' is added after '12AA'.

For serial number 17, item (i) is omitted, and item (ii) is substituted with 'Temporary or permanent transfer or permitting the use or enjoyment of Intellectual Property (IP) right' with a tax rate of 9%.
For serial number 26, a new entry for job work related to alcoholic liquor is added with a tax rate of 9%.

For serial number 27, item (i) is omitted, and item (ii) is substituted with 'Other manufacturing services; publishing, printing and reproduction services; material recovery services' with a tax rate of 9%.
For serial number 34, the tax rates for admission services to theme parks and casinos are updated to 9% and 14%, respectively.
A new classification for 'Multimodal Transport of goods from a place in India to another place in India' is added in the Annexure.

415.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 06/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2021, amending the earlier Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate). Key changes include the addition of 'or 12AB' after '12AA' in the context of tax exemptions for certain entities. Additionally, in serial number 17, item (i) has been removed, while item (ii) now specifically addresses the transfer or use of Intellectual Property rights. For serial number 26, a new entry has been added for job work related to the manufacture of alcoholic liquor, and item (id) has been updated accordingly. Serial number 27 sees the omission of item (i) and a new definition for item (ii) concerning various manufacturing services. Furthermore, serial number 34 has been revised to clarify admissions to entertainment venues, including theme parks and casinos. Finally, the reference in serial number 38 has been updated to reflect changes in the GST schedule. These amendments will take effect from 1st October 2021, impacting businesses involved in these sectors.
key_points: This notification amends Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It inserts 'or 12AB' after '12AA' in serial number 3, column (3), item (iv), clause (g).
In serial number 17, item (i) and its related entries are omitted, while item (ii) is substituted with new entries for Intellectual Property rights.
In serial number 26, a new entry for job work related to alcoholic liquor is added, and item (id) is updated to include this new entry.
In serial number 27, item (i) is omitted, and item (ii) is substituted with new entries for various manufacturing services.
In serial number 34, items (iii) and (iiia) are substituted to clarify services related to admissions to theme parks and casinos.
In serial number 38, the reference in the Explanation is updated from '234 of Schedule I' to '201A of Schedule II'.
This notification comes into effect from 1st October 2021.

416.summary: The recent Notification No. 07/2021-Central Tax (Rate), effective from 1st October 2021, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate). A significant addition is the new entry 9AB, which provides a nil tax rate for services related to the AFC Women's Asia Cup 2022, contingent upon certification from the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. Additionally, the phrase 'or 12AB' has been inserted in various entries, including serial numbers 1, 9D, 13, 74A, and 80, expanding the scope of applicable provisions. The year '2021' has been updated to '2022' for serial numbers 19A and 19B, reflecting the current timeline. Furthermore, serial number 43 has been removed entirely. A new entry, 61A, has been introduced for services granting National Permits for goods carriages, also with a nil tax rate. Lastly, for serial number 72, the phrase '75% or more of the' has been added, clarifying the conditions under which the entry applies. These changes primarily affect businesses and service providers involved in the specified sectors and events.
key_points: The notification is issued on 30th September 2021 and comes into effect from 1st October 2021.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entry 9AB is added for services related to the AFC Women's Asia Cup 2022, with a nil tax rate, requiring certification from the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
The term 'or 12AB' is added after '12AA' in multiple entries including serial numbers 1, 9D, 13, 74A, and 80.
The year '2021' is replaced with '2022' for serial numbers 19A and 19B.
Serial number 43 and its entries are omitted.
A new entry 61A is added for services related to granting National Permits for goods carriages, with a nil tax rate.
For serial number 72, '75% or more of the' is added after 'for which'.

417.summary: The recent Notification No. 07/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017. Key changes include the addition of 'or 12AB' after '12AA' for several serial numbers, which broadens the scope of applicable provisions. For serial number 10AA, the phrase 'whenever rescheduled' has been added, providing clarity on services hosted in India. A new entry, 10AB, has been introduced for services related to the AFC Women's Asia Cup 2022, which will be exempt from tax, contingent on certification from the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. Additionally, the year '2021' has been updated to '2022' for serial numbers 20A and 20B, reflecting the correct tax period. Serial number 45 has been removed entirely, while a new entry, 64A, has been added for National Permit services, also exempt from tax. Lastly, the phrase '75% or more of the' has been included in the description for serial number 75, clarifying the conditions for that entry. These changes take effect from 1st October 2021 and will impact businesses involved in these specific services.
key_points: This notification amends Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It inserts 'or 12AB' after '12AA' in column (3) against serial numbers 1, 10E, 14, 77A, and 83.
It adds the phrase 'whenever rescheduled' after 'hosted in India' in column (3) against serial number 10AA.
It introduces a new entry 10AB for services related to the AFC Women's Asia Cup 2022, with a nil tax rate, requiring certification from the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
It changes the figures '2021' to '2022' in column (5) against serial numbers 20A and 20B.
It omits serial number 45 and its related entries.
It adds a new entry 64A for services related to granting National Permits for goods carriages, with a nil tax rate.
It inserts '75% or more of the' after 'for which' in column (3) against serial number 75.

418.summary: The Notification No. 08/2021-Central Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the existing GST rates as outlined in the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate). Effective from October 1, 2021, a new entry for 'Tamarind seeds meant for any use other than sowing' has been added to Schedule I at a tax rate of 2.5%. Additionally, several entries, including S. Nos. 138 to 148 and 187A, have been removed from this schedule. A new entry for 'Bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with High Speed Diesel' has also been included. In Schedule II, the entry for S. No. 80A has been revised to clarify that it pertains to bio-diesel not supplied to Oil Marketing Companies. Several entries have been omitted from this schedule as well. Furthermore, Schedule III sees the addition of multiple new entries for various ores and concentrates, enhancing the clarity on GST rates applicable to these goods. These changes will affect businesses dealing with the specified goods, requiring them to adjust their GST calculations accordingly.
key_points: Inserts new entry 71A for 'Tamarind seeds meant for any use other than sowing' in Schedule I at 2.5%.
Omitted entries from Schedule I include S. Nos. 138 to 148 and 187A.
Inserts new entry 186A for 'Bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with High Speed Diesel' in Schedule I.
In Schedule II at 6%, substitutes entry for S. No. 80A to specify 'Bio-diesel (other than bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with High Speed Diesel)'.
Omitted entries from Schedule II include S. No. 122 and S. Nos. 127 to 132.
Inserts new entry 201A in Schedule II for renewable energy devices and parts for their manufacture.
In Schedule III at 9%, inserts multiple new entries for various ores and concentrates, including iron, manganese, copper, and others.
This notification comes into effect on October 1, 2021.

419.summary: The Notification No. 08/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the existing GST rates as outlined in the principal notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate). Effective from 1st October 2021, new entries have been added, such as S. No. 71A for 'Tamarind seeds meant for any use other than sowing' and S. No. 186A for 'Bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with High Speed Diesel' under Schedule I at a 5% rate. Additionally, several entries, including S. Nos. 138 to 148, 187A, and 234, have been removed from this schedule. In Schedule II, a new entry for renewable energy devices has been added as S. No. 201A, while other entries like S. Nos. 122 and 127 to 132 have been omitted. Furthermore, Schedule III sees the introduction of multiple new entries related to various ores and concentrates. These changes will impact businesses dealing with these specific goods, requiring them to adjust their GST calculations and compliance accordingly.
key_points: Inserts S. No. 71A for 'Tamarind seeds meant for any use other than sowing' in Schedule I - 5%.
Omitted S. Nos. 138 to 148 in Schedule I - 5%.
Inserts S. No. 186A for 'Bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with High Speed Diesel' in Schedule I - 5%.
Omitted S. No. 187A and S. No. 234 in Schedule I - 5%.
Inserts S. No. 201A for renewable energy devices in Schedule II - 12%.
Omitted S. Nos. 122 and 127 to 132 in Schedule II - 12%.
Inserts multiple new entries related to ores and concentrates in Schedule III - 18%.
This notification comes into force on 1st October 2021.

420.summary: Notification No. 09/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, brings an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it revises S. No. 86 in the Schedule, which pertains to seeds, fruit, and spores used for sowing. The new entry clarifies that it only includes seeds intended for sowing and explicitly excludes any seeds meant for other purposes. This change aims to provide clearer guidance on the classification of these items under GST. The amendment will take effect from 1st October 2021, meaning businesses dealing with seeds should be aware of this updated classification to . This notification is particularly relevant for agricultural suppliers and businesses involved in the sale of seeds, as it directly impacts how these products are categorized under GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 09/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment specifically substitutes S. No. 86 in the Schedule related to seeds, fruit, and spores used for sowing.
The new entry clarifies that it covers only seeds meant for sowing and excludes any seeds for other uses.
The changes will come into effect on 1st October 2021.

421.summary: Notification No. 09/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, brings an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it substitutes S. No. 86 in the Schedule, which pertains to seeds, fruit, and spores that are used for sowing. The new entry clarifies that it only covers seeds intended for sowing and explicitly excludes those meant for any other purpose. This change is significant for businesses involved in the sale or distribution of seeds, as it delineates the scope of what is covered under this tax category. The amendment will come into effect from 1st October 2021, meaning that businesses will need to adjust their practices accordingly to comply with this updated classification. This clarification aims to streamline the tax treatment of these agricultural products and ensure that only relevant items are taxed under this category.
key_points: This notification is No. 09/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2021.
It amends the earlier notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment specifically substitutes S. No. 86 in the Schedule regarding seeds, fruit, and spores used for sowing.
The new entry clarifies that it does not cover seeds meant for any use other than sowing.
The changes will take effect from 1st October 2021.

422.summary: Notification No. 10/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This amendment adds a new entry in the Table of the original notification, specifically after serial number 3. The new entry pertains to essential oils, including peppermint oil and various other mints. This change is significant as it clarifies the GST treatment of these products. The amendment will be effective from 1st October 2021, meaning that businesses dealing with these essential oils will need to be aware of the updated tax implications. Both unregistered and registered persons who handle these oils will be affected by this change, ensuring that they are aligned with the latest GST regulations regarding these specific products.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added in the Table after S. No. 3, specifically for essential oils.
The new entry includes essential oils such as peppermint oil and other mints.
The notification comes into effect on 1st October 2021.
The new entry applies to both unregistered and registered persons.

423.summary: Notification No. 10/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This amendment adds a new entry after serial number 3, which specifically includes certain essential oils that are not derived from citrus fruits. The newly included oils are peppermint oil, spearmint oil, water mint oil, horsemint oil, and bergamot oil. This change is effective from 1st October 2021. The notification affects both unregistered and registered persons who deal with these essential oils, ensuring they are aware of the updated classification under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act. This amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to refine tax classifications and ensure clarity in the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It inserts a new entry after S. No. 3 in the earlier notification.
The new entry includes essential oils other than citrus fruits, specifically peppermint and various mints.
The new entry is effective from 1st October 2021.
The essential oils listed are: peppermint oil, spearmint oil, water mint oil, horsemint oil, and bergamot oil.
The notification applies to both unregistered and registered persons.

424.summary: Notification No. 11/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, brings important changes to the earlier notification No. 39/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 18th October 2017. Specifically, it modifies the entry against S. No. 1 in the Table. The new entry in column (3) clarifies that it pertains to food preparations that are packaged for free distribution to economically weaker sections and includes fortified rice kernel supply for Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) or similar government-approved schemes. Additionally, in column (4), the term 'food preparations' has been replaced with 'goods' in two instances, broadening the scope of the entry. These changes are effective from 1st October 2021, impacting businesses involved in the supply of these goods, particularly those catering to social welfare programs. This amendment aims to streamline the classification and taxation of these essential supplies.
key_points: This notification is No. 11/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021.
It amends notification No. 39/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 18th October 2017.

In the Table against S. No. 1, the entry in column (3) is changed to specify food preparations intended for free distribution to economically weaker sections and fortified rice kernel supply for ICDS or similar schemes.
In column (4), the term 'food preparations' is replaced with 'goods' at two instances.
The amendments come into effect on 1st October 2021.

425.summary: Notification No. 11/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, brings amendments to the earlier Notification No. 40/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 18th October 2017. Specifically, it modifies the entry under S. No. 1 in the Table. The new entry in column (3) now specifies that food preparations intended for free distribution to economically weaker sections must be packaged in unit containers and includes Fortified Rice Kernel (Premix) for schemes like ICDS. Additionally, the term 'food preparations' in column (4) has been replaced with 'goods' to broaden the scope of the entry. These changes are effective from 1st October 2021. This amendment primarily affects suppliers involved in the distribution of these goods under government-approved programs, ensuring clarity in the classification and treatment of such items under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 40/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 18th October 2017.
In the Table of Notification No. 40/2017, S. No. 1, column (3) is updated to include specific food preparations for free distribution to economically weaker sections.
The updated entry specifies 'Food preparations put up in unit containers' and 'Fortified Rice Kernel (Premix) supply for ICDS or similar scheme'.
In column (4) of the same entry, the term 'food preparations' is replaced with 'goods' at both occurrences.
The changes in the notification come into effect from 1st October 2021.

426.summary: Notification No. 12/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, introduces exemptions from central tax for specific goods under the CGST Act. This includes important medications such as Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B, which are exempt from tax, while others like Remdesivir and Heparin are taxed at a reduced rate of 2.5%. The exemption applies to goods listed under Chapter 30 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975, and is effective from 1st October 2021 until 31st December 2021. This decision was made following recommendations from the GST Council, indicating a focus on public health needs during this period. Businesses dealing with these pharmaceuticals should take note of these changes to with the updated tax rates and exemptions. The notification aims to ease the financial burden associated with these essential medicines during a critical time.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2021-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2021.
The notification exempts specific goods from central tax under section 9 of the CGST Act.
Goods exempted include Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B, and Remdesivir, among others.

The tax rates specified for certain goods are 2.5% for Remdesivir, Heparin, Itolizumab, Posaconazole, Infliximab, Bamlanivimab & Etesevimab, Casirivimab & Imdevimab, 2-Deoxy-D-Glucose, and Favipiravir.
The exemption is effective from 1st October 2021 to 31st December 2021.
The notification is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The goods listed fall under Chapter 30 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.

427.summary: Notification No. 12/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces exemptions from integrated tax for specific goods under Chapter 30 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. The exempted goods include Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B, which are exempt at a nil rate, while Remdesivir and several other medicines such as Heparin, Itolizumab, and Favipiravir are taxed at a reduced rate of 5%. This change is significant for manufacturers and suppliers of these pharmaceuticals, as it reduces their tax burden on these critical health products. The notification is effective from October 1, 2021, and will remain in force until December 31, 2021. This measure is aimed at making essential medicines more accessible during a time of public health need, reflecting the government's commitment to support healthcare initiatives. Businesses dealing with these products should take note of these changes to benefit from the tax exemptions during the specified period.
key_points: Exempts certain goods from integrated tax as per Notification No. 12/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate).

Goods exempted include Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B (Nil rate), and Remdesivir, Heparin, Itolizumab, Posaconazole, Infliximab, Bamlanivimab & Etesevimab, Casirivimab & Imdevimab, 2-Deoxy-D-Glucose, and Favipiravir (5% rate).
The exemption applies to goods under Chapter 30 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
This notification is effective from October 1, 2021, to December 31, 2021.

428.summary: Notification No. 13/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 27th October 2021, brings specific changes to the GST rates as outlined in the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate). Notably, it omits S. No. 243 from Schedule II, which previously listed items taxed at 6%. Additionally, it removes the phrase 'in respect of Information Technology software' from S. No. 452P in Schedule III, which pertains to items taxed at 9%. These changes mean that certain goods or services that were previously categorized under these entries will no longer be subject to the specified GST rates. This amendment is part of the government's ongoing efforts to refine the GST framework based on recommendations from the GST Council. Businesses that were relying on the previously listed items in these schedules should review their tax obligations accordingly, as the removal of these entries could impact their pricing and compliance strategies.
key_points: Omission of S. No. 243 in Schedule II – 6% of Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate).
Removal of the phrase 'in respect of Information Technology software' from S. No. 452P in Schedule III – 9%.
The notification is issued under the powers of section 9(1) and section 15(5) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
This notification is dated 27th October 2021.
The principal notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) was published on 28th June 2017.
The last amendment before this notification was made by notification No. 08/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2021.

429.summary: The recent Notification No. 13/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 27th October 2021, introduces significant amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it omits S. No. 243 and its related entries from Schedule II, which previously listed items taxed at 12%. This change means that the goods or services previously categorized under this entry will no longer be subject to this tax rate. Additionally, the notification removes the phrase 'in respect of Information Technology software' from S. No. 452P in Schedule III, which pertains to items taxed at 18%. This alteration clarifies the tax treatment of certain software products, potentially impacting businesses involved in the IT sector. Overall, these changes reflect the government's ongoing efforts to streamline tax regulations and clarify tax liabilities for specific goods and services.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 27th October 2021.
Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Omission of S. No. 243 and related entries from Schedule II – 12%.
Removal of the phrase 'in respect of Information Technology software' from S. No. 452P in Schedule III – 18%.

430.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 14/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This new notification omits several entries from Schedule I, specifically S.Nos. 203, 207, 211, 216, 217, 218, 218B, 218C, 219A, 219AA, 219B, 220, 221, 222, 223, 224, 224A, and 225, which were previously taxed at 2.5%. In Schedule II, the entry for S.No. 132A is removed, and new entries are added for various woven fabrics, including those made of silk, wool, cotton, and synthetic materials. Additionally, S.No. 132B is also omitted, with new entries related to synthetic and artificial filament yarns being introduced. Furthermore, several entries in Schedule III are removed, specifically S.Nos. 159, 160, 161, 162, and 163. These amendments will come into effect on 1st January 2022, impacting businesses dealing with the specified goods and fabrics.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2021-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 18th November 2021.
Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Omissions in Schedule II (6%): S.No. 132A and S.No. 132B.
New entries added in Schedule II after S.No. 132A and S.No. 132B, including various woven fabrics of silk, wool, cotton, and synthetic materials.
Omissions in Schedule III (9%): S.Nos. 159, 160, 161, 162, and 163.
The changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

431.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 14/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Significant changes include the omission of multiple entries in Schedule I (5%), specifically S.Nos. 203, 207, 211, 216, 217, 218, 218B, 218C, 219A, 219AA, 219B, 220, 221, 222, 223, 224, 224A, and 225. In Schedule II (12%), S.No. 132A is removed, while new entries for various woven fabrics are added from S.No. 132AA to 132AH. Additionally, S.No. 132B is omitted, and new entries for synthetic and artificial filament yarns are introduced from S.No. 132BA to 132BH. The same pattern follows for S.No. 132C, which is omitted, and new entries for synthetic and artificial staple fibres are added from S.No. 132CA to 132CP. Furthermore, the description for S.No. 139 regarding twine and ropes has been updated. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses dealing with these specific goods and services.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 18th November 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule II (12%), S.No. 132A is omitted, and new entries from S.No. 132AA to 132AH are inserted for various woven fabrics.
S.No. 132B is omitted, and new entries from S.No. 132BA to 132BH are inserted for synthetic and artificial filament yarns.
S.No. 132C is omitted, and new entries from S.No. 132CA to 132CP are inserted for synthetic and artificial staple fibres.
S.No. 139 is amended to change the description of twine, cordage, ropes, and cables.
This notification will come into effect on 1st January 2022.

432.summary: Notification No. 15/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 18th November 2021, brings significant amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. The changes primarily affect the description of services listed under serial number 3. Specifically, for items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x), the terms 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' have been replaced with 'Union territory or a local authority'. A similar change is made for item (vii). Additionally, the conditions listed in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) have been completely removed. Furthermore, under serial number 26, item (i), clause (b), a new phrase has been added to exclude services related to dyeing or printing of textiles from the previous description. These amendments will take effect from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses and entities involved in the specified services.
key_points: This notification is issued on 18th November 2021.
It amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes are made in the table under serial number 3, specifically in columns (3) and (5).
For items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) in column (3), 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' is replaced with 'Union territory or a local authority'.
For item (vii) in column (3), the same replacement is made.
Entries in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) are omitted.
Under serial number 26, item (i), clause (b), the phrase 'except services by way of dyeing or printing of the said textile and textile products' is added.
This notification will come into effect from 1st January 2022.

433.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 15/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically alters the description of services listed in the table under serial numbers 3 and 26. For serial number 3, the terms 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' have been removed from items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x), now only referring to 'or a local authority'. Additionally, for serial number 26, a new clause has been added to item (i) in clause (b), which states that services related to dyeing or printing of textiles are excluded from the previous provisions. Furthermore, certain conditions listed in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) under serial number 3 have been omitted. These changes will come into effect from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses involved in these services and requiring them to adjust their operations accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 18th November 2021.
Amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the description of services in the table for serial numbers 3 and 26.
For serial number 3, modifies items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) to replace 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' with 'or a local authority'.
For serial number 26, adds a proviso to item (i) in clause (b) stating 'except services by way of dyeing or printing of the said textile and textile products'.
Omissions made in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) under serial number 3.
The changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

434.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 16/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically removes the phrase 'or a Governmental authority or a Government Entity' from the descriptions of services listed under serial numbers 3 and 3A. Additionally, new provisions have been added to serial numbers 15 and 17, clarifying that the services described in items (b) and (c) at serial number 15, as well as item (e) at serial number 17, will not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator. These changes are significant for businesses engaged in e-commerce, as they delineate the applicability of certain tax provisions. The amendments will come into effect from 1st January 2022, impacting how services are categorized and taxed under the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2021-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 18th November 2021.
Amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Omitted the phrase 'or a Governmental authority or a Government Entity' from serial numbers 3 and 3A in the description of services.
Inserted a proviso after item (c) at serial number 15 stating that items (b) and (c) do not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator.
Inserted a proviso after item (e) at serial number 17 stating that item (e) does not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator.
The changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.
