435.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 16/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Notably, the phrase 'or a Governmental authority or a Government Entity' has been removed from the descriptions of services listed under serial numbers 3 and 3A. Additionally, new provisions have been introduced for serial numbers 16 and 18, clarifying that certain items do not apply to services provided through an electronic commerce operator. These changes aim to streamline the tax framework for services and will take effect from 1st January 2022. Businesses involved in providing services through electronic commerce operators should pay particular attention to these amendments, as they may impact their tax obligations and compliance requirements moving forward.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 18th November 2021.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments include omitting the phrase 'or a Governmental authority or a Government Entity' from serial numbers 3 and 3A in the description of services.
A new proviso has been added after item (c) in serial number 16, stating that items (b) and (c) do not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator.
Another new proviso has been added after item (e) in serial number 18, indicating that item (e) does not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator.
These changes will come into effect from 1st January 2022.

436.summary: Notification No. 17/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 18th November 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 17/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The amendment expands the definition of motor vehicles to include 'omnibus or any other motor vehicle' in place of just 'motor cycle'. Additionally, a new clause is introduced that specifies the inclusion of restaurant services, excluding those from restaurants located at specified premises. The definition of motor vehicles is further clarified in the Explanation, now encompassing 'motor vehicle and omnibus' as per the Motor Vehicle Act, 1988. Furthermore, a new definition for 'specified premises' is established, which refers to hotels with accommodation tariffs exceeding ₹7,500 per day. These changes will come into effect from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses in the transportation and hospitality sectors by clarifying tax implications on services and vehicle classifications.
key_points: This notification is issued on 18th November 2021.
It amends Notification No. 17/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Clause (i) is amended to include 'omnibus or any other motor vehicle' after 'motor cycle'.
A new clause (iv) is added to include 'supply of restaurant service other than the services supplied by restaurant, eating joints etc. located at specified premises.'
In the Explanation, item (b) is amended to include 'motor vehicle and omnibus' with definitions from the Motor Vehicle Act, 1988.
A new item (c) is added to define 'specified premises' as those providing hotel accommodation with a declared tariff above ₹7,500 per unit per day.
The changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

437.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 17/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, amending the earlier Notification No. 14/2017. This amendment introduces several changes effective from 1st January 2022. Firstly, it expands the definition of vehicles in clause (i) to include 'motor cycle, omnibus or any other motor vehicle.' Additionally, a new clause (iv) has been added, which specifies that 'restaurant service' does not include services from restaurants located at 'specified premises.' The term 'specified premises' is defined as those providing hotel accommodation with a declared tariff exceeding ₹7,500 per unit per day. Furthermore, the explanation in item (b) has been updated to include 'motor vehicle and omnibus' with definitions aligned to the Motor Vehicle Act, 1988. These changes primarily affect businesses in the transportation and hospitality sectors, particularly those involved in providing restaurant services and accommodations.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 14/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Substitutes 'and motor cycle;' with ', motor cycle, omnibus or any other motor vehicle;' in clause (i).
Inserts a new clause (iv) for the supply of 'restaurant service' excluding those from specified premises.
Updates Explanation item (b) to include 'motor vehicle and omnibus' with definitions from the Motor Vehicle Act, 1988.
Defines 'specified premises' as those providing hotel accommodation with a declared tariff above ₹7,500 per unit per day.
Notification comes into effect from 1st January 2022.

438.summary: Notification No. 18/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on December 28, 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. This update includes significant changes across various schedules. In Schedule I, for instance, the entry for S. No. 2 now lists specific HS codes for fish products, while S. No. 9 has been revised to provide a detailed description of yoghurt and cream. Additionally, S. No. 14 now includes insects and other edible animal products. In Schedule II, S. No. 15 has been updated to specify various dried nuts, and S. No. 25 now covers animal or microbial fats. Schedule III has undergone extensive changes, with S. Nos. 26A to 26L being replaced with new entries for various ores and concentrates. Furthermore, Schedule IV introduces new entries for tobacco products intended for inhalation without combustion. These amendments aim to clarify product classifications and descriptions, impacting businesses dealing with these goods.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th December 2021.
Amendments were made to the notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, several entries were substituted, including S. No. 2 with new HS codes for fish products and S. No. 9 with a detailed description of yoghurt and cream.
S. No. 14 was updated to include insects and other edible animal products.
In Schedule II, S. No. 15 was amended to specify various dried nuts, and S. No. 25 was updated to include animal or microbial fats.
Schedule III saw the substitution of S. Nos. 26A to 26L with new entries for various ores and concentrates.
New entries were added in Schedule IV for products containing tobacco intended for inhalation without combustion.
Several entries across all schedules were omitted or substituted to reflect updated product descriptions and classifications.

439.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 18/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on December 28, 2021, amending the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. This notification introduces several changes across different schedules. In Schedule I, new descriptions were provided for various goods, including fish products and edible insects, under entries like S. No. 2 and S. No. 14. Schedule II also saw updates, particularly for entries related to nuts and animal fats, with S. No. 15 and others being revised. Schedule III underwent significant changes, particularly with new entries for nicotine products and various ores, replacing previous entries. Additionally, Schedule IV was updated to include new tobacco products intended for inhalation. Some entries, such as S. No. 49 in Schedule II and S. No. 41 in Schedule III, were omitted entirely. These changes affect manufacturers and suppliers of the specified goods, requiring them to update their records and compliance accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th December 2021.
Amendments were made to Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, several entries were substituted, including S. No. 2, 9, 14, 87, 107, and 127 with new descriptions for goods.
In Schedule II, entries for S. No. 15, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 41, and 144 were updated with new descriptions.
Schedule III saw changes for S. No. 26A to 26L, with new entries for products containing nicotine and various ores.
Schedule IV was amended to include new entries for tobacco products intended for inhalation.
Specific entries were omitted, such as S. No. 49 in Schedule II and S. No. 41 in Schedule III.

440.summary: Notification No. 19/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 28th December 2021, brings several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Notably, the entry for S. No. 22 has been updated to include specific HS codes: '0303, 0304, 0305, 0306, 0307, 0308, 0309'. Additionally, S. No. 43B now specifies 'Vegetables provisionally preserved, but unsuitable in that state for immediate consumption'. The entry for S. No. 49 has been revised to encompass various fresh nuts, including almonds and walnuts. A new entry, S. No. 97A, has been introduced for 'Tender coconut water', subject to certain conditions. Furthermore, S. No. 101 has been completely omitted, while the entry for S. No. 141 has been updated to '8807'. These changes will be effective from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses dealing with these specific goods and their classifications under GST.
key_points: Notification No. 19/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th December 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The entry for S. No. 43B is changed to 'Vegetables provisionally preserved, but unsuitable in that state for immediate consumption'.
The entry for S. No. 49 is revised to include various fresh nuts such as almonds, walnuts, and pistachios.

S. No. 101 and its related entries are omitted.
The entry for S. No. 141 is updated to '8807'.
These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

441.summary: On 28th December 2021, the Government of India issued Notification No. 19/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) effective from 1st January 2022. This amendment updates several entries in the tax rate schedule. Specifically, for S. No. 22, the codes for certain fish products are now listed as '0303, 0304, 0305, 0306, 0307, 0308, 0309'. The description for S. No. 43B has been revised to clarify that it pertains to vegetables that are preserved but not suitable for immediate consumption. Additionally, S. No. 49 now includes a detailed list of fresh nuts, such as almonds and walnuts. A new entry, S. No. 97A, has been introduced for tender coconut water, subject to specific branding conditions. Furthermore, S. No. 101 has been removed entirely, and the entry for S. No. 141 has been updated to '8807'. These changes will affect businesses dealing with these specific goods, ensuring they are aware of the updated classifications and descriptions for compliance.

S. No. 101 and its entries are omitted, and for S. No. 141, the entry in column (2) is updated to '8807'.

442.summary: Notification No. 20/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on December 28, 2021, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 21/2018-Central Tax (Rate). Specifically, it updates the entries in the table of the earlier notification. For S. No. 4, the previous entry has been replaced with '4414', and for S. No. 29, the entry has been updated to '7419 80'. These changes are significant as they reflect the latest classifications for goods under the GST regime. Businesses dealing with the affected goods should take note of these new codes to ensure proper compliance with GST regulations. The amendments will take effect from January 1, 2022, meaning that any transactions involving these goods after this date should use the new entries. This notification is part of the government's ongoing efforts to streamline and clarify GST rates and classifications.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 21/2018-Central Tax (Rate) dated 26th July 2018.
Changes the entry for S. No. 4 in the table to '4414'.
Changes the entry for S. No. 29 in the table to '7419 80'.
This notification comes into effect on 1st January 2022.

443.summary: Notification No. 20/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 28th December 2021, brings important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 22/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 26th July 2018. Specifically, it updates the entries in the table of the previous notification. For S. No. 4, the entry in column (2) has been substituted with '4414', while for S. No. 29, the entry has been changed to '7419 80'. These changes are significant as they may affect the classification and tax rates applicable to certain goods. Businesses dealing with the items classified under these S. No. entries should take note of these amendments to ensure proper compliance. The new entries will be effective starting from 1st January 2022, meaning that any transactions involving these goods after this date will need to adhere to the updated classifications. This notification reflects the government's ongoing efforts to refine GST regulations in response to market needs.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 22/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 26th July 2018.
The changes affect the entries in the table of the earlier notification.
For S. No. 4, the entry in column (2) is changed to '4414'.
For S. No. 29, the entry in column (2) is changed to '7419 80'.
The amendments will come into effect on 1st January 2022.

444.summary: Notification No. 21/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2021, introduces significant changes to the GST rates applicable to certain goods. It supersedes the earlier Notification No. 14/2021, which was issued on 18th November 2021. Notably, the entry for serial number 225 in Schedule I, which was taxed at 2.5%, has been completely removed. Additionally, a new entry has been added to Schedule II as serial number 171A1, which specifies that footwear with a sale value not exceeding Rs. 1000 per pair will now be taxed at 6%. This change is aimed at providing clarity and potentially easing the tax burden on lower-priced footwear. The new provisions will take effect from 1st January 2022. This notification also references the principal notification No. 01/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017, which has undergone several amendments, the most recent being Notification No. 18/2021 on 28th December 2021. Businesses dealing in footwear should take note of these changes to with the updated tax structure.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2021.
This notification supersedes Notification No. 14/2021-Central Tax (Rate) dated 18th November 2021.
In Schedule I, the entry for serial number 225 at 2.5% has been omitted.
In Schedule II, a new entry has been added as serial number 171A1 for footwear with a sale value not exceeding Rs. 1000 per pair, taxed at 6%.
This notification will come into force on 1st January 2022.
The principal notification affected is No. 01/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The last amendment to the principal notification was made by Notification No. 18/2021-Central Tax (Rate) on 28th December 2021.

445.summary: Notification No. 21/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2021, introduces significant changes to the GST rates applicable to certain goods. It supersedes the earlier Notification No. 14/2021, which was effective from 18th November 2021. Notably, the entry for 5% tax on footwear listed under serial number 225 in Schedule I has been completely removed. Additionally, a new entry has been added to Schedule II, introducing a 12% tax rate for footwear with a sale value not exceeding Rs. 1000 per pair, designated as serial number 171A1. These amendments will take effect from 1st January 2022. This notification is part of ongoing adjustments to the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework, which was originally established by the principal notification No. 1/2017 on 28th June 2017. The last amendment prior to this was made on 28th December 2021, indicating a continuous evolution of tax regulations in response to market conditions.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2021.
This notification supersedes Notification No. 14/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 18th November 2021.
In Schedule I, the entry for 5% tax on serial number 225 has been omitted.
In Schedule II, a new entry has been added for 12% tax: serial number 171A1 for footwear with a sale value not exceeding Rs. 1000 per pair.
The changes will come into effect on 1st January 2022.
The principal notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was originally published on 28th June 2017.
The last amendment before this notification was made by Notification No. 18/2021 on 28th December 2021.

446.summary: Notification No. 22/2021-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2021, introduces several amendments to the existing GST rate structure. It supersedes the earlier Notification No. 15/2021-Central Tax (Rate) from 18th November 2021. The key changes affect Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, in the table of services, the descriptions for items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) now replace the terms 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' with 'Union territory or a local authority'. A similar change is made for item (vii). Additionally, the conditions listed in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) have been completely removed. These amendments will come into effect from 1st January 2022, impacting the classification and conditions under which certain services are taxed under GST.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2021-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2021.
This notification supersedes Notification No. 15/2021-Central Tax (Rate) dated 18th November 2021.
Amendments are made to Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

In the table of Notification No. 11/2017, the description of services for items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) is changed to replace 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' with 'Union territory or a local authority'.
For item (vii), the same substitution is made as in items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x).
Entries in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) are omitted.
These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

447.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 22/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 31st December 2021, which amends previous notifications regarding the Integrated Goods and Services Tax. This new notification supersedes Notification No. 15/2021, and it specifically modifies Notification No. 08/2017. Key changes include the removal of references to 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' in the descriptions of services listed under serial number 3 for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x). Additionally, the conditions previously outlined for these items have been completely omitted. These amendments are set to come into effect from 1st January 2022. This means that businesses and entities that were previously required to consider these authorities in their service descriptions will now only need to focus on 'Union territory or a local authority', simplifying compliance for those affected by these changes.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2021.
This notification supersedes Notification No. 15/2021-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 18th November 2021.
Amendments are made to Notification No. 08/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In the table, against serial number 3, the description of services in items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) is changed to remove references to 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity'.
In item (vii), the description is similarly amended to remove 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity'.
Entries in column (5) under 'Condition' for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) are omitted.
These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

448.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2021 on 2nd June 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it updates Schedule I by changing the entry for S. No. 259A to '9503'. Additionally, a new item, 'Diethylcarbamazine', has been introduced as S. No. 231 in the List 1, following the existing entries. This means that businesses dealing with these specific goods will need to take note of the updated tax rates and classifications. The changes are effective immediately from the date of the notification, which is also 2nd June 2021. This amendment is part of the ongoing efforts to refine and clarify the GST framework in Union Territories, ensuring that the tax structure remains relevant and up-to-date. Stakeholders in the Union Territories should review these changes to with the new tax rates.
key_points: This notification is issued on 2nd June 2021.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, the entry for S. No. 259A is changed to '9503'.
A new entry for 'Diethylcarbamazine' is added as S. No. 231 in the List 1 after serial number 230.
The notification comes into force on the same date it is issued, 2nd June 2021.

449.summary: Notification No. 01/2021-Central Tax, issued on 1st May 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax. This amendment introduces new penalty structures for taxpayers based on their aggregate turnover for the tax periods of March and April 2021. Specifically, taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores will incur a penalty of 9% for the first 15 days after the due date, escalating to 18% thereafter. In contrast, those with a turnover of up to 5 crores will not face any penalty for the first 15 days, followed by a 9% penalty for the next 15 days, and an 18% penalty thereafter. This change aims to provide a more structured approach to penalties based on turnover, effective from 18th April 2021. The amendment is significant for businesses as it clarifies the financial implications of late returns, depending on their revenue size.
key_points: This notification is issued under Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax, dated 30th June 2017.
It amends the first proviso in the Table after S. No. 3 of the earlier notification.
New entries are added for taxpayers with aggregate turnover above and below 5 crores for the tax periods of March 2021 and April 2021.
Taxpayers with turnover above 5 crores will face a penalty of 9% for the first 15 days from the due date and 18% thereafter.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores will have a penalty of nil for the first 15 days, 9% for the next 15 days, and 18% thereafter.
The notification is effective from 18th April 2021.

450.summary: On June 2, 2021, the Government of India issued Notification No. 02/2021 to amend the earlier Notification No. 11/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. A significant change is the addition of a new clause (iii) in the fourth proviso of serial number 3, which allows landowner-promoters to use the tax credit charged by developers for the tax on apartments they supply. This change is beneficial for landowners involved in real estate projects, as it provides them with more flexibility in managing their tax liabilities. Additionally, in serial number 25, a new item (ib) has been introduced, which pertains to maintenance, repair, or overhaul services for ships and other vessels, with a tax rate set at 2.5%. Furthermore, the existing item (ii) in the same serial number has been updated to include this new item. These amendments are effective immediately from the date of the notification, impacting businesses involved in real estate and maritime services within the Union Territories.
key_points: The notification is dated June 2, 2021.
It amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
A new clause (iii) is added to the fourth proviso in the Explanation of serial number 3, allowing landowner-promoters to utilize tax credit from developers for tax on apartments supplied.
In serial number 25, a new item (ib) is added for maintenance, repair, or overhaul services for ships and vessels, with a tax rate of 2.5%.
The existing item (ii) in serial number 25 is amended to include the new item (ib).
The changes are effective from June 2, 2021.

451.summary: Notification No. 02/2021-Central Tax, issued on June 1, 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax. The first proviso has been revised to replace the requirement for taxpayers to furnish returns in FORM GSTR-3B with a broader statement that they are 'liable to pay tax but fail to do so'. Additionally, the heading in the Table has been changed from 'Tax period' to 'Month/Quarter'. The new provisions for penalties are now specified for taxpayers based on their aggregate turnover. For those with a turnover exceeding 5 crores, a penalty of 9% applies for the first 15 days after the due date, followed by 18% thereafter for the months of March, April, and May 2021. For taxpayers with a turnover up to 5 crores, the penalties vary: they face no penalty for the first 15 days, then 9% for the next 30 days for April 2021, and similar structures for the other months. This notification is effective from May 18, 2021.
key_points: The notification amends the earlier Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax dated 30th June 2017.
The first proviso now substitutes 'required to furnish the returns in FORM GSTR-3B, but fail to furnish the said return along with payment of tax' with 'liable to pay tax but fail to do so'.
In the Table, the heading in column 4 changes from 'Tax period' to 'Month/Quarter'.
For serial numbers 4, 5, 6, and 7 in the Table, new provisions are introduced for taxpayers based on their aggregate turnover.
Taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores face a penalty of 9% for the first 15 days and 18% thereafter for March, April, and May 2021.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores have varying penalties: Nil for the first 15 days, then 9% for the next 30 days for April 2021, and similar structures for March and May 2021.
The notification is effective from 18th May 2021.

452.summary: Notification No. 03/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 2nd June 2021, amends the earlier Notification No. 06/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 29th March 2019. The key changes include a modification in the language regarding tax liability. Specifically, the phrase 'in whose case the liability to' has been replaced with 'who shall', clarifying the responsibility for tax. Additionally, the timing of when the tax liability arises has been updated. Instead of stating that it arises on the date of the completion certificate or first occupation, it now specifies that it should occur in a tax period not later than the period in which the completion certificate or first occupation date falls. These amendments are effective immediately from the date of issuance, 2nd June 2021, and are made under the authority of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change is relevant for businesses operating in Union Territories, particularly those involved in projects requiring completion certificates.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 03/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 2nd June 2021.
It amends Notification No. 06/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019.
The amendment substitutes 'in whose case the liability to' with 'who shall' in the first paragraph.

It also changes the phrase regarding the timing of tax liability from 'shall arise on the date of issuance of completion certificate' to 'in a tax period not later than the tax period in which the date of issuance of the completion certificate... falls'.
The changes take effect from 2nd June 2021.
The amendments are made under the powers conferred by section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The principal notification No. 06/2019 was published under G.S.R. 265(E) on 29th March 2019.

453.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 04/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 14th June 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This new notification introduces a proviso in the Table against serial number 3, item (iv), stating that from 14th June 2021 until 30th September 2021, the union territory tax on certain specified services will be levied at a reduced rate of 2.5%. This change applies specifically to the services outlined in clause (f) of the original notification. The amendment was made following recommendations from the GST Council and is intended to serve the public interest. This is the second amendment to the original notification, with the previous one being made on 2nd June 2021 through Notification No. 02/2021. Businesses providing the specified services should take note of this temporary tax rate adjustment during the stated period.
key_points: Notification No. 04/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 14th June 2021.
Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts a new proviso in the Table against serial number 3, item (iv).
New proviso states that from 14th June 2021 to 30th September 2021, union territory tax on specified services will be 2.5%.
The specified services are detailed in clause (f) of the existing notification.
This amendment is made under various sections of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The amendment is based on recommendations from the GST Council.
The previous amendment to Notification No. 11/2017 was made by Notification No. 02/2021 on 2nd June 2021.

454.summary: On 15th June 2021, a corrigendum was issued to Notification No. 05/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 14th June 2021. This corrigendum specifically corrects an error in the classification of goods listed in the original notification. In the Table against Sl. No. 8, the code previously stated as '3804 94' has been amended to '3808 94'. This change is significant as it impacts how certain goods are classified under the Union Territory Tax regime. Businesses dealing with these goods should take note of this correction to ensure accurate compliance with tax regulations. The original notification and this corrigendum were published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that the information is officially documented. The reference number for this corrigendum is F.No. CBIC-190354/63/2021-TO(TRU-I)-CBEC, which can be used for further inquiries or clarifications.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 05/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 14th June 2021.
The corrigendum was issued on 15th June 2021.
It specifically amends the entry against Sl. No. 8 in the Table of the original notification.
In column (2) of Sl. No. 8, the code '3804 94' is corrected to '3808 94'.
This change affects the classification of goods under the Union Territory Tax.
The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The corrigendum is referenced under F.No. CBIC-190354/63/2021-TO(TRU-I)-CBEC.

455.summary: Notification No. 05/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 14th June 2021, introduces exemptions for specific goods from the Union Territory tax. This exemption applies to items such as Medical Grade Oxygen, Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B, and Remdesvir, among others. The tax rates for these goods vary, with most items being taxed at 2.5%, while some, like Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B, are exempt from tax entirely. Additionally, ambulances are subject to a higher tax rate of 6%. This notification is aimed at easing the financial burden during the ongoing health crisis and will remain in effect until 30th September 2021. Businesses dealing with these goods should take note of these exemptions to and benefit from the reduced tax rates during this period.
key_points: Exempts certain goods from Union Territory tax as per Notification No. 05/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 14th June 2021.
The exemption applies to goods listed in the provided table, including Medical Grade Oxygen, Tocilizumab, and Remdesvir.
The tax rates for these goods range from 0% to 6%, with most items taxed at 2.5%.
The notification is effective until 30th September 2021.

456.summary: The recent Notification No. 06/2021 amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017. Key changes include the addition of 'or 12AB' after '12AA' in the context of tax exemptions related to certain entities. In serial number 17, item (i) has been removed, and item (ii) now specifically addresses the transfer or use of Intellectual Property rights. Additionally, for serial number 26, a new entry has been introduced for job work services related to the manufacture of alcoholic liquor. The amendments also affect serial number 27, where item (i) is omitted, and item (ii) now covers various manufacturing and related services. Furthermore, serial number 34 has been updated to include admissions to theme parks and casinos, while serial number 38 corrects a reference in the Explanation. These changes will take effect from 1st October 2021, impacting businesses involved in these sectors.
key_points: This notification amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It inserts 'or 12AB' after '12AA' in serial number 3, column (3), item (iv), clause (g).
In serial number 17, item (i) and its related entries are omitted, while item (ii) is substituted with new entries for Intellectual Property rights.
For serial number 26, a new entry for job work related to alcoholic liquor is added, and existing entries are updated.
In serial number 27, item (i) is omitted, and item (ii) is substituted with new entries for manufacturing and related services.
For serial number 34, items (iii) and (iiia) are substituted to include admissions to theme parks, casinos, and sporting events.
In serial number 38, the reference in the Explanation is updated from '234 of Schedule I' to '201A of Schedule II'.
This notification is effective from 1st October 2021.

457.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 07/2021 on 30th September 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This new notification introduces several changes effective from 1st October 2021. Notably, it adds new entries for services related to the AFC Women's Asia Cup 2022, which will be hosted in India. Additionally, the phrase 'or 12AB' has been included in various entries, such as those for serial numbers 1, 9D, and 13, expanding the scope of applicable provisions. The year '2021' has been updated to '2022' for serial numbers 19A and 19B, reflecting the correct timeline for these services. Furthermore, serial number 43 has been removed entirely. A new entry for granting National Permits to goods carriages has been introduced as serial number 61A, and for serial number 72, the requirement of '75% or more of the' has been added. These changes will impact businesses involved in these specific services and events, ensuring they are aligned with the latest tax regulations.
key_points: This notification is issued on 30th September 2021.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries are added for services related to the AFC Women's Asia Cup 2022, effective from 1st October 2021.
The term 'or 12AB' is added after '12AA' in several entries including serial numbers 1, 9D, and 13.
The year '2021' is replaced with '2022' for serial numbers 19A and 19B.
Serial number 43 and its entries are omitted.
A new entry for granting National Permit to goods carriage is added as serial number 61A.
For serial number 72, '75% or more of the' is added after 'for which'.

458.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 8/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2021, which introduces several amendments to the existing tax rates. Effective from 1st October 2021, new entries have been added to Schedule I, including S. No. 71A for tamarind seeds and S. No. 186A for bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies. Additionally, multiple entries have been omitted from Schedule I, specifically S. Nos. 138 to 148 and S. No. 187A. In Schedule II, the entry for bio-diesel at S. No. 80A has been revised to clarify that it does not include bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies. Furthermore, a new entry for renewable energy devices has been added under S. No. 201A. Schedule III has also been updated with new entries for various ores, including iron and copper. Lastly, Schedule IV now includes a new entry for carbonated beverages with fruit juice under S. No. 12B. These changes will affect businesses dealing with these specific goods and services.
key_points: Notification No. 8/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
New entries added to Schedule I include S. No. 71A for tamarind seeds and S. No. 186A for bio-diesel supplied to Oil Marketing Companies.
Several entries were omitted from Schedule I, including S. Nos. 138 to 148 and S. No. 187A.
In Schedule II, the entry for S. No. 80A was substituted to specify bio-diesel not supplied to Oil Marketing Companies.
New entries were added to Schedule II for renewable energy devices under S. No. 201A.
Schedule III saw new entries for various ores and concentrates, including iron and copper ores.
Schedule IV added a new entry for carbonated beverages with fruit juice under S. No. 12B.
This notification comes into effect on 1st October 2021.

459.summary: Notification No. 09/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, brings an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it revises the entry for S. No. 86 in the Schedule. The new entry now specifies '1209 Seeds, fruit and spores, of a kind used for sowing', and it explicitly states that this entry does not include seeds intended for any purpose other than sowing. This change aims to clarify the classification of seeds under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework. The amendment will take effect from 1st October 2021, impacting businesses involved in the sale or distribution of seeds, ensuring they are aware of the specific tax implications related to sowing seeds. This clarification is crucial for compliance and accurate tax reporting in the Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is No. 09/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2021.
It amends the earlier notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The specific change is in the Schedule, where S. No. 86 is substituted.
The new entry for S. No. 86 is '1209 Seeds, fruit and spores, of a kind used for sowing'.
The new entry clarifies that it does not cover seeds meant for any use other than sowing.
This amendment will come into force on 1st October 2021.

460.summary: Notification No. 10/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2021, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, a new entry has been added after serial number 3, which pertains to essential oils other than those derived from citrus fruits. This includes oils from peppermint and various types of mints such as spearmint, water mint, and horsemint. The amendment affects both unregistered and registered persons who deal with these essential oils. Importantly, this change will take effect from 1st October 2021, meaning that businesses involved in the trade of these oils will need to be aware of the new tax implications starting on that date. This update is part of the ongoing efforts to refine the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework and ensure clarity in tax rates applicable to specific goods.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry has been added after S. No. 3 in the earlier notification.
The new entry includes essential oils other than citrus fruits, specifically peppermint and various mints.
The new entry is applicable to both unregistered and registered persons.
This amendment will come into force on 1st October 2021.

461.summary: The recent Notification No. 11/2021, issued on 30th September 2021, brings amendments to the earlier Notification No. 39/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it alters the entry against S. No. 1 in the Table of the previous notification. The new entry now includes 'Food preparations put up in unit containers intended for free distribution to economically weaker sections' and 'Fortified Rice Kernel (Premix) supply for ICDS or similar scheme'. Additionally, the term 'food preparations' has been replaced with 'goods' in column (4) of the same entry. These changes are set to take effect from 1st October 2021. This amendment primarily affects suppliers and distributors involved in the specified food preparations and fortified rice kernel supplies, ensuring clarity in the categorization of these goods under the Union Territory GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2021.
This notification amends Notification No. 39/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 18th October 2017.
In the Table of Notification No. 39/2017, against S. No. 1, the entry in column (3) has been substituted.

The new entry in column (3) specifies 'Food preparations put up in unit containers for free distribution to economically weaker sections' and 'Fortified Rice Kernel (Premix) supply for ICDS or similar scheme'.
In column (4) of the same entry, the term 'food preparations' has been replaced with 'goods' at both occurrences.
The changes are effective from 1st October 2021.

462.summary: Notification No. 12/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) introduces tax exemptions for specific medical goods, effective from October 1, 2021, until December 31, 2021. This notification exempts goods such as Tocilizumab and Amphotericin B from Union territory tax, while others like Remdesivir, Heparin, and Itolizumab are taxed at a reduced rate of 2.5%. The decision to implement these exemptions was made based on recommendations from the GST Council, highlighting the government's focus on public health needs. This means that businesses dealing with these specified medical goods will benefit from reduced tax liabilities during this period. The exemption is grounded in section 7 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring that these essential medicines remain accessible and affordable. Overall, this notification aims to support healthcare providers and patients by easing the financial burden associated with these critical treatments.
key_points: This notification is issued under Notification No. 12/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate).
The notification exempts certain goods from Union territory tax as specified in the Table.
The goods listed include Tocilizumab, Amphotericin B, and Remdesivir, among others.
The tax rates for Remdesivir, Heparin, Itolizumab, and others are set at 2.5%.
The exemption applies from October 1, 2021, to December 31, 2021.
The notification is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The exemption is applicable under section 7 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

463.summary: The recent Notification No. 13/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 27th October 2021, brings specific changes to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Notably, it removes entry S. No. 243 from Schedule II, which means that whatever goods or services were previously listed under this entry will no longer be subject to the 6% Union Territory tax. Additionally, in Schedule III, the phrase 'in respect of Information Technology software' has been omitted from entry S. No. 452P, indicating a change in how this category is treated under the 9% tax rate. These amendments may affect businesses dealing with the specified goods and services, as they will need to adjust their tax calculations and compliance accordingly. Overall, this notification reflects the government's ongoing adjustments to the Union Territory tax framework, aiming for clarity and efficiency in tax administration.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 27th October 2021.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule II, entry S. No. 243 and its related entries are omitted.
In Schedule III, for entry S. No. 452P, the phrase 'in respect of Information Technology software' is removed.

464.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 14/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the previous notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Significant changes include the omission of several entries in Schedule I at the 2.5% tax rate, specifically S.Nos. 203, 207, 211, 216, 217, 218, 218B, 218C, 219A, 219AA, 219B, 220, 221, 222, 223, 224, 224A, and 225. In Schedule II at the 6% rate, S.No. 132A is removed, and new entries for various woven fabrics, including silk and wool, are introduced. Additionally, S.No. 132B is omitted, with new entries for sewing threads and synthetic filament yarns added. The amendments also include the removal of S.No. 132C and the introduction of new entries for synthetic and artificial staple fibres. Lastly, in Schedule III at the 9% rate, S.Nos. 159, 160, 161, 162, and 163 are omitted. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses dealing with the specified goods and fabrics.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 18th November 2021.
This notification amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule II at 6%, S.No. 132A is omitted, and new entries for woven fabrics of silk, wool, cotton, and synthetic materials are added.
S.No. 132B is omitted, and new entries for sewing threads and synthetic filament yarns are added.
S.No. 132C is omitted, with new entries for synthetic and artificial staple fibres added.
In Schedule III at 9%, S.Nos. 159, 160, 161, 162, and 163 are omitted.
This notification will come into force on 1st January 2022.

465.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 15/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017. This amendment primarily affects the description of services listed in the table against serial number 3. Specifically, it removes the terms 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' from items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x), and also from item (vii). Additionally, certain conditions previously listed in column (5) for these items have been omitted. Furthermore, a new clause has been added to serial number 26, item (i), clause (b), which excludes services related to dyeing or printing of textiles from the Customs Tariff Act. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022, impacting businesses that provide these services in the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 18th November 2021.
Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the description of services in the table against serial number 3, items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) to remove 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity'.
Modifies item (vii) in the same table to also remove 'Governmental Authority or a Government Entity'.
Omissions in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) regarding conditions.
Inserts a new clause in serial number 26, item (i), clause (b) to exclude dyeing or printing services from the Customs Tariff Act.
This notification comes into effect from 1st January 2022.

466.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 16/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 18th November 2021, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the removal of the phrase 'or a Governmental authority or a Government Entity' from the descriptions of services listed under serial numbers 3 and 3A. Additionally, new provisions have been added to serial numbers 15 and 17, clarifying that certain items do not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator. This means that the services mentioned in items (b) and (c) at serial number 15, as well as item (e) at serial number 17, will not be subject to the previous restrictions when provided through an electronic commerce platform. These amendments will come into effect starting 1st January 2022, impacting businesses that engage in electronic commerce within the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 18th November 2021.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments include the omission of the phrase 'or a Governmental authority or a Government Entity' from serial numbers 3 and 3A in the description of services.
A new proviso has been added after item (c) at serial number 15, stating that items (b) and (c) do not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator.
Another new proviso has been added after item (e) at serial number 17, indicating that item (e) does not apply to services supplied through an electronic commerce operator.
These changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

467.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 17/2021 to amend the earlier Notification No. 17/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This new notification, effective from 1st January 2022, expands the definition of motor vehicles to include 'omnibus or any other motor vehicle' in place of just 'motor cycle'. Additionally, it introduces a new clause that specifies the inclusion of restaurant services that are not provided by restaurants or eating joints located at specified premises. The definition of motor vehicles in the Explanation has also been updated to encompass 'motor vehicle' and 'omnibus' as per the Motor Vehicle Act. Furthermore, the term 'specified premises' is now defined to refer to those providing hotel accommodation services with a tariff exceeding ₹7,500 per unit per day. These changes aim to clarify the scope of services and definitions under the Union Territory GST framework, impacting businesses in the hospitality and transportation sectors.
key_points: The notification is issued on 18th November 2021.
It amends Notification No. 17/2017 - Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Clause (i) is amended to include 'omnibus or any other motor vehicle' in the definition.
A new clause (iv) is added to include 'supply of restaurant service other than the services supplied by restaurant, eating joints etc. located at specified premises.'
In the Explanation, item (b) is amended to redefine terms related to motor vehicles, including 'motor vehicle' and 'omnibus'.
A new item (c) is added to define 'specified premises' as those providing hotel accommodation services with a declared tariff above ₹7,500 per unit per day.
The changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.

468.summary: The recent Notification No. 18/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on December 28, 2021, amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Key changes include updates to various entries in the tax schedules. For instance, in Schedule I, S. No. 2 now lists specific HS codes for fish products, while S. No. 9 has been revised to provide a detailed description of yogurt and cream. Additionally, S. No. 14 now includes edible insects, and S. No. 87 specifies fixed vegetable or microbial fats. Schedule II has also been updated, with S. No. 15 expanding the range of dried nuts and S. No. 25 detailing animal or microbial fats. A new entry, S. No. 41A, has been added for tender coconut water with specific branding conditions. In Schedule III, several entries related to ores and concentrates have been substituted, and S. No. 41 has been omitted. Lastly, Schedule IV introduces new entries for tobacco products intended for inhalation without combustion. These changes will affect businesses dealing with these goods, requiring them to adjust their tax calculations accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th December 2021.
Amendments were made to the notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, several entries were substituted, including S. No. 2 with new HS codes for fish products and S. No. 9 with a detailed description of yogurt and cream.
S. No. 14 was updated to include edible insects, while S. No. 87 now specifies fixed vegetable or microbial fats.
Schedule II saw changes such as S. No. 15, which now includes a broader range of dried nuts, and S. No. 25, which details animal or microbial fats.
New entries were added in Schedule II, including S. No. 41A for tender coconut water with specific branding conditions.
In Schedule III, S. Nos. 26A to 26N were substituted to include various ores and concentrates, while S. No. 41 was omitted.
Schedule IV introduced new entries for tobacco products intended for inhalation without combustion.

469.summary: Notification No. 19/2021, issued on 28th December 2021, introduces several amendments to the Union Territory Tax (Rate) notification originally established on 28th June 2017. These changes will come into effect on 1st January 2022. Notably, for S. No. 22, the classification of certain fish products has been updated to include specific HS codes: 0303 to 0309. Additionally, S. No. 43B now specifies 'Vegetables provisionally preserved, but unsuitable in that state for immediate consumption'. The entry for S. No. 49 has been revised to encompass a broader range of fresh nuts, including almonds and walnuts. A new entry, S. No. 97A, has been added for 'Tender coconut water', which includes conditions regarding branding. Furthermore, S. No. 101 has been removed entirely, while S. No. 141 has updated its classification code to '8807'. These amendments will affect businesses dealing with these specific goods in the Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is issued on 28th December 2021.
It amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes will take effect from 1st January 2022.
For S. No. 22, the entry in column (2) is changed to '0303, 0304, 0305, 0306, 0307, 0308, 0309'.
For S. No. 43B, the entry in column (3) is changed to 'Vegetables provisionally preserved, but unsuitable in that state for immediate consumption'.
For S. No. 49, the entry in column (3) is changed to include various fresh nuts such as almonds, walnuts, and pistachios.
A new entry, S. No. 97A, is added for 'Tender coconut water' with specific conditions.
S. No. 101 and its related entries are omitted, and for S. No. 141, the entry in column (2) is changed to '8807'.

470.summary: Notification No. 20/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on December 28, 2021, to amend the earlier Notification No. 21/2018 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it updates the entries in the TABLE of the previous notification. For S. No. 4, the entry in column (2) has been changed to '4414', while for S. No. 29, it has been updated to '7419 80'. These changes will impact the classification of goods under the Union Territory GST framework. The new entries will be applicable starting from January 1, 2022. Businesses dealing with the specified goods should take note of these changes to ensure correct tax application moving forward. This amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to streamline and clarify tax rates within the Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 21/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 26th July 2018.
The changes are made in the TABLE of the earlier notification.
For S. No. 4, the entry in column (2) is changed to '4414'.
For S. No. 29, the entry in column (2) is changed to '7419 80'.
This notification will come into effect on 1st January 2022.

471.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 21/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 31st December 2021, which will take effect from 1st January 2022. This notification replaces the earlier Notification No. 14/2021, dated 18th November 2021. A significant change is the removal of the 2.5% tax rate entry listed under serial number 225 in Schedule I. Additionally, a new entry has been introduced in Schedule II, specifically for footwear priced at Rs.1000 or less per pair, which will now be taxed at 6% under the new serial number 171A1. This amendment is part of ongoing adjustments to the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework, which was originally established in 2017. The last amendment prior to this was made on 28th December 2021, indicating a continuous effort to refine tax regulations. Businesses dealing with footwear priced under Rs.1000 will need to note this new tax rate for compliance.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st December 2021.
This notification supersedes Notification No. 14/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 18th November 2021.
In Schedule I, the entry for 2.5% tax rate at serial number 225 is omitted.
In Schedule II, a new entry is added after serial number 171 for 6% tax rate: '171A1 64 Footwear of sale value not exceeding Rs.1000 per pair.'
The changes come into effect on 1st January 2022.
The principal notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was originally published on 28th June 2017.
The last amendment before this notification was Notification No. 18/2021 dated 28th December 2021.

472.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 22/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 31st December 2021, which will take effect from 1st January 2022. This notification supersedes the earlier Notification No. 15/2021, which was issued on 18th November 2021. The key amendments are made to Notification No. 11/2017, specifically in the table that outlines the description of services. For items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x), the wording has been simplified from 'Union territory, a local authority, a Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' to 'Union territory or a local authority'. A similar change is made for item (vii). Additionally, the conditions listed in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) have been completely removed. These changes aim to streamline the definitions and conditions related to Union Territory tax services, impacting businesses and authorities operating in these jurisdictions.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st December 2021.
This notification supersedes Notification No. 15/2021-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 18th November 2021.
Amendments are made to Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

In the table of Notification No. 11/2017, the description of services in items (iii), (vi), (ix), and (x) is changed from 'Union territory, a local authority, a Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' to 'Union territory or a local authority'.
In item (vii), the description is similarly changed to 'Union territory or a local authority'.
Entries in column (5) for items (iii), (vi), (vii), (ix), and (x) under 'Condition' are omitted.
These changes come into effect from 1st January 2022.

473.summary: On January 1, 2020, the Central Government issued Notification No. 01/2020-Central Tax, which brings into effect specific provisions of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019. This notification specifically activates sections 92 to 112, but notably excludes sections 92, 97, 100, and 103 to 110 from coming into force. This means that the provisions related to these sections will not be applicable as of this date. The notification was published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that it is officially recognized. The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs is responsible for this notification, which is part of the government's ongoing efforts to implement tax reforms. Businesses and stakeholders in the GST framework should take note of these changes, as they may impact compliance and operational procedures moving forward. The activation of these sections signifies a step towards the full implementation of the provisions outlined in the Finance Act, which aims to streamline tax processes.
key_points: The notification is dated January 1, 2020.
It is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs.
Sections 92 to 112 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019 are now in force, except for sections 92, 97, 100, and 103 to 110.
The notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The notification is issued under the powers of sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019.

474.summary: Notification No. 01/2020-Integrated Tax, issued on January 1, 2020, establishes that the provisions of section 114 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019, will come into effect on the same date. This section is part of the Finance (No. 2) Act, which is designated as Act number 23 of 2019. The notification is officially published in the Gazette of India, specifically in the Extraordinary section. It is important for businesses and stakeholders in the GST framework to note that this notification marks the commencement of specific provisions under the Finance Act, which may impact their compliance and reporting requirements. The notification is signed by Pramod Kumar, Director of the Government of India, and is referenced under F.No.20/06/09/2019-GST. This change is significant as it indicates the government's ongoing efforts to implement and enforce tax regulations as outlined in the Finance Act, thereby affecting various stakeholders in the GST ecosystem.
key_points: The notification is titled '01/2020-Integrated Tax'.
It was issued on January 1, 2020.
The notification appoints January 1, 2020, as the effective date for the provisions of section 114 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019.
This notification is issued under the authority of sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019.
The Finance (No. 2) Act, 2019 is referenced as Act number 23 of 2019.
The notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The notification is signed by Pramod Kumar, Director, Government of India.
The reference number for this notification is F.No.20/06/09/2019-GST.

475.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 02/2020-Central Tax on 1st January 2020, amending several rules under the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. Notably, Rule 117(1A) has been updated to extend the deadline for certain compliance from 31st December 2019 to 31st March 2020, while Rule 117(4)(b)(iii) has pushed the date from 31st January 2020 to 30th April 2020. Additionally, FORM REG-01 has been revised to include new questions for businesses applying for registration as SEZ Units or Developers. Changes to FORM GSTR-3A now indicate that tax liability may arise instead of will, and it clarifies that the notice does not require a signature. Furthermore, FORM INV-01 has been entirely replaced with a new format that specifies mandatory and optional fields for e-invoicing, including cardinality definitions to guide users on how to fill out the form correctly. These changes primarily affect businesses involved in SEZ operations and those required to comply with e-invoicing regulations.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Rules, 2020' and was issued on 1st January 2020.
Rule 117(1A) is amended to change the date from '31st December 2019' to '31st March 2020'.
In Rule 117(4)(b)(iii), the date '31st January 2020' is replaced with '30th April 2020'.
FORM REG-01 is updated to include new fields for SEZ Unit and SEZ Developer registration.
FORM GSTR-3A is modified to change 'tax liability will' to 'tax liability may' and adds a note stating the notice does not require a signature.
FORM INV-01 is completely substituted with a new structure detailing mandatory and optional fields for e-invoicing.
The new FORM INV-01 includes detailed cardinality definitions for various fields, such as '0..1' for optional items and '1..1' for mandatory items.

476.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 02/2020-Integrated Tax on 26th March 2020, which amends the earlier Notification No. 4/2019-Integrated Tax from 30th September 2019. This amendment introduces a new entry in Table A that pertains to maintenance, repair, or overhaul services for aircraft, aircraft engines, and related components. Specifically, it states that the place of supply for these services will be the location of the recipient. This change is significant for businesses involved in the aviation sector, as it clarifies the tax implications for such services. The new provision will take effect from 1st April 2020, meaning that any maintenance or repair services provided after this date will follow the updated rules. This amendment aims to prevent double taxation or non-taxation of these services, ensuring a uniform application of GST rules across the country.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2020-Integrated Tax issued on 26th March 2020.
Amends Notification No. 4/2019-Integrated Tax dated 30th September 2019.
Inserts a new entry in Table A for maintenance, repair, or overhaul services related to aircraft.
New entry specifies that the place of supply for these services is the location of the recipient.
The new provision comes into effect from 1st April 2020.

477.summary: Notification No. 03/2020-Central Tax, issued on 1st January 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 62/2019-Central Tax from 26th November 2019. Specifically, it changes the deadlines for certain compliance requirements. The original deadline of 30th October 2019 has been extended to 31st December 2019, allowing more time for businesses to meet their obligations. Additionally, the deadline that was set for 31st October 2019 is now moved to 1st January 2020. This means that businesses will have until the new dates to complete their necessary filings or actions as specified in the earlier notification. The changes are aimed at providing relief to taxpayers by giving them additional time to comply with GST requirements. This amendment is particularly relevant for those who were struggling to meet the previous deadlines, ensuring they have a clearer path forward in their compliance efforts.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 62/2019-Central Tax dated 26th November 2019.
Changes the deadline from 30th October 2019 to 31st December 2019.
Changes the deadline from 31st October 2019 to 1st January 2020.
Updates the reference date in paragraph 3 from 31st October 2019 to 1st January 2020.

478.summary: Notification No. 3/2020-Integrated Tax, issued on April 8, 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 6/2017-Integrated Tax. It introduces a new proviso that outlines specific interest rates applicable to registered taxpayers who fail to file their GSTR-3B returns on time. The new table categorizes taxpayers based on their turnover. For those with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores, there is no interest for the first 15 days post due date, after which a 9% interest rate applies. Taxpayers with turnover between 1.5 crores and 5 crores can avoid interest if they file their returns by June 29 for February and March 2020, and by June 30 for April 2020. Meanwhile, those with turnover up to 1.5 crores can also avoid interest by filing their returns by June 30 for February 2020, July 3 for March 2020, and July 6 for April 2020. Importantly, this notification is effective retroactively from March 20, 2020, providing clarity on interest rates for late filings during this period.
key_points: Notification No. 3/2020-Integrated Tax was issued on 2020-04-08.
This notification amends Notification No. 6/2017-Integrated Tax dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added to the first paragraph of Notification No. 6/2017.
The new table specifies interest rates for late filing of GSTR-3B returns based on taxpayer categories.
For taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores, the interest rate is 0% for the first 15 days after the due date, then 9% thereafter.
For taxpayers with turnover between 1.5 crores and 5 crores, the interest rate is 0% if returns are filed by 29th June 2020 for February and March 2020, and by 30th June 2020 for April 2020.
For taxpayers with turnover up to 1.5 crores, the interest rate is 0% if returns are filed by 30th June 2020 for February 2020, by 3rd July 2020 for March 2020, and by 6th July 2020 for April 2020.
This notification is deemed to have come into force from 20th March 2020.

479.summary: On 10th January 2020, the Central Government issued Notification No. 04/2020-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax from 23rd January 2018. The key change made in this amendment is the adjustment of a due date specified in the third proviso of the earlier notification. Specifically, the date '10th January, 2020' has been replaced with '17th January, 2020'. This alteration impacts the timeline for compliance related to GST, providing an extended deadline for businesses to meet their obligations. As a result, businesses that were required to comply by the original date now have until the new date of 17th January 2020 to fulfill their requirements. This change is significant for those affected, as it allows additional time to prepare and with GST regulations.
key_points: The notification is dated 10th January 2020.
It amends Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax dated 23rd January 2018.
The amendment changes the third proviso to substitute '10th January, 2020' with '17th January, 2020'.
This change affects the due date for a specific compliance requirement under the GST framework.

480.summary: Notification No. 04/2020-Integrated Tax, issued on June 24, 2020, announces that the provisions of section 134 of the Finance Act, 2020 will come into effect on June 30, 2020. This means that any changes or requirements outlined in section 134 will be applicable from that date. The notification is part of the government's efforts to implement the provisions of the Finance Act, which was passed earlier in the year. It is important for businesses and stakeholders to be aware of this date, as it marks the beginning of the enforcement of specific tax provisions that may affect their operations. The notification has been published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that it is officially recognized and accessible. The issuance is signed by Pramod Kumar, indicating the authority behind this announcement. This notification is crucial for those involved in GST compliance and tax planning, as it signals upcoming changes that may require adjustments in their processes.
key_points: The notification is titled Notification No. 04/2020-Integrated Tax.
It was issued on June 24, 2020.
The provisions of section 134 of the Finance Act, 2020 will come into force on June 30, 2020.
This notification is issued under the authority of sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Finance Act, 2020.
The notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The notification is signed by Pramod Kumar, Director to the Government of India.
The reference number for this notification is F. No. CBEC-20/06/09/2019-GST.

481.summary: Notification No. 05/2020-Central Tax, issued on January 13, 2020, introduces important changes regarding the Revisional Authority under the GST framework. It designates the Principal Commissioner or Commissioner of Central Tax as the Revisional Authority for any decisions or orders made by the Additional or Joint Commissioner of Central Tax. Additionally, the Additional or Joint Commissioner is now empowered to act as the Revisional Authority for decisions or orders issued by the Deputy Commissioner, Assistant Commissioner, or Superintendent of Central Tax. This change is significant as it streamlines the hierarchy of authority within the GST administrative structure, ensuring that there is a clear process for reviewing decisions made at various levels. The revisions are grounded in section 108 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, which governs the powers of revisional authorities. This notification impacts tax officials and enhances the framework for addressing grievances or disputes arising from lower-level decisions.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2020-Central Tax was issued on 13th January 2020.
The notification authorizes the Principal Commissioner or Commissioner of Central Tax as Revisional Authority for decisions/orders by the Additional or Joint Commissioner.
The Additional or Joint Commissioner of Central Tax is authorized as Revisional Authority for decisions/orders by the Deputy Commissioner, Assistant Commissioner, or Superintendent of Central Tax.
This authorization is made under section 108 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

482.summary: Notification No. 05/2020-Integrated Tax, issued on 24th June 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 6/2017-Integrated Tax. The key change is the substitution of the first proviso regarding interest rates applicable to late GSTR-3B filings. For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores, a 9% interest rate will apply from 24th June 2020 for the tax periods of February, March, and April 2020. For those with a turnover of up to 5 crores, the interest rate will also be 9% starting from 30th June 2020, but this is contingent on their principal place of business being in specific states such as Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, and others. Different deadlines for filing GSTR-3B are set for various states, with specific dates for each month from February to July 2020. This notification is effective immediately from its issuance date, impacting registered persons who are required to file GSTR-3B returns and may have missed the due dates.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2020-Integrated Tax was issued on 24th June 2020.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 6/2017-Integrated Tax dated 28th June 2017.
The first proviso in the earlier notification is substituted with a new proviso regarding interest rates for late GSTR-3B filings.
Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover over 5 crores will have a 9% interest rate from 24th June 2020 for February, March, and April 2020 returns.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores in specified states will have a 9% interest rate from 30th June 2020 for returns due from February to July 2020.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores in other states will also have a 9% interest rate from 30th June 2020 for returns due from February to July 2020.
The notification specifies different deadlines for filing GSTR-3B for various states and turnover categories.
The changes are effective from the date of the notification, 24th June 2020.

483.summary: The recent corrigendum to Notification No. 06/2020-Central Tax, issued on 04th February 2020, makes specific amendments to the list of states mentioned in the original notification dated 03rd February 2020. Firstly, the original reference to 'Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand' has been corrected to 'Tamil Nadu, Uttarakhand', removing Uttar Pradesh from this list. Secondly, the original mention of 'Tripura, West Bengal' has been updated to include 'Tripura, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal', thereby adding Uttar Pradesh to this grouping. These changes are important for businesses and taxpayers in these states as they clarify the applicability of certain provisions under the GST framework. It is crucial for affected parties to note these amendments to ensure accurate compliance with the GST regulations as they relate to these specific states.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 06/2020-Central Tax dated 03rd February 2020.
Changes the list of states from 'Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand' to 'Tamil Nadu, Uttarakhand'.
Updates the list of states from 'Tripura, West Bengal' to 'Tripura, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal'.
The corrigendum was issued on 04th February 2020.

484.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 06/2020-Central Tax on 3rd February 2020, which extends the deadline for filing the annual return under section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act for the financial year 2017-18. This extension applies to different groups of registered persons based on their principal place of business. For those located in Chandigarh, Delhi, Gujarat, Haryana, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Punjab, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, and Uttarakhand, the new due date is now set for 5th February 2020. Meanwhile, registered persons in Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Andhra Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu, Goa, Himachal Pradesh, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Kerala, Lakshadweep, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Odisha, Puducherry, Sikkim, Telangana, Tripura, West Bengal, and Other Territory have until 7th February 2020 to file their returns. This notification aims to provide relief to taxpayers by allowing additional time to comply with their filing obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 06/2020-Central Tax issued on 3rd February 2020.
Extends the due date for filing the annual return under section 44 of the CGST Act for FY 2017-18.
For registered persons in Chandigarh, Delhi, Gujarat, Haryana, Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Punjab, Rajasthan, Tamil Nadu, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, the new due date is 5th February 2020.

For registered persons in Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Andhra Pradesh, Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu, Goa, Himachal Pradesh, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Kerala, Lakshadweep, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Odisha, Puducherry, Sikkim, Telangana, Tripura, West Bengal, and Other Territory, the new due date is 7th February 2020.

485.summary: Notification No. 06/2020-Integrated Tax, issued on 15th October 2020, brings changes to the HSN code requirements outlined in Notification No. 5/2017-Integrated Tax. Effective from 1st April 2021, the new rules state that businesses with an aggregate turnover of up to five crores must mention 4 digits of the HSN code in their tax invoices. For those with a turnover exceeding five crores, the requirement increases to 6 digits.
