Additionally, there is a provision that allows registered persons with a turnover up to five crores to omit the HSN code when issuing invoices for supplies made to unregistered persons. This amendment aims to simplify compliance for smaller businesses while ensuring that larger businesses maintain detailed reporting. Overall, this change affects how businesses categorize their goods and services, particularly in their invoicing practices.
key_points: Notification No. 06/2020-Integrated Tax was issued on 15th October 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 5/2017-Integrated Tax dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment will take effect from 1st April 2021.
The new table specifies that businesses with aggregate turnover up to five crores must use 4 digits of HSN code.
For businesses with aggregate turnover exceeding five crores, 6 digits of HSN code are required.
Registered persons with turnover up to five crores are not required to mention HSN codes for supplies made to unregistered persons.

486.summary: Notification No. 07/2020-Central Tax, issued on February 3, 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 44/2019-Central Tax from October 9, 2019. This amendment sets new deadlines for filing the GSTR-3B return for the months of January, February, and March 2020. Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 Crore in the previous financial year, whose principal place of business is in states like Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and others, must file their returns by February 22, March 22, and April 22, respectively. For taxpayers in other states, including Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and several northeastern states, the deadlines are extended to February 24, March 24, and April 24, 2020. This change aims to provide relief to smaller taxpayers in various regions, allowing them additional time to comply with their filing obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2020-Central Tax was issued on 2020-02-03.
This notification amends Notification No. 44/2019-Central Tax dated 2019-10-09.
The amendment introduces new deadlines for filing FORM GSTR-3B for January, February, and March 2020.
Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 Crore in the previous financial year in specified states must file by 22nd February, 22nd March, and 22nd April 2020.
The specified states include Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Goa, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh.
Taxpayers in other specified states must file by 24th February, 24th March, and 24th April 2020.

The other specified states include Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Uttarakhand, Haryana, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Meghalaya, Assam, West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha, and Union territories like Jammu and Kashmir, Ladakh, Chandigarh, and Delhi.

487.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 08/2020-Central Tax on 2nd March 2020, which amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This amendment, effective from 1st March 2020, specifically alters Rule 31A. The previous sub-rule (2) has been replaced to clarify how the value of lottery supplies is determined. Under the new rule, the value of a lottery supply is now deemed to be 100/128 of the ticket's face value or the price set by the Organising State, whichever is higher. This change is significant for businesses involved in lottery operations, as it directly impacts how they calculate the taxable value of their lottery tickets. The term 'Organising State' is defined in accordance with existing regulations, ensuring consistency in interpretation. This amendment follows recommendations from the GST Council and is part of ongoing efforts to refine GST regulations. The last amendment to these rules was made earlier in January 2020.
key_points: Notification No. 08/2020-Central Tax was issued on 2nd March 2020.
The rules come into effect from 1st March 2020.
Rule 31A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017 is amended.
Sub-rule (2) of Rule 31A is substituted to define the value of lottery supply.
The new value of supply for lottery is deemed to be 100/128 of the face value of the ticket or the price notified by the Organising State, whichever is higher.
The term 'Organising State' is defined as per clause (f) of sub-rule (1) of rule 2 of the Lotteries (Regulation) Rules, 2010.
This amendment follows the recommendations of the GST Council.
The principal rules were last amended by Notification No. 02/2020-Central Tax on 1st January 2020.

488.summary: Notification No. 09/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 16, 2020, introduces a special procedure for foreign airline companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013. These companies are now exempt from submitting the reconciliation statement in FORM GSTR-9C, which is typically required under section 44(2) of the CGST Act and rule 80(3) of the CGST Rules. Instead, they must submit a statement of receipts and payments related to their Indian business operations. This statement needs to be authenticated by a practicing Chartered Accountant or a firm of Chartered Accountants in India. The deadline for this submission is set for September 30 of the year following the financial year. This change aims to simplify compliance for foreign airline companies while ensuring that they still provide necessary financial information regarding their operations in India. The notification reflects the recommendations made by the GST Council to ease the regulatory burden on these entities.
key_points: Notification No. 09/2020-Central Tax issued on 16th March 2020.
Applies to foreign airline companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013.
These companies are exempt from submitting reconciliation statement in FORM GSTR-9C.
This exemption is under section 44(2) of the CGST Act and rule 80(3) of the CGST Rules.
A statement of receipts and payments for Indian operations must be submitted instead.
The statement must be authenticated by a practicing Chartered Accountant or a firm of Chartered Accountants.
The deadline for submitting this statement is 30th September of the year following the financial year.
This notification is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.

489.summary: Notification No. 10/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 21, 2020, introduces a special procedure for businesses that had their principal place of business in the erstwhile Union territories of Daman and Diu or Dadra and Nagar Haveli until January 26, 2020. These businesses must adhere to this procedure until May 31, 2020. The notification specifies tax periods for January and February 2020, with January covering the 1st to the 25th and February from the 26th to the 29th. Businesses are required to pay the applicable tax for invoices issued between January 26 and the transition date, regardless of the tax details on those invoices. Additionally, businesses can transfer their input tax credit (ITC) from their old GSTIN to the new one after filing their January 2020 return, provided they notify their jurisdictional tax officer. The transfer of ITC will be based on the balance in the electronic credit ledger, and any Union territory taxes as of January 25, 2020, will also be transferred accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2020-Central Tax issued on 21st March 2020.
Applies to persons with a principal place of business in the erstwhile Union territories of Daman and Diu or Dadra and Nagar Haveli until 26th January 2020.
These persons must follow a special procedure until 31st May 2020.
Tax periods for January and February 2020 are defined as: January 1-25, 2020, and February 26-29, 2020.
Tax must be paid based on applicable rates for invoices issued from January 26, 2020, to the transition date.
Registered persons can transfer input tax credit (ITC) from the old GSTIN to the new GSTIN after filing the January 2020 return.
The transfer of ITC requires notifying the jurisdictional tax officer and is based on the electronic credit ledger balance.
Union territory taxes as of January 25, 2020, will be transferred as a balance in the electronic credit ledger.

490.summary: Notification No. 11/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 21, 2020, introduces special procedures for registered persons classified as corporate debtors under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016. These corporate debtors must obtain a new GST registration within 30 days of the appointment of an interim resolution professional (IRP) or resolution professional (RP). If the IRP/RP was appointed before this notification, the registration must be completed within 30 days from the notification date. Once registered, they are required to file their first return under section 40 of the GST Act from the date they become liable until their registration is granted. Additionally, they can claim input tax credit on invoices received since the appointment of the IRP/RP, even if those invoices bear the GSTIN of the previous registration. Furthermore, registered persons receiving supplies from these corporate debtors can also claim input tax credit on invoices issued with the erstwhile GSTIN during the specified period. Any cash deposited in the ledger by the IRP/RP during this transition will be refundable to the previous registration.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2020-Central Tax issued on 21st March 2020.
Registered persons who are corporate debtors under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 must obtain new GST registration within 30 days of IRP/RP appointment.
If IRP/RP was appointed before this notification, registration must be completed within 30 days from the notification date.
These persons must file their first return under section 40 of the GST Act from the date they become liable until registration is granted.
They can claim input tax credit on invoices received since IRP/RP appointment, even if the invoices bear the GSTIN of the erstwhile registered person.
Registered persons receiving supplies from these corporate debtors can also claim input tax credit on invoices issued with the erstwhile GSTIN during the specified period.
Any cash deposited in the ledger by the IRP/RP from the appointment date to registration date is refundable to the erstwhile registration.

491.summary: Notification No. 12/2020-Central Tax, issued on 21st March 2020, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax from 23rd April 2019. Specifically, it adds a new proviso to paragraph 2, which allows registered persons who filed their returns using GSTR-3B instead of the required CMP-08 for the financial year 2019-20 to be exempt from submitting both GSTR-1 and CMP-08 for all tax periods within that year. This change is aimed at easing the compliance burden for these taxpayers, as they will not need to file additional statements that would typically be required. The amendment is made following recommendations from the GST Council, reflecting the government's ongoing efforts to streamline GST processes and support businesses during challenging times. This exemption is particularly beneficial for those who may have found it difficult to comply with the original filing requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2020-Central Tax was issued on 21st March 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax dated 23rd April 2019.
A new proviso is added to paragraph 2 of Notification No. 21/2019.
The new proviso states that taxpayers who filed GSTR-3B instead of CMP-08 for the financial year 2019-20 are exempt from filing GSTR-1 and CMP-08 for that year.
This exemption applies to all tax periods within the financial year 2019-20.
The amendment is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The amendment aims to simplify compliance for certain taxpayers.

492.summary: Notification No. 13/2020-Central Tax, issued on 21st March 2020, introduces new requirements for certain registered persons under the GST framework. This notification supersedes the earlier Notification No. 70/2019-Central Tax from 13th December 2019. It specifically targets registered persons whose aggregate turnover exceeds one hundred crore rupees, mandating them to prepare invoices and other prescribed documents in accordance with sub-rule (4) of rule 48 of the GST Rules. However, it does not apply to those registered persons mentioned in sub-rules (2), (3), (4), and (4A) of rule 54. This change is significant as it clarifies the documentation requirements for larger businesses, ensuring they adhere to the specified invoicing standards. The new rules will come into effect from 1st October 2020, giving affected businesses time to adjust their processes accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2020-Central Tax issued on 21st March 2020.
Supersedes Notification No. 70/2019-Central Tax dated 13th December 2019.
Applies to registered persons with an aggregate turnover exceeding one hundred crore rupees.
Mandates preparation of invoices and other prescribed documents under sub-rule (4) of rule 48.
Excludes registered persons referred to in sub-rules (2), (3), (4), and (4A) of rule 54.
Effective from 1st October 2020.

493.summary: Notification No. 14/2020-Central Tax introduces a new requirement for businesses with an aggregate turnover exceeding five hundred crore rupees. Starting from 1st October 2020, these businesses must include a Dynamic Quick Response (QR) code on their B2C invoices issued to unregistered persons. This change supersedes the earlier Notification No. 72/2019-Central Tax from December 2019. However, it does not apply to certain registered persons specified in sub-rules (2), (3), (4), and (4A) of rule 54. Additionally, if a business provides the QR code through a digital display, the invoice will still be considered compliant. This requirement aims to enhance the digital transaction process and improve the efficiency of invoice verification for businesses with significant turnover. Overall, this notification reflects the government's ongoing efforts to modernize the GST framework and streamline compliance for larger businesses.
key_points: Introduces a requirement for B2C invoices to include a Dynamic Quick Response (QR) code.
Applies to registered persons with an aggregate turnover exceeding five hundred crore rupees.
Supersedes Notification No. 72/2019-Central Tax dated 13th December 2019.
Excludes registered persons referred to in sub-rules (2), (3), (4), and (4A) of rule 54.
The notification comes into effect on 1st October 2020.
If a QR code is displayed digitally, the B2C invoice is still considered compliant.
The notification is issued under the sixth proviso to rule 46 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.

494.summary: Notification No. 15/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, extends the deadline for filing the annual return, known as GSTR-9, for the financial year 2018-2019. The new deadline is now set for 30th June 2020. This decision was made under the authority of section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and is in line with rule 80 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. The extension aims to provide relief to businesses during this period. All taxpayers who are required to file GSTR-9 for the specified financial year will benefit from this additional time. It is important for affected businesses to take note of this new deadline and ensure that their returns are submitted electronically through the common portal by the revised date.
key_points: Extends the deadline for filing the annual return GSTR-9 for the financial year 2018-2019.
New deadline for GSTR-9 is now 30th June 2020.
This extension is based on the powers conferred by section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was issued on 23rd March 2020.

495.summary: The Central Goods and Services Tax (Third Amendment) Rules, 2020, effective from April 1, 2020, introduce several significant changes to the GST framework. One major change is the requirement for applicants to undergo Aadhaar authentication for GST registration, which is now mandatory. If an applicant fails to complete this authentication, their registration will only be granted after a physical verification of their business premises within 60 days. Additionally, the rules regarding input tax credit on capital goods have been revised, allowing for more flexibility in claiming credits. Businesses with an aggregate turnover exceeding five crore rupees during the financial year 2018-2019 are now required to have their accounts audited and submit the relevant forms. Furthermore, new provisions have been introduced to address the recovery of refunds related to unutilized input tax credits if export proceeds are not realized within the stipulated time. Lastly, an undertaking has been added to the refund application form, ensuring that any refunds received must be repaid if the corresponding foreign exchange remittances are not realized. These changes will impact various businesses, particularly those involved in exports and those with significant turnover.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax (Third Amendment) Rules, 2020' and was issued on 23rd March 2020.
From 01.04.2020, applicants must undergo Aadhaar authentication for GST registration as per the new sub-rule (4A) added to rule 8.
If Aadhaar authentication is not completed, registration will require physical verification within 60 days, as stated in the amended rule 9.
Rule 25 has been substituted to allow physical verification of business premises if Aadhaar authentication fails before registration.
Changes to rule 43 include new provisions for input tax credit on capital goods, effective from 01.04.2020.
Registered persons with turnover exceeding five crore rupees in FY 2018-2019 must have their accounts audited and submit FORM GSTR-9C.
New provisions in rule 96B require refund of unutilized input tax credit to be repaid if export proceeds are not realized within the allowed period.
An undertaking has been added to FORM GST RFD-01 regarding the repayment of refunds if foreign exchange remittances are not received.

496.summary: Notification No. 17/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, introduces important changes regarding the applicability of certain provisions of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act. Effective from 1st April 2020, this notification states that the provisions outlined in sub-sections (6B) and (6C) of the CGST Act will not apply to individuals who are not citizens of India. However, there are specific exceptions to this rule. The exceptions include individuals, authorized signatories of any type, managing and authorized partners, and the Karta of a Hindu undivided family. This means that while non-citizens are generally excluded from these provisions, certain key roles within businesses and family structures are still recognized under the law. This change is significant for businesses that may involve non-citizen individuals in their operations, as it clarifies who is affected by these provisions.
key_points: Notification No. 17/2020-Central Tax issued on 23rd March 2020.
This notification comes into effect from 1st April 2020.
It specifies that provisions of sub-section (6B) or (6C) of the CGST Act do not apply to non-citizens of India.
The exceptions to this rule include individuals, authorized signatories, managing and authorized partners, and Karta of a Hindu undivided family.

497.summary: Notification No. 18/2020-Central Tax, effective from 1st April 2020, introduces a requirement for individuals seeking GST registration to authenticate their Aadhaar number. This means that anyone applying for GST registration must provide their Aadhaar details to verify their identity. However, if an individual does not possess an Aadhaar number, the notification ensures that they will be offered alternative methods of identification, as outlined in rule 9 of the GST Rules. This change is based on the powers granted under sub-section (6B) of section 25 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The aim of this notification is to streamline the registration process and enhance the verification of applicants. Therefore, individuals planning to register for GST should be prepared to provide their Aadhaar information or seek alternative identification options if necessary.
key_points: This notification is issued under Notification No. 18/2020-Central Tax.
The notification comes into effect on 1st April 2020.
Individuals must undergo Aadhaar authentication to be eligible for GST registration.
If an individual does not have an Aadhaar number, alternative identification methods will be provided as per rule 9 of the GST Rules.
This change is made under the authority of sub-section (6B) of section 25 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

498.summary: Notification No. 19/2020-Central Tax, effective from 1st April 2020, introduces a new requirement for individuals involved in GST registration. Specifically, it mandates that authorized signatories, managing and authorized partners of partnership firms, and Karta of Hindu undivided families must authenticate their Aadhaar numbers to be eligible for GST registration. This requirement is outlined in rule 8 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. For those who do not have an Aadhaar number, the notification ensures that alternative identification methods will be available, as specified in rule 9. This change aims to streamline the registration process and enhance compliance within the GST framework. The notification was issued by the Central Government following recommendations from the GST Council, indicating a significant step towards improving the identification process for GST registrants.
key_points: This notification is issued under Notification No. 19/2020-Central Tax.
The notification comes into effect on 1st April 2020.
It mandates that the authorized signatories, managing and authorized partners of partnership firms, and Karta of Hindu undivided families must authenticate their Aadhaar number for GST registration.
This requirement is specified in rule 8 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
If an Aadhaar number is not assigned, alternate identification methods will be provided as per rule 9 of the said rules.
The notification is issued by the Central Government on the recommendations of the GST Council.

499.summary: Notification No. 20/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, brings important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax from 28th June 2019. Specifically, it replaces the second and third provisos in the original notification. Now, registered persons who are required to deduct tax at source under section 51 of the GST Act and are based in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir must submit their GSTR-7 returns for the months of July to October 2019 by 24th March 2020. Additionally, those in the Union Territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh are required to file their GSTR-7 returns for the months of November 2019 to February 2020 by the same deadline of 24th March 2020. This amendment is retroactively effective from 20th December 2019, ensuring that the filing requirements are clear for affected businesses in these regions.
key_points: This notification is No. 20/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020.
It amends notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax dated 28th June 2019.
The amendment substitutes the second and third provisos in the first paragraph of the original notification.
Registered persons in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir must file GSTR-7 for July to October 2019 by 24th March 2020.
Registered persons in the Union Territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh must file GSTR-7 for November 2019 to February 2020 by 24th March 2020.
The notification is effective from 20th December 2019.

500.summary: Notification No. 21/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 45/2019-Central Tax from 9th October 2019. The key change is the addition of a new proviso specifically for registered persons whose principal place of business is in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir or the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir or Ladakh. These businesses are now required to file their details of outward supply of goods or services for the quarter of October to December 2019 using FORM GSTR-1. The deadline for this filing has been extended to 24th March 2020, providing additional time for compliance. Importantly, this amendment is deemed to be effective from 31st January 2020, which means it applies retroactively. This change is significant for businesses in these regions as it clarifies their filing obligations and provides them with an extended deadline to meet their GST requirements.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 21/2020-Central Tax on 23rd March 2020.
It amends Notification No. 45/2019-Central Tax dated 9th October 2019.
A new proviso is added for registered persons in Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh.
These registered persons must file FORM GSTR-1 for the quarter October-December 2019.
The deadline for filing GSTR-1 is extended to 24th March 2020.
The amendment is effective from 31st January 2020.

501.summary: Notification No. 22/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 46/2019-Central Tax. It specifically alters the filing deadlines for GSTR-1 for registered persons whose principal place of business is in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir. The first change extends the deadline for GSTR-1 for October 2019 to 24th March 2020 for those with an aggregate turnover exceeding 1.5 crore rupees. Additionally, a new provision has been added for registered persons in the Union Territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh, setting the deadline for GSTR-1 for the months of November 2019 to February 2020 to the same date of 24th March 2020. These amendments are effective retroactively from 20th December 2019. This notification aims to provide relief to businesses in these regions by allowing them more time to comply with their GST filing obligations.
key_points: The notification amends Notification No. 46/2019-Central Tax dated 9th October 2019.
It substitutes the first proviso for registered persons in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir regarding GSTR-1 filing.
The new deadline for GSTR-1 for October 2019 is extended to 24th March 2020 for those with turnover over 1.5 crore.
A new proviso is added for registered persons in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh.
The new deadline for GSTR-1 for November 2019 to February 2020 is also set to 24th March 2020 for those with turnover over 1.5 crore.
The changes are effective from 20th December 2019.
The notification is issued under the powers of section 37 and section 168 of the CGST Act, 2017.

502.summary: Notification No. 23/2020-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 28/2019-Central Tax from 28th June 2019. The key change is the substitution of the first proviso, which now allows registered persons whose principal place of business is in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir to submit their GSTR-1 by 24th March 2020. This extension applies specifically to those with an aggregate turnover exceeding 1.5 crore rupees in the preceding or current financial year. The tax periods affected by this amendment are from July 2019 to September 2019. Importantly, this amendment is retroactively effective from 20th December 2019. This change is significant for businesses in Jammu and Kashmir, as it provides them additional time to comply with their filing obligations for the specified months, ensuring they can meet their GST requirements without penalties for late submission during this period.
key_points: The notification is dated 23rd March 2020.
It amends Notification No. 28/2019-Central Tax, originally issued on 28th June 2019.
The amendment substitutes the first proviso in the first paragraph of Notification No. 28/2019.
The new proviso allows registered persons in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir to file GSTR-1 by 24th March 2020.
This applies to registered persons with an aggregate turnover of more than 1.5 crore rupees.
The tax periods affected are from July 2019 to September 2019.
The amendment is effective from 20th December 2019.
The notification was issued under the powers of section 37(1) and section 168 of the CGST Act.

503.summary: This notification, issued on 23rd March 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 27/2019-Central Tax, specifically changing the first proviso in the second paragraph. It now requires registered persons whose principal place of business is in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir to submit their GSTR-1 for the quarter of July to September 2019 by 24th March 2020. This is a significant update for businesses in that region, as they must ensure their compliance with this new deadline. The amendment is retroactively effective from 30th November 2019, meaning that the requirement to file GSTR-1 applies from that date. This change aims to streamline the filing process for businesses in Jammu and Kashmir, ensuring they meet their GST obligations in a timely manner. It is crucial for affected businesses to take note of this revised deadline and prepare their filings accordingly.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 27/2019-Central Tax dated 28th June 2019.
Substitutes the first proviso in the second paragraph of the original notification.
Registered persons in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir must file GSTR-1 for the quarter July-September 2019.
The new deadline for filing GSTR-1 is 24th March 2020.
This amendment is effective from 30th November 2019.

504.summary: Notification No. 25/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 23, 2020, brings important changes to the filing deadlines for GSTR-3B returns for registered persons in Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh. It amends the earlier notification No. 44/2019-Central Tax, which originally set the deadlines for these returns. The first change replaces the existing first proviso, now requiring that GSTR-3B for October 2019 must be submitted by March 24, 2020, for those registered in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir. Additionally, a new proviso has been added, mandating that GSTR-3B for the months of November 2019 through February 2020 must also be filed by March 24, 2020, for registered persons in both the Union Territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh. This amendment is effective retroactively from December 20, 2019, ensuring that businesses in these regions are aware of their updated filing obligations.
key_points: The notification No. 25/2020-Central Tax was issued on March 23, 2020.
It amends notification No. 44/2019-Central Tax, originally issued on October 9, 2019.

The first proviso in the first paragraph of notification 44/2019 is replaced to state that GSTR-3B for October 2019 must be filed by March 24, 2020, for registered persons in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir.
A new proviso is added stating that GSTR-3B for November 2019 to February 2020 must be filed by March 24, 2020, for registered persons in the Union Territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh.
This notification is deemed to come into force from December 20, 2019.
The amendment affects registered persons in Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh specifically regarding their GSTR-3B filing deadlines.

505.summary: The recent amendment to Notification No. 29/2019-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2020, specifically alters the fourth proviso regarding the filing of GSTR-3B returns. This change affects registered persons whose principal place of business is in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir. They are now required to submit their GSTR-3B returns for the months of July 2019 to September 2019 electronically through the common portal, with a new deadline set for 24th March 2020. This amendment is retroactively effective from 20th December 2019. The original notification had been previously amended by Notification No. 66/2019-Central Tax on 12th December 2019, indicating ongoing adjustments to compliance requirements for GST filings in this region. Business owners in Jammu and Kashmir should take note of this specific deadline to ensure they meet the new requirements.
key_points: This notification is an amendment to Notification No. 29/2019-Central Tax dated 28th June 2019.
The amendment substitutes the fourth proviso in the first paragraph of the original notification.
Registered persons in the erstwhile State of Jammu and Kashmir must file GSTR-3B for July 2019 to September 2019.
The due date for filing GSTR-3B for these months is set to be on or before 24th March 2020.
The amendment is effective from 20th December 2019.
The original notification was last amended by Notification No. 66/2019-Central Tax dated 12th December 2019.

506.summary: Notification No. 27/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 23, 2020, introduces a special procedure for registered persons whose aggregate turnover is up to 1.5 crore rupees in the preceding or current financial year. These businesses are required to submit their details of outward supply of goods or services in FORM GSTR-1. The notification specifies that for the quarter from April 2020 to June 2020, the deadline for submitting FORM GSTR-1 is set for July 31, 2020. Similarly, for the subsequent quarter from July 2020 to September 2020, the due date is October 31, 2020. Additionally, it mentions that the time limit for submitting details for the months of April to September 2020 will be announced in a future notification. This change primarily affects small businesses with lower turnover, simplifying their compliance process during this period.
key_points: Registered persons with aggregate turnover up to 1.5 crore rupees are notified for a special procedure.
These registered persons must furnish details of outward supply in FORM GSTR-1.
For the quarter April 2020 to June 2020, the due date for FORM GSTR-1 is 31st July 2020.
For the quarter July 2020 to September 2020, the due date for FORM GSTR-1 is 31st October 2020.
The time limit for furnishing details for April to September 2020 will be notified later.

507.summary: Notification No. 28/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 23, 2020, extends the deadline for filing GSTR-1 for registered persons whose aggregate turnover exceeds 1.5 crore rupees. This extension applies specifically to the months of April 2020 through September 2020, allowing these taxpayers to submit their GSTR-1 returns by the 11th day of the month following the month of supply. This change is made under the second proviso to sub-section (1) of section 37 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Additionally, the notification mentions that the time limit for furnishing details under section 38 for these months will be announced in a future Gazette notification. This extension is significant for businesses with higher turnover, providing them with additional time to comply with their filing obligations during this period.
key_points: Extends the due date for filing GSTR-1 for registered persons with turnover over 1.5 crore rupees.
New due date for GSTR-1 is the 11th day of the month following the month of supply.
This extension applies to the months from April 2020 to September 2020.
The notification is issued under the second proviso to sub-section (1) of section 37 of the CGST Act, 2017.
The time limit for furnishing details under section 38 for the same months will be notified later.

508.summary: Notification No. 29/2020-Central Tax, issued on March 23, 2020, outlines the filing deadlines for the GSTR-3B return for the months of April to September 2020. All registered taxpayers are required to file their GSTR-3B electronically by the 20th of the month following the reporting month. However, for taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore in certain states, including Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, and Maharashtra, the deadline is extended to the 22nd of the following month. Taxpayers in other states, such as Himachal Pradesh and Punjab, must file by the 24th. This differentiation is aimed at easing compliance for smaller businesses in specific regions. Additionally, all taxpayers must ensure that their tax liabilities are settled by debiting their electronic cash or credit ledger by the respective deadlines. This notification is part of the government's efforts to streamline GST compliance during the specified period.
key_points: The return in FORM GSTR-3B for April to September 2020 must be filed electronically by the 20th of the following month.
Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore in specified states must file GSTR-3B by the 22nd of the following month.

The specified states for the 22nd filing deadline include Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Goa, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and certain Union territories.
Taxpayers in other states must file GSTR-3B by the 24th of the following month.

The states with the 24th filing deadline include Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, Uttarakhand, Haryana, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram, Tripura, Meghalaya, Assam, West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha, and certain Union territories.
The notification is issued under section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and rule 61 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
Taxpayers must discharge their tax liabilities by debiting their electronic cash or credit ledger by the specified deadlines.

509.summary: Notification No. 30/2020-Central Tax, issued on 3rd April 2020, introduces amendments to the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. A significant change allows registered persons who choose to pay tax under section 10 for the financial year 2020-21 to electronically file an intimation in FORM GST CMP-02 by 30th June 2020. Additionally, these persons are required to submit FORM GST ITC-03 by 31st July 2020. Another important amendment is the introduction of a new proviso in sub-rule (4) of rule 36, which states that the conditions for input tax credit will now apply cumulatively for the months of February through August 2020. Consequently, businesses must ensure that their return in FORM GSTR-3B for September 2020 reflects the cumulative adjustments of input tax credit for these months. These changes are aimed at providing clarity and relief to taxpayers during the specified period.
key_points: Notification No. 30/2020-Central Tax was issued on 3rd April 2020.
The rules amend the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
A new proviso is added to sub-rule (3) of rule 3, allowing registered persons opting for section 10 to file FORM GST CMP-02 by 30th June 2020.
Registered persons must also furnish FORM GST ITC-03 by 31st July 2020.
A new proviso is added to sub-rule (4) of rule 36, applying conditions cumulatively for February to August 2020.
The return in FORM GSTR-3B for September 2020 must include cumulative adjustments of input tax credit for the specified months.

510.summary: Notification No. 31/2020-Central Tax, issued on April 3, 2020, introduces amendments to Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax, which was originally published on June 28, 2017. The key change is the addition of new provisos that outline specific interest rates applicable for late filing of GSTR-3B returns based on the taxpayer's turnover. For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores, there is no interest for the first 15 days post the due date, after which a 9% interest rate applies. Those with turnover between 1.5 crores and 5 crores can avoid interest if they file their returns by June 29, 2020, for February and March, and by June 30, 2020, for April. Similarly, taxpayers with turnover up to 1.5 crores can avoid interest by filing by June 30, July 3, and July 6, 2020, for the respective months. This notification is effective retroactively from March 20, 2020, impacting all registered persons required to file GSTR-3B during the specified periods.
key_points: Notification No. 31/2020-Central Tax was issued on April 3, 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax dated June 28, 2017.
New provisos are added to the first paragraph of Notification No. 13/2017.
The new table specifies interest rates for late filing of GSTR-3B based on turnover and tax periods.
Taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores have a nil interest rate for the first 15 days after the due date, then 9% thereafter.
Taxpayers with turnover between 1.5 crores and 5 crores have a nil interest rate if GSTR-3B is filed by June 29, 2020, for February and March, and by June 30, 2020, for April.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 1.5 crores have a nil interest rate if GSTR-3B is filed by June 30, July 3, and July 6, 2020, for February, March, and April respectively.
The notification is effective from March 20, 2020.

511.summary: The recent Notification No. 32/2020-Central Tax amends the earlier Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax by adding a new proviso that waives late fees for specific tax periods for certain classes of registered persons. This applies to taxpayers based on their aggregate turnover in the preceding financial year. For those with a turnover exceeding 5 crores, the GSTR-3B return must be filed by June 24, 2020, to avoid late fees for February, March, and April 2020. Taxpayers with a turnover between 1.5 crores and 5 crores need to file by June 29, 2020, for February and by June 30, 2020, for March and April. Meanwhile, those with a turnover up to 1.5 crores must file by June 30, July 3, and July 6, 2020, for February, March, and April respectively. This notification is effective from March 20, 2020, providing relief to taxpayers during this period.
key_points: Inserts a new proviso after the second proviso in Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax.
Waives late fee under section 47 for specific tax periods for certain registered persons.
Taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores must file GSTR-3B by June 24, 2020, to avoid late fees for February, March, and April 2020.
Taxpayers with turnover between 1.5 crores and 5 crores must file GSTR-3B by June 29, 2020, for February 2020 and by June 30, 2020, for March and April 2020.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 1.5 crores must file GSTR-3B by June 30, 2020, for February 2020, by July 3, 2020, for March 2020, and by July 6, 2020, for April 2020.
The notification is effective from March 20, 2020.

512.summary: Notification No. 33/2020-Central Tax, issued on April 3, 2020, introduces a significant amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax from January 23, 2018. A new proviso has been added, which waives the late fee for registered persons who fail to submit their GSTR-1 for the months of March, April, and May 2020, as well as for the quarter ending March 31, 2020. This waiver is applicable only if they file their GSTR-1 by June 30, 2020. This change is aimed at providing relief to taxpayers during the challenging times of the COVID-19 pandemic. The amendment was made following recommendations from the GST Council, reflecting the government's intent to ease compliance burdens. The principal notification had previously been amended by Notification No. 4/2020-Central Tax on January 10, 2020, but this latest change specifically addresses the late fee issue for the specified months and quarter.
key_points: The notification is dated April 3, 2020.
It amends Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax dated January 23, 2018.
A new proviso is added after the third proviso in the earlier notification.
The new proviso waives the late fee under section 47 for March, April, and May 2020.
The waiver applies to registered persons who file GSTR-1 by June 30, 2020.
The waiver also covers the quarter ending March 31, 2020.
The amendment is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The principal notification was last amended by Notification No. 4/2020-Central Tax on January 10, 2020.

513.summary: Notification No. 34/2020-Central Tax introduces important amendments to Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax. It adds a new requirement for individuals to submit a statement detailing their self-assessed tax in FORM GST CMP-08 for the quarter ending 31st March 2020, with a new deadline set for the 7th of July 2020. Additionally, it mandates that these individuals must also file their return in FORM GSTR-4 for the financial year ending 31st March 2020, with the deadline extended to the 15th of July 2020. These changes primarily affect those who are required to file these forms, providing them with additional time to comply with the reporting requirements. The amendments aim to ease the compliance burden during a challenging period, ensuring that taxpayers have the necessary time to prepare and submit their returns accurately. This notification is part of the government's ongoing efforts to support businesses and individuals in managing their tax obligations effectively.
key_points: Inserts a new proviso in the second paragraph of Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax requiring self-assessed tax details in FORM GST CMP-08 for the quarter ending 31st March 2020.
The deadline for submitting FORM GST CMP-08 is extended to the 7th day of July 2020.
Inserts a new proviso in the third paragraph of Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax requiring the return in FORM GSTR-4 for the financial year ending 31st March 2020.
The deadline for submitting FORM GSTR-4 is extended to the 15th day of July 2020.

514.summary: Notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax, issued on April 3, 2020, extends various compliance deadlines due to the COVID-19 pandemic. Specifically, any compliance action that was due between March 20, 2020, and June 29, 2020, now has a new deadline of June 30, 2020. This extension applies to actions such as completing proceedings, passing orders, and filing appeals or returns under the GST framework. However, it does not cover certain provisions, including Chapter IV and specific sections like 39 and 68, which pertains to e-way bills. Additionally, e-way bills generated during the period from March 20, 2020, to April 15, 2020, will now remain valid until April 30, 2020. This notification is effective from March 20, 2020, providing relief to businesses during a challenging time.
key_points: Extends time limits for compliance actions from March 20, 2020, to June 29, 2020, to June 30, 2020.
Applicable to completion of proceedings, passing orders, and filing appeals or returns under GST laws.
Excludes certain provisions from this extension, including Chapter IV and specific sections like 39 and 68 related to e-way bills.
E-way bills generated between March 20, 2020, and April 15, 2020, have their validity extended to April 30, 2020.
Notification comes into effect from March 20, 2020.

515.summary: Notification No. 36/2020-Central Tax, issued on April 3, 2020, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 29/2020-Central Tax from March 23, 2020. The key change is regarding the due dates for filing the GSTR-3B return for May 2020. Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding ₹5 crore must file their GSTR-3B by June 27, 2020. For those with a turnover of up to ₹5 crore, the due dates vary based on their state. Taxpayers in states like Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and others must submit their returns by July 12, 2020. Meanwhile, those in states such as Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and others have until July 14, 2020, to file. This amendment aims to provide additional time for compliance based on the turnover and location of the taxpayers, reflecting the government's response to the ongoing challenges faced by businesses during this period.
key_points: Notification No. 36/2020-Central Tax was issued on 2020-04-03.
This notification amends Notification No. 29/2020-Central Tax dated 2020-03-23.
For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of more than ₹5 crore, the GSTR-3B return for May 2020 is due by 27th June 2020.
For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore in specific states, the GSTR-3B return for May 2020 is due by 12th July 2020.
For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore in other specified states, the GSTR-3B return for May 2020 is due by 14th July 2020.

516.summary: Notification No. 37/2020-Central Tax, issued on April 28, 2020, establishes that certain provisions will take effect from April 21, 2020. This change is based on section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and specifically relates to clause (c) of rule 9 and rule 25 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fourth Amendment) Rules, 2019. The notification amends the earlier rules set forth in Notification No. 31/2019-Central Tax, dated June 28, 2019. This means that businesses and individuals who are subject to these rules will need to comply with the updated provisions starting from the specified date. The notification was published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that it is officially recognized. The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs is responsible for this notification, indicating its importance in the regulatory framework of GST in India. Overall, this notification clarifies the effective date for the implementation of specific GST rules, impacting all stakeholders involved.
key_points: The notification is titled Notification No. 37/2020-Central Tax.
It was issued on April 28, 2020.
The provisions come into force from April 21, 2020.
This notification is based on section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It references clause (c) of rule 9 and rule 25 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fourth Amendment) Rules, 2019.
It amends the rules made under notification No. 31/2019-Central Tax dated June 28, 2019.
The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The notification is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs.

517.summary: The Central Government has issued the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2020, effective from May 5, 2020. This amendment introduces significant changes to the filing process for registered companies. Specifically, from April 21, 2020, to June 30, 2020, companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013, can now file their GSTR-3B returns using an electronic verification code (EVC). Additionally, a new rule, 67A, has been added, which allows registered persons to submit Nil returns for GSTR-3B via SMS, verified through a One Time Password (OTP). A Nil return is defined as one with no entries in any of the tables in GSTR-3B. These changes aim to simplify the filing process and are based on recommendations from the GST Council. The principal GST rules were first published in June 2017 and have undergone several amendments, with the last one being in April 2020.
key_points: The notification is titled Central Goods and Services Tax (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2020.
It was issued on May 5, 2020, and comes into effect from the date of publication.
A new proviso is added to rule 26(1) allowing companies to file GSTR-3B using an electronic verification code (EVC) from April 21, 2020, to June 30, 2020.
A new rule 67A is introduced, allowing registered persons to file Nil returns for GSTR-3B via SMS, with verification through a One Time Password (OTP).
The definition of a Nil return is clarified as having no entries in all tables of GSTR-3B.
The amendment follows the recommendations of the GST Council.
The principal rules were first published on June 19, 2017, and last amended by notification No. 30/2020 on April 3, 2020.

518.summary: Notification No. 39/2020-Central Tax, issued on 5th May 2020, introduces important amendments to Notification No. 11/2020-Central Tax from 21st March 2020. A new proviso has been added, which clarifies that corporate debtors who have already submitted their statements under section 37 and returns under section 39 before the appointment of an Insolvency Resolution Professional (IRP) or Resolution Professional (RP) will not be included in a specific class of persons affected by this notification. Additionally, the requirement for corporate debtors to obtain new registrations has been updated. They are now required to register as distinct entities in each state or union territory where they were previously registered, with a deadline of thirty days from the appointment of the IRP/RP or by 30th June 2020, whichever is later. This change primarily impacts corporate debtors undergoing insolvency proceedings, ensuring they comply with registration requirements in a timely manner.
key_points: Notification No. 39/2020-Central Tax was issued on 5th May 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 11/2020-Central Tax dated 21st March 2020.

A new proviso is added stating that corporate debtors who have filed statements under section 37 and returns under section 39 prior to the appointment of IRP/RP are excluded from a specific class of persons.
The second paragraph of Notification No. 11/2020 is substituted to state that corporate debtors must obtain new registration in each state or union territory where they were previously registered.
The new registration must be completed within thirty days of the appointment of IRP/RP or by 30th June 2020, whichever is later.

519.summary: Notification No. 40/2020-Central Tax, issued on 5th May 2020, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax from 3rd April 2020. Specifically, it adds a new proviso to clause (ii) of the first paragraph of the earlier notification. This new provision allows for the extension of validity for e-way bills that were generated on or before 24th March 2020 and were set to expire between 20th March 2020 and 15th April 2020. These e-way bills will now be considered valid until 31st May 2020. This change is particularly relevant for businesses that had generated e-way bills during that period, as it provides them with additional time to manage their logistics without the concern of expired e-way bills. The amendment aims to ease the operational challenges faced by businesses during the specified timeframe, ensuring smoother compliance with GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 40/2020-Central Tax was issued on 5th May 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax dated 3rd April 2020.
A new proviso is added to clause (ii) of the first paragraph of Notification No. 35/2020.
The new proviso states that e-way bills generated on or before 24th March 2020, expiring between 20th March 2020 and 15th April 2020, are valid until 31st May 2020.
This extension applies specifically to e-way bills under rule 138 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.

520.summary: Notification No. 41/2020-Central Tax, issued on 5th May 2020, extends the deadline for filing the annual return, known as GSTR-9, for the financial year 2018-2019. The new deadline is set for 30th September 2020. This notification supersedes the earlier notification No. 15/2020-Central Tax, which was issued on 23rd March 2020. The extension is granted under the authority of section 44 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, along with rule 80 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This change affects all taxpayers who are required to file GSTR-9 for the specified financial year. By extending the deadline, the government aims to provide relief to businesses during challenging times, allowing them more time to prepare and submit their annual returns electronically through the common portal.
key_points: Extends the deadline for filing the annual return (GSTR-9) for the financial year 2018-2019.
New deadline for GSTR-9 is now 30th September 2020.
Supersedes the earlier notification No. 15/2020-Central Tax dated 23rd March 2020.
The extension is based on the powers conferred by section 44 of the CGST Act and rule 80 of the CGST Rules.

521.summary: Notification No. 42/2020-Central Tax, issued on May 5, 2020, brings changes to the filing deadlines for GSTR-3B returns as specified in Notification No. 44/2019-Central Tax. The sixth proviso of the earlier notification has been replaced to clarify the filing requirements for registered persons in the Union territories of Jammu and Kashmir and Ladakh. Specifically, those in Jammu and Kashmir are required to submit their GSTR-3B returns for the months of November 2019 to February 2020 by March 24, 2020. Meanwhile, registered persons in Ladakh must file their returns for November and December 2019 by the same date, March 24, 2020. For the months of January to March 2020, those in Ladakh have an extended deadline of May 20, 2020. This amendment is effective retroactively from March 24, 2020, ensuring that the specified deadlines are adhered to for the affected regions.
key_points: Notification No. 42/2020-Central Tax was issued on 2020-05-05.
This notification amends Notification No. 44/2019-Central Tax dated 2019-10-09.
The sixth proviso in Notification No. 44/2019 is replaced with new provisions regarding GSTR-3B returns.
Registered persons in the Union territory of Jammu and Kashmir must file GSTR-3B for November 2019 to February 2020 by 24th March 2020.
Registered persons in the Union territory of Ladakh must file GSTR-3B for November and December 2019 by 24th March 2020.
For January to March 2020, registered persons in Ladakh must file GSTR-3B by 20th May 2020.
This amendment is effective from 24th March 2020.

522.summary: Notification No. 43/2020-Central Tax, issued on May 16, 2020, announces that the provisions of section 128 of the Finance Act, 2020 will take effect from May 18, 2020. This means that any changes or regulations outlined in section 128 will be applicable starting on that date. The notification is part of the government's efforts to implement the provisions of the Finance Act, which may include various tax-related measures. Businesses and individuals affected by these provisions should be aware of the new rules coming into effect. The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs is responsible for this notification, indicating its importance in the context of GST compliance and administration. Stakeholders should review the implications of section 128 to understand how it may impact their operations or tax obligations.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 43/2020-Central Tax.
It was published on May 16, 2020.
The provisions of section 128 of the Finance Act, 2020 will come into force on May 18, 2020.
This notification is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs.

523.summary: Notification No. 44/2020-Central Tax, issued on 8th June 2020, establishes that certain provisions of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2020 will come into force from the same date. This notification is based on the authority granted under section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. It specifically amends the earlier notification No. 38/2020-Central Tax, which was issued on 5th May 2020. The earlier notification was also published in the Gazette of India, and this new notification clarifies that the provisions outlined in the Fifth Amendment Rules will now be effective as of 8th June 2020. This change is important for businesses and taxpayers who need to be aware of the updated rules and their effective date to with the GST framework. The notification serves to formalize the implementation of these amendments, impacting how businesses will operate under the GST regime moving forward.
key_points: Notification No. 44/2020-Central Tax was issued on 8th June 2020.
This notification comes into effect from the same date, 8th June 2020.
It is issued under the powers of section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification references rule 3 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fifth Amendment) Rules, 2020.
It amends provisions made in notification No. 38/2020-Central Tax dated 5th May 2020.
The earlier notification (38/2020) was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The provisions of the rules mentioned will be effective from the date of this notification.

524.summary: Notification No. 45/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 9, 2020, brings an important change to the earlier Notification No. 10/2020-Central Tax, which was published on March 21, 2020. The key change is the extension of the due date for a specific compliance requirement from May 31, 2020, to July 31, 2020. This amendment is made under section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, allowing the government to adjust timelines as needed. Businesses and taxpayers who were originally required to meet obligations by the end of May now have an additional two months to comply. This extension aims to provide relief and flexibility during challenging times. The notification will take effect from May 31, 2020, meaning that the new deadline applies retroactively from that date. This change is particularly relevant for those affected by the earlier deadline, allowing them to better manage their GST compliance without the pressure of the original timeline.
key_points: Notification No. 45/2020-Central Tax was issued on 09th June 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 10/2020-Central Tax dated 21st March 2020.
The amendment changes the due date from '31st day of May, 2020' to '31st day of July, 2020'.
The amendment is made under the powers conferred by section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

525.summary: Notification No. 46/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 9, 2020, introduces an important extension regarding the issuance of orders on refund claims. Specifically, for cases where a notice has been issued for the rejection of a refund claim, the time limit for issuing the order has been extended. This applies to notices issued between March 20, 2020, and June 29, 2020. The new deadline allows for an extension of fifteen days after the registered person replies to the notice or until June 30, 2020, whichever is later. This change is significant for businesses that have faced delays due to the COVID-19 pandemic, as it provides them additional time to respond and ensures that their refund claims are processed without undue pressure. The notification is effective retroactively from March 20, 2020, ensuring that businesses can benefit from this extension during a challenging period.
key_points: Extends the time limit for issuing orders on refund claims from 20th March 2020 to 29th June 2020.
The new deadline for issuing orders is now fifteen days after receiving the reply to the notice or by 30th June 2020, whichever is later.
This notification is issued under section 168A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It applies to cases where a notice for rejection of refund claim has been issued, either in full or in part.
The notification is effective from 20th March 2020.

526.summary: Notification No. 47/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 9, 2020, introduces an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax from April 3, 2020. Specifically, it changes the proviso in clause (ii) of the first paragraph regarding the validity of e-way bills. Under the new rules, any e-way bill that was generated on or before March 24, 2020, and whose validity expired on or after March 20, 2020, will now be considered valid until June 30, 2020. This extension is significant for businesses that rely on e-way bills for the transportation of goods, as it provides additional time to without the need for immediate re-issuance of e-way bills. The amendment is effective from May 31, 2020, and follows the previous amendment made by notification No. 40/2020-Central Tax on May 5, 2020. This change aims to ease the operational challenges faced by businesses during the ongoing circumstances.
key_points: This notification is No. 47/2020-Central Tax, issued on 09th June 2020.
It amends notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax dated 3rd April 2020.
The amendment substitutes the existing proviso in clause (ii) of the first paragraph.
The new proviso states that e-way bills generated on or before 24th March 2020, expiring on or after 20th March 2020, are valid until 30th June 2020.
This notification comes into effect from 31st May 2020.
The principal notification was last amended by notification No. 40/2020-Central Tax dated 5th May 2020.

527.summary: Notification No. 48/2020-Central Tax, issued on 19th June 2020, introduces amendments to the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, specifically in rule 26(1). These changes allow registered persons under the Companies Act, 2013 to file their GSTR-3B returns using an electronic verification code (EVC) for the period from 21st April 2020 to 30th September 2020. Additionally, these registered persons can also submit their GSTR-1 returns with EVC from 27th May 2020 to 30th September 2020. This amendment aims to facilitate compliance for companies during the specified period by providing an alternative verification method. The rules are effective from 27th May 2020 and are part of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Sixth Amendment) Rules, 2020. This amendment follows the previous changes made by notification No. 38/2020-Central Tax on 5th May 2020, which had already updated the principal rules first published on 19th June 2017.
key_points: Notification No. 48/2020-Central Tax was issued on 19th June 2020.
The rules come into force on 27th May 2020.
The amendment substitutes the second proviso in rule 26(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
Registered persons under the Companies Act, 2013 can file GSTR-3B using an electronic verification code (EVC) from 21st April 2020 to 30th September 2020.
Registered persons under the Companies Act, 2013 can also file GSTR-1 using EVC from 27th May 2020 to 30th September 2020.
This amendment is part of the Central Goods and Services Tax (Sixth Amendment) Rules, 2020.
The principal rules were initially published on 19th June 2017 and last amended by notification No. 38/2020-Central Tax on 5th May 2020.

528.summary: Notification No. 49/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 24, 2020, announces that specific provisions of the Finance Act, 2020, will take effect on June 30, 2020. The sections that are coming into force include 118, 125, 129, and 130, which are part of the broader changes aimed at improving the GST framework. This notification is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs, indicating the government's commitment to implementing these legislative changes. The Finance Act referenced is Act No. 12 of 2020, which includes various amendments and provisions relevant to GST. Businesses and stakeholders in the GST ecosystem should be aware of these changes as they may impact compliance and operational processes. The notification serves as an official communication to ensure that all parties are informed about the upcoming changes in the law.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 49/2020-Central Tax.
It was published on June 24, 2020.
The provisions of sections 118, 125, 129, and 130 of the Finance Act, 2020 will come into force on June 30, 2020.
This notification is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs.
The Finance Act referred to is Act No. 12 of 2020.
The notification is part of the government's efforts to implement changes in the GST framework.

529.summary: The Government of India issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 50/2020-Central Tax on June 25, 2020, correcting a specific phrase in the original notification published a day earlier. In line 40 of the original notification, the term 'turnover of taxable' has been amended to simply read 'turnover of'. This change clarifies the definition of turnover in the context of Goods and Services Tax (GST). The adjustment is significant as it impacts how businesses interpret their turnover for GST purposes. By removing the word 'taxable', the amendment broadens the scope of what is considered turnover, which may affect various compliance and reporting requirements for businesses. All stakeholders involved in GST compliance should take note of this change to ensure accurate reporting and understanding of turnover in their GST filings.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 50/2020-Central Tax.
The corrigendum was issued on June 25, 2020.
It amends the text published on June 24, 2020.
The specific change is in line 40 of the original notification.
The phrase 'turnover of taxable' is replaced with 'turnover of'.
This change affects the interpretation of the term 'turnover' in the context of GST.

530.summary: Notification No. 50/2020-Central Tax, issued on 24th June 2020, introduces amendments to the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, effective from 1st April 2020. The key change is the substitution of the Table in Rule 7, which outlines the tax rates applicable for different categories of registered persons opting for the composition levy. Manufacturers, except those producing specific goods, will now pay a reduced rate of 0.5% of their turnover. Suppliers making certain specified supplies will be taxed at 2.5% of their turnover. Additionally, other eligible suppliers under section 10 will also pay 0.5% of their taxable supplies. For registered persons who are not eligible under the standard composition levy but can opt for the provisions under sub-section 2A of section 10, the tax rate is set at 3% of their taxable supplies. These changes aim to simplify compliance for small businesses and enhance clarity regarding tax obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 50/2020-Central Tax was issued on 24th June 2020.
This notification amends the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
The amendments are effective from 1st April 2020.
Rule 7's Table has been substituted with a new Table detailing tax rates for composition levy.
Manufacturers (excluding certain goods) will pay 0.5% of turnover.
Suppliers making specific supplies will pay 2.5% of turnover.
Other eligible suppliers under section 10 will pay 0.5% of taxable supplies.
Registered persons under sub-section 2A of section 10 will pay 3% of taxable supplies.

531.summary: Notification No. 51/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 24, 2020, brings changes to the interest rates applicable for late filing of GSTR-3B returns, amending the first proviso of Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax. Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores will not incur interest for the first 15 days after the due date, but will face a 9% interest rate thereafter until June 24, 2020. For those with turnover up to 5 crores in specific states, interest is waived until June 30, 2020, and then set at 9% until September 30, 2020. Similarly, taxpayers in other states with the same turnover threshold will also benefit from the nil interest period until June 30, 2020, followed by a 9% rate until September 30, 2020. The notification outlines specific due dates for filing GSTR-3B based on the month and turnover category, ensuring clarity for affected taxpayers. This amendment aims to provide relief to businesses during the ongoing economic challenges.
key_points: Notification No. 51/2020-Central Tax was issued on June 24, 2020.
This notification amends the first proviso of Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax.
The new interest rates for late filing of GSTR-3B are specified in a table with different rates based on turnover and location.
For taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores, the interest is nil for the first 15 days and 9% thereafter until June 24, 2020.
For taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores in certain states, the interest is nil until June 30, 2020, and 9% thereafter until September 30, 2020.
For taxpayers in other states with turnover up to 5 crores, the interest is also nil until June 30, 2020, and 9% thereafter until September 30, 2020.
Specific due dates for filing GSTR-3B vary by month and turnover category.
This notification is effective from the date of issuance.

532.summary: The recently issued Notification No. 52/2020-Central Tax, dated June 24, 2020, introduces significant amendments to the earlier Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax. The most notable change is the replacement of the third proviso's table, which outlines the conditions for waiving late fees for GSTR-3B returns. Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores must submit their GSTR-3B for February, March, and April 2020 by June 24, 2020, to avoid late fees. For those with a turnover of up to 5 crores, specific deadlines have been established based on their principal place of business, with different states having varying deadlines from June to September 2020. Additionally, late fees exceeding 250 rupees for GSTR-3B returns filed for the period from July 2017 to January 2020 will be waived if these returns are submitted between July 1, 2020, and September 30, 2020. This waiver also applies if the total central tax payable is nil. These changes aim to provide relief to taxpayers during the ongoing challenges posed by the pandemic.
key_points: Notification No. 52/2020-Central Tax was issued on June 24, 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax, originally published on December 31, 2018.
The third proviso's table is replaced with a new table detailing late fee waivers for GSTR-3B returns.
Taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores must file GSTR-3B for February, March, and April 2020 by June 24, 2020, to avoid late fees.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores in specified states have different deadlines for GSTR-3B filings from June to September 2020.
Taxpayers in other states have their own deadlines for GSTR-3B filings from June to September 2020.
Late fees exceeding 250 rupees for GSTR-3B returns from July 2017 to January 2020 will be waived if filed between July 1, 2020, and September 30, 2020.
If the total central tax payable is nil, the late fee will be waived for the same period for GSTR-3B returns.

533.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 53/2020-Central Tax, which was originally published on 24th June 2020. This corrigendum, published on 25th June 2020, corrects a specific phrase in the original notification. In line 17 of the original text, the term 'third proviso' has been amended to 'fourth proviso'. This change is important for clarity and accuracy in the application of the notification. The corrigendum is officially published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i), under the reference number G.S.R. 406(E). This correction is relevant for all stakeholders who rely on the provisions outlined in Notification No. 53/2020-Central Tax, ensuring they refer to the correct legal text. The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has issued this corrigendum to maintain the integrity of the tax regulations.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 53/2020-Central Tax.
The original notification was issued on 24th June 2020.
The corrigendum was published on 25th June 2020.
In the original notification, the phrase 'third proviso' has been corrected to 'fourth proviso'.
This change is noted in line 17 of the original notification.
The corrigendum is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The reference number for the corrigendum is G.S.R. 406(E).
The corrigendum is issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs.

534.summary: Notification No. 53/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 24, 2020, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax from January 23, 2018. This amendment specifically changes the third proviso concerning late fees under section 47 of the CGST Act. It states that registered persons who do not submit their GSTR-1 by the original due date will have their late fees waived if they file by the new extended dates. These new deadlines are set for various months: for March 2020, the deadline is July 10, 2020; for April 2020, it is July 24, 2020; for May 2020, it is July 28, 2020; and for June 2020, it is August 5, 2020. Additionally, for the quarter from January to March 2020, the deadline is July 17, 2020, and for April to June 2020, it is August 3, 2020. This change is beneficial for registered taxpayers who may have missed the original deadlines, allowing them to avoid late fees if they comply with the new dates.
key_points: This notification is Notification No. 53/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 24, 2020.
It amends Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax, originally issued on January 23, 2018.
The amendment substitutes the third proviso regarding late fees under section 47 of the CGST Act.
Late fees are waived for registered persons who file GSTR-1 after the due date but before specified extended dates.

The extended dates for filing GSTR-1 are: March 2020 by July 10, 2020; April 2020 by July 24, 2020; May 2020 by July 28, 2020; June 2020 by August 5, 2020; January to March 2020 by July 17, 2020; and April to June 2020 by August 3, 2020.

535.summary: Notification No. 54/2020-Central Tax, issued on June 24, 2020, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 29/2020-Central Tax from March 23, 2020. This change specifically affects the due dates for filing the GSTR-3B return for the month of August 2020. Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore and whose principal place of business is in states like Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and others listed, must file their GSTR-3B by October 1, 2020. Meanwhile, those in other states, including Himachal Pradesh, Punjab, and Delhi, have until October 3, 2020, to submit their returns. This amendment aims to provide clarity and additional time for compliance for small taxpayers across different regions, ensuring they can meet their filing obligations without undue pressure.
key_points: Notification No. 54/2020-Central Tax was issued on June 24, 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 29/2020-Central Tax dated March 23, 2020.
For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore in specified states, the GSTR-3B return for August 2020 is due by October 1, 2020.
For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crore in other specified states, the GSTR-3B return for August 2020 is due by October 3, 2020.

536.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 55/2020-Central Tax on 27th June 2020, which amends the earlier Notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax dated 3rd April 2020. This amendment specifically changes the due dates for certain compliance requirements. The original due date of 29th June 2020 has been extended to 30th August 2020, and the due date of 30th June 2020 has been extended to 31st August 2020. This change is significant for businesses that are required to meet these deadlines, as it provides additional time for compliance. The notification is issued under the authority of section 168A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, along with references to the Integrated Goods and Services Tax and Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Acts. This extension is likely to benefit many taxpayers who may have been facing challenges in meeting the original deadlines due to various circumstances.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 35/2020-Central Tax dated 3rd April 2020.
Changes the due date from 29th June 2020 to 30th August 2020.
Changes the due date from 30th June 2020 to 31st August 2020.
Issued on 27th June 2020 by the Ministry of Finance.
Cited under section 168A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Also references sections 20 and 21 of the IGST and UTGST Acts respectively.
