591.summary: On 16th October 2020, the Government of India issued Notification No. 05/2020-Central Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new entry, 19C, to the existing table, which specifically covers 'Satellite launch services' provided by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), Antrix Corporation Limited, or New Space India Limited. Notably, these services are now exempt from tax, as indicated by the 'Nil' tax rate assigned to this entry. This change is significant for businesses involved in satellite launch services, as it reduces their tax burden. The amendment is part of the government's ongoing efforts to promote the space sector and is made under various sections of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This notification follows the previous amendment made by Notification No. 04/2020-Central Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2020, ensuring that the latest updates are reflected in the tax framework.
key_points: This notification is issued on 16th October 2020.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry 19C is added to the Table in the earlier notification.
Entry 19C specifies 'Satellite launch services' supplied by ISRO, Antrix Corporation, or New Space India.
The tax rate for these services is set to 'Nil'.
This amendment follows the previous amendment made by Notification No. 04/2020-Central Tax (Rate) dated 30th September 2020.
The amendment is made under various sections of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary.

592.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 05/2020-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 16th October 2020, which introduces a significant amendment to the earlier notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically adds a new entry in the tax rate table, following serial number 20B. The new entry, numbered 20C, pertains to 'Satellite launch services' provided by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), Antrix Corporation Limited, or New Space India Limited. Importantly, the tax rate for these services is set to 'Nil', meaning that these satellite launch services will not attract any integrated tax. This change is part of the government's ongoing efforts to promote space-related activities and is a continuation of the amendments made in the previous notification No. 04/2020, which was issued on 30th September 2020. Businesses involved in satellite launch services should take note of this new provision as it may impact their pricing and service offerings.
key_points: Notification No. 05/2020-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 16th October 2020.
Amends notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts a new entry in the Table after serial number 20B.
New entry 20C specifies 'Satellite launch services' supplied by ISRO, Antrix Corporation Limited, or New Space India Limited.
The tax rate for this new entry is set to 'Nil'.
This amendment follows the last amendment made by notification No. 04/2020 on 30th September 2020.

593.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 1/2020-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 21st February 2020, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This new notification removes S. No. 242 and its related entries from Schedule II, which pertains to a 6% tax rate. Additionally, it updates Schedule IV by replacing S. No. 228 with a new entry that specifies 'Any chapter Lottery' under the 14% tax rate. These changes will take effect from 1st March 2020. This amendment is significant for businesses involved in lotteries and those affected by the previous entries in Schedule II, as it alters the tax implications for these categories. The principal notification had been amended previously by Notification No. 27/2019 on 30th December 2019, indicating ongoing adjustments to the Union Territory tax framework.
key_points: Notification No. 1/2020-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 21st February 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Omission of S. No. 242 and its entries from Schedule II - 6%.
Substitution of S. No. 228 in Schedule IV - 14% with 'Any chapter Lottery'.
The changes will come into effect on 1st March 2020.
The principal notification was last amended by Notification No. 27/2019 on 30th December 2019.

594.summary: Notification No. 01/2020-Central Tax, issued on April 8, 2020, introduces amendments to Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax, originally dated June 30, 2017. The key change is the addition of new provisions regarding interest rates applicable for late filing of GSTR-3B returns. For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crores, there will be no interest for the first 15 days from the due date for February, March, and April 2020, after which a 9% interest rate will apply if the return is not filed by June 24, 2020. Taxpayers with a turnover between ₹1.5 crores and ₹5 crores can avoid interest if they file their returns by June 29, 2020, for February and March, and by June 30, 2020, for April. Those with a turnover of up to ₹1.5 crores will also benefit from a nil interest rate if they file by June 30, July 3, and July 6, 2020, for February, March, and April respectively. This notification is effective from March 20, 2020, and aims to provide relief to taxpayers during this period.
key_points: This notification is issued on April 8, 2020, under Notification No. 01/2020-Central Tax.
It amends Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax dated June 30, 2017.
New provisos are added regarding interest rates for late filing of GSTR-3B returns.

Taxpayers with an aggregate turnover of up to ₹5 crores will have a nil interest rate for the first 15 days from February 2020, March 2020, and April 2020, with 9% thereafter if filed by June 24, 2020.
Taxpayers with turnover between ₹1.5 crores and ₹5 crores will have a nil interest rate if GSTR-3B is filed by June 29, 2020, for February and March, and by June 30, 2020, for April.
Taxpayers with turnover up to ₹1.5 crores will have a nil interest rate if GSTR-3B is filed by June 30, 2020, for February, July 3, 2020, for March, and July 6, 2020, for April.
The notification is effective from March 20, 2020.

595.summary: Notification No. 02/2020-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 26th March 2020, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The key change is the addition of a new service category under serial number 25 in the table, specifically for 'Maintenance, repair or overhaul services in respect of aircrafts, aircraft engines and other aircraft components or parts', which will be taxed at a rate of 2.5%. Furthermore, the existing item (ii) in the table has been modified to include a reference to this new item (ia). This amendment is significant for businesses involved in the aviation sector, as it clarifies the tax implications for maintenance services related to aircraft. The new provisions will take effect from 1st April 2020, allowing businesses to prepare for the updated tax structure in advance.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 02/2020-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 26th March 2020.
It amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment adds a new entry (ia) for 'Maintenance, repair or overhaul services in respect of aircrafts, aircraft engines and other aircraft components or parts' with a tax rate of 2.5%.
In item (ii) of the existing table, the reference to item (i) is updated to include the new item (ia).
This amendment will come into effect from 1st April 2020.

596.summary: Notification No. 02/2020-Central Tax, issued on 24th June 2020, amends the earlier Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax from 30th June 2017. The key change is the substitution of the first proviso regarding interest rates applicable to registered persons who fail to file their GSTR-3B returns on time. For taxpayers with an aggregate turnover exceeding 5 crores, a 9% interest rate will apply after a grace period of 15 days from the due date for the months of February, March, and April 2020. For those with a turnover of up to 5 crores in certain states, the interest rates will be nil until specific dates in July and September 2020, after which a 9% rate will apply. Similar provisions are in place for taxpayers in northern states like Himachal Pradesh, where the nil interest period also extends until June 30, 2020. This notification is the second amendment to the original notification, following the first amendment made on 8th April 2020, and it specifically impacts those required to file GSTR-3B returns.
key_points: This notification is issued on 24th June 2020.
It amends Notification No. 10/2017 – Union Territory Tax dated 30th June 2017.
The first proviso in the original notification is substituted with a new one regarding interest rates.
Taxpayers with turnover over 5 crores will have a 9% interest rate after 15 days from the due date for February, March, and April 2020.
Taxpayers with turnover up to 5 crores in specified states will have varying interest rates based on the month, starting from nil until specific dates in July and September 2020.
For taxpayers in Himachal Pradesh and other northern states, similar nil interest rates apply until June 30, 2020, with 9% thereafter until September 30, 2020.
The notification affects registered persons required to furnish returns in FORM GSTR-3B.
This is the second amendment to the original notification, following the first amendment on 8th April 2020.

597.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 03/2020-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 25th March 2020, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017 regarding Union Territory tax rates. Effective from 1st April 2020, several changes have been made to the tax schedules. Specifically, the 2.5% tax rate for serial number 187 in Schedule I has been removed. Additionally, a new entry has been introduced in Schedule II, adding a 6% tax rate for 'All goods' under serial number 75A, while the previous entries at serial numbers 202 and 203 have been omitted. In Schedule III, the 9% tax rate for serial number 73 is also removed, and the entry for serial number 379 has been updated to 'All goods'. These amendments will impact businesses operating in the Union Territories, as they will need to adjust their tax calculations and compliance accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 03/2020-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 25th March 2020.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, the 2.5% tax rate for serial number 187 is omitted.
In Schedule II, a new entry for 6% tax is added as serial number 75A for 'All goods'.
In Schedule II, serial numbers 202 and 203 are omitted.
In Schedule III, the 9% tax rate for serial number 73 is omitted.
In Schedule III, the entry for serial number 379 is changed to 'All goods'.
