724.summary: Notification No. 18/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on September 30, 2019, introduces an exemption from Integrated tax for goods supplied to the Food and Agricultural Organisation of the United Nations (FAO). This exemption applies specifically to goods used in two projects: 'Strengthening Capacities for Nutrition-sensitive Agriculture and Food systems' and 'Green Ag: Transforming Indian Agriculture for Global Environment benefits.' To qualify for this exemption, a certification is required from an officer of the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, at least at the Deputy Secretary level, confirming the quantity and description of the goods and their intended use for the projects. This notification is effective from October 1, 2019, meaning that any relevant supplies made from this date onward will benefit from this tax exemption. This change is significant for suppliers involved in these projects, as it reduces their tax burden and supports the initiatives aimed at improving agriculture and food systems in India.
key_points: Exempts all goods supplied to the Food and Agricultural Organisation of the United Nations (FAO) from Integrated tax under section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

The exemption applies to goods used for specific projects listed in the Annexure: (1) Strengthening Capacities for Nutrition-sensitive Agriculture and Food systems, (2) Green Ag: Transforming Indian Agriculture for Global Environment benefits.
The exemption is subject to certification by an officer not below the rank of Deputy Secretary in the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
The notification comes into force on the 1st day of October, 2019.

725.summary: Notification No. 19/2019-Central Tax (Rate) introduces an exemption from Central Tax for all goods supplied to the Food and Agricultural Organisation of the United Nations (FAO). This exemption is specifically for two projects: 'Strengthening Capacities for Nutrition-sensitive Agriculture and Food systems' and 'Green Ag: Transforming Indian Agriculture for Global Environment benefits.' To benefit from this exemption, a certification is required from an officer not below the rank of Deputy Secretary in the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare. This certification must detail the quantity and description of the goods and confirm that they are intended for use in the execution of the specified projects. The exemption will take effect from October 1, 2019. This change is significant for suppliers of goods to the FAO, as it reduces their tax burden for these specific projects aimed at enhancing agricultural practices and environmental benefits in India.

726.summary: Notification No. 19/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, brings significant changes to the tax structure for various services related to hospitality and catering. This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017, which was published on 28th June 2017. Notably, hotel accommodation valued between ₹1,000 and ₹7,500 per day will now attract a tax rate of 12%. Additionally, restaurant services provided outside specified premises will be taxed at 5%, with specific conditions regarding input tax credit. Outdoor catering services, when offered outside specified premises, will also incur a 5% tax, again with input tax credit conditions. The notification introduces new definitions for terms such as 'restaurant service', 'outdoor catering', 'hotel accommodation', and 'declared tariff', clarifying the scope of these services. These amendments will come into effect from 1st October 2019, impacting businesses in the hospitality and catering sectors significantly.
key_points: Notification No. 19/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes include new tax rates for hotel accommodation, restaurant services, and outdoor catering.
For hotel accommodation valued between ₹1,000 and ₹7,500 per day, the tax rate is set at 12%.
Restaurant services provided outside specified premises are taxed at 5%, with conditions on input tax credit.
Outdoor catering services, when provided outside specified premises, are also taxed at 5%, subject to input tax credit conditions.
New definitions for 'restaurant service', 'outdoor catering', 'hotel accommodation', and 'declared tariff' have been introduced.
The amendments take effect from 1st October 2019.

727.summary: Notification No. 20/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) effective from 1st October 2019. Key changes include the substitution of entries against serial number 7, which now specifies a tax rate of 6% for hotel accommodation valued between 1,000 and 7,500 rupees per unit per day. Additionally, restaurant services provided outside specified premises will be taxed at 2.5%, subject to conditions regarding input tax credit. New definitions for terms like 'restaurant service', 'outdoor catering', and 'hotel accommodation' have been included to clarify the scope of these services. The notification also revises entries in serial numbers 10, 15, 17, and 21, omitting certain items and adding new services, ensuring that specific services are taxed at mandatory rates. This amendment impacts businesses in the hospitality and catering sectors, requiring them to adjust their tax practices accordingly.
key_points: This notification is issued on 30th September 2019 and comes into effect from 1st October 2019.

The tax rate for hotel accommodation valued between 1,000 and 7,500 rupees per unit per day is set at 6%.
Restaurant services provided other than at specified premises are taxed at 2.5%, with conditions on input tax credit.
New definitions for 'restaurant service', 'outdoor catering', 'hotel accommodation', and 'declared tariff' are added.
Changes are made to serial numbers 10, 15, 17, and 21 in the table, including the omission of certain items and the addition of new services.
The notification clarifies that certain services will attract a mandatory tax rate and cannot be levied at different rates.

728.summary: The recent Notification No. 20/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017, effective from October 1, 2019. It modifies the exemption threshold for GST registration, replacing the fixed amount of 'twenty lakh rupees' with a variable amount that qualifies for exemption based on the preceding financial year for serial numbers 7 and 47. Additionally, it introduces new entries for services related to the FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup 2020, with a Nil tax rate, under serial numbers 10AA and 85A. A new entry for services provided by intermediaries when both the supplier and recipient are outside the taxable territory has been added under serial number 12AA, which requires specific documentation. The notification also updates the year from '2019' to '2020' for serial numbers 20A and 20B. Furthermore, it includes new provisions for the storage and warehousing of various agricultural products under serial number 25B and life insurance services provided by Central Armed Police Forces under serial number 30B, both with a Nil tax rate. Lastly, 'Bangla Shasya Bima' is now included as a new entry after serial number 36.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

Changes the exemption threshold for registration from 'twenty lakh rupees' to 'such amount in the preceding financial year as makes it eligible for exemption from registration' for serial numbers 7 and 47.
Inserts new entries for services related to FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup 2020 under serial numbers 10AA and 85A with a tax rate of Nil.
Introduces a new entry for services provided by an intermediary when both supplier and recipient are outside the taxable territory under serial number 12AA, with specific documentation requirements.
Changes the year from '2019' to '2020' for serial numbers 20A and 20B.
Adds new entries for services of storage or warehousing of various agricultural products under serial number 25B with a tax rate of Nil.
Inserts a new entry for life insurance services provided by Central Armed Police Forces under serial number 30B with a tax rate of Nil.
Adds 'Bangla Shasya Bima' as a new entry after serial number 36.

729.summary: The recent Notification No. 21/2019-Central Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. One significant change is the adjustment of the exemption threshold for GST registration, which now states that it will be based on the amount that qualifies for exemption rather than a fixed figure of twenty lakh rupees. Additionally, new entries have been added to the tax rate table, including entry 9AA for services related to the FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup 2020, which will be exempt from tax, provided there is certification from the Director (Sports). Other new entries include 24B for warehousing services of various agricultural products and 29B for life insurance services provided by Central Armed Police Forces, both with a nil tax rate. The year has also been updated from 2019 to 2020 in certain entries. This notification is effective from 1st October 2019, impacting businesses involved in these specified services and sectors.

730.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 21/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2019, amending the earlier Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces significant changes, particularly for music composers, photographers, and artists, by substituting the existing entry for their services. Additionally, a new entry (11A) is created for authors supplying services to publishers, which will take effect from 1st October 2019. Authors must register under the CGST Act and submit a declaration by 31st October 2019 to opt for forward charge, making their option effective from 1st November 2019. Furthermore, two new entries (17 and 18) are added to cover services related to renting motor vehicles and lending securities under SEBI's scheme. These changes specifically apply to services rendered within the taxable territory, impacting how these services are taxed under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act.

The notification clarifies that the changes apply to services provided within the taxable territory.

731.summary: Notification No. 22/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, brings significant amendments to the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate). The key change is the replacement of serial number 9, which now specifies that services provided by music composers, photographers, and artists to music companies or producers are taxable. Additionally, a new entry, serial number 9A, has been introduced for authors supplying services to publishers regarding original literary works. Authors must register under the CGST Act and submit a declaration by 31st October 2019 to opt for forward charge, effective from 1st November 2019. Furthermore, new entries for renting motor vehicles to body corporates and for lending securities under SEBI's Securities Lending Scheme have been added as serial numbers 15 and 16. These changes will take effect from 1st October 2019, impacting authors, music professionals, and businesses involved in vehicle rentals and securities lending.

732.summary: Notification No. 22/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) from January 25, 2018. The key change is the addition of an explanation that clarifies that the provisions of the earlier notification will not apply to development rights that are supplied on or after April 1, 2019. This means that any transactions involving development rights after this date will not be governed by the rules set out in the previous notification. The new rules come into effect from October 1, 2019. This amendment is significant for businesses involved in real estate and development, as it delineates the tax treatment for development rights supplied after the specified date. Companies and individuals engaged in such transactions should take note of this change to ensure proper compliance with the updated GST framework.

The effective date of this notification is 1st October 2019.

733.summary: Notification No. 23/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax (Rate) from 25th January 2018. This means that any transactions involving development rights after this date will not be covered under the earlier tax provisions. The amendment is effective from 1st October 2019, which means businesses dealing with development rights need to be aware of this change and adjust their tax compliance accordingly. This notification is significant for developers and real estate businesses, as it directly impacts how they handle the taxation of development rights moving forward.

734.summary: Notification No. 23/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, brings an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 07/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 29th March 2019. The key change is in the Table of the earlier notification, specifically at serial number 2, where the entry has been updated to specify 'Cement falling in chapter heading 2523 in the first schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975'. This means that the classification of cement for tax purposes has been clarified and now aligns with the specified chapter heading in the Customs Tariff Act. This amendment is set to take effect from 1st October 2019, which means businesses dealing with cement should take note of this change for their tax calculations and compliance. The update aims to ensure clarity in the tax treatment of cement, which is a significant commodity in construction and infrastructure sectors.

735.summary: Notification No. 24/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, brings an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 07/2019-Central Tax (Rate) from 29th March 2019. The new entry will come into effect from 1st October 2019, meaning that any transactions involving cement after this date will need to adhere to this updated classification. Businesses should review their GST compliance in light of this change to ensure they are aligned with the new specifications.

736.summary: Notification No. 24/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces an important change regarding the treatment of certain activities by State Governments. Specifically, it states that the service of granting an alcoholic liquor licence will not be classified as a supply of goods or services under GST. This means that any fees charged for such licences, including both licence fees and application fees, will not attract GST. This decision is in line with the recommendation made during the 26th GST Council meeting held on 10th March 2018, which aimed to clarify the tax treatment of these fees. The notification references specific sections of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, ensuring that the legal framework supports this exemption. As a result, State Governments can now collect these fees without the burden of GST, which may simplify the licensing process for alcoholic beverages.
key_points: Notification No. 24/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2019.
The notification states that certain activities by State Governments will not be considered a supply of goods or services.
Specifically, the service of granting an alcoholic liquor licence is exempt from GST.
This exemption applies to fees charged for the licence, including licence fees and application fees.
The notification implements a recommendation from the 26th GST Council meeting held on 10th March 2018.
The relevant sections cited are clause (i) of section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and sub-section (2) of section 7 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

737.summary: Notification No. 25/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces an important change regarding the taxation of fees for alcoholic liquor licenses. Specifically, it states that when State Governments grant licenses for alcoholic liquor, this activity will not be classified as a supply of goods or services under GST. Consequently, any fees charged, whether termed as licence fees or application fees, will not attract GST. This decision stems from a recommendation made during the 26th GST Council meeting on 10th March 2018, aimed at clarifying the tax treatment of such fees. The relevant legal basis for this notification is found in sub-section (2) of section 7 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change primarily affects State Governments and entities involved in the licensing of alcoholic beverages, ensuring that they do not have to charge GST on these fees, thereby simplifying the tax implications for both the authorities and the applicants.

738.summary: Notification No. 25/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 22nd November 2019, introduces an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically adds an explanation to serial number 26, column (3), item (ic) of the table in the previous notification. The new explanation defines 'bus body building' to include the construction of a body on the chassis of any vehicle classified under chapter 87 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This clarification is significant for manufacturers and service providers involved in the bus body building industry, as it specifies the scope of activities covered under this term. The principal notification had previously been amended by notification No. 19/2019 on 30th September 2019, but this latest change further refines the definitions relevant to integrated tax rates. Businesses engaged in this sector should take note of this clarification to ensure they understand the implications for their operations.

739.summary: Notification No. 26/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 22nd November 2019, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. By explicitly stating that it pertains to vehicles under chapter 87, the amendment aims to eliminate any ambiguity regarding the classification and taxation of these services. This clarification will help businesses in the sector to better understand their GST obligations and with the updated definitions.

740.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 26/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 30th December 2019, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it removes serial numbers 80AA and 171A from Schedule II, which previously listed certain goods under a 12% tax rate. Additionally, it introduces new entries in Schedule III under the 18% tax rate, adding serial number 163B for woven and non-woven bags and sacks made of polyethylene or polypropylene strips, and serial number 163C for flexible intermediate bulk containers. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2020. Businesses dealing with these specific goods will need to adjust their tax calculations accordingly, as the omission and addition of these entries will impact the applicable GST rates for their products.
key_points: Notification No. 26/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th December 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule II - 12%, serial numbers 80AA and 171A and their entries are omitted.
In Schedule III - 18%, new entries are added after serial number 163A.
The new entries include serial number 163B for woven and non-woven bags and sacks of polyethylene or polypropylene strips.
Another new entry is serial number 163C for flexible intermediate bulk containers.
This notification comes into force on 1st January 2020.

741.summary: Notification No. 27/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on December 30, 2019, introduces significant changes to the GST rates applicable to certain goods. It amends the previous Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Specifically, it removes serial numbers 80AA and 171A from Schedule II, which means those items will no longer be subject to the 6% GST rate. Additionally, it adds new entries in Schedule III, specifically after serial number 163A, which introduces serial number 163B for woven and non-woven bags and sacks made from polyethylene or polypropylene, and serial number 163C for flexible intermediate bulk containers. These changes will take effect from January 1, 2020, impacting manufacturers and suppliers of these products by altering their GST obligations. Businesses dealing with these specific goods should review their pricing and compliance strategies accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 27/2019-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on December 30, 2019.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
In Schedule II - 6%, serial numbers 80AA and 171A and their entries are omitted.
In Schedule III - 9%, new entries are added after serial number 163A.
The new entries include serial number 163B for woven and non-woven bags and sacks of polyethylene or polypropylene strips.
Another new entry is serial number 163C for flexible intermediate bulk containers.
This notification comes into effect on January 1, 2020.

742.summary: Notification No. 27/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2019, brings significant changes to the earlier Notification No. 9/2017. Specifically, it alters the tax rate for certain leased plots, changing the figure from '50' to '20' in the relevant table. Additionally, the conditions for using these leased plots have been updated, mandating that they must be used for industrial or financial activities. The responsibility for monitoring compliance with these conditions now falls on the respective State Governments. Importantly, if there are any violations or changes in land use, all parties involved in the lease—original lessors, lessees, and any subsequent buyers—will be jointly liable for the integrated tax that would have been applicable without the exemption. Furthermore, lease agreements must explicitly mention this tax exemption and the associated conditions. These changes take effect from 1st January 2020, impacting businesses involved in leasing plots for industrial or financial purposes.
key_points: This notification is issued on 31st December 2019.
It amends Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In the Table against serial number 43, the figure '50' is replaced with '20' in column (3).
Column (5) is updated with new conditions regarding the use of leased plots for industrial or financial activities.
The State Government is tasked with monitoring compliance with the new conditions.
In case of violations or changes in land use, all parties involved in the lease are liable for integrated tax.
The lease agreements must state the exemption of integrated tax and the conditions attached.
This notification comes into effect from 1st January 2020.

743.summary: Notification No. 28/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2019, brings significant amendments to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it alters the provisions related to leased plots in the Table against serial number 41. The figure '50' has been reduced to '20' in column (3), indicating a change in the applicable rate. Furthermore, column (5) has been completely revised to stipulate that leased plots must be utilized for industrial or financial activities. The responsibility for monitoring this usage now lies with the respective State Governments. In cases of non-compliance or changes in land use, all parties involved in the lease agreements will be jointly liable for the central tax that would have been due without the exemption. This notification will take effect from 1st January 2020, impacting businesses engaged in leasing plots for industrial or financial purposes.
key_points: This notification is issued on 31st December 2019.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment affects the Table against serial number 41.
In column (3), the figure '50' is replaced with '20' at both occurrences.
Column (5) is entirely substituted with new provisions regarding leased plots.
The new provisions require leased plots to be used for industrial or financial activities.
The State Government is tasked with monitoring compliance with these conditions.
The notification comes into effect from 1st January 2020.

744.summary: Notification No. 28/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2019, brings changes to the earlier Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it revises serial number 17 in the Table, which deals with services related to renting motor vehicles designed for carrying passengers. The new provision states that if the cost of fuel is included in the rental charge, the service must be provided to a body corporate. Furthermore, any individual or entity providing this service to a body corporate is required to charge integrated tax at a rate of 12 percent. This amendment directly impacts body corporates located within the taxable territory, ensuring they are aware of the tax implications when renting vehicles. The last amendment to the original notification was made by Notification No. 21/2019 on 30th September 2019, highlighting the ongoing updates in GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 28/2019-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment specifically alters serial number 17 in the Table of the earlier notification.
The new entry for serial number 17 pertains to services of renting motor vehicles designed for passenger transport.
The revised entry states that if the cost of fuel is included in the consideration charged, the service is provided to a body corporate.
It specifies that any person supplying this service to a body corporate must charge integrated tax at a rate of 12 percent.
The amendment affects any body corporate located in the taxable territory.
The previous amendment to Notification No. 10/2017 was made by Notification No. 21/2019 on 30th September 2019.

745.summary: Notification No. 29/2019-Central Tax (Rate), issued on December 31, 2019, brings changes to the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017. Specifically, it revises serial number 15 in the Table, which pertains to services related to the renting of motor vehicles designed for passenger transport. The new provision states that the cost of fuel must be included in the total charge to the service recipient. This change is particularly relevant for services provided to a body corporate. Additionally, it clarifies that any individual or entity, other than a body corporate, providing this service should not issue an invoice that charges central tax at the rate of 6% to the service recipient. This amendment directly impacts bodies corporate operating within the taxable territory, ensuring clarity in tax obligations related to vehicle rental services.
key_points: This notification is issued on 31st December 2019.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment specifically alters serial number 15 in the Table of the earlier notification.
The new entry for serial number 15 relates to services of renting motor vehicles designed to carry passengers.
The revised entry specifies that the cost of fuel must be included in the consideration charged from the service recipient.
It applies to services provided to a body corporate.
Any person, other than a body corporate, supplying this service must not issue an invoice charging central tax at 6% to the service recipient.
The amendment affects bodies corporate located in the taxable territory.

746.summary: On 29th January 2019, the Government of India issued Notification No. 01/2019, which rescinds the previous Notification No. 8/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) that was effective from 28th June 2017. This change will take effect from 1st February 2019. The earlier notification, which was published in the Gazette of India as G.S.R. 717 (E), had undergone amendments, the last being through Notification No. 22/2018 on 6th August 2018. The new notification effectively nullifies the provisions of the earlier one, but it does not affect any actions that were taken before the rescission. This means that any tax matters or compliance related to the previous notification will no longer be applicable after the effective date. Businesses and individuals who were following the guidelines of Notification No. 8/2017 will need to be aware of this change and adjust their practices accordingly from February 1, 2019.
key_points: Notification No. 01/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 29th January 2019.
This notification rescinds the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The rescission of Notification No. 8/2017 is effective from 1st February 2019.
The earlier notification (No. 8/2017) was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i) as G.S.R. 717 (E).
Notification No. 8/2017 was last amended by Notification No. 22/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 6th August 2018.
The rescission applies except for actions taken before the rescission date.

747.summary: Notification No. 1/2019-Central Tax, issued on 29th January 2019, announces that the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Act, 2018 will come into effect on 1st February 2019. This amendment is made under the authority of sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Act, which is numbered 33 of 2018. The notification is part of the official Gazette of India and falls under the jurisdiction of the Ministry of Finance, specifically the Department of Revenue. N Gandhi Kumar, the Deputy Secretary to the Government of India, has signed this notification. This change is significant for businesses operating in Union Territories, as it marks the implementation of updated provisions under the GST framework specific to those regions. Stakeholders in these areas should prepare for the changes that will take effect shortly after the notification date.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 1/2019-Central Tax.
The date of effect for the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Act, 2018 is set as 1st February 2019.
This notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The amendment is made under the powers conferred by sub-section (2) of section 1 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Amendment) Act, 2018.
The Act number referenced is 33 of 2018.
The notification is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.
The Deputy Secretary to the Government of India is N Gandhi Kumar.
The notification is dated 29th January 2019.

748.summary: Notification No. 2/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 7th March 2019, introduces a 3% Union Territory Tax on the first supplies of goods or services made by registered persons with an aggregate turnover of up to fifty lakh rupees. This applies specifically to those whose turnover in the previous financial year was fifty lakh rupees or less. The tax is effective from 1st April 2019. Suppliers are required to issue a bill of supply instead of a tax invoice and must not collect any tax from their customers. Additionally, they must include specific wording on the bill of supply to indicate compliance with this notification. The notification also lists certain goods, such as ice cream, pan masala, and tobacco, that fall under this tax rate. Furthermore, when calculating aggregate turnover for eligibility, the value of exempt services related to deposits, loans, or advances is not considered. This change primarily affects small registered suppliers operating within the Union Territories.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 2/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 7th March 2019.
It specifies a 3% Union Territory Tax on first supplies of goods or services with an aggregate turnover of up to fifty lakh rupees.
This tax applies to registered persons whose turnover in the preceding financial year was fifty lakh rupees or below.
Suppliers must not collect tax from recipients and must issue a bill of supply instead of a tax invoice.
The notification comes into effect on 1st April 2019.
Certain goods like ice cream, pan masala, and tobacco are specified in the annexure for this tax rate.
Registered persons must mention specific wording on the bill of supply indicating they are paying tax under this notification.
Exempt services related to deposits, loans, or advances are excluded from the aggregate turnover calculation.

749.summary: Notification No. 02/2019-Central Tax, issued on 7th March 2019, introduces exemptions from obtaining registration under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Specifically, it allows persons engaged in the exclusive supply of goods with an aggregate turnover not exceeding forty lakh rupees to be exempt, with certain exceptions. Those required to take compulsory registration under section 24 of the Act are not eligible for this exemption. Additionally, the exemption does not apply to suppliers of specific goods listed in the notification, such as ice cream, pan masala, and tobacco products. Furthermore, individuals making intra-State supplies in states like Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, and others are also excluded from this exemption. This notification will take effect from 1st April 2019, impacting small suppliers and those dealing in the specified goods, as they will need to consider their registration requirements under the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2019-Central Tax was issued on 7th March 2019.
This notification exempts certain persons from obtaining registration under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Persons engaged in exclusive supply of goods with an aggregate turnover not exceeding forty lakh rupees are exempt, except for specific categories.
Compulsory registration is required under section 24 of the Act for certain persons.
Exemptions do not apply to those supplying goods listed in the specified table, including ice cream, pan masala, and tobacco products.
The exemption does not apply to persons making intra-State supplies in specific states including Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, and others.
The notification will come into effect on 1st April 2019.

750.summary: The Government of India issued a corrigendum on 29th March 2019 to correct certain references in Notification No. 2/2019-Union Territory Tax, which was originally published on 7th March 2019. Specifically, in line 25 of the original notification, the reference '(iii)' has been amended to '(c)', and in line 27, '(iv)' has been changed to '(d)'. These corrections are purely editorial and do not alter the substantive content or provisions of the original notification. The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, and is referenced as G.S.R. 192(E). This corrigendum is important for ensuring clarity in the legal text, but it does not impose any new obligations or changes in tax compliance for businesses. It is crucial for stakeholders to note these corrections for accurate understanding and application of the notification.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 2/2019-Union Territory Tax dated 7th March 2019.
The corrigendum was issued on 29th March 2019.
In line 25 of the original notification, '(iii)' has been corrected to '(c)'.
In line 27 of the original notification, '(iv)' has been corrected to '(d)'.
The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The original notification was published under G.S.R. 192(E).
The corrigendum is referenced under F.No.354/25/2019-TRU.
The changes do not affect the substantive provisions of the original notification.

751.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 3/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019. This corrigendum makes specific corrections in the published notification. Firstly, in the table on page 165, the term 'tax' has been amended to 'Union territory tax' in line 8, clarifying the type of tax being referred to. Additionally, in line 11 of the same table, the figure 'eighteen' has been corrected to 'nine', which likely affects the tax rate or calculation. Furthermore, on page 181, line 9, the number '18' has been revised to '18(9 + 9)', indicating a change in how the tax is structured or calculated. These changes are important for businesses operating in Union Territories as they clarify the applicable tax rates and terms. It is essential for affected businesses to review these corrections to ensure accurate compliance with the updated tax provisions.
key_points: This corrigendum is related to Notification No. 3/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019.
In the table on page 165, line 8, 'tax' is corrected to 'Union territory tax'.
In the same table on page 165, line 11, 'eighteen' is corrected to 'nine'.
On page 181, line 9, '18' is corrected to '18(9 + 9)'.

752.summary: The Government of India issued a corrigendum on 30th August 2019 to correct references in Notification No. 03/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019. Specifically, the corrigendum changes the form name from 'FORM GST ITC-03' to 'FORM GST DRC-03' in two instances: once at page 172, line 15, and again at page 177, line 3. This correction is important for businesses and taxpayers who need to use the correct form for their filings. The earlier notification mistakenly referred to the wrong form, which could lead to confusion in compliance. By clarifying the correct form, the government aims to streamline the process for taxpayers in the Union Territories. It is essential for affected parties to note these changes to ensure they are using the correct documentation moving forward.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 03/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019.
Issued on 30th August 2019 by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.
Changes the reference from 'FORM GST ITC-03' to 'FORM GST DRC-03' at page 172, line 15.
Also changes the reference from 'FORM GST ITC-03' to 'FORM GST DRC-03' at page 177, line 3.
This corrigendum corrects the form names used in the earlier notification.

753.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 03/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 29th March 2019, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This new notification introduces several changes, particularly in the tax rates for construction projects. For affordable residential apartments starting on or after 1st April 2019, the tax rate is now set at 0.75%, while for other residential apartments, it is 3.75%. Additionally, the notification mandates that the Union Territory tax must be paid in cash by debiting the electronic cash ledger. It also outlines specific conditions regarding input tax credit for both landowner-promoters and developer-promoters, ensuring that they meet certain criteria to qualify for credits. Importantly, registered persons must exercise their option to pay tax at these specified rates by 10th May 2019. These changes primarily affect real estate developers and promoters involved in residential projects, ensuring they comply with the new tax structure and conditions laid out in this notification.
key_points: Notification No. 03/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 29th March 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments include inserting additional clauses and modifying existing ones in the opening paragraph regarding the powers conferred under various sections of the Union Territory GST Act.
In the Table, the tax rate for construction of affordable residential apartments is set at 0.75% for projects commencing on or after 1st April 2019.
The tax rate for construction of residential apartments other than affordable ones is set at 3.75% for similar projects.
The notification specifies that tax must be paid in cash by debiting the electronic cash ledger.
It introduces conditions for input tax credit eligibility and obligations for landowner-promoters and developer-promoters.
The deadline for exercising the option to pay tax at specified rates is set for 10th May 2019.

754.summary: Notification No. 04/2019, issued on 29th March 2019, amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates, effective from 1st April 2019. The amendment broadens the legal references in the opening paragraph to include additional sections of the UTGST and CGST Acts. It introduces new entries 41A and 41B, which detail the tax implications for development rights and long-term leases for residential apartments. Specifically, developers will now be liable to pay tax on un-booked apartments at a reverse charge basis, with caps of 0.5% for affordable apartments and 2.5% for others. Furthermore, new paragraphs define how the value of services related to development rights and un-booked apartments will be calculated. The notification also clarifies key terms such as 'apartment', 'promoter', and 'project', ensuring that all stakeholders in the real estate sector understand their obligations and the definitions that apply to their transactions. This amendment is particularly relevant for promoters and developers involved in residential projects.
key_points: This notification is issued on 29th March 2019 and comes into effect from 1st April 2019.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The opening paragraph now references sub-sections 3 and 4 of section 7, and clause (iv) and clause (xxvii) of section 21 of the UTGST Act, along with section 15 and section 148 of the CGST Act.
New entries 41A and 41B are added to the Table, concerning the tax treatment of development rights and long-term leases for residential apartments.

Entry 41A specifies that tax on development rights for residential apartments is payable on a reverse charge basis for un-booked apartments at rates not exceeding 0.5% for affordable apartments and 2.5% for others.
Entry 41B outlines similar provisions for upfront amounts related to long-term leases, also subject to reverse charge for un-booked apartments.
Paragraphs 1A and 1B are added, defining the value of services related to development rights and un-booked apartments.
Definitions for terms like 'apartment', 'affordable residential apartment', 'promoter', and 'project' are clarified in the new provisions.

755.summary: Notification No. 05/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 29th March 2019, amends the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. It introduces new taxable services related to real estate. Specifically, it adds two new entries in the tax table: Entry 5B covers services involving the transfer of development rights or Floor Space Index (FSI) for construction projects by promoters, while Entry 5C addresses long-term leases of land (30 years or more) for construction by promoters, which can involve upfront payments or periodic rent. Additionally, the notification provides definitions for several terms relevant to real estate, such as 'apartment', 'promoter', 'project', and 'floor space index (FSI)'. These changes will take effect from 1st April 2019, impacting those involved in real estate development and leasing in Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 05/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 29th March 2019.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries are added to the Table after serial number 5A: 5B for transfer of development rights or Floor Space Index (FSI) and 5C for long-term lease of land (30 years or more).
Entry 5B specifies that services supplied by any person for transfer of development rights or FSI for construction by a promoter are taxable.
Entry 5C specifies that long-term leases of land for construction by a promoter are taxable against consideration in the form of upfront amounts or periodic rent.
New definitions are added for terms such as 'apartment', 'promoter', 'project', 'Real Estate Project (REP)', 'Residential Real Estate Project (RREP)', and 'floor space index (FSI)'.
This notification comes into effect from 1st April 2019.

756.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 06/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 29th March 2019, which will come into effect from 1st April 2019. This notification specifies that promoters who receive development rights or Floor Space Index (FSI) for construction projects after this date will be liable to pay Union Territory tax. The tax liability will arise either upon the issuance of a completion certificate for the project or when the project is first occupied, whichever occurs first. Additionally, promoters who obtain long-term leases for land intended for residential apartments after 1st April 2019 will also incur this tax liability. The consideration for which the tax is applicable includes payments made for construction services, monetary payments for FSI, and any upfront amounts paid for land leases. Furthermore, the tax on these services must be paid under the reverse charge mechanism as outlined in Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate). This change primarily affects real estate promoters and developers involved in residential projects.
key_points: Notification No. 06/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 29th March 2019.
Effective date for the notification is 1st April 2019.
Promoters receiving development rights or Floor Space Index (FSI) after 1st April 2019 are liable for Union Territory tax.
Tax liability arises on the date of issuance of completion certificate or on first occupation, whichever is earlier.
Promoters receiving long-term lease of land for residential apartments after 1st April 2019 are also liable for Union Territory tax.
Consideration for tax includes construction services, monetary payments for FSI, and upfront amounts for land leases.
Tax on services is to be paid under reverse charge basis as per Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate).

757.summary: Notification No. 07/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 29th March 2019, introduces a requirement for registered persons to pay tax on a reverse charge basis for certain supplies received from unregistered suppliers. This specifically applies to goods and services that represent shortfalls in the minimum purchase values mandated for promoters involved in construction projects. Notably, this includes cement and capital goods supplied to promoters. The notification references the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017, ensuring consistency in the treatment of these supplies. The term 'promoter' is defined in accordance with the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016, providing clarity on who is affected by this notification. Importantly, this new requirement will take effect from 1st April 2019, meaning that registered persons must be prepared to comply with these changes starting on that date.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 29th March 2019.
This notification specifies that registered persons must pay tax on reverse charge basis for certain supplies from unregistered suppliers.
The supplies affected include goods and services constituting shortfalls in minimum purchase values required by promoters for construction projects.
Cement and capital goods supplied to promoters for construction projects are specifically mentioned.
The notification refers to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The definition of 'promoter' aligns with the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016.
This notification comes into effect from 1st April 2019.

758.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 08/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which introduces a new entry, 452Q, in Schedule III of the Union Territory Tax (Rate) notification. This entry pertains to the supply of goods, excluding capital goods and cement, by unregistered persons to promoters for construction projects. The new provision will take effect from April 1, 2019. The term 'promoter' is defined according to the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016, and includes both Real Estate Projects (REP) and Residential Real Estate Projects (RREP). Notably, an RREP is characterized by having commercial apartments that do not exceed 15% of the total carpet area. This amendment is part of the ongoing updates to the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework and modifies the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on June 28, 2017. Businesses involved in real estate construction should take note of these changes, as they will impact how transactions are treated under the GST framework.
key_points: Inserts a new entry 452Q in Schedule III of the Union Territory Tax (Rate) notification.
Entry 452Q applies to the supply of goods (excluding capital goods and cement) by an unregistered person to a promoter for construction projects.
The new provision is effective from April 1, 2019.
The term 'promoter' is defined as per the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016.
The definition of 'project' includes Real Estate Projects (REP) and Residential Real Estate Projects (RREP).
RREP is defined as a REP where commercial apartments do not exceed 15% of the total carpet area.
This amendment is made under the powers of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.

759.summary: Notification No. 09/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 29th March 2019, introduces important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 02/2019. Specifically, it adds a new clause (8) to the Table, which requires registered persons who choose to pay tax under this notification to debit their electronic credit ledger for the input tax credit related to stock and capital goods. This means that they must pay an amount equivalent to the input tax credit on their inputs held in stock and capital goods. After making this payment, any remaining input tax credit in their electronic ledger will lapse. Additionally, the notification clarifies that the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017 will also apply to those paying tax under this notification, as stated in the newly added clause (iii) in the Explanation. These changes will take effect from 1st April 2019, impacting registered persons in the Union Territories who are subject to these tax regulations.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 02/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 7th March 2019.
A new clause (8) is added to the Table, stating that registered persons opting to pay tax must debit their electronic credit ledger for input tax credit on stock and capital goods.
The new clause specifies that the debit amount will be equivalent to the input tax credit on inputs held in stock and capital goods.
After this debit, any remaining input tax credit in the electronic ledger will lapse.
An additional clause (iii) is added to the Explanation in paragraph 3, stating that the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017 will apply to those paying tax under this notification.
This notification comes into effect on 1st April 2019.

760.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 10/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 10th May 2019, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, this new notification changes the due date for certain tax-related entries in the Table against serial number 3. The previous date of '10th' has been updated to '20th' in column (5) of the Table. Additionally, in Annexure IV of the same earlier notification, the date '10th' is also replaced with '20th' at both instances. This change affects businesses operating in the Union Territories, as they will need to adjust their compliance dates accordingly. The amendments are made under the provisions of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, ensuring that the tax framework remains relevant and effective for stakeholders.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 10th May 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In the Table of Notification No. 11/2017, against serial number 3, the entries in column (5) are changed from '10th' to '20th'.
In Annexure IV of Notification No. 11/2017, the figures '10th' are also replaced with '20th' at both occurrences.

761.summary: Notification No. 11/2019, issued on June 29, 2019, introduces a significant change for retail outlets located in the departure areas of international airports. These outlets are now recognized as eligible to claim refunds on the union territory tax paid for goods supplied to outgoing international tourists. An 'outgoing international tourist' is defined as a person who is not a resident of India and stays in the country for no more than six months for legitimate non-immigrant purposes. This refund process is governed by the conditions specified in rule 95A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. The notification will take effect from July 1, 2019, allowing these retail outlets to start claiming refunds for applicable taxes paid on their sales to eligible tourists. This change aims to facilitate tax-free shopping for international travelers, enhancing their experience while promoting tourism in India.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2019 issued on 29th June 2019.
This notification specifies retail outlets in the departure area of international airports as eligible for tax refunds.
Refunds apply to union territory tax paid on goods supplied to outgoing international tourists.
The definition of 'outgoing international tourist' includes non-residents staying in India for up to six months.
The refund is subject to conditions outlined in rule 95A of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.
The notification comes into effect from 1st July 2019.

762.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 12/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 31st July 2019, which introduces several amendments to the existing tax rates. Effective from 1st August 2019, new entries have been added to Schedule I, specifically at a 2.5% tax rate for 'Charger or charging station for Electrically operated vehicles' and 'Electrically operated vehicles', which includes two and three-wheeled electric vehicles as well as E-bicycles. Additionally, the definition of 'Electrically operated vehicles' has been clarified to encompass vehicles powered solely by electrical energy. Furthermore, entry 206 in Schedule II at a 6% tax rate has been removed, and in Schedule III at a 9% tax rate, the entry for inductors has been modified to exclude chargers or charging stations for electric vehicles. These changes aim to support the growing electric vehicle market and streamline the tax structure related to these products.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st July 2019.
New entries added to Schedule I at 2.5%: '234B 8504 Charger or charging station for Electrically operated vehicles'.
New entry added to Schedule I at 2.5%: '242A 87 Electrically operated vehicles, including two and three wheeled electric vehicles'.
Definition of 'Electrically operated vehicles' includes vehicles run solely on electrical energy and E-bicycles.
Entry 206 in Schedule II at 6% has been omitted.
In Schedule III at 9%, entry 375 now excludes 'Charger or charging station for Electrically operated vehicles'.
The changes come into effect on 1st August 2019.
This notification amends the principal notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

763.summary: Notification No. 13/2019, issued on 31st July 2019, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it adds a new clause (aa) to the existing Table against serial number 22, which states that electrically operated vehicles designed to carry more than twelve passengers will be subject to Union Territory Tax. This definition clarifies that such vehicles operate solely on electrical energy sourced from external means or batteries. The change is significant for local authorities that may be involved in the procurement or use of these vehicles, as it directly impacts the tax treatment of these assets. The amendment will take effect from 1st August 2019, meaning that any transactions involving these vehicles after this date will need to comply with the new tax provisions. This update reflects the government's ongoing efforts to promote environmentally friendly transportation options while ensuring appropriate tax regulations are in place.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st July 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It inserts a new clause (aa) in the Table against serial number 22.
The new clause (aa) specifies that an electrically operated vehicle meant to carry more than twelve passengers is taxable.
An 'electrically operated vehicle' is defined as one that runs solely on electrical energy from an external source or batteries.
This amendment comes into effect from 1st August 2019.

764.summary: Notification No. 14/2019, issued on 30th September 2019, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Notably, it omits S. No. 33A and its related entries from Schedule I, which previously listed certain goods at a 2.5% tax rate. Additionally, a new item, 'Marine Fuel 0.5% (FO)', has been added to S. No. 164 in the same schedule. The entry for S. No. 224 has been replaced with a more specific classification, while a new entry for 'Wet grinder consisting of stone as grinder' has been introduced after S. No. 234B. Furthermore, S. Nos. 235 to 242 have been removed entirely from Schedule I. These changes will affect businesses dealing with the specified goods, as they will need to adjust their tax calculations accordingly. The amendments will take effect from 1st October 2019, meaning businesses should be prepared to implement these changes immediately.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Omission of S. No. 33A and its related entries in Schedule I - 2.5%.
Insertion of 'Marine Fuel 0.5% (FO)' after item ii against S. No. 164 in Schedule I - 2.5%.
Substitution of entry against S. No. 224 in Schedule I - 2.5% with '63 [other than 6305 32 00, 6305 33 00, 6309]'.
New entry '234C 8509 Wet grinder consisting of stone as grinder' added after S. No. 234B in Schedule I - 2.5%.
Omission of S. Nos. 235 to 242 and their related entries in Schedule I - 2.5%.
This notification comes into force on 1st October 2019.

765.summary: Notification No. 15/2019, issued on 30th September 2019, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it adds a new entry for 'Tamarind dried' under serial number 57A, and another for 'Plates and cups made up of all kinds of leaves/flowers/bark' under serial number 114C. These additions expand the list of taxable goods under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework. The changes are set to take effect from 1st October 2019. This means that businesses dealing with these specific items will need to be aware of the new tax implications starting on that date. The amendments were made following recommendations from the GST Council, emphasizing the government's commitment to updating tax regulations in response to market needs.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It inserts a new entry for 'Tamarind dried' under serial number 57A in the Schedule.
It also adds a new entry for 'Plates and cups made up of all kinds of leaves/flowers/bark' under serial number 114C.
The changes will come into effect on 1st October 2019.

766.summary: Notification No. 16/2019, issued on 30th September 2019, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 3/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. A significant change is the addition of a new item (6) in the TABLE against S. No. 1, which pertains to petroleum operations or coal bed methane operations conducted under specific contracts as per the Hydrocarbon Exploration Licensing Policy (HELP) or Open Acreage Licensing Policy (OALP). Additionally, a new proviso has been added to Condition No. 1 in the ANNEXURE, allowing recipients of non-serviceable goods to opt for a tax rate of 9% on the transaction value, provided they present a certificate from the Directorate General of Hydro Carbons confirming the goods are non-serviceable and have been mutilated before disposal. This change is particularly relevant for businesses involved in petroleum and coal bed methane operations, as well as those dealing with non-serviceable goods. The amendments take effect from 1st October 2019.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2019.
Amends Notification No. 3/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts a new item (6) in the TABLE against S. No. 1 for petroleum operations or coal bed methane operations under specified contracts.
Adds a new proviso in the ANNEXURE against Condition No. 1, clause (e) regarding tax payment on non-serviceable goods.
The new tax rate for non-serviceable goods is set at 9% on transaction value.
The recipient must provide a certificate from the Directorate General of Hydro Carbons to qualify for the 9% tax rate.
This notification comes into effect on 1st October 2019.

767.summary: Notification No. 17/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 26/2018. Notably, the term 'gold' has been broadened to include 'gold, silver or platinum', which expands the scope of the notification to cover more precious metals. Additionally, the reference from 'heading 7108' has been updated to 'Chapter 71', aligning it with a broader classification. Furthermore, the definition of 'Chapter' in the Explanation has been revised to clarify that it refers to the headings specified in the First Schedule of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. These changes are effective from 1st October 2019. Businesses dealing with these metals should take note of these amendments as they may impact their tax obligations under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework.
key_points: Notification No. 17/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 26/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 31st December 2018.
The term 'gold' is replaced with 'gold, silver or platinum' throughout the notification.
The phrase 'heading 7108' is changed to 'Chapter 71' in the opening paragraph.
Clause (d) in the Explanation is substituted to define 'Chapter' as per the First Schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
The amendments take effect from 1st October 2019.

768.summary: Notification No. 18/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 02/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 7th March 2019. Specifically, it adds a new entry for 'Aerated Water' under the HSN code 2202 10 10 in the Annexure of the previous notification. This means that from 1st October 2019, 'Aerated Water' will be recognized under this specific HSN code for Union Territory tax purposes. This change is significant for businesses dealing with aerated water, as they will need to ensure that their products are classified correctly under this new entry. The amendment reflects the government's ongoing efforts to streamline tax classifications and ensure clarity in the taxation of goods. Businesses should review their product listings to comply with this new classification and prepare for the implementation date.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 02/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 7th March 2019.
A new entry, Sl. No. 2A, is added to the Annexure of the earlier notification.
The new entry is for 'Aerated Water' under the HSN code 2202 10 10.
The changes will come into effect on 1st October 2019.

769.summary: Notification No. 19/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) introduces an exemption from Union Territory tax for all goods supplied to the Food and Agricultural Organisation of the United Nations (FAO). This exemption specifically applies to goods intended for two projects: 'Strengthening Capacities for Nutrition-sensitive Agriculture and Food systems' and 'Green Ag: Transforming Indian Agriculture for Global Environment benefits.' To qualify for this exemption, a certification is required from an officer at least at the Deputy Secretary level in the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, confirming both the quantity and description of the goods and their intended use for the specified projects. This notification is effective from 1st October 2019, meaning that any relevant supplies made on or after this date will benefit from the tax exemption. This change is significant for suppliers involved in these projects, as it reduces their tax burden and supports the initiatives aimed at improving agriculture and food systems in India.
key_points: Exempts all goods supplied to the Food and Agricultural Organisation of the United Nations (FAO) from Union Territory tax.

The exemption applies to goods for specific projects listed in the Annexure: (1) Strengthening Capacities for Nutrition-sensitive Agriculture and Food systems, (2) Green Ag: Transforming Indian Agriculture for Global Environment benefits.
The exemption is subject to certification by an officer not below the rank of Deputy Secretary in the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
The notification comes into force on 1st October 2019.

770.summary: Notification No. 20/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017. The key changes include the introduction of a 2.5% tax rate for hotel accommodations priced between ₹1,000 and ₹7,500 per day, as well as for various restaurant and outdoor catering services. Definitions for 'restaurant service' and 'outdoor catering' have been clarified, ensuring that businesses understand the scope of these services. Additionally, new job work services related to diamonds and bus body building have been included, with tax rates set at 0.75% and 6%, respectively. The term 'specified premises' is now defined, referring to establishments offering hotel accommodations with a declared tariff exceeding ₹7,500 per day. These amendments will take effect from 1st October 2019, impacting businesses in the hospitality and catering sectors, as they will need to adjust their tax calculations and service definitions accordingly.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 20/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2019.
It amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes in the table include new entries for hotel accommodation valued between ₹1,000 and ₹7,500 per day, and various restaurant and catering services, all attracting a tax rate of 2.5%.
The definition of 'restaurant service' and 'outdoor catering' has been clarified and expanded.
New entries for job work services related to diamonds and bus body building have been added with specific tax rates of 0.75% and 6%, respectively.
The term 'specified premises' is defined as those providing hotel accommodation with a declared tariff above ₹7,500 per day.
The notification comes into effect from 1st October 2019.

771.summary: Notification No. 21/2019 amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates, effective from 1st October 2019. One significant change is in the entry against serial number 7, which now specifies that the threshold for exemption from registration under the Central GST Act is based on eligibility rather than a fixed amount. Additionally, new entries have been introduced for services related to the FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup 2020, specifically under serial numbers 9AA and 82A, which include services provided by FIFA and the right to admission to the events. The entry at serial number 14 has been updated to clarify that it includes amounts 'or equal to' a specified figure. Furthermore, the year '2019' has been updated to '2020' for entries 19A and 19B. Lastly, new provisions for services related to the storage and warehousing of various agricultural products have been added under serial number 24B. These changes will affect businesses providing or receiving these specific services in the Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes will take effect from 1st October 2019.
The entry against serial number 7 is modified to specify eligibility for exemption from registration under the Central GST Act.
New entries for services related to FIFA U-17 Women's World Cup 2020 are added under serial numbers 9AA and 82A.
The entry against serial number 14 is updated to include 'or equal to' in the relevant column.
The year '2019' is changed to '2020' in entries against serial numbers 19A and 19B.
New services related to storage and warehousing of various agricultural products are added under serial number 24B.

772.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 22/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 30th September 2019, which amends the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The amendment replaces serial number 9 in the Table to clarify that services provided by music composers, photographers, and artists to music companies or producers are taxable. Additionally, a new entry, serial number 9A, has been introduced for authors supplying services to publishers, which requires authors to register under the CGST Act and file a declaration to opt for forward charge tax payment. Furthermore, two new entries, serial numbers 15 and 16, have been added to cover the renting of motor vehicles to body corporates and the lending of securities under SEBI's Securities Lending Scheme. These changes will take effect from 1st October 2019, impacting authors, music professionals, and businesses involved in vehicle rentals and securities lending.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 30th September 2019.
This notification amends Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Serial number 9 in the Table is replaced to specify the supply of services by music composers, photographers, and artists to music companies or producers.
A new entry, serial number 9A, is added for services provided by authors to publishers, with specific conditions for tax payment.
The new entry 9A requires authors to register under the CGST Act and file a declaration to opt for forward charge tax payment.
Two additional entries, serial numbers 15 and 16, are added for renting of motor vehicles to body corporates and lending of securities under SEBI's Securities Lending Scheme.
This notification comes into effect on 1st October 2019.

773.summary: Notification No. 23/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2018, which was published on 25th January 2018. The key change is the addition of an explanation that clarifies that the provisions of this notification will not apply to development rights supplied on or after 1st April 2019. This means that any development rights transactions occurring after this date will not be covered under the previous tax rate provisions. The amendment is effective from 1st October 2019, which is crucial for businesses involved in real estate or development activities. They need to be aware that the tax implications for development rights have changed, and they should adjust their accounting and compliance practices accordingly. This change aims to provide clarity on the tax treatment of development rights in the Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is Notification No. 23/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019.
It amends Notification No. 4/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), dated 25th January 2018.
An explanation is added stating that the notification does not apply to development rights supplied on or after 1st April 2019.
The new provision comes into effect from 1st October 2019.

774.summary: Notification No. 24/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, brings an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 07/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 29th March 2019. Specifically, it updates the entry in column (2) against serial number 2 in the Table related to the taxation of cement. The new entry clarifies that it pertains to 'Cement falling in chapter heading 2523 in the first schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975'. This change is significant for businesses dealing with cement, as it clearly defines the category of cement that will be subject to Union Territory tax. The amendment will be effective from 1st October 2019, meaning that any transactions involving this specific category of cement after this date will be impacted by the new tax rate. Businesses should review their pricing and tax calculations accordingly to with this updated classification.
key_points: This notification is No. 24/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019.
It amends notification No. 07/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th March 2019.
The amendment substitutes the entry in column (2) against serial number 2 in the Table.
The new entry specifies 'Cement falling in chapter heading 2523 in the first schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975'.
This change takes effect from 1st October 2019.

775.summary: Notification No. 25/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 30th September 2019, clarifies that certain activities conducted by State Governments as public authorities will not be treated as a supply of goods or services under GST. Specifically, this pertains to the service of granting liquor licenses, which includes any fees charged, such as licence fees or application fees. This change is in line with the recommendations made during the 26th GST Council meeting on 10th March 2018, which stated that no GST should be levied on these fees for alcoholic liquor intended for human consumption. The legal basis for this notification includes clause (i) of section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and sub-section (2) of section 7 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. As a result, businesses and individuals involved in the liquor licensing process will not have to pay GST on these specific fees, simplifying the regulatory landscape for liquor licensing in the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 25/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 30th September 2019.
The notification states that certain activities by State Governments as public authorities are not considered a supply of goods or services.
Specifically, it addresses the service of granting liquor licenses for a fee, which includes licence fees and application fees.
This decision is based on the recommendations from the 26th GST Council meeting held on 10th March 2018.

The relevant legal provisions cited are clause (i) of section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and sub-section (2) of section 7 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification clarifies that no GST will be applicable on fees related to liquor licenses for human consumption.

776.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 26/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 22nd November 2019, which amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically adds an explanation to the existing provisions in the Table against serial number 26, column (3), item (ic). The new explanation defines 'bus body building' to include the construction of a body on the chassis of any vehicle classified under chapter 87 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This change is significant for businesses involved in the manufacturing or modification of vehicle bodies, as it clarifies the scope of what is considered bus body building for tax purposes. Companies engaged in this activity will now have a clearer understanding of their tax obligations under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring they can comply with the updated definitions and classifications.
key_points: Notification No. 26/2019-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 22nd November 2019.
Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts an explanation in the Table against serial number 26, column (3), item (ic).
The new explanation clarifies that 'bus body building' includes building of body on chassis of any vehicle under chapter 87 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.

777.summary: Notification No. 27/2019, issued on 30th December 2019, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it removes serial numbers 80AA and 171A from Schedule II, which previously listed certain goods at a 6% tax rate. Additionally, it adds new entries in Schedule III, which is applicable for goods taxed at 9%. The new entries include serial number 163B for woven and non-woven bags and sacks made from polyethylene or polypropylene, and serial number 163C for flexible intermediate bulk containers. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2020. Businesses dealing with these specific goods should take note of these amendments to ensure proper compliance with the updated tax rates.
