857.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 70/2018-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 34/2018-Central Tax dated 10th August 2018. This amendment specifically changes the tax period from 'July, 2017 to November, 2018' to 'July, 2017 to February, 2019'. Additionally, the due date for compliance has been extended from '31st day of December, 2018' to '31st day of March, 2019'. This change is significant for businesses that fall within this tax period, as it provides them with additional time to meet their GST obligations. The amendment was made under the authority of section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, following recommendations from the GST Council. It is important to note that this notification builds on previous amendments, including the one made by notification No. 62/2018-Central Tax on 29th November 2018. Overall, this extension aims to ease compliance for taxpayers during this period.
key_points: Amends notification No. 34/2018-Central Tax dated 10th August 2018.
Changes the period from 'July, 2017 to November, 2018' to 'July, 2017 to February, 2019'.
Extends the due date from '31st day of December, 2018' to '31st day of March, 2019'.
Issued by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs on 31st December 2018.
This amendment is made under section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The amendment follows recommendations from the GST Council.
The previous amendment was made by notification No. 62/2018-Central Tax on 29th November 2018.

858.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 71/2018-Central Tax, which amends the earlier Notification No. 43/2018-Central Tax dated 10th September 2018. This amendment specifically alters the tax period, extending it from 'July, 2017 to September, 2018' to now include 'July, 2017 to December, 2018'. Additionally, the due date for compliance has been pushed from '31st December 2018' to '31st March 2019'. This change is significant for businesses that were affected by the previous deadlines, as it provides them with additional time to meet their GST obligations. The amendment is made under the authority of section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's ongoing efforts to support taxpayers during transitional periods. Businesses should take note of these new timelines to ensure they are compliant with the updated requirements.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 43/2018-Central Tax dated 10th September 2018.
Changes the tax period from 'July, 2017 to September, 2018' to 'July, 2017 to December, 2018'.
Extends the due date from '31st day of December, 2018' to '31st day of March, 2019'.
This amendment is made under section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

859.summary: Notification No. 72/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, brings important changes to the earlier notification No. 44/2018-Central Tax from 10th September 2018. Specifically, it extends the tax period for filing returns from 'July, 2017 to November, 2018' to 'July, 2017 to February, 2019'. Additionally, the due date for submitting these returns has been pushed back from '31st December 2018' to '31st March 2019'. This amendment is made under the authority of section 37(1) and section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The previous notification had already been amended once by notification No. 63/2018-Central Tax on 29th November 2018. This change primarily affects businesses that need to file their GST returns for the specified periods, providing them with additional time to comply with the requirements.
key_points: This notification is No. 72/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018.
It amends notification No. 44/2018-Central Tax dated 10th September 2018.
The amendment changes the tax period from 'July, 2017 to November, 2018' to 'July, 2017 to February, 2019'.
The due date for filing related returns is extended from '31st December 2018' to '31st March 2019'.
The amendment is made under the powers of section 37(1) and section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The principal notification No. 44/2018 was last amended by notification No. 63/2018-Central Tax on 29th November 2018.

860.summary: Notification No. 73/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 50/2018-Central Tax, which was originally published on 13th September 2018. The key change is the addition of a new proviso that clarifies the scope of the notification. Specifically, it states that the provisions of this notification do not apply to the supply of goods or services between individuals specified under clauses (a), (b), (c), and (d) of sub-section (1) of section 51 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. This section relates to the tax collection at source (TCS) mechanism, which affects certain specified persons. The amendment was made following recommendations from the GST Council, ensuring that the rules are aligned with the operational realities of the tax system. This change is significant for businesses involved in transactions with the specified persons, as it delineates the applicability of the notification in these cases. The principal notification had previously been amended by Notification No. 61/2018 on 5th November 2018, and this latest update further refines the regulatory framework.
key_points: This notification is Notification No. 73/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018.
It amends Notification No. 50/2018-Central Tax, which was originally issued on 13th September 2018.
A new proviso is added after the second proviso in Notification No. 50/2018.

The new proviso states that the notification does not apply to the supply of goods or services between persons specified under clauses (a), (b), (c), and (d) of sub-section (1) of section 51 of the CGST Act.
Section 51 of the CGST Act pertains to the tax collection at source (TCS) mechanism.
The amendment is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The principal notification No. 50/2018 was last amended by Notification No. 61/2018-Central Tax on 5th November 2018.

861.summary: The Central Government has issued Notification No. 74/2018-Central Tax on 31st December 2018, which amends several rules under the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This notification introduces the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fourteenth Amendment) Rules, 2018, which come into effect upon publication. A significant change is the addition of sub-rule (1A) in rule 12, requiring individuals applying for registration under section 52 to specify both the State or Union territory in PART A and the principal place of business in PART B of FORM GST REG-07. Additionally, rule 45 has been amended to remove the phrase regarding the transfer of goods between job workers. New provisions in rules 46 and 49 eliminate the need for signatures on electronic invoices and bills of supply, while rule 54 clarifies that signatures are also not required for consolidated tax invoices and tickets issued electronically. Furthermore, rule 138E imposes restrictions on certain taxpayers from furnishing information in PART A of FORM GST EWB-01 if they have not filed returns for two consecutive periods. Lastly, FORM GST RFD-01 has been replaced with a new version for refund applications, impacting various categories of taxpayers including casual and non-resident taxable persons.
key_points: Notification No. 74/2018-Central Tax was issued on 31st December 2018.
The rules are called the Central Goods and Services Tax (Fourteenth Amendment) Rules, 2018.
New sub-rule (1A) in rule 12 requires applicants for registration under section 52 to specify the State or Union territory in PART A and the principal place of business in PART B of FORM GST REG-07.
In rule 45, the phrase 'or sent from one job worker to another' has been omitted from sub-rule (3).
New provisos in rules 46 and 49 state that signatures are not required for electronic invoices and bills of supply issued under the Information Technology Act, 2000.
In rule 54, new provisos clarify that signatures are not needed for consolidated tax invoices and tickets issued electronically.
Rule 138E introduces restrictions on furnishing information in PART A of FORM GST EWB-01 for certain taxpayers who have not filed returns for two consecutive periods.
FORM GST RFD-01 has been substituted with a new version for refund applications, applicable to various categories of taxpayers.

862.summary: Notification No. 75/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax from 23rd January 2018. Specifically, it adds a new proviso that waives the late fee under section 47 of the GST Act for registered persons who did not submit their FORM GSTR-1 for the period from July 2017 to September 2018 by the required deadlines. To qualify for this waiver, these individuals must file their GSTR-1 forms between 22nd December 2018 and 31st March 2019. This change is significant for those who missed the original deadlines, as it provides an opportunity to avoid late fees by complying within the specified timeframe. The amendment aims to ease the burden on taxpayers who may have faced challenges in meeting their filing obligations during that period.
key_points: Notification No. 75/2018-Central Tax was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax dated 23rd January 2018.
A new proviso is added after the existing proviso in Notification No. 4/2018.
The new proviso waives the late fee under section 47 for registered persons.
The waiver applies to those who failed to file FORM GSTR-1 for July 2017 to September 2018 by the due date.
Eligible registered persons must file FORM GSTR-1 between 22nd December 2018 and 31st March 2019 to benefit from the waiver.

863.summary: Notification No. 76/2018-Central Tax, issued on December 31, 2018, introduces a waiver on late fees for registered persons who failed to file their GSTR-3B returns from July 2017 onwards. The late fee is limited to Rs. 25 per day for those who owe central tax, while those with a nil tax liability will only incur a late fee of Rs. 10 per day. This waiver is applicable for returns submitted between December 22, 2018, and March 31, 2019. This notification supersedes earlier notifications, specifically No. 28/2017, 50/2017, and 64/2017, which addressed similar late fee issues. The provisions are enacted under section 47 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change is significant for businesses that have been delayed in filing their returns, as it reduces the financial burden of late fees during the specified period.
key_points: Waives late fee for GSTR-3B returns from July 2017 onwards.
Late fee is capped at Rs. 25 per day for delays beyond the due date.
If the central tax payable is nil, the late fee is capped at Rs. 10 per day.
The waiver applies to returns filed between December 22, 2018, and March 31, 2019.
This notification supersedes earlier notifications No. 28/2017, 50/2017, and 64/2017.
The waiver is applicable under section 47 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was issued on December 31, 2018.

864.summary: Notification No. 77/2018-Central Tax, issued on December 31, 2018, introduces an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax. Specifically, it adds a new proviso that waives the late fee for registered persons who did not submit their GSTR-4 returns for the quarters from July 2017 to September 2018 by the due date. To benefit from this waiver, these individuals must file their GSTR-4 returns within the period from December 22, 2018, to March 31, 2019. This change is significant for those who missed the deadline, as it alleviates the financial burden of late fees, encouraging compliance with GST filing requirements. The amendment aims to provide relief to taxpayers during this transitional phase of GST implementation, ensuring that they can rectify their filing without incurring additional costs. Overall, this notification is a positive step for businesses that faced challenges in meeting their filing obligations during the specified period.
key_points: This notification is No. 77/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018.
It amends notification No. 73/2017-Central Tax, dated 29th December 2017.
A new proviso is added after the first proviso in the earlier notification.
The new proviso waives the late fee under section 47 for registered persons.
This waiver applies to those who failed to file FORM GSTR-4 for the quarters from July 2017 to September 2018.
To qualify for the waiver, the GSTR-4 return must be filed between 22nd December 2018 and 31st March 2019.

865.summary: Notification No. 78/2018-Central Tax, issued on December 31, 2018, extends the deadline for filing FORM GST ITC-04, which is used for reporting goods dispatched to or received from job workers. The new deadline is set for March 31, 2019, allowing businesses more time to comply with this requirement. This extension applies to transactions that occurred between July 2017 and December 2018. This notification supersedes the earlier Notification No. 59/2018-Central Tax, which was issued on October 26, 2018, thereby providing a clearer timeline for businesses. The changes are made under the authority of section 168 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and sub-rule (3) of rule 45 of the CGST Rules, 2017. This adjustment is significant for businesses that engage job workers, ensuring they can accurately report their transactions without the pressure of an earlier deadline.
key_points: Extends the deadline for filing FORM GST ITC-04.
New deadline for FORM GST ITC-04 is March 31, 2019.
Covers goods dispatched to or received from job workers.
Applicable for the period from July 2017 to December 2018.
Supersedes Notification No. 59/2018-Central Tax dated October 26, 2018.
Issued on December 31, 2018.
Cited under section 168 of the CGST Act, 2017.
Cited under sub-rule (3) of rule 45 of the CGST Rules, 2017.

866.summary: Notification No. 79/2018-Central Tax, issued on December 31, 2018, introduces an important amendment to Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax from June 19, 2017. This amendment adds a new provision in paragraph 3, which empowers specified central tax officers to exercise their authority under sections 73, 74, 75, and 76 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act. These officers can now operate across the entire territorial jurisdiction of their corresponding central tax officer, which means they have broader authority to handle cases assigned to them. The specific cases that these officers will manage will be determined by the Board. This change is significant as it enhances the operational capabilities of central tax officers, allowing for more efficient handling of GST-related matters within their jurisdictions. Business owners and stakeholders should be aware of these changes as they may impact how GST compliance and enforcement are conducted in their areas.
key_points: Notification No. 79/2018-Central Tax was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.
The amendment inserts a new provision in paragraph 3 of Notification No. 2/2017.
The new provision allows specified central tax officers to exercise powers under sections 73, 74, 75, and 76 of the CGST Act.
These powers can be exercised throughout the territorial jurisdiction of the corresponding central tax officer.
The assignment of cases to these officers will be determined by the Board.
The changes affect the operational authority of central tax officers in their respective jurisdictions.

867.summary: Notification No. 1/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on January 25, 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017. Key changes include the addition of new sub-items that specify tax exemptions for civil structures involved in slum redevelopment and housing for Economically Weaker Sections under the Housing for All (Urban) Mission. The language in item (v) is modified to replace 'excluding' with 'including', which broadens the scope of applicable projects. Additionally, the rules governing composite supplies of works contracts provided by subcontractors to government entities have been refined, ensuring clarity on input tax credit eligibility. The notification also updates provisions for time charter services and introduces new categories for housekeeping services and fumigation in warehouses. Lastly, it clarifies how to calculate the total amount for services that involve land transfer, ensuring that the value of land is appropriately accounted for in the overall service charge. These changes primarily affect contractors and service providers involved in government projects and housing initiatives.
key_points: This notification is issued on January 25, 2018, under Notification No. 1/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate).
It amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
New sub-items are added for civil structures related to slum redevelopment and Economically Weaker Section (EWS) housing under the Housing for All (Urban) Mission.
The term 'excluding' is replaced with 'including' in item (v) for certain housing projects.
Changes are made to the composite supply of works contracts provided by subcontractors to government entities.
The entry for time charter of vessels for transport of goods is updated to include specific conditions regarding input tax credit.
New provisions are added for services related to housekeeping and fumigation in warehouses.
The definition of total amount for certain services involving land transfer is clarified.

868.summary: The Notification No. 1/2018-Central Tax (Rate), issued on January 25, 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Notably, it updates sub-item (c) under serial number 3 to now include civil structures related to slum redevelopment under the Housing for All (Urban) Mission. Additionally, two new sub-items (da) and (db) have been added to cover Economically Weaker Section (EWS) houses and those under the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme. The language in item (v) has been modified from 'excluding' to 'including', broadening the scope of applicable services. Furthermore, item (ii) under serial number 10 has been revised to clarify the tax treatment of time charters for transporting goods. The amendments also affect serial number 16, which now includes services related to land leases by government entities. Lastly, item (iii) under serial number 34 has been updated to encompass admission services to various entertainment events, reflecting a broader range of taxable activities.
key_points: Notification No. 1/2018-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on January 25, 2018.
Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
Substitutes sub-item (c) under serial number 3 to include civil structures for slum redevelopment under the Housing for All (Urban) Mission.
Inserts new sub-items (da) and (db) under serial number 3 for EWS houses and houses under the Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme.
Changes the wording from 'excluding' to 'including' in item (v) under serial number 3.
Substitutes item (ii) under serial number 10 to specify time charter of vessels for transport of goods.
Updates serial number 16 to include services by government entities related to land lease.
Substitutes item (iii) under serial number 34 to include admission services to entertainment events.

869.summary: The Notification No. 02/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the existing tax rates under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act. Notably, it adds 'or a Government Entity' to the definition of a Governmental Authority in entry 3, expanding the scope of eligible entities. A new entry, 3A, has been created for composite supplies to government entities, where the value of goods must not exceed 25%. Additionally, the notification includes a new item for online educational journals supplied to educational institutions, while excluding certain educational services. The duration for a specific service has been extended from one year to three years in entry 17. New entries for transportation services by aircraft and vessels have been added, which will be applicable until September 30, 2018. Furthermore, a new provision for motor vehicles used for transporting students and staff has been included. Life insurance services for Coast Guard personnel are now covered under a new entry, and the monetary threshold in entry 37 has been increased significantly. These changes will affect various sectors, including education, transportation, and insurance, by clarifying tax obligations and expanding the range of services covered.
key_points: Inserts 'or a Government Entity' after 'a Governmental Authority' in entry against serial number 3.
Adds new entry 3A for composite supply of goods and services to government entities with a value of goods not exceeding 25%.
Inserts new item (ba) for online educational journals supplied to educational institutions, excluding certain types of education.
Changes the duration from 'one year' to 'three years' in entry against serial number 17.
Inserts new entries 20A and 20B for transportation services by aircraft and vessel, effective until September 30, 2018.
Adds new item (c) for motor vehicles used for transporting students and staff to educational institutions.
Inserts new entry 30A for life insurance services provided by the Naval Group Insurance Fund to Coast Guard personnel.
Increases the threshold from 'fifty thousand' to 'two lakhs' in entry against serial number 37.

870.summary: The Notification No. 02/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) by introducing several key changes. Notably, it adds 'or a Government Entity' to entry 3, expanding the definition of entities eligible for certain tax exemptions. A new entry, 3A, is created for composite supplies where goods constitute no more than 25% of the total value, specifically for services provided to government entities, which will be exempt from tax. The duration for certain exemptions in entry 16 has been extended from one year to three years. Additionally, new entries 19A and 19B are introduced for transportation services by aircraft and vessels, exempt until September 30, 2018. Entry 22 now includes provisions for motor vehicles used for transporting students and faculty to educational institutions. Furthermore, life insurance services for Coast Guard personnel are now exempt under new entry 29A, and the threshold for certain exemptions in entry 36 has been raised from fifty thousand to two lakhs. Lastly, a new entry 65A has been added for services under the Right to Information Act, also exempt from tax. These changes affect various sectors, including education, transportation, and government services.
key_points: Inserts 'or a Government Entity' after 'a Governmental Authority' in entry 3 of the table.
Adds new entry 3A for composite supply of goods and services with a value of goods not exceeding 25% provided to government entities, exempt from tax.
Changes the duration from 'one year' to 'three years' in entry 16 of the table.
Introduces new entries 19A and 19B for transportation services by aircraft and vessel, exempt until September 30, 2018.
Inserts item (c) in entry 22 for motor vehicles used for transporting students and faculty to educational institutions.
Adds new entry 29A for life insurance services provided by the Naval Group Insurance Fund to Coast Guard personnel, exempt from tax.
Increases the threshold from 'fifty thousand' to 'two lakhs' in entry 36.
Inserts new entry 65A for services providing information under the Right to Information Act, exempt from tax.

871.summary: Notification No. 03/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 25th January 2018, introduces important amendments to the earlier notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. A new entry, serial number 6A, has been added to the Table, which addresses services provided by the Central Government, State Government, Union Territory, or local authorities related to the renting of immovable property to individuals registered under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This means that these government bodies can now charge Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) on such rental services. Additionally, the notification clarifies the definition of 'insurance agent' by inserting a new clause in the Explanation, aligning it with the Insurance Act, 1938. This amendment is significant for businesses and individuals engaged in renting property from government entities, as it formalizes the tax implications of such transactions. The changes reflect the government's ongoing efforts to clarify and streamline GST regulations.
key_points: This notification is No. 03/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 25th January 2018.
It amends notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry, serial number 6A, is added to the Table, concerning services by government bodies for renting immovable property.
The new entry specifies that services supplied by the Central Government, State Government, Union Territory, or local authority for renting property to registered persons are included.
The new entry is applicable to any person registered under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
An additional clause (f) is inserted in the Explanation, defining 'insurance agent' as per the Insurance Act, 1938.
The principal notification was last amended by notification No. 34/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017.

872.summary: Notification No. 03/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, introduces amendments to Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. A significant change is the addition of a new entry, serial number 5A, which addresses services provided by the Central Government, State Government, Union Territory, or local authority related to the renting of immovable property. This entry clarifies that such services are applicable to any person registered under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Furthermore, the notification includes a new clause (f) in the Explanation, which defines 'insurance agent' in accordance with the Insurance Act, 1938. These changes are important for businesses and individuals engaged in renting properties from government entities, as well as those involved in the insurance sector, ensuring clarity in the definitions and applicability of GST on these services.
key_points: This notification is dated January 25, 2018.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) issued on June 28, 2017.
A new entry, serial number 5A, is added to the Table, concerning services by government entities for renting immovable property.
The new entry specifies that services supplied by the Central Government, State Government, Union Territory, or local authority for renting immovable property to registered persons are covered.
The new entry applies to any person registered under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
An additional clause (f) is inserted in the Explanation, defining 'insurance agent' as per the Insurance Act, 1938.

873.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 4/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on January 25, 2018, which outlines specific tax liabilities for registered persons involved in the construction sector. This notification particularly affects those who supply development rights to developers, builders, or construction companies. According to the new rules, these registered persons will be liable to pay integrated tax when they transfer possession of the constructed complex, building, or civil structure. This transfer can occur through a conveyance deed or similar documents, such as an allotment letter. The consideration for these transactions can be either in the form of construction services or development rights. The notification is based on the powers granted under section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change clarifies the timing of tax liability for these transactions, ensuring that all parties involved understand when the tax obligation arises.
key_points: Notification No. 4/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 25th January 2018.
Registered persons supplying development rights to developers or builders are affected.
Liability to pay integrated tax arises when possession of the constructed complex is transferred.
Consideration can be in the form of construction services or development rights.
The relevant sections are section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

874.summary: Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, introduces specific provisions regarding the liability to pay central tax for certain registered persons. It affects those who supply development rights to developers, builders, or construction companies, as well as those providing construction services in exchange for development rights. The key change is that the liability to pay central tax will now arise at the moment when the developer or builder transfers possession or rights in the constructed complex, building, or civil structure. This transfer must be documented through a conveyance deed or similar instrument, like an allotment letter. This notification clarifies the timing of tax liability for these transactions, ensuring that both suppliers of development rights and construction services understand when their tax obligations begin. This is particularly relevant for businesses involved in real estate development and construction services.
key_points: Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax issued on 25th January 2018.
This notification pertains to registered persons supplying development rights or construction services.
Liability to pay central tax arises when possession or rights in the constructed complex are transferred.
The transfer must occur through a conveyance deed or similar instrument, such as an allotment letter.
This applies to transactions involving developers, builders, and construction companies.

875.summary: Notification No. 5/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on January 25, 2018, introduces an exemption for the inter-State supply of services related to the granting of licenses or leases for exploring or mining petroleum crude or natural gas. This exemption specifically applies to the payments made to the Central Government, particularly concerning the share of profit petroleum as outlined in the contracts established by the government. By leveraging the authority granted under section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, the government aims to facilitate the exploration and mining activities in the petroleum sector by reducing the tax burden on these transactions. This change is significant for businesses involved in the petroleum industry, as it allows them to operate with lower costs associated with inter-State service supplies. Overall, this notification reflects the government's commitment to promoting investment and operational efficiency in the energy sector.
key_points: Exempts inter-State supply of services for granting licenses or leases to explore or mine petroleum crude or natural gas from integrated tax.
This exemption applies specifically to the consideration paid to the Central Government.
The exemption is related to the Central Government's share of profit petroleum as defined in relevant contracts.
The notification is issued under the powers of section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

876.summary: Notification No. 5/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, introduces an exemption for certain services related to petroleum and natural gas. Specifically, it exempts the intra-State supply of services that involve granting licenses or leases for exploring or mining petroleum crude or natural gas from central tax. This exemption applies to the consideration paid to the Central Government, which is defined as the Central Government’s share of profit petroleum as per the relevant contract. The decision to grant this exemption was made in the public interest and follows recommendations from the GST Council. This means that businesses involved in these activities will not have to pay central tax on the specified consideration, potentially reducing their overall tax burden. This change is significant for companies operating in the petroleum and natural gas sector, as it directly impacts their financial obligations under the GST framework.
key_points: Exemption applies to intra-State supply of services.
Services covered include grant of license or lease to explore or mine petroleum crude or natural gas.
Exemption is from central tax on consideration paid to the Central Government.
Consideration refers to the Central Government’s share of profit petroleum as defined in the relevant contract.
Notification issued on 25th January 2018.
This exemption is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The exemption is granted under sub-section (1) of section 11 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

877.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 6/2018-Central Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 25th January 2018. This corrigendum, dated 1st February 2018, corrects specific wording in the original notification. Specifically, the term 'substituted' in line 3 on page 7 has been changed to 'inserted', and the same change applies to line 6 on the same page. These corrections clarify the intended meaning of the notification, ensuring that the provisions are accurately represented. This is important for businesses and tax professionals who rely on the precise language of tax notifications for compliance and reporting purposes. The changes do not introduce new provisions but rather correct the terminology used in the original document, which may affect how the rules are interpreted and applied.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 6/2018-Central Tax (Rate) dated 25th January 2018.
The corrigendum was issued on 1st February 2018.
In the original notification, the term 'substituted' in line 3 on page 7 is corrected to 'inserted'.
Similarly, 'substituted' in line 6 on page 7 is also corrected to 'inserted'.

878.summary: Notification No. 6/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 25th January 2018, introduces an exemption from integrated tax for certain imported services. This exemption specifically applies to services that fall under sub-item (c) of item 5 in Schedule II of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. It allows for the exemption to the extent of the total Customs duties that are applicable under section 3(7) of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. The focus is on royalties and license fees that are part of the transaction value, as defined in rule 10(1)(c) of the Customs Valuation Rules, 2007. This means that businesses importing these services will not have to pay integrated tax on the specified amounts, provided the appropriate Customs duties have already been paid. The notification was officially published in the Gazette of India, making it effective from the date of issuance. This change is significant for businesses involved in importing services that include royalties and license fees, as it reduces their tax burden on these transactions.
key_points: Exempts integrated tax on certain imported services as per Notification No. 6/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 25th January 2018.
The exemption applies to services covered by sub-item (c) of item 5 of Schedule II of the CGST Act, 2017.
This exemption is applicable to the extent of the aggregate of Customs duties under section 3(7) of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
The exemption specifically relates to royalties and license fees included in the transaction value as per rule 10(1)(c) of the Customs Valuation Rules, 2007.
The notification is issued under the powers of section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The effective date of this notification is 25th January 2018.

879.summary: Notification No. 06/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. This amendment introduces several new entries and makes substitutions across various schedules. For instance, Schedule I now includes Tamarind kernel powder, Mehendi paste in cones, and Rice bran, while also modifying the entry for liquefied gases for household consumers. In Schedule II, Sugar boiled confectionery and Drinking water in 20-litre bottles have updated entries, and a new entry for Fertilizer grade phosphoric acid has been added. Schedule III has seen changes that clarify exceptions for certain products, including tamarind kernel powder and drinking water. Additionally, Schedule IV has a new entry for actionable claims related to betting, and Schedule V has omitted and substituted entries concerning semi-precious stones and imitation jewellery. Lastly, Schedule VI has been updated to include new entries for precious and synthetic stones. These changes affect manufacturers, suppliers, and consumers of the specified goods, ensuring clarity in GST rates and classifications.
key_points: Inserts new entries in Schedule I for Tamarind kernel powder (76A), Mehendi paste in cones (78A), and Rice bran (103B) at specified serial numbers.
Omitted the phrase 'to household domestic consumers or' from entry 165 in Schedule I and added new entry 165A for Liquefied gases for household consumers.
In Schedule II, substitutes entries for Sugar boiled confectionery (32AA) and Drinking water in 20-litre bottles (46B), and adds new entry for Fertilizer grade phosphoric acid (57B).
In Schedule III, adds exceptions for tamarind kernel powder and drinking water in 20-litre bottles in specific entries, and substitutes entries for groundnut sweets and hair preparations.
In Schedule IV, substitutes entry for motor vehicles for transport of ten or more persons and adds new entry for actionable claims related to betting and gambling.
In Schedule V, omits certain entries and substitutes others related to semi-precious stones and imitation jewellery.
In Schedule VI, substitutes entries for all goods and semi-precious stones, and adds new entries for precious stones and synthetic stones.

880.summary: On 1st February 2018, a corrigendum was issued to amend Notification No. 7/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 25th January 2018. The changes clarify certain terms in the notification. Specifically, the word 'substituted' has been corrected to 'inserted' in two instances on page 21, ensuring that the intended meaning is accurately conveyed. Additionally, a significant change has been made to the tax rate in Schedule III, where the rate has been increased from 9% to 18%. This adjustment affects the classification of goods or services listed under this schedule, potentially impacting the tax liability for businesses dealing with these items. Business owners should review these changes closely to understand how they may affect their tax obligations and pricing strategies moving forward.
key_points: The corrigendum was issued on 1st February 2018.
It amends Notification No. 7/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 25th January 2018.
In the corrigendum, 'substituted' is changed to 'inserted' in line 34 on page 21.
Similarly, 'substituted' is changed to 'inserted' in line 37 on page 21.
The entry in Schedule III is amended from '9%' to '18%' in line 1 on page 23.

881.summary: Notification No. 07/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on January 25, 2018, introduces several amendments to the existing tax rates under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act. New entries have been added to Schedule I for a 5% tax rate, including Tamarind kernel powder, Mehendi paste in cones, and Rice bran. Schedule II for a 12% tax rate sees substitutions for Sugar boiled confectionery and Drinking water in 20-litre bottles, along with the introduction of Fertilizer grade phosphoric acid. In Schedule III, exceptions are added for tamarind kernel powder and drinking water, while groundnut sweets are redefined. Schedule IV now excludes bio-fuel buses from the motor vehicle category and introduces a new entry for betting-related claims. Schedule V omits certain entries and modifies those for semi-precious stones and imitation jewellery. Lastly, Schedule VI updates entries for all goods and semi-precious stones, adding new categories for precious stones. These changes affect various businesses dealing with these goods and services, ensuring they are aware of the updated tax implications.
key_points: Inserts new entries in Schedule I for 5% tax rate: 76A for Tamarind kernel powder, 78A for Mehendi paste in cones, and 103B for Rice bran.

Amends Schedule II for 12% tax rate: Substitutes entries for 32AA (Sugar boiled confectionery) and 46B (Drinking water in 20 litres bottles), and adds new entry 57B for Fertilizer grade phosphoric acid.
In Schedule III for 9% tax rate, adds exceptions for tamarind kernel powder and drinking water in 20 litres bottles, and substitutes entries for groundnut sweets.
In Schedule IV for 28% tax rate, substitutes entry for motor vehicles for transport of ten or more persons, excluding bio-fuel buses, and adds new entry for actionable claims in betting.
In Schedule V for 3% tax rate, omits certain entries and substitutes others related to semi-precious stones and imitation jewellery.
In Schedule VI for 0.25% tax rate, substitutes entries for all goods and semi-precious stones, and adds new entries for precious stones.
The notification amends the principal notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017, last amended by Notification No. 43/2017 on 14th November 2017.

882.summary: The Notification No. 07/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. One significant change is the substitution of the entry for S. No. 102, which now includes a broader range of animal feeds such as shrimp, prawn, poultry, and cattle feeds. Additionally, two new entries have been added: S. No. 102A for De-oiled rice bran and S. No. 102B for Cotton seed oil cake. The code for S. No. 136A has been updated to '7117', while S. No. 137 now specifies that the products listed are 'other than ghamella'. Furthermore, the entry for item (v) under S. No. 148 has been changed to 'Vibhuti'. Lastly, a new entry for S. No. 151 has been introduced, which pertains to parts used in the manufacture of hearing aids. These changes will affect manufacturers and suppliers in the agricultural and hearing aid sectors, ensuring clarity in the classification of goods under GST.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2018-Central Tax issued on 25th January 2018.
Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Substitutes entry for S. No. 102 to include various types of animal feeds.
Inserts new entries for S. No. 102A (De-oiled rice bran) and S. No. 102B (Cotton seed oil cake).
Substitutes entry for S. No. 136A to change the code to '7117'.
Adds 'other than ghamella' to the entry for S. No. 137.
Substitutes entry for item (v) under S. No. 148 to include 'Vibhuti'.
Inserts new entry for S. No. 151 for 'Parts for manufacture of hearing aids'.

883.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 8/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 25th January 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment includes several important changes to the tax rates for specific goods. Firstly, the description for aquatic and livestock feed in S. No. 102 has been updated to include various types of feed. Additionally, new entries have been added for de-oiled rice bran and cotton seed oil cake under S. No. 102A and 102B, respectively. The entry for S. No. 136A has been changed to '7117', while S. No. 137 now specifies that the products listed are 'other than ghamella'. Furthermore, the entry for item number (v) in S. No. 148 has been replaced with 'Vibhuti'. Lastly, a new entry for parts used in the manufacture of hearing aids has been introduced as S. No. 151. These changes will affect businesses dealing with these specific goods, as they may need to adjust their tax calculations accordingly.

Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Replaces entry in S. No. 102 with a new description for aquatic and livestock feed.
Inserts new entries for S. No. 102A (De-oiled rice bran) and S. No. 102B (Cotton seed oil cake).
Updates S. No. 136A by substituting the entry with '7117'.
Adds 'other than ghamella' to the entry in S. No. 137 related to agricultural products.
Substitutes the entry for item number (v) in S. No. 148 with 'Vibhuti'.
Inserts new S. No. 151 for 'Parts for manufacture of hearing aids'.

884.summary: Notification No. 8/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, introduces an exemption from central tax for intra-state supplies of certain old and used motor vehicles. This applies specifically to vehicles classified under Chapter 87, with tax rates set at 9% for petrol and diesel vehicles of specified engine capacities and lengths, while all other old and used vehicles are taxed at 6%. The notification outlines how to calculate the margin for tax purposes, either based on depreciation or the difference between selling and purchase prices. Importantly, this exemption is not available to suppliers who have claimed input tax credits or other tax benefits on these vehicles. This change is significant for businesses dealing in used motor vehicles, as it can affect pricing and tax liabilities. Suppliers should be aware of the specific conditions and with the new provisions.
key_points: Notification No. 8/2018-Central Tax issued on 25th January 2018.
Exempts central tax on intra-state supplies of specified old and used motor vehicles.
Applicable to vehicles under Chapter 87, specifically those listed in the Table with varying tax rates.
Tax rates specified: 9% for certain petrol and diesel vehicles, and 6% for all other old and used vehicles.
Conditions for margin calculation based on depreciation or selling price versus purchase price.
Exemption does not apply if the supplier has availed input tax credit or other tax credits on the goods.

885.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 9/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on January 25, 2018, which provides an exemption from central tax on inter-state supplies of certain old and used motor vehicles. This exemption applies specifically to vehicles classified under Chapter 87, including petrol and diesel vehicles with engine capacities of 1200 cc or more and lengths of 4000 mm or more, as well as Sports Utility Vehicles (SUVs) exceeding 1500 cc. The tax rates are set at 18% for these specified vehicles and 12% for all other old and used vehicles not listed in the first three entries. The notification outlines how to calculate the margin of the supplier, either based on depreciation or the difference between selling and purchase prices. However, this exemption is not available to suppliers who have claimed input tax credits or other tax credits on these goods. This change primarily affects businesses involved in the sale of used motor vehicles, allowing them to benefit from reduced tax liabilities under specific conditions.
key_points: Notification No. 9/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 25th January 2018.
Exempts central tax on inter-state supplies of specific old and used motor vehicles.
Applicable to vehicles under Chapter 87, including petrol and diesel vehicles with specified engine capacities and lengths.
Tax rates specified: 18% for certain vehicles and 12% for all other old and used vehicles.
Conditions for determining the margin of the supplier based on depreciation or selling price.
Exemption does not apply if the supplier has availed input tax credit or other tax credits on the goods.

886.summary: Notification No. 09/2018-Central Tax, issued on January 25, 2018, brings specific amendments to Notification No. 45/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated November 14, 2017. The changes primarily affect the descriptions in the Table regarding public funded research institutions and universities. Specifically, the entry for serial number 1 now includes a detailed list of institutions, such as the Indian Institute of Technology and the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore, while excluding hospitals. Additionally, the term 'Department of Scientific and Research' has been updated to 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research' in both serial numbers 1, 2, and 4. Furthermore, a new explanation has been added, clarifying that the exemptions provided align with an earlier customs notification from July 23, 1996, and this exemption is effective from November 15, 2017. These changes are significant for institutions involved in research and education, as they clarify the scope of exemptions available under GST.
key_points: This notification is issued on January 25, 2018.
It amends Notification No. 45/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated November 14, 2017.

In the Table, against serial number 1, the entry for column (2) is changed to specify 'Public funded research institution or a University or an Indian Institute of Technology or Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore or a Regional Engineering College, other than a hospital.'
In column (4) of serial number 1, 'Department of Scientific and Research' is replaced with 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research.'
For serial numbers 2 and 4, column (4) also sees 'Department of Scientific and Research' replaced with 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research.'
An additional explanation, numbered as Explanation 2, is added, stating that the exemption aligns with Notification No. 51/96-Customs dated July 23, 1996.
The new exemption is applicable from November 15, 2017.

887.summary: Notification No. 10/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 25th January 2018, brings specific amendments to Notification No. 47/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 14th November 2017. The changes primarily affect the descriptions in the Table regarding public funded research institutions and universities. Specifically, the entry for serial number 1 now includes a detailed list of institutions, clarifying that it covers various educational and research entities, excluding hospitals. Additionally, the term 'Department of Scientific and Research' has been updated to 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research' for serial numbers 1, 2, and 4, ensuring consistency in terminology. Furthermore, the notification introduces a new Explanation 2, which clarifies that the exemptions provided are in line with an earlier customs notification from 1996, effective from 15th November 2017. These amendments are significant for institutions involved in research and education, as they clarify the scope of exemptions available under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework.
key_points: This notification is Notification No. 10/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 25th January 2018.

For serial numbers 2 and 4, in column (4), 'Department of Scientific and Research' is also replaced with 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research'.
The existing Explanation after the Table is renumbered as Explanation 1, and a new Explanation 2 is added.
Explanation 2 states that the exemption aligns with Notification No. 51/96-Customs dated 23rd July 1996, effective from 15th November 2017.

888.summary: Notification No. 10/2018-Central Tax, issued on 23rd March 2018, brings a significant change to the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it extends the deadline for a certain compliance from 31st March 2018 to 30th June 2018. This amendment is made under section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, indicating that the government found it necessary to adjust the timeline in the public interest. The earlier notification had already been amended by Notification No. 38/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017. This change is officially published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that all stakeholders are informed of the new deadline. Businesses and taxpayers who were preparing for the original deadline now have additional time until the end of June 2018 to meet their obligations. This extension may provide relief to those who needed more time to comply with the requirements set forth in the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2018-Central Tax issued on 23rd March 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment changes the deadline from '31st March 2018' to '30th June 2018'.
The amendment is made under the powers of section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The previous notification was also amended by Notification No. 38/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017.
The changes are published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Subsection (i).

889.summary: Notification No. 11/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 23rd March 2018, brings an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 32/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 13th October 2017. The key change is the extension of a deadline, moving it from 31st March 2018 to 30th June 2018. This adjustment is made under the authority of section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, indicating that the government recognizes the need for this extension in the public interest. Businesses and taxpayers who were expecting to meet obligations by the end of March now have additional time until the end of June. This change provides relief and allows for better compliance planning. It is essential for affected parties to note this new deadline to ensure they align their operations accordingly.
key_points: This notification is Notification No. 11/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 23rd March 2018.
It amends Notification No. 32/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), dated 13th October 2017.
The amendment changes the date from '31st day of March, 2018' to '30th day of June, 2018'.
This change is made under the powers conferred by section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

890.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 11/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on May 28, 2018, which introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017. Specifically, a new entry has been added to the existing table, following S. No. 6. This new entry, designated as S. No. 7, relates to 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate'. Both the supplier and recipient of this certificate are classified as 'Any registered person'. This change is significant for registered persons involved in the supply of these certificates, as it clarifies the GST treatment applicable to such transactions. The amendment is made under the authority of sub-section (3) of section 9 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring that the provisions align with the recommendations of the GST Council. This update is part of the ongoing efforts to refine and clarify GST regulations for better compliance and understanding among businesses.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2018-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on May 28, 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
A new entry has been added after S. No. 6 in the table of Notification No. 4/2017.
The new entry (S. No. 7) pertains to 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate'.
The supplier of goods for this entry is 'Any registered person'.
The recipient of supply for this entry is also 'Any registered person'.
This amendment is made under the powers conferred by sub-section (3) of section 9 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

891.summary: The recent Notification No. 12/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on May 28, 2018, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry in the tax rate table, following S. No. 6. This new entry, designated as S. No. 7, relates to 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate'. Both the supplier and recipient of this certificate are categorized as 'Any registered person', meaning that any GST-registered entity can engage in this transaction. This change is significant for businesses involved in priority sector lending, as it clarifies the GST treatment of these certificates. The amendment aims to streamline the process and ensure that registered persons can easily identify their obligations regarding this specific financial instrument. Overall, this update reflects the government's ongoing efforts to refine GST regulations and enhance clarity for businesses operating in this sector.

It amends Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
A new entry is added after S. No. 6 in the table of the original notification.
The new entry (S. No. 7) pertains to 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate'.
The supplier and recipient of the 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate' are both classified as 'Any registered person'.

892.summary: Notification No. 12/2018-Central Tax, issued on 29th June 2018, brings an important change by extending the deadline for a specific compliance requirement from 30th June 2018 to 30th September 2018. This amendment modifies the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 28th June 2017. The extension is granted under section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, indicating that the government recognizes the need for additional time in the public interest. This change affects all businesses and taxpayers who were required to meet the original deadline, providing them with an extra three months to comply. The last amendment to Notification No. 8/2017 was made through Notification No. 10/2018 on 23rd March 2018, and this new notification continues to build on those previous updates. Overall, this extension aims to ease compliance pressures for businesses operating under the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2018-Central Tax issued on 29th June 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The previous deadline of 30th June 2018 is extended to 30th September 2018.
The amendment is made under the powers of section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The last amendment to Notification No. 8/2017 was made by Notification No. 10/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 23rd March 2018.

893.summary: Notification No. 13/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 29th June 2018, brings an important change to the earlier notification No. 32/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 13th October 2017. Specifically, it extends the deadline for a certain compliance requirement from 30th June 2018 to 30th September 2018. This change is made under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's response to public interest needs. The last amendment to the principal notification was made through notification No. 11/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 23rd March 2018. Businesses and stakeholders affected by this amendment should take note of the new deadline, as it provides additional time for compliance. This extension may help businesses better prepare and manage their tax obligations under the GST framework. Overall, this notification aims to facilitate smoother compliance for taxpayers during this period.

The amendment is made under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
