894.summary: Notification No. 13/2018-Central Tax introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) effective from 27th July 2018. It revises item (i) under serial number 7 to include food and drink supplies from restaurants and canteens, explicitly excluding those located in hotels with a declared tariff of ₹7,500 or more per day. The definition of 'declared tariff' has been broadened to encompass all amenities provided in accommodation units. Additionally, item (v) under serial number 7 now addresses food supplies at events and functions that are occasional in nature. The notification also clarifies multimodal transportation of goods under item (vi) in serial number 9, defining key terms related to this service. Furthermore, serial number 22 has been updated to recognize e-books as part of telecommunications and broadcasting services. Lastly, the term 'declared tariff' has been replaced with 'value of supply' in specific items, streamlining the language used in the notification.
key_points: This notification is issued under Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes will take effect from 27th July 2018.
Item (i) under serial number 7 now includes supplies of food or drinks by restaurants, canteens, and messes, excluding those in hotels with a declared tariff of ₹7,500 or more per day.
The definition of 'declared tariff' now includes all amenities provided in accommodation units.
Item (v) under serial number 7 has been amended to cover food supplies at events and functions that are occasional in nature.
Item (vi) under serial number 9 now defines multimodal transportation of goods and its related terms.
Serial number 22 has been updated to include e-books as a supply under telecommunications and broadcasting services.
The term 'declared tariff' has been replaced with 'value of supply' in items (ii), (vi), and (viii) under serial number 7.

895.summary: Notification No. 14/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate). These changes will be effective from 27th July 2018. Notably, the amendment clarifies that food or drink supplied by restaurants, canteens, or messes will be taxable unless they are located in hotels with a declared tariff of ₹7,500 or more per day. Additionally, a new item has been added to include food or drink supplied by Indian Railways or its licensees. The terms 'declared tariff' in items (ii), (vi), and (viii) have been replaced with 'value of supply'. Furthermore, item (v) now addresses food or drink supplied at event-based functions such as exhibitions and weddings. The definition of multimodal transportation of goods has been introduced in item (vi) under serial number 9, providing clarity on the terms involved. Lastly, item (i) under serial number 22 specifies that e-books are taxable under heading 9984, ensuring that digital versions of printed books are included in the tax framework.
key_points: This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes take effect from 27th July 2018.
Against serial number 7, item (i) now specifies that food or drink supplied by restaurants, canteens, or messes is taxable unless located in hotels with a declared tariff of ₹7,500 or more per day.
Item (ia) is added to include food or drink supplied by Indian Railways or its licensees.
In items (ii), (vi), and (viii), 'declared tariff' is replaced with 'value of supply'.
Item (v) now covers food or drink supplied at event-based functions like exhibitions and weddings.
For serial number 9, item (vi) now defines multimodal transportation of goods and its related terms.
For serial number 22, item (i) now specifies e-books as taxable under heading 9984.

896.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 14/2018-Central Tax on 26th July 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the removal of the phrase 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or' from entries 4 and 5, simplifying the tax structure. A new entry (9D) has been added for services provided by old age homes to residents aged 60 years or more, with a monthly fee cap of Rs 25,000. Additionally, a new entry (10A) allows for tax exemption on services related to the construction and installation of electricity distribution networks for agricultural purposes. The term 'declared tariff' has been replaced with 'value of supply' in entry 14, and the year has been updated from 2018 to 2019 in entries 19A and 19B. Several new entries have also been introduced, including those for warehousing minor forest produce and artificial insemination of livestock. These changes will affect various sectors, including elderly care, agriculture, and utilities, by clarifying tax exemptions and obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2018-Central Tax issued on 26th July 2018.
Amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Omitted the phrase 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or' from entries against serial numbers 4 and 5.
Inserted new entry 9D for services by old age homes for residents aged 60 years or more, with a monthly consideration limit of Rs 25,000.
Inserted new entry 10A for services by electricity distribution utilities for agricultural use.
Changed 'declared tariff' to 'value of supply' in entry 14.
Updated the year from 2018 to 2019 in entries 19A and 19B.
Inserted multiple new entries including services related to warehousing of minor forest produce and artificial insemination of livestock.

897.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 15/2018 on 26th July 2018, which will take effect from 27th July 2018. This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017, specifically removing the phrase 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or' from entries 4 and 5. It introduces new entries for services provided by old age homes to residents aged 60 and above, and for the import of services by United Nations organizations and foreign diplomatic missions, which will now be exempt from integrated tax under certain conditions. Additionally, a new entry has been added for services related to electricity distribution for agricultural use. The term 'declared tariff' has been updated to 'value of supply' in entry 15, and the year '2018' has been changed to '2019' in entries 20A and 20B. Other new entries include services related to warehousing of minor forest produce and services provided by the Coal Mines Provident Fund Organisation and the National Pension System Trust. These changes will impact various sectors, including agriculture, social services, and international relations.
key_points: This notification is issued on 26th July 2018 and comes into effect from 27th July 2018.
It amends Notification No. 9/2017 - Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The words 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or' are omitted from entries against serial numbers 4 and 5.
New entries are added for services by old age homes, import of services by UN organizations, and foreign diplomatic missions.
Entry 11A is added for services supplied by electricity distribution utilities for agricultural use.
The term 'declared tariff' is replaced with 'value of supply' in entry 15.
The figures '2018' are replaced with '2019' in entries 20A and 20B.
New entries are added for services related to warehousing of minor forest produce, and services by Coal Mines Provident Fund Organisation and National Pension System Trust.

898.summary: Notification No. 15/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018, introduces significant amendments to the earlier notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. A new entry has been added to the Table, specifically after serial number 10, which pertains to services provided by individual Direct Selling Agents (DSAs) to banks or non-banking financial companies (NBFCs). This entry clarifies that these services are exclusively provided by individual DSAs who are not part of a corporate body, partnership, or limited liability partnership. Additionally, the notification includes a new definition for 'renting of immovable property' in the Explanation section, which now encompasses various forms of access or use of immovable property, regardless of whether possession is transferred. These changes will take effect from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses that engage with individual DSAs and those involved in renting immovable properties.
key_points: This notification is No. 15/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018.
It amends notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added to the Table after serial number 10 for services supplied by individual Direct Selling Agents (DSAs) to banks or non-banking financial companies (NBFCs).
The new entry specifies that these services are provided by individual DSAs who are not a body corporate, partnership, or limited liability partnership.
The notification defines 'renting of immovable property' in the Explanation section, adding a new clause (g).
The definition includes allowing access or use of immovable property, with or without transfer of possession.
This notification comes into effect from 27th July 2018.

899.summary: Notification No. 16/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, introduces important amendments to Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. A new entry has been added to the Table, specifically for services provided by individual Direct Selling Agents (DSAs) to banks or non-banking financial companies (NBFCs), which is now listed as entry number 13. This means that individual DSAs, who are not part of a corporate entity or partnership, will have their services recognized under this specific category. Additionally, the definition of 'renting of immovable property' has been clarified and expanded to encompass various forms of access and use of property, including leasing and licensing arrangements. These amendments will take effect from 27th July 2018, impacting how these services are treated under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework. Businesses involved in these services should take note of these changes to ensure proper compliance.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry has been added for services supplied by individual Direct Selling Agents (DSAs) to banks or non-banking financial companies (NBFCs).
The new entry is numbered 13 in the Table of the notification.
The definition of 'renting of immovable property' has been expanded to include various arrangements related to immovable property.
The changes come into effect from 27th July 2018.

900.summary: Notification No. 16/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018, introduces important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 14/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it expands the scope of the notification by inserting 'or Union territory' after 'State Government', which means that the provisions now apply not only to state governments but also to union territories. Additionally, it includes a reference to municipalities by adding 'or to a Municipality under article 243W of the Constitution' after 'Constitution'. This change clarifies that the notification's provisions are applicable to municipalities as well. These amendments are effective from 27th July 2018, impacting how GST is administered in union territories and municipalities, thereby broadening the framework of the GST law to include more local governing bodies.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2018-Central Tax issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 14/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment inserts 'or Union territory' after 'State Government' in the first paragraph.
It also adds 'or to a Municipality under article 243W of the Constitution' after 'Constitution' in the same paragraph.
The changes take effect from 27th July 2018.

901.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 17/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) to amend the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces two key changes in the first paragraph of the earlier notification. Firstly, it adds the phrase 'or Union territory' after 'State Government', thereby clarifying that the provisions apply not only to states but also to Union territories. Secondly, it includes the phrase 'or to a Municipality under article 243W of the Constitution', which expands the scope to include municipalities as well. These changes are significant as they broaden the applicability of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) provisions to a wider range of governmental entities. The amendments will come into effect from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses and entities operating within Union territories and municipalities, ensuring they are covered under the IGST framework.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts 'or Union territory' after 'State Government' in the first paragraph.
Inserts 'or to a Municipality under article 243W of the Constitution' after 'Constitution' in the first paragraph.
This amendment is effective from 27th July 2018.

902.summary: Notification No. 17/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018, introduces a new explanation in the existing Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate). Specifically, it clarifies that the term 'business' does not encompass any activities or transactions carried out by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities when they act as public authorities. This change is significant as it delineates the scope of what constitutes a business under GST, ensuring that government activities in their public capacity are not classified as business operations. The new explanation will take effect from 27th July 2018. This amendment follows the principal notification published on 28th June 2017 and its previous amendment on 25th January 2018. Businesses and entities involved in government contracts or services should take note of this clarification to understand how it may affect their GST obligations and interpretations.
key_points: Notification No. 17/2018-Central Tax issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification inserts an explanation in the Table against serial number 3, column (3), item (vi) of Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate).
The inserted explanation clarifies that the term 'business' does not include activities by Central Government, State Government, or local authorities as public authorities.
The effective date of this notification is 27th July 2018.
The principal notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) was published on 28th June 2017.
Notification No. 11/2017 was last amended by Notification No. 1/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 25th January 2018.

903.summary: Notification No. 18/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, introduces an important clarification to Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds an explanation in the Table against serial number 3, column (3), item (vi), stating that the term 'business' does not encompass any activities or transactions conducted by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities when they act as public authorities. This change is significant as it delineates the scope of what constitutes a business under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, ensuring that government activities in their public capacity are not classified as business transactions. The amendment will be effective from 27th July 2018, impacting how government entities engage in transactions and their GST obligations. This clarification helps in understanding the boundaries of business activities for tax purposes, particularly for entities involved in public service.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
An explanation has been inserted in the Table against serial number 3, column (3), item (vi).
The new explanation clarifies that 'business' does not include activities by Central Government, State Government, or local authorities as public authorities.
The changes take effect from 27th July 2018.

904.summary: Notification No. 18/2018-Central Tax, issued on July 26, 2018, introduces several amendments to the GST rates as per the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate). A new entry 102A has been added for ethyl alcohol supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with petrol, which will be taxed at 2.5%. Additionally, the entry for S. No. 123 has been updated to clarify the types of calcareous stones covered. New entry 170A for fertilizer grade phosphoric acid is also introduced under the same tax rate. The entry for S. No. 215 now includes coir pith compost in branded unit containers. Furthermore, S. No. 219 has been revised to specify coir mats and related products. In Schedule II, S. No. 57B has been removed entirely, while a new entry for bamboo flooring has been added at 6%. Lastly, several entries in Schedule IV have been omitted, including S. Nos. 20, 21, 22, and others, which may affect businesses dealing with those goods. These changes take effect from July 27, 2018.
key_points: Inserts new entry 102A for ethyl alcohol supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with motor spirit (petrol) at 2.5% GST.
Substitutes entry for S. No. 123 to specify certain types of calcareous monumental or building stone.
Inserts new entry 170A for fertilizer grade phosphoric acid at 2.5% GST.
Modifies entry 215 to include coir pith compost in unit containers with a brand name.
Substitutes entry 219 to specify coir mats, matting, floor covering, and handloom durries.
Omitted S. No. 57B from Schedule II (6%).
Inserts new entry 96A for bamboo flooring at 6% GST.
Omitted several entries in Schedule IV (14%) including S. Nos. 20, 21, 22, 24, and others.

905.summary: Notification No. 19/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, introduces several amendments to the existing GST rates. A new entry, 102A, has been added to Schedule I, which specifies a 5% tax rate on ethyl alcohol supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with petrol. Additionally, the description for S. No. 123 in Schedule I has been updated to clarify the types of calcareous stones included. A new entry, 170A, has also been added for fertilizer grade phosphoric acid, maintaining the 5% rate. In Schedule II, S. No. 57B has been omitted, while a new entry for bamboo flooring (96A) has been introduced, also at 12%. Furthermore, a new entry for fuel cell motor vehicles (206A) has been added to Schedule II. These changes will take effect from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses involved in the production and sale of these goods, particularly in the alcohol and construction sectors.
key_points: Inserts new entry 102A for ethyl alcohol supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with motor spirit (petrol) in Schedule I at 5%.
Substitutes entries for S. No. 123 in Schedule I, changing the description of certain calcareous stones.
Inserts new entry 170A for fertilizer grade phosphoric acid in Schedule I at 5%.
Modifies entry for coir pith compost in S. No. 215 in Schedule I by adding specific wording.
Omitted S. No. 57B in Schedule II at 12%.
Inserts new entry 96A for bamboo flooring in Schedule II at 12%.
Inserts new entry 206A for fuel cell motor vehicles in Schedule II at 12%.
This notification comes into force on 27th July 2018.

906.summary: Notification No. 19/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate). New entries have been added, including Sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, and sabai grass under S. No. 92 A, and vegetable materials for making jhadoo or broom sticks under S. No. 93 B. The entry for de-oiled rice bran has been updated under S. No. 102 A, effective from 25th January 2018. Additionally, new entries for deities made of stone, marble, or wood and Khali Dona have been added under S. No. 114A and 114B. The entry for rupee notes or coins sold to the Reserve Bank of India or the Government has been revised under S. No. 117. Furthermore, a new entry for coir pith compost has been introduced under S. No. 132A, which includes specific conditions. Lastly, new entries for sanitary towels and rakhi have been added under S. No. 146A and 152, respectively. These changes affect manufacturers and suppliers dealing with these goods, ensuring clarity on their tax treatment.
key_points: Notification No. 19/2018-Central Tax issued on 26th July 2018.
Inserts new entries for Sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, and sabai grass under S. No. 92 A.
Inserts new entry for vegetable materials for jhadoo or broom sticks under S. No. 93 B.
Substitutes entry for de-oiled rice bran under S. No. 102 A effective from 25th January 2018.
Inserts new entries for deities made of stone, marble, or wood under S. No. 114A and Khali Dona under S. No. 114B.
Substitutes entry for rupee notes or coins sold to RBI or Government under S. No. 117.
Inserts new entry for coir pith compost under S. No. 132A with specific conditions.
Inserts new entries for sanitary towels and rakhi under S. No. 146A and 152 respectively.

907.summary: The Notification No. 20/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate). Effective from July 27, 2018, new entries have been added to the GST rate schedule. Specifically, entry 92 A now includes 'Sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, sabai grass', and entry 93 B covers 'Vegetable materials, for manufacture of jhadoo or broom sticks'. The entry for S. No. 102 A has been updated to specify 'De-oiled rice bran', effective from January 25, 2018. Additionally, new entries for deities made of stone, marble, or wood, and goods made from various leaves have been added. The entry for S. No. 117 has been revised to include rupee notes or coins sold to the Reserve Bank of India or the Government. Furthermore, entry 132A introduces 'Coir pith compost' with specific conditions, while entry 146A covers sanitary products like towels and tampons. Lastly, entry 152 pertains to Rakhi, excluding those made from certain materials. These changes will affect manufacturers and sellers of these goods, ensuring clarity in GST rates applicable to them.
key_points: Inserts new entry 92 A for 'Sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, sabai grass' after S. No. 92.
Inserts new entry 93 B for 'Vegetable materials, for manufacture of jhadoo or broom sticks' after S. No. 93 A.
Substitutes entry for S. No. 102 A to specify 'De-oiled rice bran' effective from 25th January, 2018.
Inserts new entries 114A and 114B for 'Deities made of stone, marble or wood' and 'Goods made of sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, sabai grass' after S. No. 114.
Substitutes entry for S. No. 117 to include 'Rupee notes or coins when sold to Reserve Bank of India or the Government of India'.
Inserts new entry 132A for 'Coir pith compost' with specific conditions after S. No. 132.
Inserts new entry 146A for 'Sanitary towels (pads), sanitary napkins, tampons' after S. No. 146.
Inserts new entry 152 for 'Rakhi (other than those made of goods falling under Chapter 71)' after S. No. 151.

908.summary: Notification No. 20/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018, introduces significant changes to the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. A new proviso has been added, which states that input tax credit will not be applicable for certain specified goods if the supplies are received on or after 1st August 2018. This affects goods listed under serial numbers 1 to 7 in the previous notification. Furthermore, any accumulated input tax credit for these goods that remains unutilized after the tax payment for July 2018 will lapse. Businesses must ensure that they utilize their input tax credit for inward supplies received up to 31st July 2018, as any remaining credit after this date will no longer be valid. This change is crucial for businesses dealing with the specified goods, as it impacts their ability to claim input tax credit moving forward.
key_points: Notification No. 20/2018-Central Tax issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 5/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added stating that input tax credit for certain goods will not apply to supplies received on or after 1st August 2018.
The goods affected are listed under serial numbers 1 to 7 in the specified table of the earlier notification.
Accumulated input tax credit for these goods will lapse if unutilized after tax payment for July 2018.
The cut-off date for inward supplies to utilize the accumulated credit is 31st July 2018.

909.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 21/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new proviso that specifically states that input tax credit will not be applicable for certain goods if the supplies are received on or after 1st August 2018. The goods affected by this change are detailed in serial numbers 1 through 7 of the specified table. Additionally, any unutilized input tax credit accumulated until July 2018 for these goods will lapse after 31st July 2018. This change is enacted under the authority of section 54(3) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Businesses dealing with these specific goods should take note of this amendment, as it directly impacts their ability to claim input tax credits moving forward.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 5/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

The amendment is made under the powers of section 54(3) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).

910.summary: Notification No. 21/2018-Central Tax, issued on 26th July 2018, introduces an exemption for intra-state supplies of specific handicraft goods from central tax rates exceeding those specified in the notification. The definition of handicraft goods includes items that are predominantly made by hand and possess aesthetic or cultural significance. The notification includes a detailed table listing various handicraft items along with their corresponding tax rates. For instance, handcrafted candles and handbags are taxed at 6%, while items like handmade carpets and coir articles are taxed at a reduced rate of 2.5%. This change aims to support artisans and promote the handicraft sector by reducing the tax burden on these goods. The exemption is effective from 27th July 2018, meaning that businesses dealing in these handicraft items can benefit from the reduced tax rates from this date onward.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2018-Central Tax issued on 26th July 2018.
Exempts intra-state supplies of specified handicraft goods from central tax exceeding certain rates.
Handicraft goods defined as predominantly handmade items with aesthetic or cultural significance.
Table lists various handicraft goods with corresponding tax rates, including 6% for handcrafted candles and handbags, and 2.5% for items like handmade carpets and coir articles.
Notification effective from 27th July 2018.

911.summary: The Notification No. 22/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on July 26, 2018, introduces exemptions for inter-state supplies of specific handicraft goods from integrated tax beyond certain rates. This includes a range of products such as handcrafted candles, handbags, and carved wood items, all taxed at 12%. Additionally, certain articles made from vegetable materials and handmade carpets are taxed at a lower rate of 5%. For handmade or hand-embroidered shawls, those with a sale value exceeding Rs. 1000 are taxed at 12%, while those below this threshold are taxed at 5%. Notably, silver filigree work and handmade imitation jewellery are taxed at a reduced rate of 3%. This notification aims to support artisans and promote the handicraft sector by making these goods more affordable in inter-state trade. Businesses dealing in these handicraft items should take note of the specific tax rates applicable to their products to .
key_points: Exempts inter-state supplies of specified handicraft goods from integrated tax exceeding certain rates.
Handcrafted candles (HSN 3406) are taxed at 12%.
Handbags, jewellery boxes, and similar items (HSN 4202) are also taxed at 12%.
Wooden products like carved wood articles (HSN 4416) are taxed at 12%.
Certain vegetable material articles (HSN 4601 and 4602) are taxed at 5%.
Handmade carpets and textile floor coverings (HSN 57) are taxed at 5%.
Handmade/hand embroidered shawls with sale value exceeding Rs. 1000 are taxed at 12%.
Silver filigree work (HSN 7113) and handmade imitation jewellery (HSN 7117) are taxed at 3%.

912.summary: Notification No. 22/2018-Central Tax, issued on 6th August 2018, extends the deadline for a specific compliance requirement from 30th September 2018 to 30th September 2019. This change amends the earlier notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017, which was previously updated by notification No. 12/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 29th June 2018. The amendment is made under section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and follows recommendations from the GST Council. This extension provides additional time for businesses to comply with the requirements set forth in the original notification. The changes are officially published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that all stakeholders are informed. This notification is part of the ongoing efforts by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue, to facilitate smoother compliance under the GST framework.
key_points: This notification is No. 22/2018-Central Tax, issued on 6th August 2018.
It amends notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The previous deadline of 30th September 2018 is extended to 30th September 2019.
The amendment is made under the powers of section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The last amendment to the principal notification was made by notification No. 12/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 29th June 2018.
The amendment is based on recommendations from the GST Council.
The changes are published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Subsection (i).
The notification is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.

913.summary: Notification No. 23/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 6th August 2018, brings an important change to the earlier Notification No. 32/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 13th October 2017. Specifically, this amendment extends the deadline for a certain compliance from 30th September 2018 to 30th September 2019. This change is made under the authority of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring that stakeholders have additional time to meet their obligations. The previous amendment to this principal notification was made on 29th June 2018, and this latest update reflects the government's ongoing efforts to facilitate compliance in the public interest. Businesses and individuals affected by this notification should take note of the new deadline, as it provides them with an extended timeframe to fulfill their requirements under the GST framework.

The last amendment to the principal notification was made by Notification No. 13/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 29th June 2018.

914.summary: Notification No. 23/2018-Central Tax, issued on 20th September 2018, introduces an important clarification to Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds an explanation in the table against serial number 41, column (3), which states that for an entity to qualify for exemption, the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory must hold 50% or more ownership. This ownership can be either direct or through another entity that is wholly owned by the government. This change is significant for entities seeking exemptions under this notification, as it clearly defines the ownership criteria necessary to qualify. The previous amendment to the principal notification was made by Notification No. 14/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018, but this new explanation provides further clarity on the ownership requirement, ensuring that businesses understand the conditions under which they can claim exemptions.
key_points: Notification No. 23/2018-Central Tax issued on 20th September 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
An explanation is inserted in the table against serial number 41, column (3).
The new explanation clarifies that for exemption, the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory must have 50% or more ownership in the entity.
Ownership can be direct or through an entity wholly owned by the government.
The principal notification No. 12/2017 was last amended by notification No. 14/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018.

915.summary: On 20th September 2018, the Government of India issued Notification No. 24/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically adds an explanation in the Table against serial number 43, column (3). The new explanation clarifies that for an entity to qualify for exemption, it must be owned 50% or more by the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory. This ownership can be either direct or through another entity that is wholly owned by the government. This change is significant for entities seeking exemptions under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, as it clearly defines the ownership criteria required to qualify for such exemptions. The previous notification was last amended by Notification No. 15/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018, which is important context for understanding the evolution of these regulations.

It amends Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
An explanation is inserted in the Table against serial number 43, column (3).
The new explanation clarifies that for exemption, the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory must have 50% or more ownership in the entity.
Ownership can be direct or through an entity wholly owned by the government.
The principal notification was last amended by Notification No. 15/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 26th July 2018.

916.summary: Notification No. 24/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the omission of S. Nos. 23 and 24 in Schedule I, along with the addition of a new entry for crude marble and travertine under S. No. 123A. In Schedule II, new entries for various natural cork products are introduced, while S. Nos. 102 and 126 are removed. Schedule III sees the addition of a new entry for retreaded tyres under S. No. 121A, with the removal of S. Nos. 142, 143, and 144. Additionally, Schedule IV omits S. Nos. 47, 135, and 151, and makes specific changes to entries for S. No. 139 and S. No. 174. These amendments will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses dealing with the specified goods and services.

In Schedule II, new entries 101A, 101B, and 101C for natural cork products are added, while S. Nos. 102 and 126 are omitted.
In Schedule III, new entry 121A for retreaded tyres is added, and S. Nos. 142, 143, and 144 are omitted.
In Schedule IV, S. Nos. 47, 135, and 151 are omitted, and changes are made to entries for S. No. 139 and S. No. 174.
The notification comes into force on 1st January 2019.

917.summary: The Notification No. 25/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the omission of S. Nos. 23 and 24 from Schedule I, while new entries have been added, such as 'Marble and travertine, crude or roughly trimmed' under S. No. 123A. In Schedule II, new entries for natural cork products have been introduced, specifically S. Nos. 101A, 101B, and 101C, while S. Nos. 102 and 126 have been removed. Additionally, Schedule III now includes 'Retreaded or used pneumatic tyres of rubber' under S. No. 121A. These changes will impact businesses dealing with these specific goods, as they will need to adjust their tax calculations accordingly. The amendments will take effect from 1st January 2019, requiring businesses to be aware of the new classifications and rates.

S. Nos. 23 and 24 have been omitted from Schedule I.

S. Nos. 102 and 126 have been omitted from Schedule II.

918.summary: Notification No. 25/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Effective from 1st January 2019, this notification replaces the previous S. No. 43A with new entries that specify frozen vegetables, enhancing clarity on the tax treatment of these goods. Additionally, it introduces a new S. No. 121A, which pertains to printed or manuscript music, ensuring that such items are correctly classified under GST. Furthermore, a new S. No. 153 has been added to cover gift items received by public officials, such as the President or Prime Minister, through government auctions, where the proceeds are intended for public or charitable causes. These changes aim to streamline the GST framework and provide clearer guidelines for businesses and officials involved in these categories.

Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Substitutes S. No. 43A with new entries for frozen vegetables.
Inserts new S. No. 121A for printed or manuscript music.
Inserts new S. No. 153 for gift items received by public officials through government auctions.
Changes come into effect on 1st January 2019.

919.summary: On 31st January 2019, a corrigendum was issued for Notification No. 26/2018-Central Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 31st December 2018. The correction pertains to a numerical error found in line 33 of the original notification, where '32' has been amended to '34'. This change is documented in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i), under the reference number G.S.R. 1263(E). The corrigendum is officially issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue, and is signed by Gunjan Kumar Verma, the Under Secretary. It is important to note that this corrigendum does not introduce any new provisions or changes to tax rates; it simply corrects a mistake in the earlier notification. This correction ensures that the information in the original notification is accurate and reflects the intended tax rate. All stakeholders relying on this notification should take note of this correction for their records.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 26/2018-Central Tax (Rate) dated 31st December 2018.
The corrigendum was issued on 31st January 2019.
In the original notification, at line 33, the number '32' is corrected to '34'.
This change appears in the published Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).

The corrigendum is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.

920.summary: Notification No. 26/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Notably, S. No. 43A has been replaced with new entries that specify frozen vegetables and provisionally preserved vegetables, which will now be classified differently for tax purposes. Additionally, a new entry, 121A, has been introduced for printed or manuscript music, which will also be subject to the integrated tax. Furthermore, entry 153 has been added to cover gift items received by the President, Prime Minister, Governors, or Chief Ministers through public auctions, where the proceeds are intended for public or charitable causes. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses and individuals involved in the sale or distribution of these goods and services.
key_points: Notification No. 26/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
S. No. 43A is replaced with new entries for frozen vegetables and provisionally preserved vegetables.
New entry 121A is added for printed or manuscript music, effective from 1st January 2019.
New entry 153 is added for gift items received by certain public officials through government auctions.
The changes will come into effect on 1st January 2019.

921.summary: Notification No. 26/2018-Central Tax, issued on December 31, 2018, introduces an exemption from central tax for the intra-State supply of gold classified under heading 7108 when supplied by a Nominated Agency. This exemption is specifically for registered persons participating in the 'Export Against Supply by Nominated Agency' scheme. To benefit from this exemption, recipients must export jewellery made from the supplied gold within 90 days of receiving it. Additionally, they are required to submit proof of export, including their GSTIN details, to the Nominated Agency within 120 days from the date of supply. If the recipient fails to provide this proof, the Nominated Agency is obligated to pay the central tax due, along with interest. This notification will take effect from January 1, 2019, impacting those involved in the gold supply chain and export activities under the specified scheme.
key_points: Exempts intra-State supply of gold under heading 7108 from central tax when supplied by Nominated Agency.
Applicable to registered persons under the scheme for 'Export Against Supply by Nominated Agency'.
Recipient must export jewellery made from the supplied gold within 90 days of supply.
Recipient must provide proof of export, including GSTIN details, within 120 days of supply.
If proof of export is not provided, the Nominated Agency must pay the central tax along with interest.
Notification comes into force on 1st January 2019.

922.summary: On 31st January 2019, the Government of India issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 27/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 31st December 2018. The key change made in this corrigendum is the correction of the figure '32' to '34' in line 1 of the original notification. This adjustment directly impacts the integrated tax rate that was previously stated. Businesses and taxpayers who rely on this notification for understanding the applicable tax rates will need to take note of this correction. It is essential for those affected to update their records and calculations based on this revised figure to with the current tax rate. This change is part of the ongoing efforts by the government to maintain accuracy in tax-related notifications and ensure clarity for all stakeholders involved.
key_points: The notification is a corrigendum to Notification No. 27/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 31st December 2018.
The corrigendum was issued on 31st January 2019.
In the original notification, the figure '32' has been corrected to '34' in line 1.
This change affects the integrated tax rate specified in the original notification.

923.summary: Notification No. 27/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces an exemption from integrated tax for the inter-State supply of gold classified under heading 7108, specifically when supplied by a Nominated Agency. This exemption is applicable under the 'Export Against Supply by Nominated Agency' scheme outlined in the Foreign Trade Policy. To benefit from this exemption, the recipient must export jewellery made from the gold within 90 days of receiving it. Additionally, they are required to submit a shipping bill or bill of export that includes their GSTIN to the Nominated Agency within 120 days from the date of supply. If the recipient fails to provide this proof of export within the stipulated time, the Nominated Agency will be responsible for paying the integrated tax along with interest. This notification will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses involved in the export of jewellery made from gold supplied under this scheme.
key_points: Exempts inter-State supply of gold under heading 7108 from integrated tax when supplied by Nominated Agency.
Applicable to supplies made under the 'Export Against Supply by Nominated Agency' scheme as per Foreign Trade Policy.
Recipient must export jewellery made from the supplied gold within 90 days of supply.
Recipient must provide shipping bill or bill of export with GSTIN to Nominated Agency within 120 days of supply.
If proof of export is not provided within the specified period, Nominated Agency must pay the integrated tax with interest.
Notification comes into effect on 1st January 2019.

924.summary: Notification No. 27/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. A significant addition is the introduction of serial number 38 in the tax rate table, which covers services related to renewable energy projects such as bio-gas plants and solar power systems. The definition of 'specified organisation' has been expanded to include Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam Limited and the Haj Committee as per the Haj Committee Act, 2002. Additionally, the term 'goods carriage' is now defined in accordance with the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988. Some terms, specifically 'school, college', have been removed from the tax rate table, streamlining the entries. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses involved in the specified services and organizations mentioned. Overall, this notification aims to clarify and enhance the GST framework related to these sectors.
key_points: Notification No. 27/2018-Central Tax was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries have been added to the tax rate table, including serial number 38 for services related to renewable energy projects.
The term 'specified organisation' now includes Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam Limited and the Haj Committee as defined in the Haj Committee Act, 2002.
The definition of 'goods carriage' is aligned with the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988.
Certain terms like 'school, college' have been removed from the tax rate table.
The notification comes into effect on 1st January 2019.

925.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 28/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 31st December 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This new notification introduces several changes effective from 1st January 2019. Notably, a new entry has been added under serial number 38, which pertains to services related to the construction or installation of renewable energy systems like solar power and wind energy. Additionally, the definition of 'specified organisation' now includes Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam Limited and the Haj Committee as per the Haj Committee Act, 2002. The notification also removes the terms 'school, college' from the explanation for serial number 7 and makes various substitutions in the entries for serial numbers 15, 17, and 34, affecting the applicable tax rates. Furthermore, a new clause has been added for admission services to cinematograph films where ticket prices exceed one hundred rupees. These changes will impact businesses involved in these sectors, particularly those providing services related to renewable energy and entertainment.
key_points: Notification No. 28/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entry added for serial number 38 regarding services related to construction or installation of renewable energy systems, effective from 1st January 2019.
The term 'specified organisation' now includes Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam Limited and Haj Committee as defined in the Haj Committee Act, 2002.
The words 'school, college' have been omitted from the explanation for serial number 7.
Changes made to the entries for serial numbers 15, 17, and 34, including the substitution of items and tax rates.
New clause (iia) added for services by way of admission to cinematograph films where ticket prices exceed one hundred rupees.
The notification will come into force on 1st January 2019.

926.summary: Notification No. 28/2018-Central Tax, issued on December 31, 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate). Effective from January 1, 2019, a new entry (21B) is added for services provided by goods transport agencies to specific government entities, which will not attract GST. Additionally, a new entry (27A) is created for banking services offered to Basic Saving Bank Deposit account holders under the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, also exempt from GST. The entry for serial number 34A is amended to include 'banking companies' alongside PSUs. The entry for serial number 66 is updated to reflect new headings, while serial number 67 is completely removed. Furthermore, a new entry (74A) is added for services provided by rehabilitation professionals in recognized establishments, exempting these services from GST. Lastly, a definition for 'financial institution' is introduced, aligning it with the Reserve Bank of India Act. These changes primarily affect service providers in transportation, banking, and rehabilitation sectors.
key_points: Inserts new serial number 21B for services by a goods transport agency to certain government entities, effective from January 1, 2019.
Inserts new serial number 27A for services provided by banking companies to BSBD account holders under PMJDY, effective from January 1, 2019.
Amends entry for serial number 34A to include 'banking companies' in the context of PSUs.
Substitutes the entry for serial number 66 to 'Heading 9992 or Heading 9963'.
Omits serial number 67 and its related entries.
Inserts new serial number 74A for services by rehabilitation professionals recognized under the Rehabilitation Council of India Act, effective from January 1, 2019.
Adds a new clause (zaa) defining 'financial institution' as per the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

927.summary: The Notification No. 29/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), effective from January 1, 2019. It adds a new entry, serial number 22B, which specifies that services provided by a goods transport agency to certain government entities will be exempt from tax. Additionally, a new entry, serial number 28A, is created for services offered by banking companies to Basic Saving Bank Deposit account holders under the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana. The amendment also modifies the description for serial number 35A to include 'banking companies' and updates the entry for serial number 69 to refer to different headings. Serial number 70 has been removed entirely. Furthermore, a new entry, serial number 77A, is added for rehabilitation professionals providing services at designated establishments. Lastly, the notification clarifies the definition of 'financial institution' in relation to the Reserve Bank of India Act. These changes primarily affect transport agencies, banking institutions, and rehabilitation service providers.
key_points: Inserts new serial number 22B for services provided by a goods transport agency to certain government entities, effective from January 1, 2019.
Inserts new serial number 28A for services provided by banking companies to BSBD account holders under PMJDY, effective from January 1, 2019.
Amends entry against serial number 35A to include 'banking companies' in the description.
Substitutes the entry for serial number 69 to 'Heading 9992 or Heading 9963'.
Omits serial number 70 and its related entries.
Inserts new serial number 77A for services provided by rehabilitation professionals at specified establishments, effective from January 1, 2019.
Adds a definition for 'financial institution' in paragraph 2, clause (zaa) referencing the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

928.summary: Notification No. 29/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces amendments to Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) effective from 1st January 2019. A new proviso has been added to the first entry in the table, which exempts services provided by goods transport agencies to certain government entities, including departments of the Central and State Governments, local authorities, and specific governmental agencies that are registered solely for tax deduction purposes. Additionally, new entries have been included for services provided by business facilitators to banking companies, agents of business correspondents to business correspondents, and security services to registered persons, with similar exemptions for government entities. The explanation section has also been updated to confirm that these provisions apply to the Parliament and State Legislatures. These changes primarily affect service providers in the transport, banking, and security sectors, clarifying the tax obligations and exemptions for services rendered to government bodies.
key_points: This notification is issued on 31st December 2018 and comes into effect on 1st January 2019.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added to serial number 1 in the table, excluding certain services provided by goods transport agencies to specific government entities from tax.

New entries are added for services provided by business facilitators to banking companies, agents of business correspondents to business correspondents, and security services provided to registered persons.
The new entries specify that certain services are exempt from tax when provided to government entities that are registered only for tax deduction purposes.
The explanation is amended to clarify that the provisions apply to both the Parliament and State Legislatures.
The notification affects goods transport agencies, business facilitators, business correspondents, and security service providers.
The changes aim to clarify tax exemptions for specific services provided to government entities.

929.summary: Notification No. 30/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) effective from 1st January 2019. A new proviso has been added to serial number 2, which exempts services provided by goods transport agencies to certain government entities from tax. Additionally, new entries have been created for services provided by business facilitators to banking companies and agents of business correspondents. Security services provided to registered persons are now included, with specific exemptions for government entities and registered persons under section 10 of the GST Act. Furthermore, the explanation section has been updated to clarify that these provisions also apply to the Parliament and State Legislatures. This notification builds upon the previous amendments made by Notification No. 16/2018, ensuring clarity and consistency in the application of GST rates for these services.
key_points: This notification is issued on 31st December 2018 and comes into effect on 1st January 2019.
It amends Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added to serial number 2, stating that certain services by goods transport agencies to government entities are exempt from tax.
New entries for services provided by business facilitators to banking companies and agents of business correspondents are added as serial numbers 14 and 15.
Security services provided to registered persons are now included as serial number 16, with specific exemptions for government entities and certain registered persons.
The explanation section is amended to clarify that the provisions apply to both Parliament and State Legislatures.
The notification references the previous amendment made by Notification No. 16/2018 dated 26th July 2018.

930.summary: Notification No. 30/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces a significant clarification regarding the applicability of services under Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate). Specifically, it adds a new explanation to the existing provisions in the Table against serial number 9, column (3), item (vi). This new explanation states that the provisions do not apply to any service other than the transport of goods from one location in India to another. Additionally, the previous explanation in this item has been renumbered as Explanation 1. These changes are set to come into effect on 1st January 2019. This amendment is crucial for businesses involved in providing services, as it clearly delineates the scope of services that fall under this notification, ensuring that only transport services are covered. The principal notification, which was first published on 28th June 2017, has undergone previous amendments, the last being on 26th July 2018, highlighting the ongoing adjustments in GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 30/2018-Central Tax issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification inserts a new explanation in the Table against serial number 9, column (3), item (vi) of Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate).
The new explanation clarifies that the item does not apply to services other than transport of goods within India.
The existing explanation in the same item is renumbered as Explanation 1.
The changes take effect from 1st January 2019.
The principal notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) was published on 28th June 2017 and last amended by notification No. 17/2018-Central Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018.

931.summary: Notification No. 31/2018-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018, introduces a significant clarification to Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. It adds a new explanation that specifies that the provisions of the item do not apply to any service other than the transport of goods from one location in India to another. This means that if you are providing services that do not involve the transport of goods, the previous rules may not apply to you. Additionally, the existing explanation in the same item has been renumbered as Explanation 1 to accommodate this new clarification. This change is effective from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses involved in the transport of goods within India, as they will need to consider this clarification in their GST compliance and reporting. Overall, this notification aims to provide clearer guidance on the applicability of GST rates for specific services.
key_points: Inserts a new explanation in Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The new explanation clarifies that the item does not apply to services other than transport of goods within India.
The existing explanation in the same item is renumbered as Explanation 1.
This notification comes into effect on 1st January 2019.

932.summary: Notification No. 01/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017, which was issued on June 28, 2017. This new notification, effective from January 25, 2018, adds new provisions for civil structures related to Economically Weaker Section (EWS) houses under the Housing for All (Urban) Mission, specifically through the introduction of sub-items (da) and (db). Additionally, it modifies the wording in item (v) from 'excluding' to 'including', which broadens the scope of certain services. The entry for serial number 9 now encompasses a wider range of fuels, including natural gas and petroleum products. Furthermore, the entry for serial number 16 clarifies the tax treatment of land lease services provided by government entities. Changes to serial number 23 refine the tax implications for house-keeping services, particularly those supplied through electronic commerce operators. Lastly, the notification specifies that for certain supplies involving land transfer, the value will be deemed to be one third of the total amount charged, providing clarity for businesses involved in such transactions.
key_points: This notification is dated January 25, 2018, and is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
New sub-items (da) and (db) are added for civil structures related to Economically Weaker Section (EWS) houses under the Housing for All (Urban) Mission.
The term 'excluding' is replaced with 'including' in item (v) for certain services.
The entry for serial number 9 now includes 'natural gas, petroleum crude, motor spirit, high speed diesel or aviation turbine fuel'.
The entry for serial number 16 is updated to include services by the government to governmental authorities regarding land lease.
Changes are made to the entries for serial number 23 regarding house-keeping services and their tax implications.
The value of certain supplies involving land transfer is clarified to be one third of the total amount charged.

933.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 1/2018-Central Tax on 1st January 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax from 27th June 2017. This amendment specifically alters the tax rate mentioned in clause (i) of the earlier notification, reducing it from one percent to half percent. Additionally, clause (iii) has been revised to clarify that the half percent applies specifically to the turnover of taxable supplies of goods, rather than the broader term 'turnover'. These changes are significant for businesses operating in Union Territories, as they directly affect the tax obligations related to their taxable supplies. The amendments aim to streamline tax rates and ensure clarity in tax calculations for businesses, thereby potentially easing the compliance burden for taxpayers in these regions.
key_points: Notification No. 1/2018-Central Tax issued on 1st January 2018.
Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax dated 27th June 2017.
Changes the rate from 'one per cent.' to 'half per cent.' in clause (i).
In clause (iii), modifies 'half per cent. of the turnover' to 'half per cent. of the turnover of taxable supplies of goods'.

934.summary: The Notification No. 02/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) introduces several amendments to the earlier notification No. 12/2017. It adds 'or a Government Entity' to entry 3, expanding the definition of entities eligible for certain tax benefits. A new entry 3A has been created for composite supplies of goods and services to government entities, where the value of goods does not exceed 25%. Additionally, the duration for certain services has been extended from one year to three years in entry 16. New entries 19A and 19B have been added for transportation services by aircraft and vessels, valid until September 30, 2018. Entry 22 now includes provisions for motor vehicles used for transporting students and staff to educational institutions. Furthermore, entry 29A has been introduced for life insurance services for Coast Guard personnel. The limit in entry 36 has been increased from fifty thousand to two lakhs. Lastly, entry 39A has been added for financial services intermediaries in multi-services SEZs with IFSC status, enhancing the scope of services covered under the Union Territory Tax framework.
key_points: Inserts 'or a Government Entity' after 'a Governmental Authority' in entry 3 of the table.
Adds new entry 3A for composite supply of goods and services provided to government entities, with a value of goods not exceeding 25%.
Changes the duration from 'one year' to 'three years' in entry 16 of the table.
Introduces new entries 19A and 19B for transportation services by aircraft and vessel, respectively, with a validity until September 30, 2018.
Inserts a new item (c) in entry 22 for motor vehicles used for transporting students and staff to educational institutions.
Adds new entry 29A for life insurance services provided by the Naval Group Insurance Fund to Coast Guard personnel.
Increases the limit from 'fifty thousand' to 'two lakhs' in entry 36.
Inserts new entries 39A for financial services intermediaries located in multi-services SEZs with IFSC status.

935.summary: Notification No. 2/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2018, introduces a significant change for businesses operating in the Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Starting from 1st April 2018, no e-way bill will be required for the movement of goods within this territory, regardless of the value of the consignment. This decision is based on the recommendations of the GST Council and is supported by provisions in both the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. Specifically, it is enacted under section 22 of the UT GST Act and section 164 of the CGST Act, with reference to rule 138 of the CGST Rules. This change simplifies the logistics process for businesses in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, allowing for smoother movement of goods without the administrative burden of generating e-way bills. Overall, this notification aims to facilitate trade and ease compliance for local businesses.
key_points: Notification No. 2/2018-Central Tax issued on 31st March 2018.
This notification applies to the Union Territory of Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
No e-way bill is required for the movement of goods within the Union Territory, regardless of consignment value.
The notification comes into effect from 1st April 2018.
It is issued under section 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It also references section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The decision was made based on recommendations from the GST Council.
The rule referenced is sub-rule 14 of rule 138 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.

936.summary: Notification No. 03/2018, issued on 25th January 2018, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 13/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. A new entry, numbered 5A, has been added to the table, which pertains to services provided by the Central Government, State Government, Union territory, or local authority related to the renting of immovable property. This service is specifically for individuals or entities registered under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Additionally, the notification includes a new clause in the Explanation that defines 'insurance agent' in accordance with the Insurance Act, 1938. These changes are made under the authority of section 7(3) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This amendment is significant for businesses and individuals involved in renting property and those in the insurance sector, as it clarifies the tax implications and definitions relevant to their operations.
key_points: This notification is issued on 25th January 2018.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry (5A) is added to the table for services supplied by the Central Government, State Government, Union territory, or local authority for renting immovable property.
Entry 5A specifies that these services are provided to persons registered under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification also adds a new clause (f) in the Explanation, defining 'insurance agent' as per the Insurance Act, 1938.
The amendment is made under the powers conferred by section 7(3) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The principal notification was last amended by Notification No. 33/2017 on 13th October 2017.

937.summary: Notification No. 3/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2018, introduces a significant change regarding the e-way bill requirements for the Union Territory of Chandigarh. Effective from 1st April 2018, this notification states that no e-way bill is necessary for the movement of goods that both starts and ends within Chandigarh, regardless of the consignment's value. This means that businesses operating in Chandigarh can transport goods without the administrative burden of generating an e-way bill, simplifying logistics and potentially reducing costs. The notification is based on the powers granted under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and follows recommendations from the GST Council. This change is particularly beneficial for local businesses and traders who frequently move goods within the territory, allowing for smoother operations and enhanced efficiency.
key_points: Notification No. 3/2018-Central Tax issued on 31st March 2018.
This notification states that no e-way bill is required for goods movement within the Union Territory of Chandigarh.
The exemption applies regardless of the value of the consignment.
The notification comes into effect from 1st April 2018.
It is issued under section 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It also references section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The decision was made based on recommendations from the GST Council.

938.summary: The Notification No. 4/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on January 25, 2018, introduces specific tax liabilities for certain registered persons under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act. It applies to those who supply development rights to developers, builders, or construction companies, as well as those providing construction services in exchange for development rights. The key change is that the liability to pay Union Territory tax will arise at the moment when the developer or builder transfers possession or rights in the constructed complex to the supplier of development rights. This transfer must be documented through a conveyance deed or a similar instrument, such as an allotment letter. This notification clarifies the timing of tax liability for these transactions, ensuring that both suppliers and recipients understand when the tax obligation occurs.
key_points: Notification No. 4/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 25th January 2018.
This notification is based on section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It also references section 148 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Registered persons supplying development rights to developers or builders are affected.
Registered persons supplying construction services in exchange for development rights are also included.
The liability to pay Union Territory tax arises when possession or rights in the constructed complex are transferred.
The transfer must be formalized through a conveyance deed or similar instrument, such as an allotment letter.

939.summary: Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2018, introduces a significant change regarding e-way bills in the Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli. Starting from 1st April 2018, businesses will no longer need to generate an e-way bill for the movement of goods that both starts and ends within this Union Territory, regardless of the consignment's value. This change is based on the powers conferred by section 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The notification was issued following recommendations from the GST Council and amends the existing requirements under rule 138 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017. This update simplifies the logistics process for businesses operating within Dadra and Nagar Haveli, allowing for smoother movement of goods without the need for e-way bill compliance.
key_points: Notification No. 4/2018-Central Tax issued on 31st March 2018.
Effective date of the notification is 1st April 2018.
No e-way bill is required for goods movement within the Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli.
This applies regardless of the value of the consignment.
The notification is issued under section 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It is also based on section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification follows recommendations from the GST Council.
It amends the requirement for e-way bills as per rule 138 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017.

940.summary: Notification No. 5/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on January 25, 2018, introduces an exemption for certain services related to petroleum and natural gas. Specifically, it exempts the intra-State supply of services that involve granting licenses or leases for exploring or mining petroleum crude or natural gas from the Union Territory tax. This exemption applies to the consideration that is paid to the Central Government, which is defined as the Central Government’s share of profit petroleum in the relevant contracts. This change is significant for businesses involved in the petroleum sector, as it reduces their tax burden on these specific transactions. The exemption is granted under the authority of section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's intent to support the industry by alleviating some of the tax liabilities associated with these activities.
key_points: Exemption applies to intra-State supply of services related to granting licenses or leases for exploring or mining petroleum crude or natural gas.
The exemption is from the Union Territory tax on the consideration paid to the Central Government.
The consideration refers specifically to the Central Government’s share of profit petroleum as defined in relevant contracts.
This notification is issued under the powers of section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

941.summary: Notification No. 5/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2018, introduces a significant change regarding the movement of goods within the Union Territory of Daman and Diu. Effective from 1st April 2018, businesses will no longer need to generate an e-way bill for transporting goods that both start and end within this territory. This applies to all consignments, irrespective of their value, simplifying the logistics process for businesses operating in Daman and Diu. The decision is aimed at easing the compliance burden on traders and enhancing the efficiency of goods movement within the Union Territory. As a result, businesses can expect reduced paperwork and faster transit times for their goods, which can lead to cost savings and improved operational efficiency. This change is particularly beneficial for local businesses that frequently move goods within the region, allowing them to focus more on their core operations rather than administrative requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 5/2018-Central Tax issued on 31st March 2018.
This notification is effective from 1st April 2018.
No e-way bill is required for the movement of goods within the Union Territory of Daman and Diu.
This applies regardless of the value of the consignment.

942.summary: The Notification No. 06/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on January 25, 2018, brings several amendments to the previous notification No. 1/2017. New entries have been added to Schedule I, including Tamarind kernel powder, Mehendi paste in cones, and Rice bran, while the phrase 'to household domestic consumers or' has been removed from an existing entry. Schedule II sees the introduction of Sugar boiled confectionery and Drinking water in 20-litre bottles, along with Fertilizer grade phosphoric acid. Notably, two entries have been omitted from Schedule II. In Schedule III, changes include the substitution of terms related to groundnut sweets and the addition of exceptions for specific products. Schedule IV now includes actionable claims related to betting and gambling. Furthermore, Schedule V has been amended to update entries for semi-precious stones and imitation jewellery, while Schedule VI has seen substitutions for all goods and the introduction of new entries for precious stones. These changes affect various sectors, including food, agriculture, and manufacturing, ensuring that the tax structure remains relevant and updated.
key_points: Inserts new entries in Schedule I for Tamarind kernel powder (S. No. 76A), Mehendi paste in cones (S. No. 78A), and Rice bran (S. No. 103B).
Omitted the phrase 'to household domestic consumers or' from S. No. 165 in Schedule I.
Introduces new entries in Schedule II for Sugar boiled confectionery (S. No. 32AA), Drinking water in 20 litres bottles (S. No. 46B), and Fertilizer grade phosphoric acid (S. No. 57B).
Substitutes entries in Schedule III for groundnut sweets and adds exceptions for certain products.
Omitted S. No. 103 and S. No. 104 in Schedule II.
Introduces new entries in Schedule IV for actionable claims related to betting and gambling.
Amends Schedule V to substitute entries for semi-precious stones and imitation jewellery.
In Schedule VI, substitutes entries for all goods and introduces new entries for precious stones.

943.summary: The Government of India has issued a corrigendum to Notification No. 6/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 25th January 2018. This corrigendum, dated 1st February 2018, makes specific corrections to the language used in the original notification. Specifically, the term 'substituted' in line 34 of the original document has been changed to 'inserted', and the same change applies to line 37. These corrections clarify the intended meaning of the notification, ensuring that the provisions regarding the Union Territory Tax are accurately represented. This is important for businesses operating in Union Territories, as it affects how they interpret and apply the tax rates specified in the original notification. The changes do not introduce new provisions but rather correct the terminology used, which could impact compliance and reporting for affected businesses.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 6/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 25th January 2018.
The corrigendum was issued on 1st February 2018.
In the original notification, the term 'substituted' in line 34 is corrected to 'inserted'.
In the original notification, the term 'substituted' in line 37 is also corrected to 'inserted'.
