944.summary: Notification No. 6/2018-Central Tax, issued on 31st March 2018, introduces a significant change regarding the movement of goods within the Union Territory of Lakshadweep. Starting from 1st April 2018, businesses will no longer need to generate an e-way bill for the transportation of goods that both starts and ends within Lakshadweep. This exemption applies to all consignments, irrespective of their value, simplifying the logistics process for businesses operating in this region. The decision aims to ease compliance burdens and facilitate smoother movement of goods within the territory. This change is particularly beneficial for local businesses and traders who frequently transport goods without crossing state borders. By eliminating the e-way bill requirement, the government is promoting efficiency and reducing administrative overhead for businesses in Lakshadweep.
key_points: Notification No. 6/2018-Central Tax issued on 31st March 2018.
Effective from 1st April 2018.
No e-way bill is required for goods movement within the Union Territory of Lakshadweep.
This applies regardless of the value of the consignment.

945.summary: The Notification No. 07/2018, issued on 25th January 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017 concerning Union Territory Tax rates. Notably, the entry for S. No. 102 has been updated to include a comprehensive list of aquatic and animal feeds, which now encompasses shrimp feed, prawn feed, poultry feed, and various types of cattle feed. Additionally, two new entries have been added under S. No. 102A and 102B for 'De-oiled rice bran' and 'Cotton seed oil cake', respectively. The entry for S. No. 136A has been changed to '7117', while S. No. 137 now specifies that the products used in agriculture, horticulture, or forestry are 'other than ghamella'. Furthermore, the entry for S. No. 148 item (v) has been revised to 'Vibhuti'. Lastly, a new entry has been introduced under S. No. 151 for 'Parts for manufacture of hearing aids'. These changes affect businesses dealing with these specific goods and ensure clarity in the tax structure for these items.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 25th January 2018.
Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

Substitutes entry for S. No. 102 to include 'Aquatic feed including shrimp feed and prawn feed, poultry feed & cattle feed, including grass, hay & straw, supplement & husk of pulses, concentrates & additives, wheat bran & de-oiled cake [other than rice-bran]'.
Inserts new entries for S. No. 102A as 'De-oiled rice bran' and S. No. 102B as 'Cotton seed oil cake'.
Substitutes entry for S. No. 136A to '7117'.
Adds 'other than ghamella' to the entry for S. No. 137.
Substitutes entry for S. No. 148 item (v) to 'Vibhuti'.
Inserts new entry for S. No. 151 as 'Parts for manufacture of hearing aids'.

946.summary: Notification No. 07/2018-Central Tax, issued on 18th May 2018, officially rescinds the earlier notification G.S.R. 316(E) dated 31st March 2018. This change will take effect from 25th May 2018. The decision to rescind is made under the authority of section 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. The notification was issued following recommendations from the GST Council, indicating a shift in regulatory measures. This means that any provisions or guidelines previously established under G.S.R. 316(E) will no longer be in effect after the specified date. Businesses and stakeholders in the Union Territories should take note of this change as it may impact their compliance and operational procedures under the GST framework. The publication of this notification in the Gazette of India ensures that all concerned parties are informed of the latest regulatory updates.
key_points: Notification No. 07/2018-Central Tax was issued on 18th May 2018.
This notification rescinds G.S.R. 316(E) dated 31st March 2018.
The rescindment of G.S.R. 316(E) takes effect from 25th May 2018.
The rescindment is based on the powers under section 22 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It also references section 164 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was issued following the recommendation of the GST Council.
The notification is published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).

947.summary: Notification No. 8/2018, issued on January 25, 2018, introduces an exemption from central tax for intra-state supplies of certain old and used motor vehicles under Chapter 87, specifically those classified under tariff item 8703. The notification specifies a tax rate of 9% for petrol and diesel vehicles with certain engine capacities and lengths, while a lower rate of 6% applies to other old vehicles. The calculation of the supplier's margin for tax purposes is defined based on either the depreciation claimed under the Income Tax Act or the difference between the selling and purchase prices. Importantly, this exemption will not apply if the supplier has availed input tax credit on these goods. This change primarily affects suppliers of used motor vehicles, allowing them to benefit from reduced tax rates under specified conditions, thereby potentially lowering their tax liability on such transactions.
key_points: Notification No. 8/2018 issued on 25th January 2018.
Exempts central tax on intra-state supplies of specified old and used motor vehicles.
Applicable to vehicles under Chapter 87, specifically tariff items 8703.
Tax rates specified: 9% for certain petrol and diesel vehicles, 6% for other old vehicles.
Conditions for margin calculation based on depreciation or selling price.
Exemption does not apply if input tax credit has been availed on the goods.

948.summary: Notification No. 08/2018-Central Tax, issued on 18th May 2018, rescinds the earlier notification G.S.R. 317(E) dated 31st March 2018. This change will take effect from 25th May 2018. The rescindment is based on the powers conferred under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This means that any provisions or guidelines that were previously established under G.S.R. 317(E) will no longer be in effect after the specified date. Businesses and stakeholders who were following the guidelines in the earlier notification should take note of this change, as it may impact their compliance and operational procedures. The notification reflects the government's ongoing adjustments to GST regulations to streamline processes and ensure clarity in tax administration.
key_points: Notification No. 08/2018-Central Tax was issued on 18th May 2018.
This notification rescinds G.S.R. 317(E) dated 31st March 2018.
The rescindment of G.S.R. 317(E) takes effect from 25th May 2018.
The notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

949.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 09/2018 on 25th January 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 45/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 14th November 2017. The amendment specifically updates the description for serial number 1 in the Table, clarifying that the exemption applies to 'Public funded research institution or a University or an Indian Institute of Technology or Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore or a Regional Engineering College, other than a hospital'. Additionally, the term 'Department of Scientific and Research' is replaced with 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research' in both serial number 1 and also for serial numbers 2 and 4. Furthermore, a new explanation has been added, which states that the exemption will be in accordance with Notification No. 51/96-Customs from 23rd July 1996, and this is effective from 15th November 2017. This change primarily affects educational and research institutions that fall under these categories, ensuring they are aware of the updated terms for tax exemptions.
key_points: This notification is issued on 25th January 2018.
It amends Notification No. 45/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 14th November 2017.

In the Table, against serial number 1, the entry in column (2) is changed to specify 'Public funded research institution or a University or an Indian Institute of Technology or Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore or a Regional Engineering College, other than a hospital'.
In the same row, column (4) changes 'Department of Scientific and Research' to 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research'.
For serial numbers 2 and 4, column (4) also changes 'Department of Scientific and Research' to 'Department of Scientific and Industrial Research'.
An additional explanation, numbered as Explanation 2, is added after the existing Explanation 1.
Explanation 2 states that the exemption aligns with Notification No. 51/96-Customs dated 23rd July 1996, effective from 15th November 2017.

950.summary: Notification No. 9/2018-Central Tax, issued on 18th May 2018, rescinds the earlier notification G.S.R. 318(E) dated 31st March 2018. This change will take effect from 25th May 2018. The rescission is based on the powers granted under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This means that any provisions or guidelines that were previously established under G.S.R. 318(E) will no longer be in effect after the specified date. Businesses and stakeholders who were relying on the earlier notification should take note of this change, as it may impact their compliance and operational procedures. The government has made this decision following recommendations from the GST Council, indicating a shift in regulatory approach or requirements. It is essential for affected parties to review their practices in light of this rescission to ensure alignment with the current GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 9/2018-Central Tax was issued on 18th May 2018.
This notification rescinds G.S.R. 318(E) dated 31st March 2018.
The rescission of G.S.R. 318(E) takes effect from 25th May 2018.
The notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

951.summary: On 23rd March 2018, the Government of India issued Notification No. 10/2018 to amend the earlier Notification No. 8/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This amendment specifically changes the deadline for a certain compliance requirement from '31st March 2018' to '30th June 2018'. This change is made under the authority of section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and follows recommendations from the GST Council. The principal notification, which was originally published on 28th June 2017, has undergone previous amendments, including one on 13th October 2017. This extension of the deadline may affect businesses operating in Union Territories, allowing them additional time to comply with the tax regulations. It is important for businesses to note this new date to ensure they are aligned with the updated requirements.
key_points: The notification is issued on 23rd March 2018.
It amends Notification No. 8/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment changes the date from '31st day of March, 2018' to '30th day of June, 2018'.
This amendment is made under the powers of section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The amendment is based on recommendations from the GST Council.
The principal notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Subsection (i).
The earlier amendment was made by Notification No. 38/2017 dated 13th October 2017.

952.summary: Notification No. 10/2018-Central Tax, issued on 21st May 2018, officially rescinds the earlier notification G.S.R. 315(E) dated 31st March 2018. This change will take effect from 25th May 2018. The rescission is made under the authority of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This means that any provisions or guidelines that were previously established in G.S.R. 315(E) will no longer be applicable after the effective date. Businesses and stakeholders who were following the guidelines in the rescinded notification will need to be aware of this change and adjust their practices accordingly. The notification reflects the government's ongoing efforts to streamline GST regulations and ensure clarity in compliance requirements. Overall, this is a significant update for those operating under the Union Territory GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2018-Central Tax was issued on 21st May 2018.
This notification rescinds G.S.R. 315(E) dated 31st March 2018.
The rescission of G.S.R. 315(E) takes effect from 25th May 2018.
The notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

953.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 11/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on May 28, 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. This amendment introduces a new entry in the tax rate table, specifically after serial number 6. The new entry, numbered 7, relates to 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate'. Both the supplier and recipient of this certificate are classified as 'Any registered person'. This change is significant as it clarifies the tax treatment of Priority Sector Lending Certificates under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax framework. Registered persons involved in the supply or receipt of these certificates will need to be aware of this new classification for compliance and reporting purposes.
key_points: This notification is dated May 28, 2018.
It amends Notification No. 4/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.
A new entry has been added after S. No. 6 in the table of the original notification.
The new entry is S. No. 7, which pertains to 'Priority Sector Lending Certificate'.
The supplier of goods for this entry is 'Any registered person'.
The recipient of supply for this entry is also 'Any registered person'.

954.summary: Notification No. 11/2018-Central Tax, issued on 21st May 2018, officially rescinds the earlier notification G.S.R. 319(E) dated 31st March 2018. This change will take effect from 25th May 2018. The rescission is made under the authority of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This means that any provisions or guidelines that were established in the earlier notification G.S.R. 319(E) will no longer be applicable after the specified date. Businesses and stakeholders who were following the guidelines in G.S.R. 319(E) should take note of this change and adjust their compliance and operational practices accordingly. The notification reflects the government's ongoing efforts to streamline GST regulations and ensure clarity in tax administration. It is essential for affected parties to stay informed about these changes to maintain compliance with the current GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2018-Central Tax was issued on 21st May 2018.
This notification rescinds G.S.R. 319(E) dated 31st March 2018.
The rescission of G.S.R. 319(E) takes effect from 25th May 2018.
The notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

955.summary: On 29th June 2018, the Government of India issued Notification No. 12/2018 to amend the earlier Notification No. 8/2017 regarding the Union Territory Tax (Rate). The key change in this notification is the extension of a deadline from 30th June 2018 to 30th September 2018. This extension is significant for businesses operating in Union Territories as it provides them additional time to comply with the tax requirements set forth in the earlier notification. The amendment is made under section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, which allows the government to make such changes in the interest of the public. This notification follows the last amendment made by Notification No. 10/2018 on 23rd March 2018, which had previously updated the same notification. Businesses should take note of this new deadline to ensure they are aligned with the latest tax regulations.
key_points: This notification is issued on 29th June 2018.
It amends Notification No. 8/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The previous deadline of 30th June 2018 is extended to 30th September 2018.
The amendment is made under the powers of section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The last amendment before this was made by Notification No. 10/2018 on 23rd March 2018.

956.summary: Notification No. 12/2018-Central Tax, issued on 28th September 2018, introduces a new requirement for electronic commerce operators in Union Territories without a legislature. Starting from 1st October 2018, these operators, who are not acting as agents, are mandated to collect a tax at a rate of 0.5% on the net value of taxable supplies made through their platforms by other suppliers. This means that if you are an electronic commerce operator, you will need to ensure that this tax is collected on behalf of the suppliers using your platform for intra-Union Territory transactions. The requirement is grounded in the provisions of Section 22(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act and Section 52(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. This change is significant as it formalizes the tax collection process for e-commerce transactions in these regions, impacting how businesses operate in the digital marketplace.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2018-Central Tax issued on 28th September 2018.
This notification applies to electronic commerce operators, excluding agents.
Operators must collect a tax at a rate of 0.5% on the net value of intra-Union Territory taxable supplies.
The taxable supplies must be made through the electronic commerce operator by other suppliers.
The consideration for these supplies is to be collected by the electronic commerce operator.
This notification comes into effect from 1st October 2018.
The provisions are based on Section 22(1) and Section 52(1) of the GST Acts.
This applies specifically to Union Territories without a legislature.

957.summary: Notification No. 13/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier notification No. 11/2017. Notably, it revises the entries for serial number 7, which pertains to the supply of food and drinks by restaurants and similar establishments. The updated definition now excludes those establishments that have a declared tariff of ₹7,500 or more per day. Additionally, item (v) under serial number 7 has been modified to include food supplies at event-based functions, expanding the scope of taxable services. Furthermore, the notification clarifies the definition of 'multimodal transportation' under serial number 9, ensuring that businesses involved in this type of transport understand their obligations. Lastly, serial number 22 now includes e-books as part of telecommunications and broadcasting services. These changes will take effect from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses in the food service and transportation sectors, as well as those dealing with digital content.
key_points: This notification is No. 13/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018.
It amends notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments include changes to the entries for serial number 7 regarding food and drink supplies by restaurants and similar establishments.
For item (i) under serial number 7, the definition now excludes establishments with a declared tariff of ₹7,500 and above per day.
Item (v) under serial number 7 has been updated to include food supplies at event-based functions.
The definition of 'multimodal transportation' has been clarified under serial number 9.
Serial number 22 has been updated to include e-books as a supply under telecommunications and broadcasting services.
This notification comes into effect from 27th July 2018.

958.summary: Notification No. 13/2018-Central Tax, issued on 28th September 2018, introduces a requirement for electronic commerce operators to collect a tax of 1% on the net value of inter-Union Territory taxable supplies made through their platforms by other suppliers. This means that if you are an electronic commerce operator, you are responsible for collecting this tax when the payment for these supplies is processed through you. The new rule is effective from 1st October 2018, and it is established under the authority of several sections from the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, and the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. This change primarily affects electronic commerce operators who facilitate transactions between suppliers and customers across Union Territories without a legislature. It is crucial for these operators to understand their new obligations to with the tax collection requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2018-Central Tax was issued on 28th September 2018.
The notification mandates that every electronic commerce operator must collect 1% of the net value of inter-Union Territory taxable supplies.
This applies to supplies made through the operator by other suppliers.
The consideration for these supplies must be collected by the electronic commerce operator.
The notification comes into effect from 1st October 2018.
It is based on the powers conferred by various sections of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, and Central Goods and Services Tax Act.

959.summary: Notification No. 14/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017. Key changes include the removal of the phrases 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or' from entries against serial numbers 4 and 5. New services have been added, such as those provided by old age homes and electricity distribution utilities, which will now be exempt from tax. Additionally, the term 'declared tariff' has been replaced with 'value of supply' in entry 14, and the year '2018' has been updated to '2019' in entries 19A and 19B. A clarification has also been made that Central and State Educational Boards are recognized as Educational Institutions for the purpose of conducting examinations. These changes will be effective from 27th July 2018, impacting various service providers and educational institutions within the Union Territories.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments include omitting specific phrases from entries against serial numbers 4 and 5.
New entries are added for services by old age homes, electricity distribution utilities, and various other services.
The term 'declared tariff' is replaced with 'value of supply' in entry 14.
The year '2018' is updated to '2019' in entries 19A and 19B.
A clarification is added stating that Central and State Educational Boards are considered Educational Institutions for examination services.
This notification comes into effect from 27th July 2018.

960.summary: Notification No. 14/2018-Central Tax, issued on 8 October 2018, establishes the Authority for Advance Ruling in several Union territories, which is crucial for resolving GST-related queries. This notification specifies the members who will serve on this authority, including Joint Commissioners and Deputy Commissioners from the respective territories. For instance, in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Sh. D. P. S Khushwah and Sh. Subhankar Ghosh are appointed as members. Similarly, Chandigarh's authority includes Sh. Kumar Gaurav Dhawan and Sh. Rakesh Kumar Popli. The same structure is followed for Daman and Diu, Dadra and Nagar Haveli, and Lakshadweep, ensuring that each territory has qualified officials to address GST matters. This notification is effective immediately upon its publication in the official Gazette, providing clarity and support for taxpayers in these regions regarding their GST queries and issues.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2018-Central Tax issued on 8 October 2018.
Establishes the Authority for Advance Ruling in various Union territories.
Lists members for the Authority for Advance Ruling for Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Chandigarh, Daman and Diu, Dadra and Nagar Haveli, and Lakshadweep.
Members include Joint Commissioners and Deputy Commissioners from respective Union territories.
The notification comes into force on the date of its publication in the official Gazette.

961.summary: Notification No. 15/2018, issued on 26th July 2018, amends the earlier Notification No. 13/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. It introduces a new entry in the tax rate table, specifically for services provided by individual Direct Selling Agents (DSAs) to banks or non-banking financial companies (NBFCs). This new entry clarifies that the services must be supplied by individual DSAs who are not part of a corporate entity, partnership, or limited liability partnership. Additionally, the notification provides a new definition for 'renting of immovable property,' which encompasses various arrangements for allowing access or use of property, regardless of possession transfer. These changes are effective from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses that engage individual DSAs and those involved in renting immovable properties. This amendment aims to clarify the tax implications for these specific services and arrangements under the Union Territory GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry (serial number 11) is added to the Table for services supplied by individual Direct Selling Agents (DSAs) to banks or non-banking financial companies (NBFCs).
The new entry specifies that the services are provided by individual DSAs who are not a body corporate, partnership, or limited liability partnership firm.
The notification defines 'renting of immovable property' in a new clause (g) added to the Explanation.
The definition includes allowing access or use of immovable property, with or without transfer of possession.
This notification comes into effect from 27th July 2018.

962.summary: Notification No. 15/2018-Central Tax, issued on 8 October 2018, establishes the Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling in several Union Territories. This authority is crucial for taxpayers seeking clarity on GST matters. The notification specifies the members of the authority for each territory, including the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Chandigarh, Daman and Diu, Dadra and Nagar Haveli, and Lakshadweep. For instance, in Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Sh. Rakesh Kumar Sharma and Sh. Udit Prakash will serve as members. Similarly, Chandigarh's authority will include Ms. Manoranjan Kaur Virk and Sh. Ajit Balaji Joshi. The notification is effective immediately upon its publication in the official Gazette, which means that the newly formed Appellate Authorities can start functioning right away. This development is significant for businesses operating in these Union Territories, as it provides them with a structured mechanism to resolve disputes related to advance rulings on GST.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2018-Central Tax issued on 8 October 2018.
Establishes the Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling in various Union Territories.
Lists members for the Appellate Authority for Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Chandigarh, Daman and Diu, Dadra and Nagar Haveli, and Lakshadweep.
Members include Chief Commissioners and Commissioners from respective Union Territories and Central Tax Zones.
The notification comes into effect on the date of its publication in the official Gazette.

963.summary: Notification No. 16/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, introduces important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 14/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds the phrase 'or Union territory' after 'State Government' in the first paragraph, which broadens the scope of the notification to include Union territories alongside states. Additionally, it includes the phrase 'or to a Municipality under article 243W of the Constitution,' which recognizes the role of municipalities in the context of Union Territory taxation. These amendments are significant as they clarify the applicability of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (UTGST) to both Union territories and municipalities, ensuring that the tax framework is inclusive. The changes will come into effect from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses operating in Union territories and municipalities by aligning their tax obligations under the revised provisions.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 14/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment adds the phrase 'or Union territory' after 'State Government' in the first paragraph.
It also adds 'or to a Municipality under article 243W of the Constitution' after 'Constitution' in the first paragraph.
The changes will take effect from 27th July 2018.

964.summary: Notification No. 17/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 26th July 2018, introduces a clarification to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017. Specifically, it adds an explanation to the Table against serial number 3, in column (3), item (vi), stating that the term 'business' does not encompass activities or transactions carried out by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities when they act as public authorities. This change is significant as it delineates the scope of what constitutes a business under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act. The new provision will take effect from 27th July 2018. The principal notification was initially published on 28th June 2017 and was previously amended by notification No. 1/2018 on 25th January 2018. This clarification is essential for entities operating in the Union Territories to understand the boundaries of their business activities in relation to government functions.
key_points: Notification No. 17/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification inserts an explanation in the earlier notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate).
The inserted explanation clarifies that the term 'business' does not include activities by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities as public authorities.
The change applies specifically to the Table against serial number 3, in column (3), item (vi) of the earlier notification.
This notification comes into effect from 27th July 2018.
The principal notification No. 11/2017 was published on 28th June 2017.
The last amendment to the principal notification was made by notification No. 1/2018 on 25th January 2018.

965.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 18/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018, which introduces several amendments to the previous notification regarding tax rates. A new entry, S. No. 102 A, has been added for ethyl alcohol supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with petrol, which will be taxed at 2.5%. Additionally, S. No. 123 has been updated to clarify the exceptions for certain types of calcareous stones. Another new entry, S. No. 170A, has been introduced for fertilizer grade phosphoric acid, also at 2.5%. The entry for coir pith compost in S. No. 215 now specifies that it must be in unit containers and bear a brand name. In Schedule II, S. No. 57B has been omitted, while a new entry for bamboo flooring (S. No. 96A) has been added, which will be taxed at 6%. These changes will take effect from 27th July 2018, impacting businesses involved in the specified goods and services.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 26th July 2018.
New entry added: S. No. 102 A for ethyl alcohol supplied to Oil Marketing Companies for blending with motor spirit (petrol) at 2.5%.
S. No. 123 updated to specify certain exceptions for calcareous monumental or building stone.
New entry added: S. No. 170A for fertilizer grade phosphoric acid at 2.5%.
S. No. 215 now includes coir pith compost in unit containers with a brand name.
S. No. 57B omitted from Schedule II-6%.
New entry added: S. No. 96A for bamboo flooring at 6%.
This notification comes into force on 27th July 2018.

966.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 19/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018, which introduces several amendments to the previous notification No. 2/2017. New entries have been added to the tax rate schedule, including Sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, and sabai grass under S. No. 92 A, as well as vegetable materials for making jhadoo or broom sticks under S. No. 93 B. Additionally, the exemption for de-oiled rice bran under S. No. 102 A is clarified to apply from 25th January 2018. New entries for deities made of stone, marble, or wood, and Khali Dona have been added after S. No. 114. The amendment also updates S. No. 117 to include rupee notes or coins sold to the Reserve Bank of India or the Government of India. Furthermore, a new entry for coir pith compost has been introduced after S. No. 132 A, with specific conditions outlined. Lastly, sanitary towels and rakhi have been added as new entries after S. No. 146 and S. No. 151, respectively. These changes will come into effect on 27th July 2018.
key_points: Notification No. 19/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 26th July 2018.
New entries added for Sal leaves, siali leaves, sisal leaves, and sabai grass under S. No. 92 A.
New entry for vegetable materials for making jhadoo or broom sticks added under S. No. 93 B.
S. No. 102 A is amended to specify that the exemption for de-oiled rice bran applies from 25th January 2018.
New entries for deities made of stone, marble, or wood and Khali Dona added after S. No. 114.
S. No. 117 is amended to include rupee notes or coins sold to the Reserve Bank of India or the Government of India.
New entry for coir pith compost added after S. No. 132 A with specific conditions.
New entries for sanitary towels, pads, and rakhi added after S. No. 146 and S. No. 151 respectively.

967.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 20/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 26th July 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new proviso that specifies that input tax credit accumulated on supplies received from 1st August 2018 onwards will not be applicable to certain goods. These goods are identified in the notification under serial numbers 1 to 7. Additionally, any unutilized input tax credit that has been accumulated for these goods, up until July 2018, will lapse after 31st July 2018. This change primarily affects businesses dealing with the specified goods, as they will no longer be able to claim input tax credit for purchases made after the specified date, and any remaining credits from prior purchases will be forfeited. It is crucial for affected businesses to be aware of these changes to manage their tax credits effectively.
key_points: Notification No. 20/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 26th July 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 5/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added stating that input tax credit accumulated on supplies received on or after 1st August 2018 will not apply to certain goods.
The goods affected are listed under serial numbers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 6A, 6B, 6C, and 7 of the specified table.
Any unutilized input tax credit for these goods, accumulated until July 2018, will lapse after 31st July 2018.

968.summary: Notification No. 21/2018, issued on July 26, 2018, provides an exemption from excess Union Territory tax for intra-state supplies of certain handicraft goods. This exemption is applicable to goods that are predominantly handmade and possess aesthetic or cultural significance. The notification includes a detailed table listing various handicraft items along with their respective tariff items and tax rates. Most of these goods are taxed at a rate of 6%, while some items, such as mats and handmade lace, are taxed at lower rates of 2.5% or even 1.5%. Examples of the exempted goods include handcrafted candles, handbags, wooden frames, and handmade carpets. This decision was made based on the recommendations of the GST Council, emphasizing the importance of supporting the handicraft sector in the public interest. Business owners dealing in these handicraft items should take note of these tax rates to and benefit from the exemptions provided.
key_points: Notification No. 21/2018 issued on 26th July 2018.
Exempts intra-state supplies of specified handicraft goods from excess Union Territory tax.
Handicraft goods are defined as predominantly handmade items with aesthetic or cultural significance.
The exemption applies to various goods listed in a table with specific tariff items and corresponding tax rates.
Most handicraft goods listed are taxed at a rate of 6%, while some are taxed at 2.5% or 1.5%.
Examples of exempt goods include handcrafted candles, handbags, wooden frames, and handmade carpets.
The notification is based on the recommendations of the GST Council and is in the public interest.

969.summary: On 6th August 2018, the Government of India issued Notification No. 22/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The key change in this notification is the extension of the deadline for compliance from 30th September 2018 to 30th September 2019. This amendment is made under section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and follows recommendations from the GST Council. The last amendment prior to this was Notification No. 12/2018, dated 29th June 2018. This change is significant for taxpayers operating in Union Territories, as it provides them with additional time to meet their tax obligations. The notification was published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that the information is officially recorded and accessible. Overall, this extension aims to ease compliance pressures for businesses in the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 22/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 6th August 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment changes the deadline from '30th day of September, 2018' to '30th day of September, 2019'.
The last amendment before this was Notification No. 12/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 29th June 2018.
The amendment is made under the powers of section 8(1) of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The amendment is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The change affects the compliance timeline for taxpayers in Union Territories.

970.summary: On 20th September 2018, the Government of India issued Notification No. 23/2018 to clarify the ownership criteria for exemptions under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax. This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017, which was published on 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds an Explanation in the Table against serial number 41, stating that for an entity to qualify for exemption, the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory must hold 50% or more ownership. This ownership can be either direct or through another entity that is wholly owned by the government. This change aims to provide clearer guidelines on the eligibility for tax exemptions, ensuring that entities with significant government ownership are properly recognized. The principal notification had previously been amended by Notification No. 14/2018 on 26th July 2018, but this latest amendment focuses specifically on ownership criteria.
key_points: Notification No. 23/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 20th September 2018.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
An Explanation has been inserted in the Table against serial number 41, column (3).
The new Explanation clarifies that for exemption, the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory must have 50% or more ownership in the entity.
Ownership can be direct or through an entity wholly owned by the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territory.
The principal notification No. 12/2017 was last amended by notification No. 14/2018 on 26th July 2018.

971.summary: Notification No. 24/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the addition of new goods entries, such as 'Marble and travertine, crude or roughly trimmed' under the new S. No. 123A, while S. Nos. 23 and 24 are removed from Schedule I, which has a 2.5% tax rate. Additionally, S. No. 198A has been re-numbered to 198AA, with a new entry for 'Natural cork, raw or simply prepared' added as S. No. 198A. In Schedule II, new entries for various natural cork products have been introduced, and S. Nos. 102 and 126 have been omitted. Schedule III also sees the introduction of 'Retreaded or used pneumatic tyres' under the re-numbered S. No. 121B. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses dealing with the specified goods and services.
key_points: Notification No. 24/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries for goods such as 'Marble and travertine, crude or roughly trimmed' under S. No. 123A are added.
S. Nos. 23 and 24 in Schedule I with a 2.5% tax rate are omitted.
S. No. 198A is re-numbered to 198AA, and a new S. No. 198A for 'Natural cork, raw or simply prepared' is added.
In Schedule II, new entries for natural cork products are added, and S. Nos. 102 and 126 are omitted.
Schedule III sees the addition of 'Retreaded or used pneumatic tyres' under S. No. 121A, which is re-numbered to 121B.
This notification comes into effect on 1st January 2019.

972.summary: Notification No. 25/2018, issued on 31st December 2018, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. Specifically, it replaces S. No. 43A with new entries that detail the tax treatment for frozen vegetables and provisionally preserved vegetables. Additionally, a new entry, 121A, is added to include printed or manuscript music, which will be taxed under the specified category starting from 1st January 2019. Furthermore, entry 153 is introduced to cover gift items received by the President, Prime Minister, Governors, or Chief Ministers through public auctions, where the proceeds are intended for public or charitable causes. These changes are significant for businesses dealing in these goods and services, as they clarify the tax implications and with the updated tax structure. The amendments are effective immediately from the start of 2019.
key_points: Notification No. 25/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
S. No. 43A is replaced with new entries for frozen vegetables and provisionally preserved vegetables.
New entry 121A is added for printed or manuscript music, effective from 1st January 2019.
New entry 153 is added for gift items received by certain public officials through government auctions.
The amendments come into force on 1st January 2019.

973.summary: On 31st January 2019, a corrigendum was issued to Notification No. 26/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 31st December 2018. The key change made in this corrigendum is the correction of a figure in the notification text; specifically, the number '32' has been amended to '34'. This correction is noted in line 1 of the original notification. The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, and is referenced as G.S.R. 1269(E). This change is important for businesses and stakeholders who rely on the accurate figures in tax notifications for compliance and reporting purposes. The corrigendum is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue, and is referenced under F.No.354/432/2018-TRU (pt). This adjustment ensures that the tax rates are correctly stated, which is crucial for the proper application of Union Territory tax rates.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 26/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 31st December 2018.
The corrigendum was issued on 31st January 2019.
In the original notification, the figure '32' has been corrected to '34'.
This change appears in line 1 of the notification text.
The original notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The original notification was published under G.S.R. 1269(E) on 31st December 2018.
The corrigendum is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.
The reference number for the corrigendum is F.No.354/432/2018-TRU (pt).

974.summary: Notification No. 26/2018 provides an exemption from Union Territory tax for the intra-State supply of gold classified under heading 7108 of the Customs Tariff Act. This exemption applies specifically when the gold is supplied by a Nominated Agency as part of the 'Export Against Supply by Nominated Agency' scheme. To benefit from this exemption, the recipient must export jewellery made from the supplied gold within 90 days. Additionally, they are required to submit proof of export, such as a shipping bill or bill of export, to the Nominated Agency within 120 days from the date of supply. If the recipient fails to provide this proof within the stipulated time, the Nominated Agency is responsible for paying the applicable Union Territory tax along with interest. This notification is effective from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses involved in the export of jewellery made from gold supplied under this scheme.
key_points: Exempts intra-State supply of gold under heading 7108 from Union Territory tax.
Applicable when supplied by Nominated Agency under the 'Export Against Supply by Nominated Agency' scheme.
Recipient must export jewellery made from the gold within 90 days of supply.
Recipient must provide proof of export to the Nominated Agency within 120 days of supply.
If proof of export is not provided, the Nominated Agency must pay the Union Territory tax with interest.
Notification comes into force on 1st January 2019.

975.summary: Notification No. 27/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018, brings several amendments to the earlier notification No. 11/2017. One significant change is the introduction of a new entry, serial number 38, which pertains to services related to renewable energy projects such as bio-gas plants and solar power systems. Additionally, the definition of 'specified organisation' has been expanded to include Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam Limited and entities defined under the Haj Committee Act, 2002. The term 'goods carriage' is now explicitly defined according to the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988. Other modifications include the removal of 'school, college' from item (i) under serial number 7, and various adjustments to existing items in the tax rate table. These changes will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses involved in the specified services and sectors.
key_points: This notification is No. 27/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 31st December 2018.
It amends the earlier notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries have been added to the tax rate table, including serial number 38 for services related to renewable energy projects.
The definition of 'specified organisation' now includes Kumaon Mandal Vikas Nigam Limited and entities defined under the Haj Committee Act, 2002.
The term 'goods carriage' is now defined as per the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988.
Certain items in the table have had their descriptions modified, such as the removal of 'school, college' from item (i) under serial number 7.
The notification will come into effect on 1st January 2019.

976.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 28/2018 on 31st December 2018, which amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This new notification introduces several changes effective from 1st January 2019. Notably, it adds new entries for services provided by goods transport agencies to government departments and local authorities, classified under serial number 21B, and for banking services offered to Basic Saving Bank Deposit account holders under the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, listed as serial number 27A. Additionally, the description for serial number 34A has been updated to include 'banking companies'. The entry for serial number 66 has been revised to refer to different headings, while serial number 67 has been removed entirely. Furthermore, a new entry for rehabilitation services provided by recognized professionals has been added under serial number 74A. Lastly, the notification clarifies the definition of 'financial institution' by referencing the Reserve Bank of India Act. These changes will affect service providers and financial institutions operating in the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 28/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 12/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries added for services provided by goods transport agencies (serial number 21B) and banking services for BSBD account holders (serial number 27A).
Entry for serial number 34A updated to include 'banking companies' in the description.
Entry for serial number 66 substituted to read 'Heading 9992 or Heading 9963'.
Serial number 67 and its entries have been omitted.
New entry added for rehabilitation services provided by professionals recognized under the Rehabilitation Council of India Act (serial number 74A).
The definition of 'financial institution' has been added to include the meaning from the Reserve Bank of India Act.

977.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 29/2018 on 31st December 2018, amending the earlier Notification No. 13/2017 regarding Union Territory Tax rates. This new notification introduces a proviso that exempts services provided by goods transport agencies to specific government departments and local authorities. Additionally, it adds new entries for services provided by business facilitators to banking companies and agents of business correspondents to business correspondents, which are now listed as serial numbers 12 and 13. Furthermore, entry 14 specifies that security services provided to registered persons are subject to certain exemptions, particularly for government entities and registered persons under section 10 of the GST Act. An important addition is clause (h) in the Explanation, which clarifies that these provisions also apply to the Parliament and State Legislatures. These amendments will take effect from 1st January 2019, impacting businesses and government entities involved in these services.
key_points: Notification No. 29/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 31st December 2018.
This notification amends Notification No. 13/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new proviso is added to serial number 1, stating that certain services by goods transport agencies to government entities are exempt.
New entries for services provided by business facilitators to banking companies and agents of business correspondents to business correspondents are added as serial numbers 12 and 13.
Entry 14 introduces security services provided to registered persons, with specific exemptions for government entities and certain registered persons.
An additional clause (h) is added to the Explanation, stating that the provisions apply to Parliament and State Legislatures as well.
The changes come into effect on 1st January 2019.

978.summary: Notification No. 30/2018-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on December 31, 2018, introduces a significant clarification to Notification No. 11/2017. Specifically, it adds a new explanation to the existing provisions regarding the supply of services. The new explanation states that the rules outlined in item (vi) against serial number 9 do not apply to any service other than the transport of goods from one location in India to another. This change means that businesses providing services unrelated to the transport of goods will not be affected by the previous stipulations in this item. Additionally, the existing explanation in this item has been renumbered as Explanation 1 to accommodate this new clarification. This notification is set to take effect from January 1, 2019, ensuring that all stakeholders are aware of the updated scope of applicability regarding service supplies under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act.
key_points: Inserts a new explanation in Notification No. 11/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) against serial number 9, column (3), item (vi).
The new explanation clarifies that the item does not apply to services other than transport of goods within India.
The existing explanation in the same item is renumbered as Explanation 1.
This notification comes into effect on January 1, 2019.

979.summary: On 30th June 2017, a corrigendum was issued to amend Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) which was originally published on 28th June 2017. The amendment specifically alters line 42 of the notification. The original text listed only the code '8702 10', but this has now been expanded to include additional codes: '8702 10, 8702 20, 8702 30, 8702 90'. This change broadens the scope of the vehicles subject to the compensation cess. Additionally, the phrase 'ten or more' has been revised to 'not more than 13', which likely affects the classification or conditions under which the cess applies. These changes are important for manufacturers and dealers of vehicles falling under these codes, as they may influence the applicable rates and compliance requirements. It is essential for affected parties to review these updates to ensure they are aligned with the new specifications.
key_points: The corrigendum was issued on 30th June 2017.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In line 42, 'ten or more' is changed to 'not more than 13'.

980.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) on 28th June 2017, which establishes the rates of compensation cess applicable to various goods starting from 1st July 2017. This notification outlines specific cess rates for a range of products, including pan-masala at 60%, aerated waters and lemonade at 12%, and various types of tobacco products with rates varying significantly, such as cigarettes which are taxed at 5% plus additional amounts per thousand. The detailed schedule in the notification categorizes goods by their respective chapters, headings, and tariff items, providing clarity on the applicable rates. This change affects manufacturers, suppliers, and consumers of these goods, as they will need to account for the new cess rates in their pricing and compliance processes. The introduction of these rates is part of the broader implementation of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) framework aimed at standardizing tax structures across the country.
key_points: Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification specifies the rates of compensation cess for various goods effective from 1st July 2017.
Cess rates range from 0% to 290% depending on the type of goods, such as pan-masala (60%) and cigarettes (5% + specific amounts per thousand).
The notification includes a detailed schedule listing goods by chapter, heading, and tariff item with corresponding cess rates.

981.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 01/2017 on 28th June 2017, which officially announces that the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017 will come into effect on 1st July 2017. This means that from this date, all provisions of the Act will be applicable. The notification was published in the Official Gazette of India, indicating its formal recognition and importance. It is issued under the authority of the Ministry of Finance, specifically the Department of Revenue, highlighting the government's commitment to implementing the GST framework. This Act is crucial as it provides for the compensation to states for any revenue loss due to the introduction of GST. Businesses and stakeholders in the economy should be aware that the provisions of this Act will start impacting them from the appointed date, shaping the landscape of indirect taxation in India.
key_points: The notification is dated 28th June 2017.
It appoints 1st July 2017 as the date when the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017 comes into force.
This notification is issued under the powers of sub-section (3) of section 1 of the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017.
The notification is published in Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i) of the Official Gazette of India, Extraordinary.
The notification is referenced as G.S.R. …..(E).
The notification is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.

982.summary: On 1st July 2017, a corrigendum was issued for Notification No. 2/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate), which was originally published on 28th June 2017. The change specifically corrects line 38 of the notification, where the phrase 'scheme of classification of services' was amended. The new wording clarifies that it refers to the 'scheme of classification of services annexed to notification No. 11/2017 -Central Tax (Rate)', which was also published on the same date. This correction ensures that readers understand the exact source of the classification scheme for services, linking it directly to the appropriate notification. This change is important for businesses and stakeholders who rely on accurate references for compliance and understanding of the compensation cess framework. The amendment does not alter any rates or tax periods but provides clarity on the classification scheme's origin.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 2/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
The corrigendum was issued on 1st July 2017.
In line 38 of the original notification, the phrase 'scheme of classification of services' is corrected.
The corrected phrase now reads 'scheme of classification of services annexed to notification No. 11/2017 -Central Tax (Rate)'.
Notification No. 11/2017 -Central Tax (Rate) was also published on 28th June 2017.
The reference to the scheme is now linked to the specific notification number and its publication details.
The corrigendum clarifies the correct source of the classification scheme for services.

983.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 2/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) on 28th June 2017, which outlines the cess rates applicable to specific services. This notification specifies that the cess will be levied on the transfer of the right to use goods and the transfer of rights in goods without transferring the title. For these services, the cess rate will be the same as the rate applicable to similar goods. Importantly, any other supply of services will not attract any cess, meaning it will be taxed at a nil rate. This notification is effective from 1st July 2017, impacting businesses involved in the specified services. Companies and service providers should take note of these changes to ensure proper compliance with the new cess rates as outlined in the notification.
key_points: Notification No. 2/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
Cess is levied on specific services as per the table provided in the notification.
Services include transfer of the right to use goods and transfer of rights in goods without title.
The cess rate for these services is the same as that applicable on the supply of similar goods.
Any other supply of services is subject to a nil rate of cess.
The notification comes into effect from 1st July 2017.

984.summary: On 18th July 2017, the Government of India issued Notification No. 3/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically alters the entries in column (4) of the Schedule for several items. For instance, the entry for S. No. 10 is now '5% + Rs. 2076 per thousand', while S. No. 11 has been updated to '5% + Rs. 3668 per thousand'. Similarly, S. No. 12 also reflects '5% + Rs. 2076 per thousand'. Other changes include S. No. 13, which is now '5% + Rs. 2747 per thousand', S. No. 14 updated to '5% + Rs. 3668 per thousand', and S. No. 15, which now states '36% + Rs. 4170 per thousand'. These changes will impact businesses that deal with the specified goods, as they will need to apply the new rates accordingly from the effective date of this notification.
key_points: Notification No. 3/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) was issued on 18th July 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments include changes to the entries in column (4) of the Schedule for specific items.
For S. No. 10, the entry is changed to '5% + Rs. 2076 per thousand'.
For S. No. 11, the entry is changed to '5% + Rs. 3668 per thousand'.
For S. No. 12, the entry is changed to '5% + Rs. 2076 per thousand'.
For S. No. 13, the entry is changed to '5% + Rs. 2747 per thousand'.
For S. No. 14, the entry is changed to '5% + Rs. 3668 per thousand'.
For S. No. 15, the entry is changed to '36% + Rs. 4170 per thousand'.

985.summary: Notification No. 04/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate), issued on July 20, 2017, introduces an exemption for intra-State supplies of second hand goods. This exemption is specifically for registered persons who are engaged in the buying and selling of second hand goods. To qualify for this exemption, these registered persons must pay the goods and services tax compensation cess based on the value of their outward supply of second hand goods. Importantly, this exemption applies when the supplier of these second hand goods is not registered. The notification is grounded in the provisions of the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017, as well as the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change is significant for businesses involved in the second hand goods market, as it alleviates the tax burden when dealing with unregistered suppliers, thereby encouraging trade in this sector.
key_points: Exemption applies to intra-State supplies of second hand goods.
The exemption is for registered persons dealing in buying and selling second hand goods.
The registered person must pay GST compensation cess on the value of their outward supply of second hand goods.
This exemption is applicable when the supplier of the second hand goods is not registered.
The notification is issued under section 11 of the Goods and Services Tax (Compensation to States) Act, 2017.
It is also linked to section 11 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was issued on July 20, 2017.

986.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 5/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) on 11th September 2017, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces new entries for vehicles under serial numbers 47 and 48, which now include specific categories of vehicles with both spark-ignition and compression-ignition engines, respectively. For instance, ambulances and three-wheeled vehicles are included, with a cess rate of 15% for both categories. Additionally, serial number 52 has been updated to apply a cess rate of 17% for vehicles with engine capacity not exceeding 1500cc. New categories have also been added, such as serial number 52A for vehicles exceeding 1500cc at a rate of 20%, and serial number 52B for SUVs, which are taxed at 22%. The definition of SUVs has been clarified to include vehicles over 4000 mm in length and with a ground clearance of 170 mm or more. This notification impacts manufacturers and dealers of these vehicles, as they will need to adjust their pricing and compliance accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 5/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) was issued on 11th September 2017.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Serial numbers 47 and 48 in the Schedule are replaced with new entries for vehicles with both spark-ignition and electric motors, and compression-ignition and electric motors.

For serial number 47, the new entries include ambulances, three-wheeled vehicles, and motor vehicles with engine capacity not exceeding 1200cc and length not exceeding 4000 mm, with a cess rate of 15%.
For serial number 48, the new entries include similar categories but for diesel vehicles with engine capacity not exceeding 1500cc, also at a cess rate of 15%.
Serial number 52 is amended to include motor vehicles with engine capacity not exceeding 1500cc at a cess rate of 17%.
New serial number 52A is introduced for vehicles exceeding 1500cc at a cess rate of 20%, and 52B for SUVs at a cess rate of 22%.
The definition of SUVs includes vehicles over 4000 mm in length and with a ground clearance of 170 mm or more.

987.summary: Notification No. 6/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate), issued on 13th October 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier notification No. 2/2017, which was published on 28th June 2017. This amendment adds a new entry, serial number 2A, specifically for the leasing of motor vehicles that were purchased and leased before 1st July 2017. The compensation cess for this category is set at 65% of the rate that applies to the supply of similar goods where there is a transfer of title. Importantly, this new entry will not be applicable after 1st July 2020. This change primarily affects businesses involved in leasing motor vehicles that were acquired before the GST implementation date, as they will now have a specific cess rate applicable to their transactions. It clarifies the tax treatment for these leases, ensuring that businesses are aware of the cess rate they need to apply during the specified period.
key_points: This notification is No. 6/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) issued on 13th October 2017.
It amends the earlier notification No. 2/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry, serial number 2A, is added for 'Leasing of motor vehicles purchased and leased prior to 1st July 2017'.
The cess rate for this new entry is set at 65% of the rate applicable on the supply of similar goods involving transfer of title.
The new entry specifies that it will not apply on or after 1st July 2020.

988.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 7/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) on 13th October 2017, establishing a cess rate of 65% for motor vehicles classified under tariff items 8702 and 8703. This rate is derived from the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Importantly, this notification states that the cess provisions will cease to apply after 1st July 2020. To qualify for this cess rate, specific conditions must be met: the motor vehicle must have been purchased by the lesser before 1st July 2017 and supplied on lease before that date. Additionally, the supplier must be a registered person who acquired the vehicle prior to 1st July 2017 and has not claimed any input tax credit on it. This notification primarily affects businesses involved in the leasing of motor vehicles, ensuring they comply with the specified conditions to benefit from the reduced cess rate.
key_points: Notification No. 7/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) issued on 13th October 2017.
The notification specifies a cess rate of 65% on motor vehicles under tariff items 8702 and 8703.
This cess rate is applicable as per Notification No. 1/2017-Compensation Cess (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The provisions of this notification will not apply after 1st July 2020.
Conditions for applicability include that the motor vehicle must have been purchased by the lesser before 1st July 2017 and supplied on lease before the same date.
The supplier of the motor vehicle must be a registered person who purchased the vehicle before 1st July 2017 and did not claim input tax credit on it.

989.summary: Notification No. 01/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 19, 2017, establishes June 22, 2017, as the effective date for several provisions of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Specifically, this notification activates sections 1 through 5, 10, and 22 through 30, along with sections 139, 146, and 164 of the Act. This means that from June 22, businesses and individuals will need to comply with the rules and regulations outlined in these sections. These sections cover various aspects of GST, including definitions, registration requirements, and the framework for tax administration. The Central Board of Excise and Customs is responsible for this notification, indicating the government's commitment to implementing GST. This change affects all taxpayers and businesses operating in India, as they will need to align their operations with the new GST framework starting from the specified date.
key_points: The notification is dated June 19, 2017.
It appoints June 22, 2017, as the date when specific provisions of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, will come into force.
The provisions coming into force include sections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 10, 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 139, 146, and 164.
This notification is issued by the Central Board of Excise and Customs.

990.summary: Notification No. 01/2017-Integrated Tax, issued on 19th June 2017, establishes 22nd June 2017 as the date when specific provisions of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will take effect. The sections that will come into force include 1, 2, 3, 14, 20, and 22. This means that from this date, the foundational aspects of the Integrated GST framework will be operational, impacting how businesses engage with the tax system. The notification is a crucial step in implementing the GST regime, which aims to streamline tax processes across India. It is important for businesses to be aware of these changes as they will need to comply with the new provisions starting from the appointed date. This marks a significant transition in the taxation landscape, facilitating a unified tax structure for goods and services across the country.
key_points: The notification is dated 19th June 2017.
It appoints 22nd June 2017 as the effective date for certain provisions.
Sections 1, 2, 3, 14, 20, and 22 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will come into force on this date.
This notification is issued under the powers of sub-section (3) of section 1 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

991.summary: On 29th July 2019, a corrigendum was issued for Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax, originally published on 19th June 2017. This corrigendum specifically addresses a wording error in the English version of the notification. In the document, on page 22, line 3, the phrase 'the Central Board of Excise and Customs' has been corrected to 'the Government'. This change is significant as it clarifies that the authority responsible for the notification is the Government rather than the Central Board of Excise and Customs. The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i), and this correction helps ensure that the legal text accurately reflects the intended authority. The reference number for this corrigendum is G.S.R. (E), and it was issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue. This update is important for all stakeholders who refer to this notification for compliance and understanding of GST regulations.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax dated 19th June 2017.
The corrigendum was issued on 29th July 2019.
The specific change is in the English version of the notification on page 22, line 3.
The phrase 'the Central Board of Excise and Customs' is corrected to 'the Government'.
This change clarifies the authority responsible for the notification.
The notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i).
The reference number for this corrigendum is G.S.R. (E).
The corrigendum is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.

992.summary: Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 19, 2017, establishes the appointment and jurisdiction of various Central Tax officers under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This notification designates roles for Principal Chief Commissioners, Chief Commissioners, Principal Commissioners, and other officers, granting them specific powers related to tax administration. The notification includes detailed tables that outline the territorial jurisdictions of these officers. For instance, Table I specifies the jurisdictions of Principal Chief Commissioners and Chief Commissioners over their subordinate officers, while Table II details the jurisdictions of Principal Commissioners and Commissioners of Central Tax. Additionally, Tables III and IV define the jurisdictions of Commissioners of Central Tax (Appeals) and Commissioners of Central Tax (Audit), respectively. This structured approach aims to streamline tax administration and ensure clarity in the roles and responsibilities of tax officers across different regions.
key_points: Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax was issued on June 19, 2017.
The notification appoints various levels of Central Tax officers, including Principal Chief Commissioners and Assistant Commissioners.
It vests these officers with powers under the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Territorial jurisdictions for these officers are specified in Tables I, II, III, and IV of the notification.
Table I details the jurisdiction of Principal Chief Commissioners and Chief Commissioners over Principal Commissioners and Commissioners of Central Tax.
Table II outlines the jurisdiction of Principal Commissioners and Commissioners of Central Tax.
Table III specifies the jurisdiction of Commissioners of Central Tax (Appeals) and Additional Commissioners of Central Tax (Appeals).
Table IV describes the jurisdiction of Commissioners of Central Tax (Audit).

993.summary: Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax, issued on 19th June 2017, introduces important provisions regarding the registration of certain online services under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act. Specifically, it empowers the Principal Commissioner of Central Tax in Bengaluru West to grant registration for online information and database access or retrieval services. These services are provided by entities located in non-taxable territories and are received by non-taxable online recipients. This means that businesses or individuals offering such services from outside India can now seek registration through the designated authority in Bengaluru. The notification clarifies the definitions of the services and recipients involved, ensuring that there is no ambiguity in the terms used. This change is effective from 22nd June 2017, allowing affected parties to understand their registration requirements under the GST framework. Overall, this notification streamlines the process for non-resident service providers to comply with Indian GST regulations.
key_points: Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax was issued on 19th June 2017.
This notification empowers the Principal Commissioner of Central Tax, Bengaluru West, to grant registration.
The registration pertains to online information and database access or retrieval services.
These services are provided by a person located in a non-taxable territory.
The services must be received by a non-taxable online recipient.
The notification comes into effect on 22nd June 2017.
Definitions for 'online information and database access or retrieval services' and 'non-taxable online recipient' are referenced from the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

994.summary: The 'Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017' notification, effective from 22nd June 2017, outlines the procedures and requirements for registered persons opting for the composition levy under section 10. To opt for this scheme, individuals must file an intimation using FORM GST CMP-01 within 30 days after the appointed day. The tax rates for those eligible for the composition scheme are set at 1% for manufacturers, 2.5% for specific suppliers, and 0.5% for other eligible suppliers. Additionally, registered persons are required to clearly state 'composition taxable person' on their bills of supply and signage at their business locations. The option to pay tax under section 10 remains valid as long as the registered person continues to meet all specified conditions. If a registered person wishes to withdraw from the composition scheme, they must file FORM GST CMP-04 within seven days of the event that triggers the withdrawal. This notification is crucial for businesses looking to simplify their tax compliance under GST.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017'.
It comes into effect from 22nd June 2017.
Registered persons opting for composition levy must file intimation in FORM GST CMP-01 within 30 days after the appointed day.
The option to pay tax under section 10 is effective from the beginning of the financial year if filed under sub-rule (3) of rule 3.
The tax rates for composition levy are 1% for manufacturers, 2.5% for specific suppliers, and 0.5% for other eligible suppliers.
Registered persons must mention 'composition taxable person' on their bills of supply and signage.
The option to pay tax under section 10 remains valid as long as all conditions are met.
Withdrawal from the composition scheme requires filing FORM GST CMP-04 within seven days of the event.

995.summary: Notification No. 03/2017-Integrated Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, establishes 1st July 2017 as the date when specific provisions of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will take effect.
