1101.summary: Notification No. 7/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, introduces exemptions from integrated tax for specific inward supplies of goods. This includes supplies made by the Canteen Stores Department (CSD) to Unit Run Canteens and authorized customers, as well as goods supplied by Unit Run Canteens to authorized customers. The notification clarifies that the terms 'tariff item', 'sub-heading', 'heading', and 'Chapter' are defined according to the First Schedule of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. Furthermore, the interpretation rules of this Customs Tariff Act will apply to the notification. Importantly, these exemptions will take effect from 1st July 2017, benefiting entities involved in these specific transactions by relieving them from the integrated tax obligations under the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 7/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification exempts certain inward supplies of goods from integrated tax under section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The exempted supplies include goods supplied by the Canteen Stores Department (CSD) to Unit Run Canteens.
It also includes goods supplied by the CSD to authorized customers.
Additionally, it covers goods supplied by Unit Run Canteens to authorized customers.
The notification defines 'tariff item', 'sub-heading', 'heading', and 'Chapter' as per the First Schedule to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
The rules for interpreting the Customs Tariff Act apply to this notification.
This exemption comes into effect from 1st July 2017.

1102.summary: Notification No. 07/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 28, 2017, introduces exemptions from central tax for specific supplies of goods. It exempts the supply of goods by the Canteen Stores Department (CSD) to Unit Run Canteens, as well as to authorized customers. Additionally, it also exempts the supply of goods from Unit Run Canteens to authorized customers. All these exemptions are applicable under section 9 of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act, 2017. This means that these specific transactions will not incur central tax, which can help reduce costs for the involved parties. The exemptions are effective from July 1, 2017, allowing businesses and canteens to benefit from this tax relief immediately. This notification is significant for those in the supply chain involving CSD and Unit Run Canteens, as it directly impacts their tax obligations.

Exempts supplies of goods by the CSD to authorized customers from central tax under section 9 of the CGST Act.

This notification is effective from July 1, 2017.

1103.summary: On July 5, 2017, a corrigendum was issued to Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), which originally came out on June 28, 2017. This corrigendum specifically amends Sl No. 10 in the Table of the original notification. The tax rate for 'Rental services of transport vehicles with or without operators' was initially set at 9%, but it has now been corrected to 18%. This change directly impacts businesses that provide these rental services, as they will now need to charge a higher integrated tax rate of 18% instead of the previously stated 9%. This adjustment is crucial for compliance and accurate tax reporting for those in the transport rental sector. All taxpayers involved in these services should take note of this updated tax rate to ensure they are aligned with the latest regulations.
key_points: This is a corrigendum to Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
The corrigendum was issued on 5th July 2017.
In the Table of the original notification, Sl No. 10 was amended.
Column (3) and column (4) for Sl No. 10 originally stated a tax rate of 9%.
The amended text now states a tax rate of 18% for the same services.
The services affected are 'Rental services of transport vehicles with or without operators'.
This change applies to all taxpayers providing the specified rental services.

1104.summary: The corrigendum to Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces a significant amendment regarding the valuation of taxable services related to the transportation of goods by vessels from outside India. Specifically, it states that if the value of such services is not available, it will be deemed to be 10% of the CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) value of the imported goods. This change is crucial for businesses involved in importing goods, as it clarifies how to determine the taxable value when certain information is missing. Additionally, the notification updates the numbering of existing clauses, changing the previous clause '4' to '5' and '5' to '6'. It also corrects the reference to the scheme of classification of services, aligning it with the details provided in Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate). These amendments ensure that the notification is accurate and provides clear guidance for compliance in the context of integrated tax on imported goods.
key_points: Inserts a new clause stating that if the value of taxable service for transportation of goods by a vessel from outside India to a customs station in India is not available, it is deemed to be 10% of the CIF value of imported goods.
Changes the numbering of the existing clause from '4' to '5' in the notification.
Replaces 'scheme of classification of services' with 'scheme of classification of services annexed to notification No. 11/2017 -Central Tax (Rate)'.
Changes the numbering of another existing clause from '5' to '6' in the notification.

1105.summary: The Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on June 28, 2017, outlines the integrated tax rates applicable to various services under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. It establishes that all services classified under Chapter 99 will incur an 18% tax rate. Construction services, including works contracts, are also subject to an 18% tax. For accommodation services, hotels with a declared tariff between ₹1,000 and ₹2,500 per day will be taxed at a reduced rate of 12%. Passenger transport services provided by rail in first class or air-conditioned coaches are taxed at a lower rate of 5%. Additionally, goods transport services by rail and services provided by goods transport agencies are taxed at 5% and 12%, respectively. Financial services, particularly those related to chit funds, will incur a tax rate of 12%. This notification is crucial for service providers and businesses in these sectors to understand their tax obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on June 28, 2017.
The notification specifies integrated tax rates for various services under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
All services under Chapter 99 are taxed at 18%.
Construction services, including works contracts, are taxed at 18%.
Accommodation services in hotels with a declared tariff of ₹1,000 to ₹2,500 per day are taxed at 12%.
Passenger transport services by rail in first class or air-conditioned coaches are taxed at 5%.
Goods transport services by rail and services of goods transport agencies are taxed at 5% and 12%, respectively.
Financial services, including chit fund services, are taxed at 12%.

1106.summary: Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 28, 2017, introduces an exemption from central tax for registered persons receiving intra-State supplies from unregistered suppliers. This exemption is applicable only if the total value of such supplies does not exceed five thousand rupees in a single day. The exemption is granted under section 11 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, following recommendations from the GST Council. This means that registered businesses can benefit from this exemption, reducing their tax burden when sourcing goods or services from unregistered suppliers, as long as they stay within the specified limit. The notification will take effect from July 1, 2017, allowing businesses to plan their purchases accordingly. This change aims to support smaller transactions and ease the compliance burden for registered taxpayers engaging with unregistered suppliers.
key_points: Exemption from central tax for intra-State supplies received by registered persons from unregistered suppliers.
This exemption applies only if the total value of supplies does not exceed five thousand rupees in a day.
The exemption is granted under sub-section (1) of section 11 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification is effective from July 1, 2017.
The exemption is based on recommendations from the GST Council.
The notification is issued by the Ministry of Finance, Department of Revenue.
The notification number is 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate).

1107.summary: Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, introduces exemptions for specific inter-State supply of services from Integrated Tax. Notably, services provided by entities registered under section 12AA of the Income-tax Act for charitable activities are exempt from tax. Additionally, pure services offered to government authorities in relation to functions assigned to Panchayats or Municipalities are also exempt. Business entities with an aggregate turnover of up to twenty lakh rupees in the previous financial year can benefit from these exemptions. The notification outlines various transportation services for passengers and goods that qualify for tax exemption under certain conditions. However, it is important to note that some services, including renting of immovable property and transport of goods or passengers, are excluded from these exemptions. The notification categorizes these services under various headings, providing clarity on the specific conditions that apply to each exemption. This change primarily affects service providers and businesses engaged in these specified activities.
key_points: Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification exempts certain inter-State supply of services from Integrated Tax as specified in the table.
Services provided by entities registered under section 12AA of the Income-tax Act for charitable activities are exempt from tax.
Pure services provided to government authorities related to functions entrusted to Panchayats or Municipalities are also exempt.
Services provided to business entities with an aggregate turnover of up to twenty lakh rupees in the preceding financial year are exempt.
Transportation services for passengers and goods under specified conditions are exempt from tax.
Certain services, such as renting of immovable property and transport of goods or passengers, are excluded from these exemptions.
The notification includes various headings under which these services fall, detailing specific conditions for exemption.

1108.summary: Notification No. 09/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 28, 2017, introduces an exemption for certain intra-State supplies of goods or services. Specifically, this exemption applies to deductors under section 51 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, when they receive supplies from suppliers who are not registered. However, this exemption is conditional; the deductor must not be liable to be registered under any provisions other than sub-clause (vi) of section 24 of the Act. This means that if a deductor falls under this specific category, they will not have to pay the central tax that would typically apply to such transactions. The exemption is effective from July 1, 2017, which means that any qualifying transactions occurring on or after this date will benefit from this tax relief. This change is significant for businesses that engage with unregistered suppliers, as it can reduce their tax burden in specific scenarios.
key_points: Exempts intra-State supplies of goods or services received by a deductor under section 51 from central tax.
Applies to supplies from suppliers who are not registered.
Condition for exemption: the deductor must not be liable to be registered except under sub-clause (vi) of section 24.
Notification comes into effect from 1st July 2017.

1109.summary: On 25th September 2017, a corrigendum was issued to amend Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), originally published on 28th June 2017. The amendment specifically alters the description of services listed under serial number 3 in the notification's table. Previously, the notification defined services as those supplied by individual advocates, including senior advocates, to business entities. The new wording expands this definition to encompass services provided by firms of advocates as well. Additionally, the explanation for 'legal service' has been clarified to include not just representational services, but also advice, consultancy, and assistance in any branch of law. This change is significant for advocates and law firms, as it impacts how their services are classified for tax purposes, ensuring a broader understanding of what constitutes legal services in the context of integrated tax.
key_points: The corrigendum was issued on 25th September 2017.
It amends Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The change is in the description of services under serial number 3 in the table.
The original text specified services supplied by individual advocates to business entities.
The amended text broadens the definition to include services provided by firms of advocates.
The explanation for 'legal service' is clarified to include advice, consultancy, and assistance in law.
The amendment affects how legal services are classified for tax purposes.
This change is relevant for advocates and firms providing legal services to business entities.

1110.summary: Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, introduces a significant change in how integrated tax is applied to certain services. Effective from 1st July 2017, the notification mandates that the integrated tax on specified services will be paid on a reverse charge basis by the recipient. This applies to various categories of services, including those provided by goods transport agencies, individual advocates, arbitral tribunals, and directors of companies, among others. The recipient of these services must be located in the taxable territory. For instance, if a goods transport agency provides services to a factory, the factory will be responsible for paying the tax. The notification also clarifies definitions for terms such as 'body corporate' and specifies that the person who pays freight for transportation is considered the service recipient. This change impacts businesses that engage with these service providers, requiring them to be aware of their new tax obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification specifies that integrated tax on certain services will be paid on a reverse charge basis by the recipient.
The services affected include those supplied by individuals, goods transport agencies, arbitral tribunals, and others as detailed in the table.
The notification comes into effect on 1st July 2017.
Specific categories of services include transportation of goods by road, legal services by advocates, and services provided by directors to their companies.
The recipient of the services must be located in the taxable territory.
The notification clarifies definitions for terms like 'body corporate' and the treatment of freight payers as service recipients.

1111.summary: Notification No. 10/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 28, 2017, introduces an exemption for intra-State supplies of second hand goods from central tax. This exemption specifically targets registered persons who are engaged in the buying and selling of second hand goods. The key condition is that these registered persons must receive the goods from suppliers who are not registered. While they are exempt from central tax on these supplies, they are still required to pay central tax on the value of their own outward supplies, as determined under rule 32(5) of the GST Rules. This change is made under the authority of section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and is effective from July 1, 2017. This notification is significant for businesses involved in the second hand goods market, as it reduces their tax burden when sourcing from unregistered suppliers.
key_points: Exempts intra-State supplies of second hand goods from central tax.
Applies to registered persons dealing in buying and selling second hand goods.
Exemption is for supplies received from unregistered suppliers.
Registered persons must pay central tax on the value of their outward supply as per rule 32(5).
This exemption is under section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Notification comes into effect from July 1, 2017.

1113.summary: Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 28, 2017, outlines the central tax rates applicable to various services under the GST regime. It establishes a uniform tax rate of 9% for all services classified under Chapter 99. Construction services, which include the construction of complexes and buildings, are also taxed at 9%, but specific conditions apply, such as the requirement for completion certificates. Additionally, services related to distributive trade, accommodation, food and beverage, and transport are similarly taxed at 9%. Passenger transport services have differentiated rates, with some services taxed at 2.5% and others at 6%, depending on the mode of transport. Rental services for transport vehicles are taxed at 2.5%, with conditions regarding the use of input tax credits. Financial services, particularly those related to chit funds, are taxed at 6%, also subject to input tax credit conditions. This notification impacts service providers across various sectors, ensuring clarity on tax obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax was issued on June 28, 2017.
The notification specifies the central tax rates for various services under the GST framework.
All services under Chapter 99 are taxed at a rate of 9%.
Construction services, including complex and building construction, are taxed at 9%, with specific conditions regarding completion certificates.
Distributive trade services, accommodation, food and beverage services, and transport services are also taxed at 9%.
Passenger transport services have varying rates, with some at 2.5% and others at 6%, depending on the type of transport.
Rental services of transport vehicles are taxed at 2.5%, with specific conditions on input tax credit.
Financial services, including chit funds, are taxed at 6%, with conditions on input tax credit.

1114.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 12/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 28th June 2017, which will take effect from 1st July 2017. This notification states that no refund of unutilized input tax credit will be permitted for certain services outlined in sub-item (b) of item 5 of Schedule II of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change is based on the recommendations made by the GST Council and is rooted in the provisions of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, specifically clause (xiii) of section 20, as well as sub-section (3) of section 54 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. Businesses providing the specified services will need to be aware that they cannot claim refunds for any unutilized input tax credit related to these services. This could impact cash flow and tax planning for those affected, as they will not be able to recover input tax credits that they may have previously relied upon.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
Effective date of the notification is 1st July 2017.
No refund of unutilized input tax credit is allowed for specific services.
The services affected are specified in sub-item (b) of item 5 of Schedule II of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
This decision is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The notification is issued under clause (xiii) of section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It also references sub-section (3) of section 54 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

1115.summary: Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 28, 2017, introduces exemptions for specific intra-State supply of services from central tax as per the CGST Act. Notably, services provided by entities registered under section 12AA of the Income-tax Act for charitable activities are exempt from tax. Additionally, the transfer of a going concern and pure services provided to government entities or local authorities are also exempt. Transportation services for passengers and goods are included in this exemption, provided they meet certain conditions. Furthermore, services offered by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities to business entities with an aggregate turnover of up to twenty lakh rupees in the previous financial year are exempt from tax. This notification covers a wide range of service headings, including construction, renting of residential dwellings, and various transportation services, thereby significantly impacting service providers and consumers in these sectors.
key_points: Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax was issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification exempts certain intra-State supply of services from central tax under section 9 of the CGST Act.
Services provided by entities registered under section 12AA of the Income-tax Act for charitable activities are exempt from tax.
Services related to the transfer of a going concern are also exempt from tax.
Pure services provided to government entities or local authorities are exempt from tax.
Transportation services for passengers and goods under specified conditions are exempt from tax.
Certain services provided by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities to business entities with turnover up to twenty lakh rupees are exempt.
The exemption applies to various service headings, including construction, renting of residential dwellings, and transportation services.

1116.summary: On 25th September 2017, a corrigendum was issued to amend Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate), originally published on 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically changes the description of services provided by advocates in the tax table. Previously, the notification detailed services supplied by individual advocates, including senior advocates, to business entities, and mentioned contracts through other advocates. The new wording simplifies this to state that services provided by individual advocates or firms of advocates are considered legal services. Additionally, the definition of 'legal service' has been clarified to encompass advice, consultancy, or assistance in any branch of law, and it explicitly includes representational services before any court, tribunal, or authority. This change is significant for advocates and firms providing legal services, as it streamlines the language and clarifies the scope of services covered under the tax rate.
key_points: The corrigendum was issued on 25th September 2017.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment affects the description of services provided by advocates in the tax table.
The previous description included specific mention of services to business entities and contracts through other advocates.
The new description simplifies the language to include services provided by advocates or firms of advocates by way of legal services.
The definition of 'legal service' is clarified to include advice, consultancy, or assistance in any branch of law.
The explanation now explicitly states that 'legal service' includes representational services before courts, tribunals, or authorities.

1117.summary: Notification No. 13/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, comes into effect from 1st July 2017. This notification allows the United Nations and specified international organisations to claim refunds on integrated tax paid for goods and services intended for official use. Additionally, foreign diplomatic missions and consular posts in India can also claim these refunds, provided they have a certificate from the Ministry of External Affairs. For services, an undertaking must be signed by the head of the mission, while for goods, a certificate confirming their official use is required. Importantly, these goods cannot be disposed of within three years of receipt to retain refund eligibility. If the Ministry of External Affairs withdraws the certificate, the refund will no longer be available. The term 'specified international organisation' refers to those recognised by the Central Government under the United Nations (Privileges and Immunities Act) 1947. This notification clarifies the conditions under which these entities can claim refunds, ensuring compliance with the stipulated requirements.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
Effective from 1st July 2017.
United Nations and specified international organisations can claim refunds of integrated tax on goods/services for official use.
Foreign diplomatic missions and consular posts can claim refunds of integrated tax based on a certificate from the Ministry of External Affairs.
Refunds for foreign missions require an undertaking for services and a certificate for goods confirming their official use.
Goods must not be disposed of within three years of receipt to maintain refund eligibility.
Withdrawal of refund eligibility occurs if the Ministry of External Affairs withdraws the certificate.
Specified international organisations are defined as those declared by the Central Government under the United Nations (Privileges and Immunities Act) 1947.

1118.summary: Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, introduces a reverse charge mechanism for specific services starting from 1st July 2017. This means that the recipient of these services, rather than the supplier, will be responsible for paying the central tax. The services affected include those provided by goods transport agencies, individual advocates, arbitral tribunals, and various other service providers to business entities and partnership firms located in the taxable territory. For instance, if a goods transport agency provides services to a factory, the factory will need to pay the tax instead of the agency. Additionally, the notification specifies that the person who pays freight for transportation is treated as the service recipient. It also includes definitions for terms like 'Body Corporate' in line with the Companies Act, 2013, ensuring clarity on who is liable under this new tax structure. This change primarily impacts businesses that engage these service providers, shifting the tax burden to the recipients.
key_points: Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax was issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification specifies that certain services will be taxed on a reverse charge basis.
The services affected include those provided by goods transport agencies, advocates, arbitral tribunals, and others as listed in the table.
The reverse charge applies to recipients such as factories, business entities, and partnership firms located in the taxable territory.
The notification comes into effect on 1st July 2017.
It clarifies that the person liable to pay freight for transportation is considered the recipient of the service.
Definitions for terms like 'Body Corporate' are provided as per the Companies Act, 2013.

1119.summary: Notification No. 14/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, introduces a significant change regarding the payment of Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) for specific services. From 1st July 2017, the responsibility for paying tax on inter-State supplies for services such as passenger transportation by radio-taxi, motorcab, maxicab, and motorcycle will fall on the electronic commerce operator. Additionally, the notification includes accommodation services provided in hotels, inns, guest houses, clubs, campsites, and other commercial lodging facilities, with an exception for those suppliers who are liable for registration under specific clauses of the IGST Act. The notification also clarifies the definition of 'radio taxi' and aligns terms like 'maxicab', 'motorcab', and 'motorcycle' with the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988. This change primarily affects electronic commerce operators involved in these service categories, shifting the tax liability from service providers to the operators.
key_points: The notification is issued under Notification No. 14/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It specifies that the tax on inter-State supplies for certain services will be paid by the electronic commerce operator.
The affected services include transportation of passengers by radio-taxi, motorcab, maxicab, and motorcycle.
Accommodation services in hotels, inns, guest houses, clubs, campsites, or other commercial lodging places are also included, except for certain registered suppliers.
The notification defines 'radio taxi' as a taxi in two-way radio communication with a control office and GPS or GPRS tracking.
Terms like 'maxicab', 'motorcab', and 'motorcycle' are defined as per the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988.
This notification comes into effect from 1st July 2017.

1120.summary: Notification No. 14/2017-Central Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, introduces an important clarification regarding the treatment of certain government activities under GST. It states that specific services provided by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities, which are related to functions assigned to Panchayats as per Article 243G of the Constitution, will not be classified as a supply of goods or services. This means that these activities will not attract GST, providing relief to local authorities engaged in such functions. The notification is effective from 1st July 2017, meaning any relevant activities conducted from this date onward will fall under this exemption. This change primarily affects local authorities and government bodies involved in the execution of Panchayat functions, ensuring they are not burdened with GST compliance for these specific services.
key_points: Notification No. 14/2017-Central Tax issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification specifies that certain activities by the Central Government, State Government, or local authorities are not considered a supply of goods or services.
The specific activities exempted are those related to functions entrusted to a Panchayat under Article 243G of the Constitution.
The effective date for this notification is 1st July 2017.

1121.summary: Notification No. 15/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on June 30, 2017, provides a significant tax exemption for units or developers operating within Special Economic Zones (SEZs). Specifically, it exempts all goods and services imported for authorized operations from the integrated tax that would typically apply under the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This exemption is grounded in the powers granted by section 6 of the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017. The exemption is applicable from July 1, 2017, meaning that any goods or services imported for authorized operations in SEZs after this date will not incur the integrated tax. This change is aimed at promoting investment and operational efficiency within SEZs, thereby enhancing their attractiveness for businesses. As a result, businesses operating in these zones can benefit from reduced tax liabilities, potentially leading to lower operational costs and increased competitiveness in the market.
key_points: Exempts all goods or services imported by a unit or developer in the Special Economic Zone from integrated tax.
The exemption applies specifically to goods or services imported for authorised operations.
This exemption is granted under sub-section (1) of section 6 of the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017.
The relevant section of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975 being referenced is sub-section (7) of section 3.
This notification is effective from 1st July 2017.

1122.summary: Notification No. 15/2017-Central Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, introduces a significant change regarding the refund of unutilised input tax credit under the GST framework. Effective from 1st July 2017, this notification specifies that businesses will not be able to claim refunds for unutilised input tax credit related to certain services. The services impacted are detailed in sub-item (b) of item 5 of Schedule II of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. This means that if your business provides these specific services, you will not be eligible for a refund of any unutilised input tax credit you may have accumulated. This change is crucial for businesses to understand, as it directly affects cash flow and tax planning strategies. It is essential for affected businesses to review their service offerings and adjust their financial strategies accordingly to accommodate this new regulation.
key_points: Notification No. 15/2017-Central Tax issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification comes into effect from 1st July 2017.
It states that no refund of unutilised input tax credit will be allowed for specific services.
The services affected are those specified in sub-item (b) of item 5 of Schedule II of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act.

1123.summary: Notification No. 16/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on June 30, 2017, introduces significant changes to the GST rates applicable to fertilizers. Effective from July 1, 2017, the notification adds new entries under Schedule I for a 5% tax rate, specifically for various types of mineral or chemical fertilizers. These include nitrogenous fertilizers (serial number 182A), phosphatic fertilizers (182B), potassic fertilizers (182C), and a category for fertilizers containing combinations of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium (182D). Additionally, the notification removes certain entries from Schedule II that were previously listed under the 12% tax rate, specifically serial numbers 66 through 69. This amendment is a direct update to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on June 28, 2017, reflecting the government's ongoing adjustments to the GST framework for agricultural products. Businesses dealing in these fertilizers should take note of these changes to ensure proper compliance with the updated tax rates.
key_points: Inserts new entries in Schedule I for 5% tax rate: serial numbers 182A, 182B, 182C, and 182D.
New entries pertain to various types of mineral or chemical fertilizers, specifically nitrogenous, phosphatic, potassic, and combinations thereof.
Omissions in Schedule II for 12% tax rate: serial numbers 66, 67, 68, and 69 and their related entries.
The notification is effective from July 1, 2017.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated June 28, 2017.

1124.summary: Notification No. 16/2017-Central Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, outlines the eligibility for GST refunds for the United Nations and certain specified international organizations. These entities can claim refunds on central tax paid for goods and services, provided they obtain a certificate confirming that these are for official use. Additionally, foreign diplomatic missions and consular posts in India can also claim refunds, but they must meet specific conditions. They need a certificate from the Ministry of External Affairs, which is based on reciprocity. For services, an undertaking must be signed by the head of the mission, confirming the services are for official or personal use. When it comes to goods, a certification is required to ensure they are used by the mission and cannot be disposed of within three years. This notification is effective from 1st July 2017, impacting how these organizations manage their tax refunds in India.
key_points: Notification No. 16/2017-Central Tax was issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification specifies the United Nations and certain international organizations as eligible for GST refunds.
Foreign diplomatic missions and consular posts in India can also claim refunds under specific conditions.
Refunds for the United Nations require a certificate confirming goods/services are for official use.
Foreign diplomatic missions must provide a certificate from the Ministry of External Affairs for refunds.
An undertaking must be signed by the head of the foreign mission for services received for official or personal use.
Goods must be certified as used by the mission and cannot be disposed of within three years.
The notification is effective from 1st July 2017.

1125.summary: On July 5, 2017, the Government of India issued Notification No. 17/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), which rescinds the earlier Notification No. 15/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) that was published on June 30, 2017. This means that any provisions or rules established in the previous notification are no longer in effect, except for actions that were already taken before this new notification was issued. The rescission is made under the authority granted by section 6 of the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017. This change is significant for businesses and entities that were relying on the provisions of the earlier notification, as they will need to adjust their operations and compliance accordingly. The notification was officially published in the Gazette of India, ensuring that the information is accessible to all stakeholders. This move reflects the government's ongoing efforts to streamline and clarify tax regulations under the GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 17/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 5th July 2017.
This notification rescinds Notification No. 15/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 30th June 2017.
The rescission applies except for actions taken before this notification.
The rescinded notification was published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, under number G.S.R. 740 (E).
The rescission is based on the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of section 6 of the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017.

1126.summary: Notification No. 17/2017-Central Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, introduces a significant change in how tax is handled for certain services starting from 1st July 2017. Specifically, it mandates that electronic commerce operators will be responsible for paying the tax on intra-State supplies related to passenger transportation services, including those provided by radio-taxis, motorcabs, maxicabs, and motorcycles. Additionally, this notification covers accommodation services offered in various commercial lodging facilities such as hotels and guest houses. However, there is an important exception: if the service provider is liable for registration under section 22(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, they will not fall under this requirement. The notification also clarifies the definitions of terms like 'radio taxi', 'maxicab', 'motorcab', and 'motorcycle' based on the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988. This change primarily affects electronic commerce operators and service providers in the transportation and accommodation sectors.
key_points: The notification is dated 28th June 2017 and comes into effect from 1st July 2017.
It specifies that the tax on intra-State supplies for certain services will be paid by the electronic commerce operator.
The services affected include transportation of passengers by radio-taxi, motorcab, maxicab, and motorcycle.
It also includes accommodation services provided in hotels, inns, guest houses, clubs, campsites, or other commercial places for lodging.
An exception is made for service providers who are liable for registration under section 22(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act.
Definitions for 'radio taxi', 'maxicab', 'motorcab', and 'motorcycle' are provided as per the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988.

1127.summary: Notification No. 18/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on July 5, 2017, provides an exemption from integrated tax for services imported by units or developers operating within Special Economic Zones (SEZs). This exemption applies specifically to services used for authorized operations, which are defined under the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017. The exemption is granted under the authority of section 6(1) of the same Act and is based on recommendations made by the GST Council. Essentially, this means that any services imported by these SEZ units or developers will not incur the integrated tax that would typically be applicable under section 5 of the Act. This change is significant for businesses operating in SEZs as it reduces their tax burden on imported services, potentially enhancing their operational efficiency and competitiveness in the market.
key_points: Exemption from integrated tax for services imported by units or developers in Special Economic Zones.
Applicable for authorized operations as defined under the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017.
The notification is issued under section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017.
This exemption is granted based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The notification is dated 5th July 2017.
The exemption applies to the whole of the integrated tax leviable under section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Service Tax Act, 2017.

1128.summary: Notification No. 18/2017-Central Tax, issued on 30th June 2017, introduces important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate). Effective from 1st July 2017, this notification adds new entries in Schedule I, specifically for fertilizers, which will now be taxed at a rate of 2.5%. The new serial numbers 182A to 182D cover various types of fertilizers, including nitrogenous, phosphatic, potassic, and mixed fertilizers in small packages. Additionally, the notification removes certain entries from Schedule II that were previously taxed at 6%, specifically serial numbers 66 to 69. This change is significant for businesses dealing in fertilizers, as it clarifies the tax rates applicable to different types of fertilizers and eliminates some previously listed items from the higher tax bracket. Overall, these amendments aim to streamline the GST framework for the agricultural sector.
key_points: Notification No. 18/2017-Central Tax issued on 30th June 2017.
Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts new entries in Schedule I for 2.5% tax rate for specific fertilizers: serial numbers 182A to 182D.
New entries include nitrogenous, phosphatic, potassic fertilizers, and mixed fertilizers in small packages.
Omissions made in Schedule II for 6% tax rate, specifically removing serial numbers 66 to 69.
Changes take effect from 1st July 2017.

1129.summary: Notification No. 19/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 18th August 2017, introduces amendments to the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment adds new entries to Schedule III under the 18% tax rate, specifically after serial number 452. The new entries include a range of tractor parts such as tyres (serial number 452A), tubes for tractor tyres (452B), and various components like hydraulic pumps (452D), gear boxes (452G), and bumpers (452E). Each new entry is assigned a unique serial number from 452A to 452O, detailing the specific parts and assemblies related to tractors. This change is significant for manufacturers and suppliers of agricultural machinery, as it clarifies the tax rates applicable to these essential components, ensuring they are correctly categorized under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework.
key_points: This notification is No. 19/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 18th August 2017.
It amends the earlier notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries are added to Schedule III – 18% after serial number 452.
The new entries include various tractor parts such as tyres, tubes, hydraulic pumps, and gear boxes.
Specific serial numbers for the new entries range from 452A to 452O.
The notification includes detailed descriptions for each new item related to tractors.

1130.summary: Notification No. 19/2017-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 18th August 2017, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically adds new entries to Schedule III under the 9% tax rate, following serial number 452. The new entries include various components and parts for tractors, such as tyres (HSN 4011 70 00), tubes for tractor tyres (HSN 4013 90 49), and agricultural diesel engines with a cylinder capacity exceeding 250 cc (HSN 8408 20 20). Additionally, it includes hydraulic pumps, bumpers, brakes, gear boxes, and steering wheels, each with their respective HSN codes. This change is significant for manufacturers and suppliers of tractor parts, as it clarifies the applicable GST rates for these items, ensuring they are correctly categorized under the new tax structure.
key_points: This notification is Notification No. 19/2017-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 18th August 2017.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
New entries are added to Schedule III under the 9% tax rate after serial number 452.
The new entries include various tractor parts such as tyres, tubes, hydraulic pumps, and more.
Specific items added include: Tyre for tractors (4011 70 00), Tube for tractor tyres (4013 90 49), and Agricultural Diesel Engine (8408 20 20).
Additional parts like bumpers, brakes, gear boxes, and steering wheels for tractors are also included.
The notification specifies the HSN codes for each new entry related to tractors.

1131.summary: The recent Notification No. 20/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on August 22, 2017, amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Key changes include updates to the table entries for various services. For instance, the composite supply of works contracts to government authorities has been clarified under serial number 3. Additionally, serial number 8 now specifies that transport of passengers by motorcab includes the cost of fuel. The services of goods transport agencies (GTA) have been better defined in serial number 9, while serial number 10 addresses renting of motorcabs with fuel costs included. Furthermore, serial number 26 has been revised to focus on textiles and textile products, and serial number 27 now includes printing services for newspapers and books. Lastly, an addition to serial number 34 includes 'or planetarium' after 'drama'. These changes affect businesses involved in construction, transportation, and printing services, ensuring they are aligned with the latest GST provisions.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the entries for serial number 3 in the table regarding composite supply of works contracts supplied to government authorities.
Updates serial number 8 to include transport of passengers by motorcab with fuel cost included.
Modifies serial number 9 to clarify services of goods transport agency (GTA) for transportation of goods.
Revises serial number 10 for renting of motorcab with fuel cost included.
Alters serial number 26 to specify textiles and textile products under Chapters 50 to 63.
Substitutes serial number 27 to include services related to printing of newspapers and books.
Inserts 'or planetarium' after 'drama' in serial number 34.

1132.summary: The recent amendment through Notification No. 20/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 22nd August 2017 updates several provisions from the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate). It specifically revises the entries for composite supply of works contracts, which now includes construction services for historical monuments, irrigation works, and various housing projects under government schemes. Additionally, the tax rate for passenger transport by motorcab and services provided by goods transport agencies has been set at 6%, with stipulations regarding input tax credit. The notification also clarifies the services related to printing, including newspapers and Braille books, and modifies the entry for textiles. Furthermore, it adds 'or planetarium' to the entertainment services category, broadening the scope of applicable services. These changes will affect businesses involved in construction, transportation, and printing, ensuring they align with the updated tax framework.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the entries for composite supply of works contracts for various public projects, including historical monuments and irrigation works.

1133.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 21/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on August 22, 2017, amending the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate). This new notification introduces several important changes. Firstly, it adds a new entry (10A) for services related to the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2017, which will be exempt from tax, provided that a certification is obtained from the Director (Sports) of the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports. Additionally, two new entries (12A and 12B) have been added for services provided by Fair Price Shops to both Central and State Governments under the Public Distribution System, also exempt from tax. The notification further updates entry 36(h) to specify the 'Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme' and changes entry 36(j) to refer to the 'Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana'. Lastly, it clarifies that a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) is recognized as a partnership firm under the GST framework. These amendments primarily affect entities involved in sports services, public distribution, and agricultural insurance.
key_points: Inserts a new entry 10A for services related to FIFA U-17 World Cup 2017, with a tax rate of Nil, requiring certification from the Director (Sports), Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
Inserts new entries 12A and 12B for services provided by Fair Price Shops to Central and State Governments under the Public Distribution System (PDS), with a tax rate of Nil.
Amends entry 36(h) to replace 'Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme or the Modified National Agricultural Insurance Scheme' with 'Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme (RWCIS)'.
Amends entry 36(j) to replace 'National Agricultural Insurance Scheme (Rashtriya Krishi Bima Yojana)' with 'Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)'.
Inserts a new clause in the Explanation of paragraph 3, stating that a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) is considered a partnership firm.

1134.summary: The Notification No. 21/2017-Central Tax, issued on August 22, 2017, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate). A new entry, 9A, has been added for services provided by FIFA and its subsidiaries related to the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2017, which will be exempt from tax, provided there is certification from the Director (Sports). Additionally, entries 11A and 11B have been created for services offered by Fair Price Shops to the Central and State Governments under the Public Distribution System, also exempt from tax. The notification further updates entry 35 by replacing the previous insurance schemes with the Restructured Weather Based Crop Insurance Scheme and the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana. Lastly, it clarifies that a Limited Liability Partnership, as defined under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, is recognized as a partnership firm. These changes primarily affect entities involved in these specific services and insurance schemes, streamlining tax obligations in these areas.

1135.summary: The recent Notification No. 22/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 22nd August 2017 brings important changes to the earlier Notification No. 10/2017. Specifically, it introduces a new requirement for goods transport agencies (GTA), stating that they must not have paid integrated tax at the rate of 12% to qualify under the specified category. This means that GTAs will need to ensure they meet this condition to benefit from the provisions outlined in the notification. Additionally, the amendment clarifies the definition of a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP), stating that it will also be recognized as a partnership firm under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008. This change is significant for businesses operating as LLPs, as it aligns their status with that of traditional partnership firms for GST purposes. Overall, these amendments aim to provide clearer guidelines for GTAs and LLPs in the context of integrated tax regulations.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Inserts a condition for goods transport agencies (GTA) stating they must not have paid integrated tax at the rate of 12%.
Adds a new clause (e) in the Explanation defining a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) as a partnership firm.
The amendment is effective from the date of the notification, 22nd August 2017.

1136.summary: The recent Notification No. 22/2017-Central Tax introduces important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a requirement for goods transport agencies (GTA) that they must not have paid central tax at the rate of 6% to be classified under the specified category. This change clarifies the tax obligations for GTAs and ensures compliance with the GST framework. Additionally, the notification includes a new definition for Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs), stating that they will be recognized as partnership firms under the GST law. This is significant for businesses operating as LLPs, as it aligns their classification with traditional partnership firms, potentially affecting their tax treatment. These amendments were officially published on 22nd August 2017, and businesses in the transport sector and those structured as LLPs should take note of these changes to understand their tax responsibilities better.

1137.summary: Notification No. 23/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 22nd August 2017, introduces an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 14/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a new clause (iii) that addresses services related to house-keeping, such as plumbing and carpentering. This means that these services are now recognized under the integrated tax framework. However, there is an important exception: if the service provider is required to register under clause (v) of section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) Act, then this exemption does not apply. This change is significant for service providers in the house-keeping sector, as it clarifies their tax obligations and potential exemptions. The amendment was made following recommendations from the GST Council, indicating a responsive approach to the needs of the service industry.

1138.summary: Notification No. 23/2017-Central Tax, issued on 22nd August 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 17/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a new clause (iii) that addresses services related to housekeeping, such as plumbing and carpentering. This amendment clarifies that these services are exempt from GST unless the service provider is required to register under section 22(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act. This means that small service providers who do not meet the registration threshold can offer these services without charging GST. However, if they are liable for registration, they must comply with GST regulations. This change is significant for service providers in the housekeeping sector, as it directly impacts their tax obligations and compliance requirements.

1139.summary: Notification No. 24/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 21st September 2017, brings significant changes to the earlier Notification No. 8/2017. It specifically amends the entries in the Table against serial number 3, replacing item (vi) with new definitions for services provided to various government entities. These services now include construction, erection, commissioning, installation, and maintenance of civil structures, as well as educational, clinical, and cultural establishments. Additionally, it covers residential complexes that are predominantly meant for self-use or for employees. Furthermore, a new item (vii) has been introduced to categorize construction services that do not fit into the previous definitions. This amendment is crucial for businesses involved in providing construction services to government bodies, as it clarifies the scope of services and their tax implications under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act.

1140.summary: Notification No. 24/2017-Central Tax, issued on 21st September 2017, amends the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The key change is in the Table under serial number 3, specifically item (vi), which now details the types of services provided to government entities. These services include construction, maintenance, and renovation of structures intended for educational, clinical, cultural, and residential use. The amendment specifies that these services are meant predominantly for self-use or for the use of employees. This change aims to clarify the scope of services that fall under this category, ensuring that they are aligned with public interest and the objectives of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This notification will primarily affect contractors and service providers engaged in such projects for government bodies.

1141.summary: Notification No. 25/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 21st September 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry to the tax table after serial number 84. This new entry, numbered 85, pertains to services that provide the right to admission to events organized under the FIFA U-17 World Cup 2017. Importantly, the tax rate for these services is set to Nil, meaning there will be no integrated tax charged for admissions to these events. This change is made under the authority of section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, reflecting the government's intent to promote such events by reducing the tax burden. Businesses involved in organizing or providing services related to these events will benefit from this amendment, as it allows them to offer admission without the additional cost of integrated tax.

Businesses involved in organizing or providing admission services for the FIFA U-17 World Cup will benefit from this exemption, encouraging attendance and participation in this significant sporting event.

1143.summary: Notification No. 26/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on September 21, 2017, provides an exemption from integrated tax for the inter-state supply of heavy water and nuclear fuels. This exemption specifically applies to supplies categorized under Chapter 28 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. The supplies must be made by the Department of Atomic Energy and are intended for the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd. This decision is based on the recommendations of the GST Council and is deemed necessary for public interest. The exemption is granted under section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, which outlines the conditions for integrated tax levies. This means that the Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd will not incur integrated tax on these specific supplies, potentially reducing their operational costs and supporting their activities in the nuclear energy sector.

1144.summary: Notification No. 26/2017-Central Tax, issued on September 21, 2017, provides a complete exemption from central tax for the intra-state supply of heavy water and nuclear fuels. This exemption is specifically applicable to goods classified under Chapter 28 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975, and is granted by the Department of Atomic Energy. The Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd is the designated recipient of these exempted goods. The exemption is significant as it removes the entire central tax liability under section 9 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This decision was made in the public interest and follows recommendations from the GST Council. The notification highlights the government's commitment to supporting the nuclear energy sector by easing tax burdens on essential supplies. Overall, this exemption is aimed at facilitating the operations of the Nuclear Power Corporation and promoting the use of nuclear energy in India.

1145.summary: The recent Notification No. 27/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on September 22, 2017, amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Key changes include a revised definition of 'registered brand name' in Schedule I, which now includes goods that bear a brand name with enforceable rights. New entries have been added for 'Walnuts', 'Tamarind, dried', and 'Roasted Gram', while specific exclusions for aquatic and poultry feeds have been removed from certain entries. Additionally, in Schedule II, 'walnuts' has been omitted from one entry, and another entry has been modified to exclude 'tamarind, dried'. Schedule III now includes 'Custard powder' and 'Medical grade sterile disposable gloves' in relevant entries. Lastly, Schedule IV has been updated to include 'medical grade sterile disposable gloves' and 'plastic raincoats' in the relevant entries. These changes affect businesses dealing with these goods, ensuring they are aware of the updated tax implications.

1147.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 28/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 22nd September 2017, which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment modifies the descriptions for several goods listed under serial numbers 27, 29, 45, 65-75, 77, 78, 101, and 108, specifically regarding brand names and the conditions under which actionable claims can be foregone. New entries have been added for cotton seed oil cake (serial number 102A), Khadi fabric (130A), and idols made of clay (135A). Additionally, the entry for serial number 102 has been updated to include specific codes. The definition of 'brand name' and 'registered brand name' has also been revised for clarity. Furthermore, two new annexures have been introduced: one outlines the conditions for voluntarily foregoing claims on brand names, while the other lists various indigenous handmade musical instruments. This notification primarily affects manufacturers and sellers of the specified goods, ensuring they are aware of the updated definitions and conditions related to brand names.

New entries have been added, including serial number 102A for 'Cotton seed oil cake', 130A for 'Khadi fabric' sold through the Khadi and Village Industries Commission, and 135A for 'Idols made of clay'. The notification also provides clear definitions for 'brand name' and 'registered brand name', which will help businesses understand their obligations regarding branding and packaging. This amendment is made under the authority of section 54(3)(ii) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and section 20 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change affects manufacturers and sellers of corduroy fabrics, as they will now have a specific classification under the integrated tax rate framework. The inclusion of corduroy fabrics aims to clarify the tax treatment for this category, ensuring that businesses dealing with these fabrics are aware of their obligations under the GST regime.

1151.summary: Notification No. 30/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on September 22, 2017, provides an exemption from integrated tax for inter-State supplies of skimmed milk powder or concentrated milk. This exemption is specifically applicable when these goods are supplied to a distinct person for the purpose of milk production, particularly for distribution through dairy cooperatives. However, it is important to note that the exemption does not apply if the goods are intended for further supply as skimmed milk powder or concentrated milk. The exemption is grounded in section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and follows the recommendations of the Goods and Services Tax Council. The relevant tariff item for this exemption is 0402, as outlined in the notification. This change is significant for businesses involved in the dairy sector, particularly those engaged in the production and distribution of milk, as it may reduce their tax burden on specific supplies.

1152.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 30/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 29th September 2017, which introduces a new entry in the existing tax framework. Specifically, it adds entry 9B to the Table of Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate), which relates to the supply of services associated with transit cargo to the landlocked countries of Nepal and Bhutan. This service will now be taxed at a rate of 'Nil', meaning there will be no GST applicable on these services. This change is made under the authority of section 11(1) of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and aims to facilitate smoother trade and transit operations with these neighboring countries. The amendment follows earlier updates, including the last one made by Notification No. 25/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 21st September 2017. Businesses involved in providing these transit services will benefit from this exemption, potentially reducing their operational costs and encouraging trade with Nepal and Bhutan.

Specifically, it adds a new entry, numbered 10B, to the existing table of services. This entry relates to the supply of services associated with transit cargo to the landlocked countries of Nepal and Bhutan. Notably, the tax rate for this service is set to Nil, meaning that no integrated tax will be levied on these services. This change is significant for businesses involved in logistics and transportation services to these countries, as it reduces their tax burden. The amendment is made under the authority of section 6(1) of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and follows a previous amendment made by Notification No. 25/2017 on 21st September 2017. This update reflects the government's ongoing efforts to facilitate trade with neighboring countries by easing tax implications on transit services.

1154.summary: The recent amendment through Notification No. 31/2017-Central Tax (Rate) modifies several entries in the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Notably, it expands the definition of entities eligible for certain tax benefits by replacing 'Government, a local authority or a Governmental authority' with a broader term that includes 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, a local authority, a Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' in item (iii) of serial number 3. Additionally, it clarifies that services provided to a Government Entity must be related to a work entrusted by the government. The amendment also introduces new definitions for composite supply of works contracts, particularly those involving earthwork. Furthermore, it updates the conditions for input tax credit related to passenger transport and natural gas transportation. Lastly, the notification defines 'Governmental Authority' and 'Government Entity', providing clarity on the entities involved in these transactions. These changes primarily affect businesses engaged in government contracts and services, ensuring they understand the updated definitions and conditions for compliance.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

Substitutes 'Government, a local authority or a Governmental authority' with 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, a local authority, a Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' in item (iii) of serial number 3.
Changes 'a local authority or a Governmental authority' to 'a local authority, a Governmental Authority or a Government Entity' in item (vi) of serial number 3.
Introduces a proviso in items (iii) and (vi) of serial number 3, stating services to a Government Entity must relate to a work entrusted by the government.
Replaces item (vii) in serial number 3 with new definitions for composite supply of works contracts involving predominantly earth work.
Updates item (vi) of serial number 8 regarding transport of passengers, specifying conditions for input tax credit.
Changes item (v) of serial number 9 regarding transportation of natural gas, adding conditions for input tax credit.
Defines 'Governmental Authority' and 'Government Entity' in the context of the notification.

1155.summary: Notification No. 32/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) introduces an important exemption for registered persons in India. Specifically, it exempts the inter-State supply of goods or services received by these registered persons from any supplier who is not registered. This exemption is granted under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, highlighting the government's effort to support registered businesses engaging with unregistered suppliers. The exemption is applicable to all registered persons, allowing them to benefit from this provision without incurring integrated tax on such supplies. This notification is effective until 31st March 2018, meaning businesses have a limited timeframe to take advantage of this exemption. The move is aimed at promoting ease of doing business and encouraging transactions between registered and unregistered suppliers, which can enhance market dynamics and support smaller suppliers. Overall, this notification reflects the government's commitment to facilitating smoother business operations within the GST framework.
key_points: Exemption from integrated tax for inter-State supply of goods or services received by registered persons from unregistered suppliers.
This exemption is granted under sub-section (1) of section 6 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The exemption applies to all registered persons.
The exemption is valid until 31st March 2018.

1156.summary: The recent Notification No. 32/2017-Central Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017. It updates the terminology in serial number 5, replacing 'governmental authority' with a broader definition that includes various government bodies. New entries have been added, such as entry 9C, which allows for tax-free services provided by Government Entities to government bodies against grants. Entry 21A introduces tax-free services from goods transport agencies to unregistered persons, with specific exclusions for registered entities. Additionally, entry 23A pertains to tax-free access to roads or bridges on an annuity basis. The notification also revises serial number 41 to clarify the conditions under which upfront amounts for long-term leases of industrial plots are applicable. Lastly, it updates the definitions of 'Governmental Authority' and 'Government Entity' to reflect the participation criteria and functions they serve. These changes primarily affect government entities and service providers interacting with them.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Substitutes 'governmental authority' with 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or Governmental Authority' in serial number 5.
Inserts new entry 9C for services supplied by a Government Entity to various government bodies against grants, with a tax rate of Nil.
Inserts new entry 21A for services provided by a goods transport agency to unregistered persons, with a tax rate of Nil, excluding certain registered entities.
Inserts new entry 23A for access to a road or bridge on payment of annuity, with a tax rate of Nil.
Substitutes the entry in serial number 41 regarding upfront amounts for long-term leases of industrial plots, specifying conditions and entities involved.
Revises the definition of 'Governmental Authority' and introduces a new definition for 'Government Entity' in paragraph 2.

1157.summary: The recent Notification No. 33/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 9/2017, which was issued on 28th June 2017. One significant change is the replacement of the term 'governmental authority' in serial number 5 with a broader definition that includes the Central Government, State Government, Union territories, local authorities, and governmental authorities. Additionally, a new entry, 10C, has been added to exempt services provided by a Government Entity to various authorities against grants from tax. Another new entry, 22A, allows goods transport agencies to provide services to unregistered persons without tax, with specific exclusions. Entry 24A introduces a tax exemption for access to roads or bridges on payment of annuity. Furthermore, the amendment clarifies the upfront amounts related to long-term leases of industrial plots in serial number 43. Lastly, the definitions of 'Governmental Authority' and 'Government Entity' have been revised to provide clearer criteria for these terms. These changes primarily affect government entities and service providers in the transport and infrastructure sectors.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Substitutes 'governmental authority' with 'Central Government, State Government, Union territory, local authority or Governmental Authority' in serial number 5.
Inserts new entry 10C for services supplied by a Government Entity to various authorities against grants, with a tax rate of Nil.
Inserts new entry 22A for services provided by a goods transport agency to unregistered persons, with a tax rate of Nil, excluding certain recipients.
Inserts new entry 24A for service by way of access to a road or bridge on payment of annuity, with a tax rate of Nil.
Substitutes entry in serial number 43 regarding upfront amounts for long-term leases of industrial plots, clarifying the entities involved.
Revises clause (zf) to define 'Governmental Authority' and introduces a new definition for 'Government Entity'.

1158.summary: Notification No. 33/2017-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 13th October 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment adds a new entry to the existing Table of services. Specifically, it includes serial number 10, which covers the supply of services by the members of the Overseeing Committee to the Reserve Bank of India. This means that any services provided by these committee members to the Reserve Bank will now be recognized under this specific entry. The members of the Overseeing Committee are those appointed by the Reserve Bank of India. This change is significant for those involved in providing such services, as it clarifies the tax treatment applicable to these transactions. Overall, this amendment helps in better defining the scope of services that fall under the GST framework, ensuring that both service providers and recipients are aware of their tax obligations.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 33/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017.
It amends Notification No. 13/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added to the Table after serial number 9.
The new entry (serial number 10) pertains to the supply of services by members of the Overseeing Committee to the Reserve Bank of India.
The specific service providers mentioned are the members of the Overseeing Committee constituted by the Reserve Bank of India.

1159.summary: Notification No. 34/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 13th October 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment adds a new entry to the tax rate table, specifically after serial number 11. The new entry, numbered 12, relates to the supply of services provided by the members of the Overseeing Committee to the Reserve Bank of India. This means that any services rendered by these committee members to the Reserve Bank will now be recognized under this specific entry. The members of the Overseeing Committee are those appointed by the Reserve Bank itself. This change clarifies the tax treatment of such services, ensuring that they are properly categorized under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework. Businesses and entities involved in providing these services should take note of this amendment for their compliance and reporting purposes.
key_points: This notification is No. 34/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 13th October 2017.
It amends the earlier notification No. 10/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added to the Table after serial number 11.
The new entry (serial number 12) pertains to the supply of services by members of the Overseeing Committee to the Reserve Bank of India.
The specific service providers are the members of the Overseeing Committee constituted by the Reserve Bank of India.

1160.summary: The recent amendment through Notification No. 34/2017-Central Tax (Rate) modifies several entries in the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Notably, in Schedule I, S. No. 29 now specifies '0802, 0813', while new entries have been introduced for dried mango slices and khakhra. Additionally, S. No. 101A has been added for certain namkeens with specific branding conditions. The entry for kerosene oil in S. No. 164 has been updated to include specific bunker fuels. Furthermore, S. No. 181A introduces medicaments manufactured according to recognized formulae. The amendment also omits S. No. 201A. In Schedule II, S. No. 16 has been revised to clarify the types of dried fruits, and S. No. 46 has been modified to include specific conditions for namkeens. These changes will affect manufacturers and suppliers in the food and medicament sectors, ensuring clarity in tax rates and compliance requirements.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, S. No. 29 changes the entry to '0802, 0813'.
New entries added: S. No. 30A for '0804 Mangoes sliced, dried' and S. No. 99A for '1905 or 2106 Khakhra, plain chapatti or roti'.
S. No. 101A added for '2106 90 Namkeens, bhujia, mixture, chabena' with specific conditions.
S. No. 164 updates kerosene oil entry to include specific bunker fuels.
S. No. 181A added for '30 Medicaments' manufactured according to specified formulae.
S. No. 201A is omitted.
In Schedule II, S. No. 16 updates dried fruit entry, and S. No. 46 modifies namkeen entry with specific conditions.

1161.summary: The recent Notification No. 35/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017. Key changes include updates to various entries in the tax schedules. For instance, in Schedule I, S. No. 29 now lists '0802, 0813' instead of '0801', and new entries have been added for dried mangoes and khakhra. Additionally, S. No. 101A introduces specific conditions for namkeens and bhujia.
