The kerosene oil and bunker fuel entry in S. No. 164 has been revised to specify certain types. Furthermore, S. No. 181A now covers medicaments manufactured according to specific guidelines. New entries for real zari thread have also been included. A significant addition is a new proviso in the ANNEXURE that outlines the responsibilities of brand owners and packers regarding brand name claims. These changes affect businesses dealing with the specified goods and services, ensuring clarity in tax obligations and compliance.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, S. No. 29 changes from '0801' to '0802, 0813'.
New entries added: S. No. 30A for '0804 Mangoes sliced, dried' and S. No. 99A for '1905 or 2106 Khakhra, plain chapatti or roti'.
S. No. 101A added for '2106 90 Namkeens, bhujia, mixture, chabena' with specific conditions.
S. No. 164 updated to include specific kerosene oil and bunker fuels.
S. No. 181A added for '30 Medicaments' manufactured under specific guidelines.
S. No. 218A added for '5605 0010 Real zari thread (gold) and silver thread'.
Proviso added in the ANNEXURE regarding brand name claims and packing responsibilities.

1162.summary: Notification No. 35/2017-Central Tax, issued on October 13, 2017, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate). A new entry, 122A, has been added to the Schedule for 'Duty Credit Scrips' following entry 122. Additionally, entry 150 has been inserted, which pertains to the supply of goods by a Government entity to various government bodies against grants. The definition of 'Government Entity' has also been clarified, specifying that it includes any authority or body established by law or with significant government control. Furthermore, a new proviso in ANNEXURE I addresses the situation where the owner of a brand name and the packager are different entities. It requires the brand owner to file an affidavit with the tax commissioner, stating they are voluntarily relinquishing their rights on the brand name for the purpose of packaging. These changes affect businesses involved in government contracts and those dealing with brand names and packaging.
key_points: Inserts new entry 122A for 'Duty Credit Scrips' after S. No. 122 in the Schedule.
Inserts new entry 150 for 'Supply of goods by a Government entity' after S. No. 149 in the Schedule.
Defines 'Government Entity' in the Explanation, including bodies set up by an Act or with 90% government participation.
Adds a proviso in ANNEXURE I regarding brand name claims and packing responsibilities.

1163.summary: The Notification No. 36/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on October 13, 2017, introduces several important amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017. Firstly, it adds a new entry for 'Duty Credit Scrips' as serial number 122A in the Schedule, which is significant for businesses dealing with these scrips. Additionally, it includes a new entry (serial number 150) that pertains to the supply of goods by government entities to various government bodies, which will now be recognized under specific conditions involving grants. The definition of 'Government Entity' has also been clarified, encompassing bodies established by legislation or with substantial government equity. Furthermore, a new requirement has been introduced in ANNEXURE I, mandating that if the owner of a brand name is different from the person packing the goods, the brand owner must file an affidavit with the relevant tax authorities. This change aims to streamline processes and clarify responsibilities in the context of brand ownership and product packaging.
key_points: Inserts serial number 122A for 'Duty Credit Scrips' after S. No. 122 in the Schedule.
Inserts serial number 150 for 'Supply of goods by a Government entity to various government bodies against grants' after S. No. 149 in the Schedule.
Defines 'Government Entity' in the Explanation, including authorities or boards set up by an Act or with 90% government participation.
Adds a proviso in ANNEXURE I regarding the filing of an affidavit by the owner of a brand name when different persons are involved in packing and ownership.

1164.summary: Notification No. 36/2017-Central Tax (Rate), issued on 13th October 2017, introduces an important amendment to the earlier notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry in the tax rate table, which is now listed as serial number 6. This entry covers 'Used vehicles, seized and confiscated goods, old and used goods, waste and scrap'. The suppliers of these goods can be the Central Government, State Government, Union territory, or a local authority. Importantly, any registered person can be the recipient of these supplies. This change is significant for businesses dealing in such goods, as it clarifies the tax implications for transactions involving these items. The amendment is made under the authority of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring that the tax framework remains updated and relevant for all stakeholders involved.
key_points: Inserts a new entry in the notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The new entry is added after S. No. 5 in the existing table.
The new serial number 6 pertains to 'Used vehicles, seized and confiscated goods, old and used goods, waste and scrap'.
The supplier of these goods includes the Central Government, State Government, Union territory, or a local authority.
The recipient of the supply is any registered person.
This amendment is made under the powers of sub-section (3) of section 9 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was issued on 13th October 2017.

1165.summary: On 13th October 2017, the Government of India issued Notification No. 37/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), which amends the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment introduces a new entry in the tax rate table, specifically after Serial No. 5. The new entry, numbered 6, covers 'Used vehicles, seized and confiscated goods, old and used goods, waste and scrap'. This means that these categories of goods can now be supplied by the Central Government, State Government, Union Territory, or a local authority. Any registered person can receive these supplies. This change is significant for businesses dealing with such goods, as it clarifies the suppliers and recipients involved in these transactions under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax framework.
key_points: This notification is issued on 13th October 2017.
It amends Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added after Serial No. 5 in the table of the earlier notification.
The new entry (Serial No. 6) pertains to 'Used vehicles, seized and confiscated goods, old and used goods, waste and scrap'.
The suppliers for this new entry include Central Government, State Government, Union Territory, or a local authority.
The recipients of this supply are any registered person.

1166.summary: Notification No. 37/2017-Central Tax, issued on 13th October 2017, introduces a central tax rate of 65% on intra-State supplies of motor vehicles, specifically those classified under Chapter 87 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This rate is derived from the central tax applicable under Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Importantly, the notification outlines specific conditions for this tax rate to apply: the motor vehicle must have been purchased by the lesser before 1st July 2017, and the supplier must be a registered person who has not claimed input tax credit on the vehicle. Additionally, it is crucial to note that the provisions of this notification will cease to apply after 1st July 2020. The notification also clarifies terms related to tariff items, sub-headings, headings, and chapters, ensuring that the rules for interpretation from the Customs Tariff Act are applicable. This change primarily affects businesses involved in the leasing and supply of motor vehicles, particularly those who meet the specified conditions.
key_points: Notification No. 37/2017-Central Tax issued on 13th October 2017.
This notification specifies a central tax rate of 65% on intra-State supplies of motor vehicles.
The tax rate applies to goods under Chapter 87 of the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
The rate is based on the central tax applicable under Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Conditions for applicability include that the motor vehicle must have been purchased by the lesser before 1st July 2017.
The supplier must be a registered person and should not have availed input tax credit on the vehicle.
The provisions of this notification will not apply after 1st July 2020.
The notification includes definitions for tariff items, sub-headings, headings, and chapters as per the Customs Tariff Act.

1167.summary: Notification No. 38/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 13th October 2017, outlines the integrated tax rates for inter-State supplies of motor vehicles. Specifically, it sets the tax rate at 65% of the integrated tax that would otherwise apply under Notification No. 1/2017. However, this notification has a significant cutoff date; its provisions will not apply after 1st July 2020. To qualify for this tax rate, certain conditions must be met. The motor vehicle must have been purchased by the lesser prior to 1st July 2017, and the supplier must be a registered person who also purchased the vehicle before this date without claiming any input tax credit on it. This notification is crucial for businesses involved in the leasing or supply of motor vehicles, as it clarifies the tax obligations and conditions that must be adhered to for compliance.
key_points: Notification No. 38/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 13th October 2017.
This notification specifies integrated tax rates for inter-State supplies of motor vehicles.
The tax rate for motor vehicles is set at 65% of the integrated tax applicable under Notification No. 1/2017.
The provisions of this notification will not apply on or after 1st July 2020.
Conditions for applicability include that the motor vehicle must have been purchased by the lesser before 1st July 2017.
The supplier must be a registered person who purchased the vehicle before 1st July 2017 and did not claim input tax credit on it.

1168.summary: Notification No. 38/2017-Central Tax, issued on 13th October 2017, brings an important change to the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, it removes the proviso under Paragraph 1 of the earlier notification. This change means that the exemption provided in Notification No. 8/2017 will now be applicable to all registered persons without any additional conditions until 31st March 2018. This is significant for businesses as it clarifies the duration and scope of the exemption, allowing them to benefit from it without the constraints that may have been previously imposed by the omitted proviso. Therefore, all registered taxpayers should take note of this amendment as it directly impacts their tax obligations and planning for the specified period.
key_points: Notification No. 38/2017-Central Tax was issued on 13th October 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment involves the omission of the proviso under Paragraph 1 of Notification No. 8/2017.
The exemption from Notification No. 8/2017 will now apply to all registered persons until 31st March 2018.

1169.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 39/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017, which amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment updates several entries in the tax rate table, specifically for serial numbers 3, 8, 9, 10, 15, 17, 26, and 27. Notably, the definitions of 'Governmental Authority' and 'Government Entity' have been introduced, clarifying their roles and requirements. The conditions for services supplied to Government Entities have been revised, mandating that these services must be related to works entrusted by various government bodies. Additionally, the notification modifies tax rates for services such as transportation and leasing of motor vehicles, and introduces new provisions for job work and printing services under specific chapters of the Customs Tariff Act. Furthermore, it clarifies the conditions under which input tax credits can be claimed for certain services, ensuring that businesses understand their obligations and entitlements under the updated GST framework.
key_points: Notification No. 39/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 13th October 2017.
Amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the wording in Table entries for serial numbers 3, 8, 9, 10, 15, 17, 26, and 27.
Introduces new definitions for 'Governmental Authority' and 'Government Entity'.
Updates conditions for services supplied to Government Entities, requiring procurement for specific works.
Modifies tax rates for various services, including transportation and leasing of motor vehicles.
Inserts new items related to job work and printing services under specific chapters of the Customs Tariff Act.
Clarifies input tax credit conditions for certain services.

1170.summary: Notification No. 39/2017-Central Tax, issued on October 18, 2017, establishes a central tax rate of 2.5% for intra-State supplies of certain food preparations classified under tariff items 19 or 21. These goods are specifically intended for free distribution to economically weaker sections of society, as part of government-approved programs. To qualify for this tax rate, suppliers must secure a certificate from an officer at least at the rank of Deputy Secretary, confirming that the food preparations have been distributed as intended. This certificate must be presented within five months from the supply date, although the jurisdictional commissioner has the discretion to extend this period if necessary. The notification also clarifies that the terms used, such as tariff items and headings, are defined according to the Customs Tariff Act of 1975, and the interpretation rules from this Act will apply to this notification. This change primarily affects suppliers of food preparations who are involved in programs aimed at supporting economically weaker sections.
key_points: The central tax rate of 2.5% is notified for intra-State supplies of specific goods.
This notification applies to food preparations under tariff items 19 or 21.
These goods must be intended for free distribution to economically weaker sections.
Suppliers must obtain a certificate from a Deputy Secretary level officer confirming the distribution.
The certificate must be produced within five months from the date of supply.
The jurisdictional commissioner may allow an extension for the certificate submission.
The definitions of tariff items, sub-headings, headings, and chapters refer to the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
Interpretation rules from the Customs Tariff Act apply to this notification.

1171.summary: Notification No. 40/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 18th October 2017, establishes a 5% integrated tax on inter-State supplies of certain food preparations. These preparations must be packaged in unit containers and intended for free distribution to economically weaker sections of society under a program approved by either the Central or State Government. To qualify for this tax rate, suppliers must obtain a certificate from an officer at least at the rank of Deputy Secretary, confirming that the goods have been distributed as intended. This certificate must be submitted within five months from the date of supply, although extensions may be granted by the relevant jurisdictional commissioner. The notification also clarifies that the definitions and classifications of goods are based on the Customs Tariff Act, 1975, and that its interpretation rules apply here. This change primarily affects suppliers involved in the distribution of food preparations under government-approved programs, ensuring they understand the tax implications and compliance requirements.
key_points: The notification is dated 18th October 2017.
It introduces a 5% integrated tax rate on inter-State supplies of specific goods.
The goods affected are food preparations in unit containers for free distribution to economically weaker sections.
These goods must be supplied under a program approved by the Central or State Government.
A certificate from a Deputy Secretary level officer is required to confirm the distribution of these goods.
The certificate must be produced within five months of the supply date, or within an extended period allowed by the jurisdictional commissioner.
The notification references the Customs Tariff Act, 1975 for tariff classifications.
Interpretation rules from the Customs Tariff Act apply to this notification.

1172.summary: Notification No. 40/2017-Central Tax introduces an exemption for the intra-State supply of taxable goods by registered suppliers to registered recipients for export, reducing the central tax rate to 0.05%. This exemption is contingent upon several conditions. Firstly, the registered recipient must export the goods within 90 days of receiving the tax invoice. Additionally, they must include the GSTIN of the supplier and the tax invoice number in their shipping documentation. The recipient must also be registered with an Export Promotion Council or a recognized Commodity Board. An order for the goods at the concessional rate must be placed with the supplier, and a copy of this order should be submitted to the supplier's tax officer. Furthermore, the goods must be transported directly to the port or a registered warehouse for export. If the recipient aggregates supplies from multiple suppliers, the goods must first go to a registered warehouse before export. Failure to export within the stipulated time will disqualify the recipient from this exemption.
key_points: Exemption applies to intra-State supply of taxable goods by a registered supplier to a registered recipient for export.
The central tax rate is reduced to 0.05% for eligible transactions under section 9 of the CGST Act.
Goods must be exported within 90 days from the date of the tax invoice issued by the registered supplier.
The registered recipient must provide the GSTIN of the supplier and the tax invoice number in the shipping bill or bill of export.
The registered recipient must be registered with an Export Promotion Council or a Commodity Board recognized by the Department of Commerce.
An order must be placed on the registered supplier for procuring goods at the concessional rate, with a copy provided to the jurisdictional tax officer.
Goods must be moved directly to the port or a registered warehouse before export.
If aggregating supplies from multiple suppliers, goods must first go to a registered warehouse before being exported.

1173.summary: Notification No. 41/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on October 23, 2017, introduces an exemption for the inter-State supply of taxable goods intended for export. This exemption applies to registered suppliers who sell to registered recipients, allowing them to pay only 0.1% integrated tax instead of the full rate. To qualify, several conditions must be met: the supplier must issue a tax invoice, and the recipient must export the goods within 90 days. Additionally, the recipient must include the supplier's GSTIN and invoice number in the shipping documentation and be registered with an Export Promotion Council or Commodity Board. The recipient must also place a concessional order with the supplier and ensure that goods are transported directly to the export point or a registered warehouse. If multiple suppliers are involved, goods must first go to a registered warehouse before export. Importantly, if the recipient fails to export the goods within the stipulated time, the exemption will not apply.
key_points: Exempts inter-State supply of taxable goods for export from integrated tax above 0.1% under section 5 of the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
Conditions for exemption include the registered supplier issuing a tax invoice and the registered recipient exporting goods within 90 days.
The registered recipient must indicate the supplier's GSTIN and tax invoice number in the shipping bill or bill of export.
The registered recipient must be registered with an Export Promotion Council or a Commodity Board recognized by the Department of Commerce.
An order must be placed on the registered supplier for procuring goods at a concessional rate, with a copy provided to the jurisdictional tax officer.
Goods must move directly from the supplier to the port or a registered warehouse before export.
If aggregating supplies from multiple suppliers, goods must first go to a registered warehouse before moving to the export location.
The registered supplier loses exemption if the registered recipient does not export the goods within 90 days.

1174.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 41/2017-Central Tax on 14th November 2017, amending the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the substitution of S. Nos. 1 and 2 in Schedule I, which now specify certain goods subject to a 2.5% GST, while S. Nos. 3 to 6 have been omitted. In Schedule II, S. Nos. 2 to 10 have been removed, and S. No. 11 has been updated to include condensed milk. New entries have been added for diabetic foods and medical grade oxygen. Additionally, the amendments clarify conditions related to registered brand names and actionable claims for various goods. Notably, products like jute twine, coir cordage, and specific leather items have been added or substituted in the schedules. These changes will affect manufacturers and suppliers of the specified goods, ensuring they are aware of the updated GST rates and conditions.
key_points: Notification No. 41/2017-Central Tax was issued on 14th November 2017.
Amendments were made to Notification No. 1/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, S. No. 1 and S. No. 2 were substituted to include specific goods under 2.5% GST.
S. Nos. 3, 4, 5, and 6 were omitted from Schedule I.
In Schedule II, S. Nos. 2 to 10 were omitted, and S. No. 11 was substituted to include condensed milk.
New entries were added to Schedule II, including diabetic foods and medical grade oxygen.
Several entries in both schedules were updated to clarify conditions regarding registered brand names and actionable claims.
Specific goods like jute twine, coir cordage, and various leather products were added or substituted in the schedules.

1175.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 42/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 27th October 2017, which introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. Specifically, a new entry, numbered 10D, has been added to the existing table after serial number 10C. This new entry relates to the supply of services that have their place of supply in Nepal or Bhutan, and it specifies that these services will be taxed at a rate of Nil. Importantly, payments for these services must be made in Indian Rupees. This amendment is made following the recommendations of the GST Council and aims to facilitate trade with neighboring countries. The principal notification had previously been amended by Notification No. 33/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017, but this latest change specifically addresses the tax treatment of cross-border services with Nepal and Bhutan.
key_points: Notification No. 42/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 27th October 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 9/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added to the table after serial number 10C.
The new entry is 10D, which pertains to services supplied with a place of supply in Nepal or Bhutan.
The tax rate for these services is set to Nil.
Payments for these services must be made in Indian Rupees.
The amendment is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The principal notification was last amended by Notification No. 33/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 13th October 2017.

1176.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 42/2017-Central Tax (Rate) on 14th November 2017, which amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This new notification replaces the entries for S. Nos. 8 and 9 in the Schedule, specifying new classifications for fresh or chilled goods and other goods with certain conditions. Additionally, S. Nos. 10 to 24 have been omitted entirely. New entries have been introduced after S. No. 30, specifically for fresh or chilled goods (S. No. 30A) and other goods (S. No. 30B). The description in S. No. 46 has been updated to include 'dried' goods. Furthermore, new entries for specific roots and tubers have been added after S. No. 46. Lastly, the definition of 'registered brand name' has been revised to include brands registered after 15th May 2017. These changes will affect businesses dealing with the specified goods and brands, requiring them to adjust their GST classifications accordingly.
key_points: Notification No. 42/2017-Central Tax (Rate) was issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 2/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
S. Nos. 8 and 9 in the Schedule are replaced with new entries for fresh or chilled goods and other goods with specific conditions.
S. Nos. 10 to 24 are omitted from the Schedule.
New entries are added after S. No. 30 for fresh or chilled goods and other goods under S. Nos. 30A and 30B.
S. No. 46 is amended to include 'dried' in the description of goods.
New entries are added after S. No. 46, including S. Nos. 46A and 46B for specific roots and tubers.
The definition of 'registered brand name' is updated to include brands registered after 15th May 2017.

1177.summary: The Government of India has issued Notification No. 43/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 14th November 2017, which amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Key changes include the substitution of entries for S. No. 1 and S. No. 2 in Schedule I, which now specify that all goods (excluding fresh or chilled items) in unit containers must bear a registered brand name. Additionally, S. Nos. 3 to 6 have been omitted from Schedule I. In Schedule II, several entries have been removed, specifically S. Nos. 2 to 10, while S. No. 11 has been updated to include condensed milk. New entries have also been introduced for diabetic foods and medical grade oxygen, among others. This notification clarifies product descriptions and conditions for various goods, impacting manufacturers and suppliers in these categories. The changes aim to streamline GST rates and ensure clarity in the classification of goods.
key_points: Notification No. 43/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 1/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, S. No. 1 and S. No. 2 entries are substituted to include all goods (other than fresh or chilled) in unit containers with registered brand names.
S. Nos. 3, 4, 5, 6 are omitted from Schedule I.
In Schedule II, S. Nos. 2 to 10 are omitted, and S. No. 11 is substituted to include condensed milk.
New entries are added in Schedule II, including diabetic foods and medical grade oxygen.
Several entries in both schedules are updated to clarify product descriptions and conditions.
New serial numbers and entries are added for various goods, including aircraft engines and jute products.

1178.summary: Notification No. 43/2017-Central Tax, issued on 14th November 2017, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry, labeled as 4A, to the existing table. This entry pertains to '5201 Raw cotton' and is applicable to 'Agriculturist' and 'Any registered person'. This means that registered persons dealing with raw cotton will now have specific provisions under the GST framework. The changes will take effect from 15th November 2017, ensuring that those involved in the agricultural sector, particularly in raw cotton production and sales, are aware of this new classification. This amendment aims to clarify the tax treatment for raw cotton, thereby aiding compliance and understanding for those in the agricultural industry.
key_points: Notification No. 43/2017-Central Tax issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry, 4A, is added to the table, specifying '5201 Raw cotton' for 'Agriculturist' and 'Any registered person'.
The amendment comes into effect from 15th November 2017.

1179.summary: Notification No. 44/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 14th November 2017, brings significant amendments to the earlier Notification No. 2/2017 dated 28th June 2017. Key changes include the substitution of entries for S. Nos. 8, 9, 21, and 22, which now specify new classifications for various goods, particularly those that are fresh or chilled. Additionally, S. Nos. 10 through 17 have been omitted entirely. New entries, such as 30A and 30B, have been introduced for specific goods, enhancing clarity on tax applicability. The definition of 'registered brand name' has also been revised to encompass brands registered after 15th May 2017, ensuring that businesses are aware of the updated criteria. This notification will take effect from 15th November 2017, impacting businesses dealing with the specified goods and requiring them to adjust their tax calculations accordingly.
key_points: This notification is issued under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It amends Notification No. 2/2017 - Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The changes in the Schedule include substitutions for S. Nos. 8, 9, 21, and 22, among others.
New entries are added after S. No. 30 and S. No. 43, including 30A and 30B for specific goods.
The definition of 'registered brand name' is updated to include brands registered after 15th May 2017.
The notification comes into effect on 15th November 2017.

1180.summary: Notification No. 44/2017-Central Tax, issued on 14th November 2017, introduces amendments to the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017. The key changes involve the substitution of entries for Sl. No. 6A, 6B, and 6C in the tax rate table. Specifically, Sl. No. 6A now covers 'Knotted netting of twine, cordage or rope; made up fishing nets and other made up nets, of textile materials', which broadens the scope of taxable items under this category. Sl. No. 6B has been updated to include 'Corduroy fabrics', while Sl. No. 6C now specifies 'Narrow woven fabrics, other than goods of heading 5807; narrow fabrics consisting of warp without weft assembled by means of an adhesive (bolducs)'. These amendments will come into effect from 15th November 2017, impacting manufacturers and suppliers of these textile products by clarifying their GST obligations.
key_points: Notification No. 44/2017-Central Tax was issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendments specifically substitute entries for Sl. No. 6A, 6B, and 6C in the table.
Sl. No. 6A now includes 'Knotted netting of twine, cordage or rope; made up fishing nets and other made up nets, of textile materials'.
Sl. No. 6B now includes 'Corduroy fabrics'.
Sl. No. 6C now includes 'Narrow woven fabrics, other than goods of heading 5807; narrow fabrics consisting of warp without weft assembled by means of an adhesive (bolducs)'.
These changes will take effect from 15th November 2017.

1181.summary: Notification No. 45/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 14th November 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it adds a new entry in the tax rate table, designated as Sl. No. 4A, which covers '5201 Raw cotton'. This entry is applicable to both 'Agriculturist' and 'Any registered person', indicating that registered individuals dealing with raw cotton will be affected by this change. The amendment is set to take effect from 15th November 2017, meaning that any transactions involving raw cotton after this date will need to adhere to the updated tax provisions. This change is significant for those in the agricultural sector and registered businesses dealing with raw cotton, as it clarifies the tax obligations related to this commodity.
key_points: Notification No. 45/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added to the table after Sl. No. 4, specifically Sl. No. 4A.
Sl. No. 4A pertains to '5201 Raw cotton' and is applicable to 'Agriculturist' and 'Any registered person'.
The changes in this notification come into effect from 15th November 2017.

1182.summary: Notification No. 45/2017-Central Tax, issued on 14th November 2017, introduces an exemption from central tax exceeding 2.5% for specific goods supplied to designated institutions. This exemption applies to public funded research institutions, research institutions, departments of the central and state governments, and Regional Cancer Centres. The goods eligible for this exemption include scientific and technical instruments, computers, software, and live animals intended for experimental purposes. To qualify for the exemption, institutions must provide certificates from their heads and relevant government departments at the time of supply. This notification is effective from 15th November 2017, meaning that any qualifying supplies made after this date can benefit from the reduced tax rate. This change is significant for institutions engaged in research, as it lowers their tax burden on essential equipment and materials needed for their work.
key_points: Notification No. 45/2017-Central Tax issued on 14th November 2017.
Exempts certain goods from central tax exceeding 2.5% when supplied to specified institutions.
Goods include scientific instruments, computers, software, and live animals for experimental purposes.
Conditions for exemption include certificates from heads of institutions and specific government departments.
Applies to public funded research institutions, research institutions, departments of central and state governments, and Regional Cancer Centres.
The exemption is effective from 15th November 2017.

1183.summary: Notification No. 45/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 14th November 2017, introduces an amendment to the earlier Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The key change is the addition of a new entry in the tax rate table, specifically Sl. No. 4A, which relates to 'Raw cotton' classified under code 5201. This entry is designated for 'Agriculturist' and is applicable to 'Any registered person'. The amendment aims to clarify the tax treatment of raw cotton under the Integrated Goods and Services Tax (IGST) framework. This change will be effective from 15th November 2017, meaning that registered persons dealing with raw cotton will need to be aware of this new classification and its implications for their tax obligations. Overall, this amendment is significant for those in the agricultural sector, particularly those involved in the production and sale of raw cotton.
key_points: Notification No. 45/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 4/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
A new entry is added in the table after Sl. No. 4, specifically Sl. No. 4A.
The new entry 4A pertains to 'Raw cotton' with the classification code 5201.
The applicable category for this entry is 'Agriculturist'.
The amendment is applicable to 'Any registered person'.
This notification comes into effect from 15th November 2017.

1184.summary: Notification No. 46/2017-Central Tax, issued on 14th November 2017, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) effective from 15th November 2017. One significant change is the replacement of 'Services provided' with 'Composite supply of works contract' in the context of works contracts. Additionally, the provisions for food services provided by restaurants and canteens have been clarified. Specifically, if these services are provided for cash or other consideration and the accommodation tariff is ₹7,500 or more, they will be taxed at 2.5% without input tax credit. This applies to both food services within hotels and those outside. Furthermore, item (iv) under serial number 7 has been removed, and item (ix) has been clarified to indicate that food and beverage services not covered by previous items will have a different tax rate. Lastly, the amendment includes the addition of 'manufacture of handicraft goods' under serial number 26, aligning it with definitions from another notification. These changes primarily affect businesses in the hospitality and food service sectors.
key_points: This notification is issued on 14th November 2017 and comes into effect from 15th November 2017.
It amends Notification No. 11/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.

In the Table, against serial number 3, 'Services provided' is replaced with 'Composite supply of works contract as defined in clause (119) of section 2 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017'.

At serial number 7, item (i) is substituted to specify that food services provided by restaurants, canteens, etc., for cash or other consideration, are taxed at 2.5% without input tax credit if the accommodation tariff is ₹7,500 or more.
Item (iii) under serial number 7 is also substituted with similar provisions for food services provided within hotels and similar establishments.
Item (iv) under serial number 7 is omitted.
Item (ix) is clarified to state that food and beverage services not covered by previous items will attract a different tax rate.
At serial number 26, item (i) is amended to include 'manufacture of handicraft goods' with a reference to its definition in another notification.

1185.summary: Notification No. 46/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 14th November 2017, amends the earlier Notification No. 5/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. This amendment specifically updates the entries for Sl. No. 6A, 6B, and 6C in the tax rate table. The new entry for Sl. No. 6A now describes 'Knotted netting of twine, cordage or rope; made up fishing nets and other made up nets, of textile materials'. For Sl. No. 6B, the entry has been updated to 'Corduroy fabrics', while Sl. No. 6C now includes 'Narrow woven fabrics, other than goods of heading 5807; narrow fabrics consisting of warp without weft assembled by means of an adhesive (bolducs)'. These changes will take effect from 15th November 2017. This amendment follows the previous update made by Notification No. 29/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 22nd September 2017, ensuring that the tax rates reflect current market practices and product classifications.
key_points: This notification is issued as Notification No. 46/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 14th November 2017.
It amends Notification No. 5/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
The amendment substitutes entries for Sl. No. 6A, 6B, and 6C in the table.
Sl. No. 6A now includes 'Knotted netting of twine, cordage or rope; made up fishing nets and other made up nets, of textile materials'.
Sl. No. 6B now includes 'Corduroy fabrics'.
Sl. No. 6C now includes 'Narrow woven fabrics, other than goods of heading 5807; narrow fabrics consisting of warp without weft assembled by means of an adhesive (bolducs)'.
The changes come into effect from 15th November 2017.
The principal notification was last amended by Notification No. 29/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) on 22nd September 2017.

1186.summary: Notification No. 47/2017-Central Tax, issued on 14th November 2017, introduces several amendments to the earlier Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. The most significant change is the substitution of the entry for serial number 11A, which now specifies that services provided by Fair Price Shops to the Central Government, State Government, or Union Territories for the sale of food grains and other essentials under the Public Distribution System will be recognized. Additionally, serial number 11B and its entries have been completely removed. A new entry, serial number 79A, has been added, which pertains to services for admission to protected monuments as defined under the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958, with a tax rate set at 'Nil'. These changes will take effect from 15th November 2017, impacting businesses involved in these services and the relevant government bodies.
key_points: Notification No. 47/2017-Central Tax issued on 14th November 2017.
Amends Notification No. 12/2017-Central Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Substitutes entry for serial number 11A to include services by Fair Price Shops to government bodies for food distribution.
Omitted serial number 11B and its related entries.
Inserts new serial number 79A for services related to admission to protected monuments under the Ancient Monuments and Archaeological Sites and Remains Act, 1958.
New entry for serial number 79A specifies a tax rate of 'Nil'.
This notification comes into effect from 15th November 2017.

1187.summary: Notification No. 47/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 14th November 2017, introduces an exemption from integrated tax exceeding 5% for specific goods supplied to designated institutions. This includes scientific instruments, computers, software, and live animals intended for experimental purposes. The exemption applies to public funded research institutions, research institutions, departments of the Central and State Governments, and Regional Cancer Centres. To qualify for this exemption, institutions must provide certificates from relevant government officers and heads of institutions, confirming that the goods are required solely for research purposes. The notification clarifies that the exemption is effective from 15th November 2017, allowing these institutions to benefit from reduced tax liabilities on essential research-related supplies. This change aims to support research activities by easing the financial burden of tax on necessary equipment and materials.
key_points: Notification No. 47/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 14th November 2017.
Exempts certain goods from integrated tax exceeding 5% when supplied to specified institutions.
Goods include scientific instruments, computers, software, and live animals for experimental purposes.
Conditions for exemption include certification from relevant government officers and heads of institutions.
Applicable to public funded research institutions, research institutions, departments of Central and State Governments, and Regional Cancer Centres.
The exemption is effective from 15th November 2017.

1188.summary: The recent Notification No. 48/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) amends the earlier Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017. One significant change is the replacement of 'Services provided' with 'Composite supply of works contract' in the context of works contracts. Additionally, for food services provided by restaurants and canteens, if the declared tariff is below ₹7,500 per day, a 5% tax applies. Conversely, if these services are provided in hotels or similar establishments with a declared tariff of ₹7,500 or more, they are also taxed at 5%. The notification also omits a previous item related to food services and introduces 'manufacture of handicraft goods' as a taxable service under serial number 26. The definition of 'handicraft goods' aligns with the earlier Notification No. 32/2017-Central Tax. These changes will take effect from 15th November 2017, impacting businesses involved in food services and handicraft manufacturing.
key_points: This notification amends Notification No. 8/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
It substitutes the phrase 'Services provided' with 'Composite supply of works contract' in serial number 3, item (vi).
For serial number 7, item (i), it specifies that food services provided by restaurants, canteens, etc., are taxable at 5% if the declared tariff is below ₹7,500 per day.
For serial number 7, item (iii), it states that food services provided in hotels, etc., are taxable at 5% if the declared tariff is ₹7,500 or above per day.
Item (iv) under serial number 7 is omitted.
For serial number 26, item (i), it adds 'manufacture of handicraft goods' as a taxable service.
The term 'handicraft goods' is defined as per Notification No. 32/2017-Central Tax dated 15th September 2017.
This notification comes into effect from 15th November 2017.

1189.summary: Notification No. 50/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate), issued on 14th November 2017, introduces an important amendment to the earlier Notification No. 30/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) from 22nd September 2017. Specifically, it modifies the language in Table, column (4), expanding the scope of distribution entities. Previously, the text referred only to 'dairy cooperatives,' but it has now been updated to include 'companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013.' This change allows companies, in addition to dairy cooperatives, to be involved in the distribution process under the specified tax provisions. The amendment takes effect from 15th November 2017, meaning that businesses involved in the distribution of goods through these entities should take note of this change. This adjustment reflects the government's intent to broaden the eligibility for tax benefits or provisions related to integrated tax for a wider range of distributors in the dairy sector.
key_points: Notification No. 50/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) was issued on 14th November 2017.
This notification amends Notification No. 30/2017-Integrated Tax (Rate) dated 22nd September 2017.

The amendment changes the wording in Table, column (4) from '[for distribution through dairy cooperatives]' to '[for distribution through dairy cooperatives or companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013)]'.
The effective date of this amendment is 15th November 2017.

1190.summary: Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, establishes the tax rates for goods supplied within Union Territories. The notification outlines six different tax rates: 2.5%, 6%, 9%, 14%, 1.5%, and 0.125%, applicable to various categories of goods listed in six schedules. Schedule I, for example, includes goods like frozen fish, UHT milk, and certain vegetables, all taxed at 2.5%. Other schedules specify higher rates for different goods, with Schedule II at 6%, Schedule III at 9%, Schedule IV at 14%, Schedule V at 1.5%, and Schedule VI at 0.125%. This tax is specifically levied on intra-State supplies, meaning it applies to transactions occurring within the same Union Territory. Businesses dealing in these goods need to be aware of the specific rates applicable to their products to with the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act.
key_points: The notification is dated 28th June 2017.
It specifies Union Territory tax rates for goods in six schedules: 2.5%, 6%, 9%, 14%, 1.5%, and 0.125%.
Schedule I lists goods taxed at 2.5%, including various types of fish, milk products, and certain vegetables.
Schedule II includes goods taxed at 6%, while Schedule III is for goods taxed at 9%.
Goods in Schedule IV are taxed at 14%, Schedule V at 1.5%, and Schedule VI at 0.125%.
The tax applies to intra-State supplies of goods as described in the corresponding entries of the schedules.

1191.summary: Notification No. 01/2017-Central Tax, issued on 21st June 2017, establishes that specific provisions of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will take effect from 22nd June 2017. The sections that are being activated include sections 1 through 5, along with sections 17, 21, and 22. This means that the foundational aspects of the Union Territory GST framework will now be operational, allowing for the implementation of GST in Union Territories. Businesses operating in these regions will need to be aware of these provisions as they outline the basic structure and definitions relevant to the GST regime. This notification is crucial for ensuring that the GST system is effectively established in Union Territories, impacting all stakeholders involved in the supply of goods and services in these areas.
key_points: The notification is dated 21st June 2017.
It appoints 22nd June 2017 as the date when certain provisions of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 come into force.
The provisions coming into force include sections 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 17, 21, and 22 of the Act.
This notification is issued under the powers conferred by sub-section (3) of section 1 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

1192.summary: The corrigendum issued on 12th July 2017 amends the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. It specifies that 'Coffee, whether or not roasted or decaffeinated' should now be referred to as 'Coffee roasted, whether or not decaffeinated'. Additionally, a new entry for 'Bran, sharps and other residues...' has been added on page 84. The notification also corrects the code from '2710 19 00' to '2711 19 00' on page 86 and modifies the classification from '84 or 85' to '84, 85 or 94' on page 89. Furthermore, a new entry for various citrus fruits has been included on page 96. Several updates to item codes have been made on page 98, affecting lines 5 through 8 and line 37. Lastly, a new entry for 'Road tractors for semi-trailers...' has been added on page 132. These changes impact businesses dealing with these specific goods and require them to update their records accordingly.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes 'Coffee, whether or not roasted or decaffeinated' to 'Coffee roasted, whether or not decaffeinated' on page 81, line 29.
Inserts new entry 'Bran, sharps and other residues...' on page 84 after line 12.
Changes '2710 19 00' to '2711 19 00' on page 86, line 36.
Changes '84 or 85' to '84, 85 or 94' on page 89, line 21.
Inserts new entry for 'Citrus fruit...' on page 96 after line 28.
Updates codes for various items on page 98, lines 5-8 and line 37.
Inserts new entry 'Road tractors for semi-trailers...' on page 132 after line 29.

1193.summary: The recent corrigendum issued on July 27, 2017, modifies several entries in the earlier Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from June 28, 2017. Key changes include the replacement of '1106 10 90' with '0713' in S. No. 59 and 61 of Schedule I. Additionally, S. No. 92 now includes '1702 or 1704' and expands the description of Palmyra sugar to include 'mishri, batasha, bura'. Other updates in Schedule I include changing 'Agarbatti' to 'Agarbatti, lobhan' in S. No. 185 and correcting entries for 'saima', 'gotasitara', and 'glzal' in S. No. 220. Furthermore, S. No. 234 now includes a new entry for photovoltaic cells. In Schedule II, S. No. 14 has been updated to include 'and desiccated coconuts'. Lastly, in Schedule III, S. No. 55 has been revised to clarify the exclusion of fountain pen and ball pen ink. These changes affect businesses dealing with these goods, ensuring they are aligned with the updated tax classifications.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In Schedule I, S. No. 59 and 61, replaces '1106 10 90' with '0713'.
In Schedule I, S. No. 92, updates column (2) to read '1702 or 1704' and column (3) to include 'mishri, batasha, bura' with 'Palmyra sugar'.
In Schedule I, S. No. 185, changes 'Agarbatti' to 'Agarbatti, lobhan'.
In Schedule I, S. No. 220, modifies entries for 'saima', 'gotasitara', and 'glzal' to 'salma', 'gota, sitara', and 'gizai' respectively.
In Schedule I, S. No. 234, adds 'Photo voltaic cells, whether or not assembled in modules or made up into panels' after entry (g).
In Schedule II, S. No. 14, adds 'and desiccated coconuts' after 'shelled or peeled'.
In Schedule III, S. No. 55, changes '(Fountain pen ink and Ball pen ink)' to '(other than Fountain pen ink and Ball pen ink)'.

1194.summary: On 30th June 2017, a corrigendum was issued to amend Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate), which was originally published on 28th June 2017. The first change clarifies that the reference to '30' now includes '30 or any Chapter', providing broader applicability. Additionally, the phrase 'other than those' has been removed from the notification, which may affect the interpretation of certain tax provisions. Furthermore, the notification omits the specific wording regarding 'goggles and the like, corrective, protective or other', which could impact the classification of these items under the tax regime. These amendments are important for businesses dealing with Union Territory taxes, as they may need to adjust their understanding and application of the tax rates and classifications based on these changes. Overall, these corrections aim to enhance clarity and precision in the tax regulations.
key_points: The corrigendum was issued on 30th June 2017.
It amends Notification No. 1/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
In the notification, '30' is replaced with '30 or any Chapter' on page 87, line 15.
The phrase 'other than those' is removed from page 108, line 54.
The words 'goggles and the like, corrective, protective or other' are omitted from page 122, line 22.

1195.summary: This corrigendum, issued on 12th July 2017, modifies Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it updates the description of 'Dried leguminous vegetables, shelled, whether or not skinned or split' to clarify that this category does not include products that are packaged in unit containers and carry a registered brand name. Additionally, the corrigendum removes the phrase '[proposed GST Nil]' from the notification, indicating that there is no longer a proposal for a nil GST rate on these items. These changes are significant for businesses dealing with dried leguminous vegetables, as they will need to be aware of the new classification and any implications for GST rates. This update ensures clarity in the tax treatment of these products, helping businesses comply with the revised regulations.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes the description of 'Dried leguminous vegetables, shelled, whether or not skinned or split' to exclude those put up in unit containers with a registered brand name.
Removes the phrase '[proposed GST Nil]' from the notification.
The corrigendum was issued on 12th July 2017.

1196.summary: This corrigendum, issued on 27th July 2017, amends the earlier Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) from 28th June 2017. Specifically, it modifies the entries in the Schedule related to certain tax rates. For S.No. 59, the previous entry of '9' has been updated to '7, 9 or 10', allowing for a broader range of applicable tax rates. Additionally, for S.No. 102, the entry has been corrected from '2302' to '2301, 2302', which clarifies the classification of goods under the Union Territory Tax. These changes are important for businesses operating in the Union Territories, as they directly affect the tax rates applicable to certain goods. It is essential for affected businesses to review these changes to ensure they are applying the correct tax rates in their transactions.
key_points: Amends Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) dated 28th June 2017.
Changes S.No. 59 in the Schedule: replaces '9' with '7, 9 or 10' in column (2).
Changes S.No. 102 in the Schedule: replaces '2302' with '2301, 2302' in column (2).
The corrigendum was issued on 27th July 2017.

1197.summary: The Government of India issued Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) on 28th June 2017, which exempts certain intra-State supplies of goods from the Union Territory tax as per section 7 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This exemption applies to a wide range of goods listed in the accompanying Schedule, which includes 138 specific categories such as live animals, various types of meat, fish, dairy products, and a variety of fruits and vegetables. Notably, the exemption is granted to goods that are not packaged in unit containers and do not carry a registered brand name. This means that businesses dealing in these specific goods can benefit from reduced tax liabilities, thereby potentially lowering their overall costs. The notification is part of the government's efforts to streamline tax regulations and support local businesses within the Union Territories. It is essential for businesses in these sectors to be aware of this exemption to take full advantage of the tax relief provided.
key_points: Notification No. 2/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification exempts certain intra-State supplies of goods from the Union Territory tax under section 7 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The exemption applies to goods listed in the Schedule, including live animals, meat, fish, dairy products, fruits, vegetables, and various agricultural products.
The Schedule specifies detailed tariff items and descriptions for 138 categories of goods eligible for exemption.
Goods such as live poultry, fresh milk, and various types of fruits and vegetables are included in the exemption list.
The exemption is applicable to goods that are not put up in unit containers and do not bear a registered brand name.
This notification is part of the broader framework of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

1198.summary: Notification No. 02/2017-Central Tax, issued on June 27, 2017, allows eligible registered persons with an aggregate turnover of up to seventy five lakh rupees to opt for a composition scheme under the GST framework. This scheme enables manufacturers to pay a tax rate of one percent of their turnover in the Union Territory. For those engaged in specific supplies, the tax rate is set at two and a half percent, while other suppliers can pay half percent of their turnover. However, manufacturers of certain goods, specifically ice cream (tariff item 2105), pan masala (tariff item 2106), and all tobacco products (tariff item 24), are excluded from this composition levy. This notification is based on the provisions of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, ensuring that small businesses can benefit from simplified tax compliance while maintaining clarity on which goods are affected.
key_points: Eligible registered persons with an aggregate turnover not exceeding seventy five lakh rupees can opt for a composition scheme.
Manufacturers can pay one percent of their turnover in the Union Territory as tax.
Persons making specific supplies can pay two and a half percent of their turnover in the Union Territory.
Other suppliers can pay half percent of their turnover in the Union Territory.
Manufacturers of certain goods listed in the Table are not eligible for the composition levy.
The specified goods include ice cream (tariff item 2105), pan masala (tariff item 2106), and all tobacco products (tariff item 24).
The notification is issued under section 10 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification was published on June 27, 2017.

1199.summary: Notification No. 3/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on June 28, 2017, introduces an exemption for intra-State supplies of certain goods related to petroleum operations from Union Territory tax, specifically at a rate of 2.5%. This exemption applies to goods listed in the notification's Table, which includes items necessary for petroleum exploration and operations, such as drilling rigs, seismic survey equipment, and oil field chemicals. To qualify for this exemption, the goods must be supplied to designated entities like the Oil and Natural Gas Corporation or contractors involved in petroleum operations. Additionally, suppliers must adhere to specific conditions, including obtaining certificates from the Directorate General of Hydro Carbons to confirm that the goods are required for the stated operations. The notification outlines detailed eligibility criteria and conditions that must be met to with the exemption provisions. Overall, this notification is significant for businesses engaged in the petroleum sector, as it reduces their tax burden on essential operational supplies.
key_points: Notification No. 3/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on June 28, 2017.
This notification exempts intra-State supplies of specific goods related to petroleum operations from Union Territory tax.
The exemption applies to goods listed in the Table under column (3) and is subject to conditions in column (5).
The applicable tax rate for these goods is set at 2.5%.
The goods must be supplied to specific entities such as Oil and Natural Gas Corporation or contractors involved in petroleum operations.
Conditions for exemption include the requirement of certificates from the Directorate General of Hydro Carbons.
The notification includes a detailed list of goods eligible for the exemption, including drilling rigs, seismic survey equipment, and oil field chemicals.
The exemption is contingent upon compliance with various conditions outlined in the notification.

1200.summary: Notification No. 03/2017-Central Tax, issued on 28th June 2017, announces that specific provisions of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will take effect from 1st July 2017. The sections that will come into force include 6 to 16, 18 to 20, and 23 to 26. This means that the rules and regulations outlined in these sections will now be applicable, impacting businesses operating in Union Territories. The notification is a crucial step in implementing the GST framework in these regions, ensuring that the tax structure is established and operational. This change is significant for businesses as it marks the beginning of compliance requirements under the new GST regime in Union Territories. The notification is issued under the authority granted by the Union Territory GST Act, emphasizing the government's commitment to a unified tax system across India.
key_points: The notification is dated 28th June 2017.
It appoints 1st July 2017 as the effective date for certain provisions.
Sections 6 to 16, 18 to 20, and 23 to 26 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 will come into force.
This notification is issued under the powers of sub-section (3) of section 1 of the Union Territory GST Act.

1201.summary: Notification No. 4/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, introduces specific goods that are subject to Union Territory tax under a reverse charge mechanism, effective from 1st July 2017. This means that the recipient of these goods, who must be a registered person, is responsible for paying the tax. The goods listed include cashew nuts (tariff item 0801), bidi wrapper leaves (1404 90 10), tobacco leaves (2401), and silk yarn (5004 to 5006). For cashew nuts, bidi wrapper leaves, and tobacco leaves, the supplier is an agriculturist, while silk yarn is supplied by a manufacturer of silk yarn from raw materials. Additionally, lottery supplies are specified, with the supplier being a State Government, Union Territory, or local authority, and the recipient being a lottery distributor or selling agent. This notification clarifies the tax obligations for these specific goods and the roles of suppliers and recipients in the Union Territories.
key_points: Notification No. 4/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
This notification specifies goods subject to Union Territory tax on a reverse charge basis.
The effective date for this notification is 1st July 2017.
Goods listed include cashew nuts (0801), bidi wrapper leaves (1404 90 10), tobacco leaves (2401), and silk yarn (5004 to 5006).
The supplier for cashew nuts, bidi wrapper leaves, and tobacco leaves is an agriculturist, while silk yarn is supplied by a manufacturer.
The recipient for all these goods must be a registered person.
Lottery supplies are provided by State Governments, Union Territories, or local authorities, with the recipient being a lottery distributor or selling agent.

1202.summary: Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax, issued on 30th June 2017, officially designates www.gst.gov.in as the Common Goods and Services Tax Electronic Portal. This portal is crucial for various GST-related activities, including registration, tax payments, filing returns, and managing integrated tax and electronic way bills. The notification is based on section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and aligns with section 146 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Importantly, this notification took effect on 22nd June 2017, meaning that the portal has been operational for these purposes since that date. The GST Network, a company established under the Companies Act, 2013, is responsible for managing this website. This change impacts all stakeholders involved in GST processes, ensuring they have a centralized platform for their tax-related activities.
key_points: Notification No. 4/2017-Central Tax was issued on 30th June 2017.
This notification designates www.gst.gov.in as the Common Goods and Services Tax Electronic Portal.
The portal will facilitate registration, payment of tax, furnishing of returns, computation, and settlement of integrated tax and electronic way bill.
The notification is issued under section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It is also in accordance with section 146 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The notification is effective from 22nd June 2017.
The GST Network manages the website as per the Companies Act, 2013.

1203.summary: Notification No. 5/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, outlines specific goods for which businesses will not be able to claim a refund of unutilised input tax credit. This applies when the tax rate on inputs exceeds the tax rate on the output supplies of these goods. The goods affected include various woven fabrics such as silk, wool, cotton, and manmade materials, as well as several railway-related items like locomotives and coaches. This notification is effective from 1st July 2017. The legal basis for this notification is found in the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, specifically in section 54, and the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. Businesses dealing in these specified goods should be aware that they will not receive refunds for any accumulated input tax credit related to these items, which could impact their cash flow and pricing strategies.
key_points: Notification No. 5/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification specifies goods for which no refund of unutilised input tax credit will be allowed.
The affected goods include various woven fabrics (silk, wool, cotton, manmade materials) and railway-related items.
This notification comes into effect from 1st July 2017.

The relevant sections cited are clause (ii) of the proviso to sub-section (3) of section 54 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

1204.summary: The Government of India has issued the 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) Rules, 2017', which will take effect from July 1, 2017. This notification modifies several existing rules to adapt the Central Goods and Services Tax framework for the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Specifically, Rule 1 now refers to the new Union Territory rules instead of the Central rules. Additionally, Rule 90(4) clarifies that any deficiencies noted in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central rules will also apply here. Changes in Rule 117(1) require applicants to specify certain claims related to the Central Sales Tax Act in their applications. Furthermore, clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) have been removed, simplifying the requirements. Rule 119 has been updated to mandate that individuals must declare their stock in FORM GST TRAN-1 within ninety days of the appointed day. Lastly, an explanation has been added to clarify that references to section 140 of the Central GST Act should be understood as referring to section 18 of the Union Territory GST Act. These changes primarily affect businesses operating in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, ensuring compliance with the new local GST framework.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) Rules, 2017'.
These rules come into effect from July 1, 2017.
Rule 1 substitutes 'Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017' with 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) Rules, 2017'.
Rule 90(4) now states that deficiencies communicated in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central GST Rules are also deemed communicated under these rules.
In Rule 117(1), the second proviso is replaced to require specific claims under the Central Sales Tax Act to be detailed in applications.
Clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) are omitted.
Rule 119 is replaced to require a declaration of stock held by a principal and agent in FORM GST TRAN-1 within ninety days of the appointed day.
An explanation is added clarifying that references to section 140 of the Central GST Act refer to section 18 of the Union Territory GST Act.

1205.summary: Notification No. 6/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017 and will take effect from 1st July 2017. This notification specifically identifies the Canteen Stores Department (CSD), which operates under the Ministry of Defence, as eligible to claim a refund of fifty percent of the Union Territory tax paid on goods it receives. This refund is applicable for goods that CSD acquires for the purpose of supplying them to Unit Run Canteens or to authorized customers. The legal basis for this notification is rooted in section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, and section 55 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017. This change primarily affects the CSD, allowing it to reduce its tax burden on goods supplied, thereby potentially lowering costs for the canteens and customers it serves.
key_points: Notification No. 6/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification comes into effect from 1st July 2017.
Canteen Stores Department (CSD) under the Ministry of Defence is specified in this notification.
CSD is entitled to claim a refund of fifty percent of the applicable Union Territory tax.
The refund applies to inward supplies of goods received by CSD for subsequent supply.
The subsequent supply is to Unit Run Canteens of CSD or authorized customers of CSD.
This notification is issued under the powers conferred by section 21 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
It is also in accordance with section 55 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.

1206.summary: The Government of India has issued the 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Chandigarh) Rules, 2017', which will take effect from July 1, 2017. This notification modifies several rules from the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules to apply specifically to Chandigarh. Notably, Rule 1 replaces references to the Central GST Rules with the new Union Territory rules. Additionally, Rule 90(4) clarifies that any deficiencies noted in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central GST framework will also apply under these new rules. In Rule 117(1), the second proviso has been updated to require detailed claims under the Central Sales Tax Act, while clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) have been removed. Furthermore, Rule 119 mandates that individuals must declare their stock in FORM GST TRAN-1 within ninety days of the appointed day. Lastly, an explanation has been added to clarify that references to section 140 of the Central GST Act should be interpreted as referring to section 18 of the Union Territory GST Act. These changes primarily affect businesses operating in Chandigarh, ensuring they comply with the new local GST regulations.
key_points: The notification is titled 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Chandigarh) Rules, 2017'.
These rules come into effect from July 1, 2017.
Rule 1 substitutes 'the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017' with 'the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Chandigarh) Rules, 2017'.
Rule 90(4) states that deficiencies communicated in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central GST Rules are also deemed communicated under these rules.
Rule 117(1) substitutes the second proviso to specify claims under the Central Sales Tax Act, 1956.
Clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) are omitted.
Rule 119 requires a declaration in FORM GST TRAN-1 for stock held by a principal and agent within ninety days of the appointed day.
An explanation clarifies that references to section 140 of the Central GST Act refer to section 18 of the Union Territory GST Act.

1207.summary: Notification No. 7/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate), issued on 28th June 2017, introduces an exemption from Union Territory tax for specific inward supplies of goods. This exemption applies to goods supplied by the Canteen Stores Department (CSD) to Unit Run Canteens and to authorized customers. Additionally, it covers goods supplied by Unit Run Canteens to authorized customers. The exemption is applicable to all tariff items, sub-headings, headings, or chapters as outlined in the Customs Tariff Act, 1975. This means that businesses involved in these transactions will not have to pay Union Territory tax on these specific supplies. The notification is effective from 1st July 2017, which means that any relevant transactions occurring on or after this date will benefit from this tax exemption. This change is significant for entities dealing with CSD and Unit Run Canteens, as it reduces their tax burden on these supplies.
key_points: Notification No. 7/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) was issued on 28th June 2017.
The notification exempts certain inward supplies of goods from Union Territory tax under section 7 of the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017.
The exempted supplies include goods supplied by the Canteen Stores Department (CSD) to Unit Run Canteens.
It also includes goods supplied by the CSD to authorized customers and goods supplied by Unit Run Canteens to authorized customers.
This exemption applies to all tariff items, sub-headings, headings, or chapters as specified in the Customs Tariff Act, 1975.
The notification is effective from 1st July 2017.

1208.summary: The Notification No. 7/2017 introduces the 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Dadra and Nagar Haveli) Rules, 2017', which will take effect from July 1, 2017. These rules adapt the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, specifically for the Union Territory of Dadra and Nagar Haveli, with several modifications. Notably, Rule 1 replaces references to the Central GST Rules with the new title. Additionally, Rule 90(4) clarifies that any deficiencies noted in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central GST Rules will also apply under these new rules. In Rule 117(1), the second proviso is updated to require detailed claims under the Central Sales Tax Act, 1956. Furthermore, clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) have been removed. Lastly, Rule 119 mandates that individuals subject to certain provisions must electronically declare their stock in FORM GST TRAN-1 within ninety days of the appointed day. These changes primarily affect businesses operating in Dadra and Nagar Haveli, ensuring they comply with the new local GST framework.
key_points: The rules are titled 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Dadra and Nagar Haveli) Rules, 2017'.
These rules come into effect from July 1, 2017.
The Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017, are adapted for Dadra and Nagar Haveli with specific modifications.
Rule 1 substitutes 'Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017' with 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Dadra and Nagar Haveli) Rules, 2017'.
Rule 90(4) specifies that deficiencies communicated in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules are also applicable under these rules.
Rule 117(1) substitutes the second proviso to specify claims under the Central Sales Tax Act, 1956.
Clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) are omitted.
Rule 119 requires a declaration of stock held by a principal and agent to be submitted electronically in FORM GST TRAN-1 within ninety days of the appointed day.

1209.summary: Notification No. 08/2017-Union Territory Tax (Rate) introduces an exemption for registered persons receiving intra-State supplies of goods or services from unregistered suppliers. This means that if a registered business purchases goods or services from a supplier who is not registered under the Union Territory Goods and Services Tax Act, they will not have to pay the Union Territory tax on those purchases, provided the total value of such supplies does not exceed five thousand rupees in a single day. This exemption is aimed at easing the tax burden on registered businesses and is based on recommendations from the GST Council. The exemption will take effect from July 1, 2017, allowing businesses to benefit from this provision immediately. However, businesses need to monitor their daily purchases from unregistered suppliers to ensure they stay within the five thousand rupee limit to qualify for this exemption.
key_points: Exempts intra-State supplies of goods or services received by a registered person from unregistered suppliers from Union Territory tax.
The exemption applies only if the total value of such supplies does not exceed five thousand rupees in a day.
This exemption is based on the recommendations of the GST Council.
The notification is effective from July 1, 2017.

1210.summary: The Government of India has issued the 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Daman and Diu) Rules, 2017', which will be effective from July 1, 2017. This notification adapts the Central Goods and Services Tax Rules, 2017 specifically for the Union Territory of Daman and Diu, incorporating several modifications. Notably, Rule 1 now refers to the 'Union Territory Goods and Services Tax (Daman and Diu) Rules, 2017' instead of the Central rules. Additionally, Rule 90(4) has been amended to clarify that any deficiencies noted in FORM GST RFD-03 under the Central rules will also apply here. Changes in Rule 117(1) require applicants to provide detailed claims related to the Central Sales Tax Act, while clauses (a) and (b) of Rule 117(4) have been removed. Furthermore, Rule 119 mandates that individuals must declare their stock in FORM GST TRAN-1 within ninety days of the appointed day.
